Chapter 37 - 37: New Member
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The smell of curry, spices, the sound of an egg being roasted, and the chit-chat of the new mother and daughter pair rang throughout the kitchen for a while before the humble table was filled with tasty-looking dishes.

Other than meat, there was almost everything there. Han Li hadn't bothered to buy meat earlier even though he no longer had any financial issues. But there were some cold fish that Yue Li cut and fried using her very own cooking techniques and spices exclusive to this world.

Looking at the table, Yue Li and Liyue couldn't help but smile.

"You should be the one to call him here this time. Go, I will set the plates," Yue Li said, patting the girl's back.

Liyue nodded. She knew the etiquette. But just as she was about to go and call Han Li, he came into the kitchen with a smile.

Looking at the table, he took a whiff.

"Smells great..." he said with a smile and immediately sat down. Yue Li motioned at Liyue to serve him.

She swallowed and briefly looked at his side profile before taking the rice and serving it to him.

"You guys also sit down," Han Li said and dragged his mother to sit beside him. On his right.

Liyue also served food for Yue Li. "Now, you also sit down." Han Li made her sit to his left.

She was a bit shy but held it back and got comfortable. As comfortable as she could get sitting mere inches away from a man.

Han Li took it by him to serve her food and with a nod, he said, "Let's eat."

Yue Li also nodded and they dug into the dish. Mother was indeed a great cook that once they started eating, even Han Li who had tasted all sorts of delicacy, couldn't help but praise inside. Earth's cooking style was indeed different from theirs.

Even though Yue Li lacked some spices and tools, her intuition as a cook was top-notch.

"Mom, take some eggs from there," Han Li said.

Yue Li paused, took the plate of fried eggs, and passed it to him.

Han Li looked at her face and saw a rice stuck on her cheek. He casually took it and put it into his mouth.

Liyue couldn't help but pause in the middle of her eating. 'They are really close...' Yue Li didn't even feel the action to be noticeable. It was such a normal thing for them. But to her, it seemed so amazing.

Han Li got his portion and he passed some to Liyue. "Mom has a knack for any dish with eggs, try it."

She tentatively took a piece and gently placed it into her mouth. It was still warm and immediately after eating, she couldn't help but flinch.

'Spicy...!' It was the spiciest thing she had ever eaten.

But Han Li was already ready with water and gave it to her with a smile. "How is it?" He asked.

She drank the cool water and after a bit, calmed her tastebuds down. "It is spicy but I like it. How did you do it... Mother, I never saw you doing something different from normal."

"She has a knack with spices that even I couldn't really figure out with all the time I spent watching her. It will take time for you to learn," Han Li said, quite proud of his mother's talent.

After that, the three silently ate the rest of the food with occasional small talk in between. And before they knew it, they were full and the plates were about empty.

Han Li satisfactorily drank the cool lemon juice that she had made and with a sigh, said, "Good food, cook meat tomorrow..."

Yue Li wondered whether it was too extravagant. True, they had gotten a small fortune but what about after it was over? But she didn't ask. She had to let her son maintain his face infront of his new wife-to-be.

They washed their hands and soon, they were clean.

Liyue hesitated but asked, "I am going back for now... Can I take the rest of the food for my mother?"

Yue Li nodded. "I will pack it for you, and he will take you home."

"Is there enough left for her mother?" Han Li asked. He hadn't even thought of that.

"There is plenty left, even for her father." Yue Li said, rolling her eyes at him. Of course she would cook for them too.

But she might have indeed gone a bit overboard though. The budget wasn't considered at all.

...

As Han Li picked his teeth with a small D.I.Y toothpick, Liyue and Yue Li washed the dishes together. Yue Li rejected her help but Liyue didn't listen and just silently kept helping.

After that, they packed the leftovers and Yue Li looked at Han Li and ordered, "Take her home."

"You are not coming?" Han Li asked with curiosity. She was a bit too proactive for the girl, right?

Yue Li looked at Liyue and smiled. "No, you guys should also have some good alone time after all. Speak with each other."

She didn't know why but the more time she spent with the girl, the more she liked her. Her silent but caring attitude somehow touched right on her heart.

She could also see that the girl had a sense of inferiority complex. So, Doctor Yue Li had to fix it. Such a cute girl should immediately become her son's wife.

"I guess I will take her home then..." Han Li took her hand. "I guess it is also the better thing for me to do by meeting my Mother-in-law too. Would she be asleep?"

Liyue's eyes lit up and she shook her head. She said with enthusiasm Han Li hadn't seen on her before, "Mom only sleeps after I do. She would be very happy if you could visit her. She also wanted to see me married."

"It's only natural I visit her. Mom, you should also get acquainted with her when you get the chance."

"Okay, for now... Tell her that I would like to meet her," She said while looking into Han Li's eyes. She knew they had a way of healing people like Liyue's mother after all.

Han Li held the gaze for a while before smiling. He would have to make some plans.

Chapter 38 - 38: Mother-in-Law
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The moonlight paved their way forward. Han Li and his fiance, Shan Liyue walked towards her home with calm steps. Liyue had the urge to start a conversation but couldn't bring herself to speak. Han Li was also silent.

They each had a small cover filled with packed and fresh food in their hands and as she walked, Liyue needed something to focus her gaze on, so she looked at the packet in Han Li's hands.

'Why is it so stable? His hand is so steady…'

It was like the package didn't know it was being held. As if walking didn't affect the cover's movements.

Han Li briefly paused and looked at a house some distance away. With lanterns outside lighting up the surroundings of the house that was considerably bigger than his own thatched house.

"…That's my house," she said in a weak voice.

"I know," Han Li nodded and looked at her and she involuntarily looked back at him. "Let's go meet your mother first."

Liyue nodded and they both walked up to the house. She used a key to open the door and took one of the lanterns to light the way ahead. Han Li entered the house and followed her.

She took the food from his hands and placed them on a table in the dining area. "Mom is bedridden in our room… we sleep together since she might need my help if the pain suddenly hits."

Han Li nodded. She took a deep breath and brought him towards a room that had some candles lit inside.

Entering the room, she looked at her mother to make sure she was not in any kind of embarrassing situation and invited Han Li in. Li Yao, her mother, was looking at them with brightened eyes.

"Mom…" slightly bashful, Liyue helped her mother sit up slightly. Han Li, on the other hand, looked at her in amusement.

Indeed, no wonder the Fat guy had a daughter as beautiful as his fiance. His mother-in-law was quite the beauty. She had long black hair and pretty black eyes. Due to being an Awakened, his senses were clear even though the room was dimly lit. Li Yao was a beautiful woman with bountiful assets.

Her plain white robes only added to the mature charm. Even though she looked tired and sleepy, her eyes had this vibrancy and life that he rarely saw in women her age and status. Even though she was more than 45, she barely seemed more than 35.

"Are you the boy going to marry Liyue?" she asked with an interested tone.

Han Li smiled. "Yes, I am."

"You surely caught quite the catch, Liyue." Li Yao chuckled: "Handsome and polite to boot."

Liyue blushed and said, "Mom, he is here to speak with you…"

Li Yao smiled. "I know him. Boy, are you the girl Yue Li's son?"

"I am," Han Li nodded. His mother was quite famous due to the scandal of his birth and as someone from the Starlight Zone, it was not surprising that she knew her. "Do you know my mother?"

"I don't really know her but I have seen her before… I saw her many times. She was one of the most popular girls in her prime after all. You won't believe how many men wanted to woo her."

"Ah, I heard about that…" Han Li smiled thinking about that part of her history. "She went for a small journey outside of the clan at that point."

That was the main reason she ran away for a while. And on the way back, she came with a child, and that child was him.

"What is your name?" she asked.

Liyue brought a chair for him and he sat on it before answering. "Han Li. I met Uncle Li earlier and spoke about our marriage… I thought I should also come to meet you."

"Her Father?" Li Yao nodded hesitantly, but then said with a smile: "By the way, you can just call me Mother-in-law, even Mother alone is fine."

Her daughter blushed from the side.

"So, Mother-in-law, what do you think about me marrying Liyue?" Han Li asked while looking at the blushing Liyue. This girl indeed had charm.

"I am fine with it. If my fatso husband allowed you to marry her, he must have known you are the best choice. He rarely ever makes bad decisions. And look at my daughter blushing… I never saw it before the first marriage was canceled. I bet she likes you."

'Mom!' Liyue exclaimed in her mind out of embarrassment. Had Han Li not been there, she would have immediately run away.

Han Li chuckled. "I should have brought some wine for you. You seem like the perfect person to have a cup with."

Li Yao's eyes brightened. "Do that next time or I won't give my daughter to you. Bring me some great wine!"

"Sure!" Han Li smirked.

Liyue wondered in her mind, 'He likes wine?'

Han Li then asked, "By the way, can I look at your condition?"

Li Yao raised an eyebrow.

"As I said, I have a bigger background behind me. My father is not from the Starlight Clan after all.  So I learned some medicine in my younger years… maybe I can at least relieve some of your pain?"

"Oh…" She knew Han Li's father was often rumored to be some mysterious man. Nobody knew who impregnated Yue Li after all.

Han Li smirked inside, 'Father Fruit is such a nice man.'

"I am not sure but I can indeed check."

Liyue asked nervously, "What does she have to do?" Her father had told her Han Li might have a prestigious background. So, she was hopeful his tricks worked!

"Nothing much, just give me your hand." Han Li showed her his hand. Li Yao didn't hesitate and placed her soft hands on his.

A red Spark awakened in his other hand. Lust Spark could absorb the emotion of Lust and transform it into Energy. All Sparks could absorb Soul Energy. Even Starlight Sparks could absorb it. But each person's soul energy was different from one another.

That made it impossible for someone to absorb another person's Energy.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 39 - 39: Trying a small trick on Mother-in-Law
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

That made it impossible for someone to absorb another person's Energy.

It was like each of them had a different signature. Yue Li could absorb the Soul Energy inside her using her new Spark very easily because it had her own Soul Energy to begin with. But absorbing others' Soul Energy was a whole different matter.

It was impossible if they did not have a Spark like the Lust Spark.

Among the seven 'Great Daos', 'Life' was the most potent and rare 'Great Dao' that is seen throughout the Seven Continents.

And Lust was a sub-dao of the Life Dao. And having its Spark, it allowed him to absorb any other's Soul Energy as his own. He just had to convert it into Lust which they felt.

Holding her hand, he used the spark-lit hand to see her curious face. She was looking at the red Spark she had never seen before in intrigue. What Spark is this?

Han Li gently held her soft hands. No person was ever completely bare of Lust, even the monks had a hint of Lust always active in them. Why mention? Even eunuchs had a hint of Lust that was too weak to be felt.

The moment Han Li's hands that had the Lust Spark came in contact with her, the Lust hidden deep in her got transformed into Soul Energy and got absorbed by him.

It was a low, almost negligible amount, but as someone who had endured the weight of the Energy for half of her whole life. Li Yao immediately felt a difference.

She took a deep breath and suddenly, that difference faded.

"I guess it won't be that easy." Han Li sighed.

"No, I felt a difference for a bit there… what did you do?" Li Yao asked, did such simple methods really exist?

She had believed it to be extremely expensive to heal. To the point that even selling her family's whole assets wouldn't have counted enough for it. But Han Li could heal her like this?

"My Clan's spark is a bit unique," Han Li used the fruit clan trick again and said, "But this way, it is impossible to heal you. Even without a Spark, you still have the 'Empty Space' inside you. So it would keep attracting Energy till it fills you to the maximum again. We need a much faster method."

He would have to make her extremely Lustful and absorb all that energy in one go. He could've done the same with Yue Li, but he wanted to leave her energy for her and not absorb it himself.

Even for Li Yao, if he wanted, he could easily spend another bit of Sacred Coins and get an Energy Absorption stone. If he used it along with the 'Dead's Return Diagram' he could heal her and also make her have astonishing talent. Even a new Spark.

Just like his little Mom.

But he had no plans to. He was basically putting his hands into one of the seven Ancient Sacred Demon's Empty Spaces by using the diagram. That was just courting death if he constantly used it.

Full of unknown possibilities. An Eldritch horror popping up in his front yard won't be a far-fetched possibility anymore.

But there was no need to. He could just absorb her Energy after making her Lustful.

As for Fatty Pang Li?

He wouldn't mind his Wife becoming healthier…right?

"Is it possible to heal her in the end?" Liyue asked. If she could be healed, everything was okay.

Becoming rich enough to heal her was one of her greatest ambitions that she considered an impossible goal. It was also her father's goal. Even though she didn't particularly like her father, she knew he wasn't a bad man.

"It is possible to heal her but it will take a bit of time and getting used to. But it is possible for sure. But keep in mind, do not reveal what I did today to anyone. Even Father-in-law for now."

Li Yao hesitated but nodded. She knew how big of a favor this was. And she had enough shame to keep it to themselves at least.

Anyway, since it was Liyue's husband, they were all one family from now. If she can indeed heal her, Liyue would surely have to make him heal her father too. She knew it was taking a bit of an advantage of him but this was a life-changing power.

Han Li patted her hands, "Don't worry, we will heal you Mother-in-law, and I will make sure it is an enjoyable process. We can also get to know each other more."

Liyue also nodded. That was true, her mother was very lonely without any friends visiting. Han Li would be a great company for her.

Han Li looked at them and smiled. He had no guilty conscience for having erotic thoughts about his mother-in-law. And since Liyue was his wife now, he would also tell her about it before it really happens.

Otherwise, there was no meaning in choosing Liyue to be his wife. He didn't want someone who was disgruntled and hateful of him to be his wife. He wanted someone loving and loyal.

Before touching her mother, he would obviously have to get her explicit permission.

As for Pang Li? He was a whole other matter.

Anyway, Li Yao had a lot of Soul Energy that he couldn't waste.

Li Yao nodded. "Yes, you should try to make this ability work. I am your first patient right?"

"Oh? You are." Han Li nodded.

"You should learn to use that power more using me and make this your business. My Liyue would never have to worry about money then."

"I plan to when I grow a bit more stronger… I don't want malicious attention."

"Isn't your Mother alone at home? You should go back now," Li Yao said with a smile, "But make sure you come soon with a wine. And also introduce me to your mother."

"I will. She also wanted to see you." Han Li stood up from his seat and looked at Liyue. She had a pondering but slightly red face. "Can I take her out for a minute?"

Li Yao smiled. "Ask her."

The girl always cared too much about her own opinion after all.

Han Li looked at her and took her hand, breaking her out of her thoughts. "Come, Let's have a conversation."

Chapter 40 - 40: Ah, the moon comes out in night
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and his fiance stood side by side just outside her home.

"I guess your father is still out," he said.

Liyue nodded. She was a bit nervous. What did Han Li want to speak with her?

"Now that it is sure that you will be mine from now on. I have some conditions I hope you can fulfill…" His voice lingered for a while before he continued, "Nothing much, only two conditions."

Liyue nodded. Her neck was slightly red… How can he so casually say something like you are mine…

"So, first of all. After our marriage, I want you to look after my mom very well. Especially if I am not around for some reason." Han Li looked into her eyes, "Understood?"

She nodded. That was not much, she just had to see Yue Li like her own mother.

"Second thing, this is very important. Well, don't reveal anything that you feel is a secret of ours to anybody other than me and Mom." He had no plans of revealing Yue Li's Luck-Chat System or his own secrets.

What she had to know, she will know. But if she ended up seeing something, it would be troublesome. Thus, he warned her beforehand.

He didn't really mind though. His own important secrets would be secrets in his own mind and the only secrets Yue Li had were her previous life on Earth and the System. He had firmly warned her not to reveal anything like that, so she wouldn't.

Yue Li also understood that these things might be coveted treasures. She wasn't one to look for troubles herself.

Liyue found the second condition to be somewhat weird but didn't linger on it too much and nodded. Maybe they had some secrets about the clan that Han Li's father came from that they didn't want others to know.

"Also, this is not a condition but I am just a human. Your husband to boot. So, you should try speaking to me."

She was so nervous every time. She had barely spoken to him ever since they met. Nevertheless, she nodded.

Han Li looked at her and took her hand. "I do know the way to break the ice though. Should I use that instead?"

She looked at him. His red eyes shined like a ruby and its focus made her avert her gaze.

Han Li patted her head and brought her into his embrace. She was startled but she couldn't resist. "Can you hear the heartbeats?"

Indeed. She could feel her own thundering heartbeats and Han Li's calm ones. But she could no longer nod since her head was buried in his chest and he was patting the top of her head.

Her face flushed. "I…I can hear," she said in fluster.

Her hands were on his chest, his hands were wrapped around her waist and he was so tightly pulling her. She couldn't help but be flustered.

She had never been this close to a man. Not to mention someone so dominating.

"Do you still feel nervous? What kind of weak girl are you?" Han Li once more patted her head, "You will have to grow stronger than that. Now look up at me."

Feeling his hold loosen slightly, she relievedly looked up at Han Li's face.

'So close!' her heart skipped a beat. Han Li closed the distance and his lips met hers. He didn't give a deep kiss but just a small peck on her lips.

She stood in his embrace startled like a kitten. Then immediately pushed him away in a fluster.

Han Li looked at her with a curious smirk as he stumbled a step back.

Really, A beauty no matter what, does look beautiful. The moonlight on her blushing skin in this late night and the chirping sound of cicadas were just… Beautiful. It felt so alive.

She looked at him with a red face and touched her lips.

"Wh…what was that?" she muttered.

Han Li smiled. "Nothing, just something to make you more comfortable."

Liyue clenched her hands. Why was her heart so fast? She couldn't calm down at all!

This was not the kind of nervousness she was used to.

"Anyway, as Mother-in-law said, my Mom is still alone… I will go for now." Han Li chuckled, waved his hands, and turned around. Before Liyue could process everything, he was far into the distance.

She just stood there and looked at him as her hair flowed in the night wind. She tucked it into place with a hand. '…he undid my hair because he… hugged me too hard.'

Her heart couldn't help but skip a beat. Her hands couldn't help but clench itself.

On the other hand, some distance away, a fatty lied under a bush and sighed. "Youth, such a wonderful thing."

Had it been his own house, Han Li would sure have beaten him half to death today. But this time, he didn't mind. He was in somewhat of a carefree mood.

As for Liyue?

She was also someone who had gone through the 3rd Awakening, but she didn't even notice the obvious movement.

…

"Ah the Air smells like fine ash, ah the sun feels hot, ah the fire burns…" Han Li sang an old poem that came to mind and silently vanished into the shadows of night.

It was said that someone who had plowed the fields long enough would know when a harvest was soon. It was the same for him.

He could intuitively tell the energy in the surrounding atmosphere was rising. The day of Energy Abundance was soon, and this time, Starlight Clan was going to host the Awakening Ceremony.

And for that, a white flag would be risen. A flag representing peace that should not be touched upon. If that moment of peace was to be touched upon…

Han Li smirked. It was near time to remove some pests from his field.

"Ah? What was it again? Whatever… Ah, the moon comes out in night… Ah the fat man is fat…"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 41 - 41: Study the Dao, Mom!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

It was a new dawn. The sun's shining glare brightened the skies and woke the world up.

Inside Han Li's humble abode. He was deep in sleep with his mother lying on top of him.

She was rather fitting for him to hug tightly above him. She was just the perfect size. Han Li woke up and felt a small weight on top of him. It was his mother.

"You woke up earlier than me again…" Han Li said, slightly groggy from sleep.

He wasn't one to sleep deeply, usually, he slept while being on alert and that was his habit.

But after coming back in time, he was relaxed. Especially in his mother's presence.

So, he ended up having somewhat of a deep sleep these days.

"Yeah, I woke up way earlier than you." Yue Li looked at his face and said with a morning smile. "I set up tea, breakfast, and even snacks already."

Han Li hugged her a bit tighter. "You came and slept on top of me after doing all that?"

Yue Li laid her head on his chest. "Yeah, you slept more comfortably when I did that."

"I see…" Han Li gently patted her head. "You smell so nice. Did you have a bath?"

"Yes. I did," she replied, "The water was so cold."

"If I had brushed… I would have kissed you deeply now," Han Li said.

"Oh…am I irritating you now?" Yue Li asked with a pout.

"Of course, you are always irritating," Han Li said, "You even slept before I came back home yesterday."

He had wanted to have some fun but she just didn't want to allow that. She slept before he reached back home.

Yue Li said with feigned anger. "It's not like you didn't do anything while I was asleep. Even when I woke up, your fingers were inside… why did you think I had to bathe so early in the morning?"

Han Li chuckled.

She displayed no shame and was truly one-of-a-kind. She had always boldly expressed her love for him without any hesitation.

Even when he was not allowed to touch her down there, she never showed shyness. She was rather bold even then.

"Did it feel good though?" Han Li asked with a smile in his voice.

Yue Li blushed a bit. "Of course, it felt good. You can do it every time from now."

"It's always me pleasuring you. You will have to do it for me too." Han Li said, really, he also deserved some. Even though he liked pleasuring her more.

Yue Li nodded. This time really a bit shy.

Having said that, Han Li added a bit seriously, "I have some bad news for you. Tomorrow is the Awakening ceremony and today, I want you to learn all the basics of Cultivation. You will have to study a lot today."

Yue Li smiled. "I also want to continue the training, it's quite fun."

Before they both Awakened, they rarely had something that they both liked doing together. Of course, except love. But Yue Li had always wished there was something like Animes or Games here that she could use to spend time with him.

This training was like a replacement for that.

"I will go brush first, and then tea, food, and stuff before training begins. And… if you study everything I give today clearly…"

"If I do?"

"I will give you a gift. A big Gift."

"Okay," Yue Li smiled, she liked surprises. "Then I will also give you a small surprise."

"Oh? What surprise?"

Yue Li blushed and moved her face to his side. She whispered in his ear, "When I woke up, your thing was standing and it looked painfully hard. So I tried to calm it down… I thought you would wake up but you didn't."

Han Li looked at her with surprise clearly written on his face. "Really? What did you do?"

Yue Li blushed more. "I gave it a kiss and used my hands to play with it."

"No wonder I didn't have a morning wood. You better do that when I am awake next time."

Yue Li nodded. Han Li gently laid her to the side and said, "Let's get to work."

There was no time to waste.

…

After washing himself. Han Li and Yue Li had their breakfast. He also got enough Banana fries to eat today since instead of him, Yue Li had gone to the markets early morning.

She had even bought meat. Of course, she hadn't cooked it yet.

Their breakfast was just bread and omelet. And some fried vegetables that she liked to cook.

Having filled their stomachs, he no longer allowed her to slack and brought her to the room. Then, taking a book and ink, he started her studies.

"Okay, Mom, tell me all you know about Cultivation," Han Li asked.

Yue Li pondered and said, "I don't know a lot about it."

"Say what you know."

"I know that the first Stage of cultivation is 'Awakening'," She counted, "Second is Evolution and the third is Power Birth."

Han Li nodded, "Correct names. But I like to call Awakening as the Second Stage of cultivation and Spark Acquiring as the first Stage. Even though more than 99% of the people are born with a spark and cultivate already."

Yue Li nodded. "Then it goes first stage Spark Acquiring, then Awakening, Evolution, and Power Birth. Right?"

"Yes, there are more Stages after that and in the end, the 7th Stage is the Stage the strongest cultivators we ever know were at. Sacred Demons."

"So we are at the second Stage and the 7th Stage is the most powerful? That much is simple." Even she can learn it easily.

"That's just the Cultivation Stages. Knowing that makes no difference. What you have to know is what they entail and what you have to do to reach the next stage. Also, what happens after you reach the next stage is something important too."

"Okay!" Yue Li nodded, pumped.

"So, tell me what else do you know about Cultivators?"

"Cultivators are strong people who get more stronger the higher stage they are in."

"Correct."

"They have many magical capabilities that…Mortals can't even understand."

"Not exactly wrong."

"They live for much longer than us."

"Yes, but you are now a cultivator too. 'Us' is the cultivators now and 'They' are the mortals."

"Oh…"

"There is also more you have to know, this is just the most basic of things. I will tell you some of the important things. Try to memorize and write it down on the note."

Yue Li took the ink and paper. Then listened.

"Our talent, as your system shows, is a very, very important thing in the cultivation world. The higher your Stage, the more impactful it will be."

She wrote down what he said. He waited till she was over with it and then continued.

"For Average-Level Talents, the Empty Space inside them would just have a 1-meter distance. That would be the limit of their Soul Sense too. I can at the moment only see about 1.5 meters with my Soul Sense when the Spark is out."

She noted.

"Then for the High-Level Talents, the Empty Space inside them would be 3 meters wide. And they can also sense that much when the spark is out."

She noted while nodding.

"Then there are people with the Sacred Talent. Like you for example. They have a 5-meter Empty Space and they can see 5 meters when the Spark is out."

She noted that too.

"The area of the Empty Space determines many things other than senses too Mom, It determines how much Energy you can store in your body. And that in turn determines how long you can sustain your Powers that you will gain after Evolving.

People with Average-Talent would easily tire themselves using the powers once or twice whereas you can use it 10 times and you would still not be completely tired out."

"Okay!" She noted.

"And last thing for now, every Stage you go up in cultivation. For example, from Awakening Stage to the Evolution Stage, The Empty Space would double in size. Your 5 meters would become ten and that ten would become 20 when you reach Power Birth."

"Uh? Then wouldn't that mean people with Average Talent would only have 2-meter Empty Spaces when they become Evolution Stage?!" Even she had 5 meters already.

"Yes, they could only sense around 2 Meters of them. And even if they become Power Birth Stage cultivators, they can only see 4 Meters around them."

Of course, the Stage after that was quite different but Yue Li doesn't have to know about it now.

"Isn't your talent Average too Xiao Li?" Yue Li asked worriedly.

"Not for long, if you put in effort, it should be easy… I just have to absorb that Lust out of you." Han Li chuckled.

Yue Li didn't mind and wrote down what she understood.

Han Li smiled. "But all of cultivation is always dependent on the Spark. There are many things you have yet to learn. This is just the basics, so don't get smug."

"I am not smug." Yue Li wrote the last word down.

"I bet learning about the 7 Great Daos would be even more fun."

Yue Li flinched. "Let me learn all of this first!"

"Yeah, that much is enough for now. Study."

"…I used to think it would be the other way," Yue Li smiled, "Aren't mothers supposed to be the ones to teach their kids?"

"Your son is too good. Don't compare him to the rest."

"Maybe…" Yue Li smiled more.

"Sweetness won't let you rest. Study, study, my dumb mom."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 42 - 42: The Differences
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lands were rather dim due to the dark curtains that hid the world from the sun. The clouds hung there in the heavens like an ominous warning of an incoming downpour.

Han Li was seated above his house's roof in the lotus position and looking towards the distance where a small flag slowly rose up the flag pole and fluttered. He could hear distant drumbeats too.

An Awakening Ceremony was going to take place. Han Li gently breathed in.

He could feel the world brimming with energy and vitality. Today was a day of Energy Abundance. It was like a wind that blows over at least once a month.

The energy of the world peaks and even depressed people would naturally feel more lighthearted today. It doesn't really affect the Awakening Stage cultivators or their talent.

But it was considered a lucky day. As for the Evolution Stage and higher-stage cultivators?

It was a day for them to absorb energy 10 times faster than normal. Some even travel along the Energy Abundance like following the wind to have faster cultivation.

But Han Li didn't mind about any of that. At the moment, his mother was with Liyue and they were both in the Ceremony area of Starlight Zone. Near the flagpole.

As he waited, he also tried taking out his own Spark and absorbing the Energy of the world surrounding him. But sadly, it didn't work.

Of course, by breathing alone, anyone with a Spark could absorb Energy, but there was no meaning to such Energy for Han Li.

That Energy can only fill the 1.5-meter radius and not expand it. Whereas, Lust converted into Energy could do exactly that.

It was like a different sort of Energy. It raised his potential itself as a living being.

That was why he wanted all the Lust Energy he could absorb. That was why Sparks which came in the same category as his own was somewhat unique.

All of the Sparks that came under the 'Life Dao' of the seven Great Daos could do that.

He nevertheless kept his spark active and that expanded his senses to 1.5 meters around himself. He couldn't see minor stuff like an ant's expression, but he could see anything in that distance like he had a pair of eyes there.

'I should absorb as much Lust Energy as I can and quickly make myself an Evolution Stage at least,' he pondered.

As someone who knew what the world was like, he knew being weak was a sin. Power should always be chased after.

Especially when his ambition was to control Life and Death.

Pondering about some things, suddenly, Han Li's head tilted to the side.

A wan, yellow-faced elder was walking closer to his home with a paper in his hand. His actions were sneaky and his eyes were furtive. Seeing that no one was around, he quickly pasted the gum-soaked paper into the door and walked away like nothing had happened.

Han Li looked at him with a casual smile.

The smile the infamous Red-eyed demon of sacred lust was often said to have.

From the top of the thatched roof, he had a great vantage point. There was no way for the man to see him but he could clearly see the man nervously move faster and faster the more distance he reached.

It was only natural that he was afraid. He was messing with one of the three Great Clans of the city. It was on the day of their Awakening Ceremony to boot.

Han Li didn't immediately follow him and calmly moved into his home. Reaching outside it, he casually took the gum-soaked paper and read its contents. A growing furrow on his forehead.

"Immediately come out of the Starlight Zone after you see this. Your mother is in our hands and we will return her if you bring the Black book she bought. Otherwise, we will send you her dissected corpse. Of course, we won't hesitate to enjoy her thoroughly before doing that.

And I am warning you, we have our men on the lookout, If you try contacting the clan, your mother… You won't even know her fate."

Reading it, Han Li immediately frowned. His eyes became utterly cold for a split second before it calmed down.

This was not the same message he got in the last life. What changed?

He was sure his mother was safe. Or he won't be this relaxed. But something was different from the last time.

'Are my actions affecting the future events subtly and changing things from how they should be?' he frowned more. 'I didn't do anything directly related to that merchant or the Raven Clan cultivator. What changed?'

Crumpling the paper in his hand, he walked forward. Following the path that Shan Shan had taken.

He already had a plan on how to deal with these people, but he was now slightly confused.

…

Moving out of the Starlight Zone, Han Li soon reached the gateway to the outside world. The way out of Southern Lust City. But having reached there, he froze.

His mind raced and he immediately reached many conclusions. Unlike last time when he had gone out of the gateway — when there was only one Evolution Stage cultivator looking after it — there were two people this time.

One was the same gate guard who looked slightly burly and menacing. The other was an irritable young man who constantly made issues for the merchants entering and going out.

Han Li moved closer and looked at him for a long second. He was the first fiancee of Liyue. The young man who was supposedly talented enough to become an Evolution Stage.

Seeing Han Li, the young man obviously didn't figure out their connection, but irritably made trouble for him too. The black-haired, effeminate youth yelled out loud, "Hey, you? Where are you coming from? Do you don't know merchants come first in line? Why are you trying to use it before them? Huh?"

Han Li just stared at him with his red eyes. Calmly and calculatively. He asked with a similarly calm tone, "What is your name?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 43 - 43: Demon and a Butterfly's flutter.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

He asked with a calm tone, "What is your name?"

Li Ming frowned with irritation. But no matter how irritated he was, he wasn't allowed to just break the law. He was here due to boredom and shame, he didn't want to get punished for something dumb he might end up doing.

And he was a cultured person. Names must be given if asked.

"Li Ming of the Starlight Clan. Who are you?" Most people won't be bold enough to speak back to gate guards. He wasn't a gate guard but he seemed like one…right?

Even the Evolution Stage cultivator on the side didn't mind him after all.

"Han Li of the Starlight Clan," Han Li replied. Unbothered by such measly status.

Li Ming squinted at the merchants around who might have heard his yells earlier and thought: 'Dammit, you should have looked like one of us if you came from one of the three great clans.'

Now his words would have made him appear like a lesser. Han Li's eyes were red, unlike the signature night black of most Starlights. So, he couldn't recognize him.

It was not anything like the other two clans either. But he had indeed heard of an Han Li around who was supposedly a great cook. That guy was supposed to be his wedding cook too.

"Well, if you are one of the three great clan's member. You should be considered differently." The young man nodded and stood to the side with a look that said he expected this development.

Han Li looked at him with a side-eye. By now, he could guess why Li Ming was here instead of being at the Awakening Ceremony's venue.

No matter what, he was someone who had his marriage canceled and many people would look at him mockingly. He must have wanted to avoid scrutiny for a while and must have paid the Evolution Stage guard here to let him spend time here.

He could vent his frustrations and appear to be helping out the clan by taking on duty instead of being a coward who hides.

But that didn't happen in the first two timelines, which made Shan Shan who entered inside have a different circumstance. Unlike the calm and unspeaking guard, Li Ming was an irritable guy who at least acted like he was working. And that made Shan Shan much more nervous than he was before.

After entering the clan grounds, he should have been extremely nervous and immediately wrote down the blackmail letter. That letter indeed felt like someone who is in a rush wrote it too.

Having concluded these details, Han Li understood why this timeline already started having differences.

'At this point, even a butterfly flapping its wings might affect future events,' Han Li thought: 'I have to be a bit more cautious about my future plans.'

He lost interest in the irritated brat and moved out of the gateway without any further thought. And once out, he once more smiled that casual smile.

Among the small number of people outside, on a side, an old, wan, and yellow-faced elder was looking at the gateway with an expectant look. Seeing Han Li, his eyes shined in relief and he immediately came walking over.

Near enough, he muttered to Han Li, "Follow me."

Han Li didn't even bother to act nervous and followed.

Shan Shan looked around as he walked and soon, some distance away from the city walls, they walked past the clearing and entered a dark forest that was filled with shadows.

Han Li looked up in search of the Raven Clan cultivator. He didn't see the man but the blocked light of the cloud-shrouded sun that could barely pass the foliage made the area quite dark.

"Did you bring the book?" Shan Shan asked with an intimidating tone.

Han Li ignored him and searched for the Raven clan cultivator. From ancient times to now, there was no way for a cultivator to head-on fight against a higher-stage cultivator.

No matter what kind of a talent a cultivator had, a higher Stage cultivator was just a different breed. No matter what advantages a low-level Cultivator may have over the higher Stage Cultivator, it was basically impossible to fight a higher Stage head-on.

It was considered extremely great if someone could even endure one attack from a Stage higher.

So, when dealing with people like that, you should always have your focus on them.

Insignificant and harmless flies like Shan Shan didn't matter. Had his Spark been covert, he would have used it. But it was too bright and would stand out.

So he just used his enhanced senses in search of the man.

Rustle*

The bushes rustled from his back and a hand gently rested on his shoulders. "Had you not heard what he asked? Where is the book?"

Han Li's hands tightened for a second and without any sudden movement, he walked forward calmly and turned back to look at the man who had killed him once.

He walked slowly since a quick move might trigger a warrior's instinct to attack immediately.

Kevin looked at him with squinted eyes. Why was this person so calm?

He had expected a pathetic man out for love and stupid due to his emotions. This was out of his expectations.

But that only made him more curious. He had no reason to worry when it came to weak beings below the Evolution Stage. Below that Stage, everyone might as well be ants to him.

Han Li on the other hand examined him. Flowing black hair that was actually a flock of raven feathers and eyes that showed an intense chaotic gleam. And a face that just screamed calm insanity.

More than anything else, a black, ancient, and rusty sword lay on his back with a lusterless shine.

Han Li then looked back at his face, he couldn't help but chuckle. "It's been quite a while since I last saw one right to the face like you."

"Oh? What?" Kevin asked curiously with a tilted head.

"A Demon," he said, "It's been a while since I saw another one."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 44 - 44: Demon's [l]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The white flag rose. Drumbeats rang out like deep rumbles. People's chatter kept ringing out like a wild cluster of bird cries. Among the more than 300 people surrounding the venue were two people who stood to the side.

Yue Li, and Shan Liyue.

Looking up at the sky, and seeing the darkening clouds, Yue Li had a worried and slightly anxious expression. The whooshing wind didn't aid the situation either.

"What is it, Mother?" Liyue asked with a pat on her shoulder.

Yue Li's thoughts were broken and she looked around. She said with a shake of her head, "Nothing, it seems it might rain soon. I wonder what Xiao Li is up to."

"He said he would come before the ceremony actually began, right?" It was difficult to hear her words amidst the noise of the crowd, so she spoke loudly.

Liyue had gone early in the morning to Han Li's and wanted to visit the ceremony along with him and Yue Li, but he had made Yue Li and her come here alone.

He said he would be here before the actual ceremony began.

Yue Li took a deep breath, yesterday's event flashing through her head. Most of the day, she and Han Li had spent time teaching her about minor things in cultivation.

Not many complex things but just filling up her basics. But unlike what she expected, Han Li didn't play around with her body when the night came.

That had made her anxious a while into her sleep and she opened her eyes to look at him. What she saw then, that image was still etched deeply in her mind.

It was deep in the night, but Han Li wasn't doing anything and just looking infront of him with an indifferent gaze. Seated in the lotus position like a statue, emotionless and just calm.

Seeing him like that, her heart had shivered.

Only when he had turned around and looked at her did his monolith-like gaze soften back to normal. 'Go back to sleep,' he said.

She struggled to sleep after that and when the morning came, she didn't know why but she couldn't bring herself to question it. She began to speak but then closed her mouth.

Their interactions had been quite awkward the whole day even though it seemed normal. And when Liyue enthusiastically came and braved herself to ask them to visit the ceremony together, he had asked them to come here by themselves.

Everything made it suspicious for Yue Li.

A distant rumble of thunder echoed through the dark and gloomy lands, rather than the usual celebratory atmosphere of the ceremonies.

"Ah, isn't that the first Awakening kids? How nostalgic." Someone from the side said.

Yue Li and Liyue immediately looked towards the small stage just below the flagpole, and Liyue whispered, "They are the 13-year-old kids who will go through the First Awakening today. What a coincidence…" She smiled. "Exactly 13 kids came to Awaken."

Nodding, Yue Li looked at them. Children wet behind their ears, filled with excitement and nervousness, looking at the crowd of people watching them with many expressions. She still remembered her Xiao Li on his 13th birthday; just rolled to a corner of the bed and silently asleep.

As if nothing bothered him even though he felt alienated from the rest.

"Hello Everyone!" A woman with an iron mask climbed on stage and clapped her hands. The people around became silent.

"This time, I will be the one hosting the first two 'smaller' Awakings, and the clan head and other elders will be holding the Third Awakening and attempts at Evolution that some of our Clan members plan to go through today."

Someone around whispered, "Did you hear Lady Fang Li reached the Evolution Stage when she was 29? She is not a great genius but she is sure talented!"

Yue Li just kept watching. In total, there were 11 Evolved Cultivators — Stage 3 Cultivators — in Starlight Family and this woman, Fang Li, was one of them.

She was one of the 11 special people among more than 500 clan members and was thus a very popular person. Not to mention she was the youngest.

The Awakening ceremony began and the children were sat in a circle for the 400 or more audience to see.

Then, Fang Li lazily told the kids what to do and they all started to one by one bring out their Spark from inside their Empty Space.

All of them only made it come out one Barrier and once they did so, they looked around in disbelief at their enhanced senses. They were not Yue Li or Han Li for them to go through all 3 Awakenings at once.

They were kids and thus their minds and souls weren't strong enough to handle the extreme stress that came with the added senses. 18 was the age preferable for a mature mind that could hold the data that comes from all sides and not just explode.

If ordinary kids had 3 breakthroughs at once. Most would have severe disabilities for life like dementia, mental ailments, or even die in the worst cases.

Cultivation was not all fun and games.

As she looked at every kid smiling and walking away from the platform, and the cheering crowd, Han Li's teachings came back to her one by one.

She had learned many such basic things yesterday and that made her ponder.

"Next will be the second Awakening, kids at the appropriate age come up," Fang Li said out loud.

This time, there was only one boy and soon after going up, he immediately finished his awakening and ran away in embarrassment. Being alone above the platform was much harder than being there with others.

Yue Li chuckled at the kid's actions and said to Liyue, "I had always thought if Xiao Li went there, he would be like that, shy and flustered."

"He gets shy?" Liyue asked curiously, indeed, speaking with Yue Li was becoming easier for her.

"Yes, he does get shy. He is just like all the other kids his age, he gets playful, he gets sad, he gets excited at some things too," Yue Li paused, "But he shows only me most of his sides. Nobody really knows him. He doesn't even have a good friend other than me."

"…I thought he looked cool and calm all the time, though," Liyue murmured, "It's a bit hard to think he can get sad."

"He does. But I rarely see him angry though," Yue Li looked at Fang Li slowly climbing down the stage and spoke, "But when he does, it scares me a lot."

"…Why?"

"Because I can't tell if he is angry or not…" A drop of cold rain fell on Yue Li's cheeks. "Ah! It's raining. Do you want to go back home to see him?"

There would be an Awakening ceremony next month for her to see the Evolving cultivators. She wanted to see why Han Li had that expression yesterday. Did someone piss him off?

Liyue looked at her, but just as she was about to speak, a bolt of thundering lightning flashed in the sky and a downpour of rain followed right after. They had to run and take cover.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 45 - 45: Demon's [II]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The sound of gentle rain tackling the leaves and foliage of the dark forest kept ringing in their ears.

It was extremely dark and almost night-like in the shadows of the forest. Especially due to the clouds that had long covered the sun's brilliance.

But neither Kevin Raven nor Han Li had any trouble seeing each other's faces. They were no mortal humans. They were cultivators with senses that surpassed humans by folds.

Especially the Raven. "Demon? Who? Me?" Kevin pointed at himself and laughed. "That's a good one. Really.

The first time I heard someone call me that."

Han Li whispered, "A cultivator of the Ethereal type makes their Spark leave the confines of their body. That will make their senses expand."
