"But that leaves them unprotected. This time, it is not going to be a gentle expansion of senses like it was when we Awakened, it will be Senses on a whole other level. He could even sense an ant's footsteps in a 6-meter radius of him. And that comes with a severe toll on our psyches."

Li Yun gritted his teeth, all around him, he could feel it. There was dust flying around, and a faint sensation of coldness lingered after the rain. Some people in that radius smelled horrible.

All these senses assaulted his brain with no barriers. At the same time, he felt as if he had left his body behind.

Yet, the weakening heartbeats of his own sounded clear to him. He felt as if he was losing himself.

"Steady your breathing," said a calm but loud voice.

Li Yun instinctively did as it said.

"Let your heart beat freely, don't try to constrain it."

He felt as if he would really lose himself if he let it beat freely. He kept his heart tight.

Han Li shook his head from the seating area in pity. But he was as calm as ever.

When the Spark first comes out of the body, it is the same as a dead Power Born's Spark rising up to the sky. The world will attract it.

And thus the connection the Cultivator feels with it will keep reducing and reducing. But if they let that feeling dominate them. It was over.

This was taught multiple times to these people by their seniors. But at that moment, they just end up losing themselves.

The Spark rose higher and higher from the man for a while before it vanished like a flame in the wind.

The whole crowd turned silent. Li Yun opened his eyes with a bitter expression and before he could do anything, coughed up some blood.

Li Xingguang sighed and patted his back. He didn't say anything and Li Yun's eyes leaked some bit of tears.

But the man held it back and wiped his face before standing up.

The Head of the clan looked at the man for a second. Li Yun clenched his hands.

"Don't be bitter… Cultivation is like this…" Li Xingguang looked at the people and sighed once more.

"Hea…Family Head, did I fail because my mind wasn't strong enough? Because I didn't let my heart beat freely as you said?" He asked. He could already feel his chest starting to ache weakly, that was probably his disease from now.

"Maybe? I don't know. It is a battle with the world to take the Spark under your control… I just advised what I felt were good methods. Even I don't know what really is the reason for your failure."

Han Li looked at the interaction with a raised brow. On a day when they were supposed to show the clan their strength, the Head himself was showing his weakness.

On the other hand, he was also rather sure the Head should know the real reason for the Failure. It was simply a weak mind.

In that moment of Breakthrough, the mind should be empty of any other thought and focus entirely on the Spark.

And this man had directly focused on something like his heart. It was just courting failure.

"But not all hope is lost, Young Friend Li Yun," The Head said, "They say heavens always leave a path open. Maybe, you might not be someone who will soar high into the sky, but what about your descendants? They will make you proud. They are our hope for the future."

Li Yun sighed.

"Indeed, not all… are meant for the path of cultivation."

Xingguang patted his back and turned around with a smile. Soon, his figure faded into the surroundings and he vanished.

Han Li looked at the scene with deep eyes. So, Xingguang doesn't really care much about the impression of the clan and is just doing everything while he's at it.

No wonder. If this man really cared, the clan should've been much more united.

But Han Li knew he was the same. The higher the Stage of cultivation you reached, the more meaningless a Clan or Family became.

Of course, he would never think like that about Yue Li. Since she was half his reason for existing.

But a Clan under him or something like that?

They were always more of a burden than an asset. He would never want one if it didn't really help in his ambition.

Most wouldn't willingly take that burden to heart. Especially when they knew most of the clan members were just going to live for 100 years at most and they would live far more than that.

Even the Head himself was more than 200 years old after all.

But still, the man was experienced enough to push any probable suicidal thoughts away from Li Yun, he gave Li Yun some hope to look forward to before heading off.

But, by the way that he acted, Han Li could tell he didn't really care too much about anything happening here.

'I didn't remember him like that…' he contemplated. 'Indeed, my past self at this age was far too naive. I shouldn't completely rely on those memories as judgment.'

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 51 - 51: Talent of his Fiancee
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After the Evolution Ceremony, Yue Li and Liyue had saddened faces and were rather emotional. But Han Li made them walk home.

Once back, he started his teaching immediately.

"Tell me, Mom. What did you learn? And why do you think he failed?" He asked. That was the reason he went there anyway.

Yue Li sat on the bed crosslegged alongside Liyue who had a curious face as she listened on.

During this trip, she had come to know that her fiance was a very educated person. He was knowledgeable about cultivation.

As far as she hard, he only spoke about basic things that anyone could read and understand. But there was this genuine sense of confidence in his words. As if he knew his words were true and absolute.

"Did he fail because he didn't let his heart free?" Yue Li asked, curious.

Even the chief didn't know the secret after all.

Han Li then looked at Liyue.

For a second, she was confused, but then asked, "Is it because he let the Spark fly too high? It seemed like it was going away from him."

Shaking his head, Han Li said, "I don't know for sure but I am sure of one thing. The man was afraid in the whole ordeal even though he seemed calm and casual."

"Afraid?"

"Look at his age. That in itself says he is a very cowardly person," Han Li pointed out, "He could have had his Evolution attempt any day after 18. But the fact that he let it go until 37 makes it obvious he was afraid."

The girls became silent.

It was true. Most people push away Evolution in fear and only when they feel that they might soon near their lifespan's end will they attempt it.

But by 50 and such years, it becomes impossible to really achieve Evolution. So usually, people do it at their 35 or some daring ones even during their twenties.

Most didn't want to live in pain. It was only natural that many feared the outcome.

Especially when they hear commonly of people their own age, their friends, failing their Evolution. They just push it aside.

"He was even afraid at that precious moment, That's an absolute no. That is why I say mental training is a must before you attempt Evolution."

"I am training my mind too?" asked Yue Li in intrigue.

"What do you think training in Knife does to you? If you became an Awakened Master of Knives, your mind will be focused enough to cut a fly as it passed by you by predicting its movements."

That made her pause. She wouldn't lie, that seemed slightly cool.

"As for you Liyue, you will also have to choose a weapon and train under me."

Liyue hesitated. She didn't really care about Evolving… but she nodded in the end.

"I said it before and I'll say it again." He looked straight into their eyes, "If you do what I say, just as I say. Evolution won't be that great of a barrier for us. At least, the Ethereal Sparks have it much better in Evolution. Do you know what the Transformation Types have to go through?"

The girls shook their heads.

Han Li smirked, half of his face exposed to the bright sunlight streaming in through the windows."I am telling you, you don't want to go through that. If they are of the Raven Clan for example, in their attempt on Evolution, their whole physique changes.

"Their hair gets torn from the scalp and feathers grow out to replace it. Their skin gets torn and then gets replaced with more rougher skin like that of leather. And as all this is going on, they can't even faint. If they faint, their disabilities are nothing like that of some Li Yun. They become permanent wastes."

Yue Li and Liyue had their faces gradually go pale.

"Isn't the Transformation type's Evolution much harsher than the Ethereal… are they that strong?"

"No, they are only just as capable as Ethereals. But Ethereals have an easier path but lower chances. It's hard to even figure out how they failed." Ethereal was Ethereal, not many can really understand its rules and functions that easily.

Even Han Li himself would never dare to say he knew what it all meant. He was also just like them, a normal Cultivator. He just had far more experience than them.

If he had to choose one of the two, Transformation or Ethereal as his Evolution, he would go for Transformation. With that, he had some kind of assurance in what he was facing.

But with this one, he couldn't really know his enemy. And an unknown Enemy was always more dangerous. So, before his own and his Mother's Evolution, he would like to make their chances as high as possible.

He was almost certain that he would not fail. Among the ones who succeded, his mind would always be better. As for his body, it was just average now, but he had plans to train it heavily before ever attempting Evolution.

There was almost nothing that Han Li lacked in comparison to the best Awakened Cultivators to ever exist. But his Mother and Liyue were far from that.

Yue Li certainly had her talent, but her mind was far too unfocused. Liyue was extremely indecisive too.

They were like fluffy clouds. They looked cute and were very hug-worthy. But the cultivation world didn't lack clouds.

What they needed to become was sharp metal. But at the same time, Han Li didn't want them to lose their innocence.

"From now, this very moment. We begin our training once more," Han Li calmly said, "Mom, you go take your knife and Liyue, For now, just watch. We will go to the weapon shop later and choose your weapon."

And reaching there, Han Li also realized that he hadn't checked how talented she was.

"Show me your Spark too."

Liyue opened up her palm and a baby-fist-sized white Starlight Spark lit up on top of it.

Han Li's eyelids twitched.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 52 - 52: Looking ahead
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Whoosh. Tak.

A carrot's end was barely cut straight and Yue Li cleared the sweat away from her forehead. She also put away her Spark and breathed ruggedly.

"Not even nearly at Average proficiency but there is progress," Han Li nodded and then looked at Liyue who was walking around the room with a long stick in her hands.

He had wanted to take her to the weapon shop but still tried out some things that were in their home to see whether any of them suited her.

And indeed a long stick, or a makeshift spear, really suited her. Even her nervous attitude suited the weapon clearly. Of course, the spear had its dominant sides that she ill have to learn.

But just like Yue Li, she had no talent for coordinating herself while her Senses were expanded because of the Spark. So, she was doing exactly what Yue Li did on the first day.

Trying to walk with the Spark at hand. At first, she was very embarrassed to do such things but seeing Yue Li do what he said, she also followed.

Han Li looked at her sweating profusely as she walked and nodded in approval. This kind of training was also given to Adults who chose to have their Evolution in the clan.

Even Li Yun would have been given some of these pieces of training for the last few weeks by one of the Eleven Evolved before he chose to go through his Evolution. But the Clan never bothers to attempt to make someone a master at something.

Being as proficient as a Master at something was not just up to training. Talent, circumstances, and suitability all mattered.

The most that training alone can make some reach would be High Proficiency. As for true Mastery of any Stage on any weapon, to reach it meant you were part of the Elites of your Stage.

Seeing that they were doing fine, he chose to focus on his own matters. He had to train his body to strength after all.

They would also have to start doing it, but for now, he decided to let them focus on their senses and self-control first.

Taking out his white robe, he got comfortable in some tight white pants and started doing some pushups, The girls looked at him for a while and then focused back on their own training.

Han Li's body was on the slimmer side and his muscles were not very prominent. But his body overall felt like someone who had worked for his food.

It wasn't eye-catching and handsome, but it wasn't in any way ugly.

"Don't focus on me and just keep your training up. Do not slack even if you feel there is no progress."

The girls nodded to themselves and started putting in more effort. Even Liyue who was at first reluctant to put in effort was feeling enthusiastic.

It was natural since as it was the start, they could obviously feel some progress. But the hard part was when they didn't feel any progress. They would really start to feel weary then.

Han Li put them out of his mind and focused on his own pushups. Even taking twenty, he was already feeling the urge to stop. But he didn't and continued.

50…60. Sixty was the limit his body could take, so he rested and stood to the side. Doing exercises like those without proper warming up was a stupid plan.

But he just wanted to see his limits for now.

60 was a weak amount for his hands to start giving up. So it will take him a considerable amount of effort to get his body in an overall great condition.

If he had Evolved, he could have chosen to practice a Body Cultivation Method but for Awakening, there was nothing like that. Only Magical Resources and raw hard work amounted to something.

Breathing calmly, he pondered. It would take months, no, years for his body to become an actually perfect mortal body. But with some unique resources, that could change.

Luck-Chat System would be a major support in this goal. But he would still have to gain resources of equal value and kind to Trade with it.

As he was thinking of it, he also thought about his talent. Delving his senses into his 'Empty Space' his Red glaring Spark was surrounded by the harsh darkness.

And moving the Spark towards the outside, he could feel two steps was the limit.

He brought it out. His Spark was already two-baby-fist sized and he could now sense everything in a 2 meter radius of himself.

'My current Talent should be Average at both Soul and Body. But my Body Talent should be slightly better.'

In Lust Spark absorbing Energy from sex, it mostly absorbed his and his mother's Lust and it usually drained Yue Li's Energy to give it to him.

But the drained Energy can be replenished by Yue Li through the Energy outside. But the absorbed Energy was used not only as replenishment for Han Li but also as a potential heightening Energy.

It was used to directly increase his limits.

'Life Dao really is profound…' Han Li sighed, 'Even though I once wielded its powers I still can't understand its limits. But this time for sure, I will know what it really is.'

For now, he extended his hand to the side of the bed and took out a cloth-wrapped sword. Uncovering it, he looked at it with focus.

Even if someone else got their hands on the sword, they could never easily bind it to them. But if someone who was extremely good with the Dao of Life or someone…

Someone who was an inheritor of one of the other 6 Sacred Eyes and by proxy a Sacred Demon inheritor were to get his hands on it. It was basically giving Han Li's life to their hands.

They were the only type who could steal his sword. That was why when Yue Li used her Spark on his Sword, he had no fear.

As for Kevin who bound the sword to himself. That was just the basics of the basic binding of a treasure like this. The man had attempted to use his own blood to make the sword his.

That was why when Han Li finally bound it for real using his Life Type Spark, Lust Spark, the sword had started bleeding out the blood that was forced into it.

But if, if someone else of Life type came and used his sword. That would be Han Li's death too.

This sword was a major weakness of his infront of some people like himself.

He had to be extremely cautious with it.

Huu...

He breathed out. Sacred Lust Continent might seem peaceful, but he had seen far too much to believe in that.

For now, everything was orderly and in control of the major powers of the continent. But for how long?

He knew it was not for long. Chaos will surely arise.

Only with strength that could literally awe armies can he stand against it when the time comes.

Armies with might that mortals couldn't even fathom.

For now though... he hid the crimson shine of his eyes.

It was time to train.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 53 - 53: The old story
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Once upon a time, long lost in the pages of history was an ancient tree.

It existed in the desolate world where no wind, earth, water, or fire had yet come to.

In nothingness, it existed as the sole something.

It looked vast, way bigger than anything a man could ever hope to see.

When the first signs of life started to appear in the world; the emptiness was filled with land, water, fire, and wind. The tree witnessed and withstood all that and stood watch over the birthing world calmly.

It wouldn't budge when the oceans at one part slammed into its humongous branches. It wouldn't flinch when a part of the forest on one side burned atop its roots. Its leaves, big enough to let giants take cover under it, were seen throughout the world.

The first creature to have ever been born looked at the tree in awe. The creature lived under its shade, breathed the air it provided, and drank the water slipping through its majestic leaves like a waterfall.

The creature didn't know what the tree was. It wasn't intelligent enough to tell this from that.

Until one day, the tree bore fruit.

The fruit in itself was humongous, way beyond the size of the unknown creature itself. But it climbed the vines, traveled the branches, fought the heinous weather, and slowly reached the fruit up above.

It didn't know how long it took to consume the fruit. Years? Months? Days?

Didn't know, the creature hadn't the concept of time. All it knew was it was wrapped in a comfortably red embrace as it chewed at the insides of his newfound dish.

And in due time, the fruit was entirely consumed. on a good day, the creature saw the shine of light again.

But this time, it was no longer the simple, mindless creature that had no mind of its own.

It stood mightily under the shade of the Ancient tree, looking up with tears in its eyes. Below its feet, it could see the waves of the once fearsome ocean slamming hard, in a different direction, there was a desert that stretched long.

It had become a giant. Its legs were deep enough to stretch into the ocean and still stand with its chest above water.

What was it? It didn't know. How did it come to be? It didn't know.

But even in its humongous form, it could only look up at the tree, at its massive crown that stretched above the sky itself.

Had it had the thoughts of an intelligent man, it would have thought, was the tree the pillar of heavens?

But no, the creature had yet to know himself. Nevertheless, amidst the calmness of the world and naivety of itself, one word came to its mind.

Life.

***

Sighing, Han Li gently put down the sword, his red spark of lust vanishing and hiding the unique rust back to its normalcy.

Yue Li and Liyue stood to the side with contemplative faces.

"It was said that in ancient times, every living was way bigger than the current humans or beasts. We are all small in comparison," he said, "It was the start of the Seven Demon Era."

The girls nodded with intrigued faces. History that old was always great to follow.

Liyue asked curiously, "Is that creature one of the Seven Sacred Demons?"

He gazed intently at the sword, running his fingers along its rusty surface before giving a nod, saying: "That creature is the Sacred Demon of Lust. One of the greatest lifeforms the world had yet seen."

There was more about him in the Rust but Han Li knew it was not yet time to tell them the rest of the story. Lust Demon's legendary tales were famous across the continent and artifacts like these were not rare.

The number of exaggerated stories about him was numerous. So, having a sword like this was not very suspicious. Except for the unique rust that might interest some people.

But this was not one of those ridiculous stories that were merely myth, this was the direct copy of history left behind by the Demon himself.

Yue Li took her cup and gently sipped on some tea. After their training in the morning, Han Li took out the sword and started telling the story.

The tea and snacks at the side were her contribution. She asked, "The story says Lust Demon is the first creature to have ever been born, is that true?"

Han Li's eyes twitched as he speechlessly looked at her. She just wanted to sound intelligent by asking at least something from the story. Liyue had asked after all.

"It says he was, so he should be," he speechlessly said and looked at them.

Today, both of them had spent enough time attempting to get their weapons, Knife or Spear, under control. And he already had some ideas of what they could do.

"You are more hard-working than I expected. Your form is good and if you continue to do this for a month or so, you should reach Average proficiency," Han Li said, looking at Liyue who was sitting on the side.

She was startled out of her thoughts and nodded.

Han Li smiled and looked at his mother who was throwing side eyes at him as she enjoyed her own tea. "A knife is a more common tool and you are familiar with it, so it will be easier for you to reach higher proficiency. Mastery will be hard but an Average and High level of proficiency won't be impossible for you to attain in a shorter time."

Yue Li put down the cup and looked at him with squinted eyes. "How long till I reach this Average Proficiency in Knife?"

"One or two more weeks at most and at this pace, you could be called average."

Smiling proudly, Yue Li elegantly took her cup and sipped some more tea.

"But don't be proud, for using it your whole life, today's progress is pitiful. You can barely cut a carrot," Han Li snorted, "I expected more from you."

Yue Li put the cup down and looked at him with a wronged expression. Han Li's heartstrings were touched and he moved next to her and patted her head. "It's good enough since you worked hard."

Sad, Yue Li muttered, "You have been too mean lately."

Smiling, he took the tea from her hands and sipped it. "Nice tea, thank you for the extra cup."

Yue Li looked at him with irritation. "Go away already, it was my tea! At least leave me half."

Putting the empty cup down, Han Li innocently looked at her. She glared at him.

Liyue looked at their interactions with curious eyes. They felt more like siblings rather than a mother-and-son duo to her.

She still didn't feel like a part of this place, but the more she was with them, the more she didn't want to separate.

"Mother Yue, can you come with me to meet Mom?" Liyue suddenly asked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 54 - 54: Young Master rizz
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li looked at Yue Li and Liyue's backs as they entered Liyue's home. Then, he yawned and walked to the side.

His Mom and Liyue's mother were going to speak and meet with each other for the first time. But that didn't really interest him, he had just come alongside for the sake of it.

Standing outside and looking up at the moon hidden half under whisk-like dark clouds, he took the flagon of alcohol on his side and took a sip.

The fruity taste glided down his throat, sending a warm sensation throughout his body. "It's weak but it does have its charm," he said.

Walking from the side leading to the merchant area, a fat man came towards him and said, "I was wondering where the smell of wine came from. No wonder, it was you. When did you come?"

Han Li raised the flagon. The morning rain's effects had yet to vanish and today's night was especially piercing. "How is it fatso, want a drink?" he asked with a shake of the flagon.

"I would have," Pang Li sighed and put the bag in his hand down, "But as you know, my defect after failure is around the stomach. Alcohol doesn't go well with me."

Taking another gulp, Han Li breathed out some turbid air. "Uh, with that kind of pain… it really is quite troublesome."

Pang Li nodded and sat down to the side. He was already dirty from all the mud and rain, it didn't really matter to him if his back got muddy. "Try not to fail in your Evolution if you don't want to experience something like that."

Han Li corked the flagon and put it to the side. "I always wanted to ask you. Why do you work so hard now? Your daughter is safe in my hands and your wife is also not going to have much to look forward to by you gaining some small fortune. So what is it that makes you work so hard?"

Turning silent, Pang Li simply leaned on his house's walls. "Maybe alcohol doesn't hurt once in a while."

Han Li passed him the flagon. The man undid the cork and took a deep sip.

Then, he turned silent for a long while before answering. "I never had anything to my name, no house, no family, nothing."

Rising an eyebrow, Han Li's eyes twitched. A big backstory?

"But I never flinched and became capable enough to marry a woman. That's Li Yao. I ended up marrying her, and similarly, in the end, Liyue is also my daughter. I am sure I will die in 20 or 30 years at most… I want to at least leave behind good memories of myself and something by which to remember me."

"So, the riches you leave behind would be your memento?" Han Li asked.

"Yes. It will be."

"Can I ask you something Fatty? How long had it been since you and Li Yao spoken?"

The fat man turned completely silent.

Han Li nodded to himself. He had some suspicions but now it was sure. Whenever Liyue was late at night, she would always return saying her Mother was alone at home.

Why? What happened to her father?

He simply wasn't someone who asked or talked with his family much.

"Are you guys in some kind of fight?" he asked.

"Not really, we are in a happy relationship and we do care for the little girl. It's just that, in the past few years of our lives, we both became slightly distant," Pang Li said, "In one part, we both cannot have intimate times."

Han Li nodded. Just like Yue Li couldn't be touched under her legs when she used to have leg pain, Liyue's mother was the same.

The people who lost in their Evolution mostly always lose their sexual capabilities. Even Pang Li, his stomach having that kind of pain would have surely made him lose such powers.

Who could even get hard when they felt like their soul would tear in pain if they hardly worked that part of their body?

Han Li didn't even allow his mother to cook while standing when she faced it after all.

"And then, there was Liyue's matter. Li Yao doesn't understand that what I did can only affect my daughter in a good way. But I know." Pang Li didn't elaborate on that but Han Li knew what he meant.

It was the whole thing about benefits and losses. If Liyue had a big loss due to marriage, that was her virginity. But in benefits, the possibility for benefits was high.

Especially when the Husband would never be able to keep a woman under a cage in this city and zone. They had unwritten rules.

"So, after that, you guys rarely spoke, huh?" Han Li smiled.

He didn't really want to break this couple for no reason since he could get women who had failed in their Evolution anywhere. But his absorbing Li Yao's Soul Energy was good for them rather than bad.

But there was a problem. If he ever touched a woman, he didn't like her to be touched by another. His property was his.

He wouldn't let heaven steal from him or man either. It was just like his life and death, he liked it being under his control.

"Pang Li, I have a question for you. If you could heal yourself and your wife, and leave your daughter a fantastic life, would you take it? But there is a catch, you will never see Li Yao or Liyue ever again."

Pang Li chuckled, "If I indeed had such an option, I would take it without a blink. Old people do lose their love, I and Li Yao didn't even marry because of love in the first place. If I can ease her pain and fulfill my role as a father with one move, half of my life's purpose is complete."

"Half?" Han Li asked in intrigue. It was rare that he ever got curious about a man.

"I also have an ambition. Really, what man doesn't have one?" Pang Li looked at him with a side-eye. "I want to have a business, a great business somewhere where I am the richest person people could think of."

Nodding, Han Li said, "Then add that to the list, you will get that alongside the earlier offers."

"I would agree with no more thought."

Han Li smirked, "Go to Sarak City and visit the Highland Merchant Association, tell them that you are the Lord of coins sent by Gramura. Believe me, you will be the richest man in that town from then on. As for Li Yao and Liyue, don't worry about them anymore. I will take care of them."

Pang Li looked at the young man deeply and then chuckled. Sarak City was just one day away from here, so if he was scammed, he could come back any time.

He stood up and once more looked at the young man for the last time. In his mind, Han Li was a young master from a big clan who came and saw his daughter. He fancied her and he wanted her, in that same manner, Han Li saw his wife next and he wanted her too.

As a young master, he should have enough money to heal Li Yao too. He didn't have to know the rest, He understood Han Li was giving him a deal for his wife and daughter instead of a simple question.

He was an intelligent businessman, he, of course, looked for more benefits. If he said no, everything would just be the same, only dumb idiots would choose that in his opinion.

Now Li Yao and Liyue were set for life and he was the same. As long as Han Li was true and not just pulling some kind of ridiculous prank.

But he was sure in his talent to read the room, so Pang Li chuckled heartily and instead of heading home, walked away from it.

Today was a good day, no, a great day. He had achieved his life's purpose.

He hadn't gotten rich in this city, but it was the same. Now there was just a 1 day travel to Sarak and he just had to spend his remaining life in his own way.

Han Li looked at him with a chuckle. Before he knew it, the man had understood his wishes and even seen through him. In reading the room at least, Pang Li was better than himself.

'But really, now I became the young master who takes women everywhere he goes…' He chuckled even louder.

As for the Sarak City? That was a place where some people were waiting for an heir to come. In truth, in Han Li's past life, after the Heir finally arrived; it was revealed that the one who said this specific code was to be chosen as an heir even if he was a commoner before.

But that code was something only an absurdly lucky person could find. Nevertheless, this time, even before the code was revealed, Han Li knew it. So it was his to give or take.

And he didn't mind losing it since he had many such encounters in his past life.

Losing one was nothing.

'Now I have to slowly make Li Yao mine…'

As for healing Pang Li?

He was not the only person with a Life Dao-type Spark who could absorb Soul Energy out of others. There were clans out there who would heal people who failed in Evolution for a price.

With the amount the fatty gets, it won't be too much to spend some to heal himself.

Pang Li also understood the game.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 55 - 55: Heart that strives
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After Uncle Li left, Han Li took the flagon of wine and went into the room where Yue Li and Li Yao were having a fun conversation.

"Yes, She is learning spear and I am learning knife. Han Li says she is doing great."

Merchants from one of the three clan's zones, merchants from different cities, and even individuals sold their things here. If he could find any good products that might help him in improving his physique quicker, it was here.

"Look at this here, fruits coming right up from Sarak, bountifully harvested and fresh! Just 4 Sacred coins for a handful!"

"Great Leather from a wolf! Cold is coming and this here's just 12 Sacred Coins!"

The sound of the traders yelling out their wares and bargaining filled his ears but having his Spark appear inside his mouth, Han Li didn't care and his gaze and Soul Sense looked around for potentially useful things.

Ordinary plants, fruits, and such were different from 'Magical Resources' and it was hard to tell at a glance. Even for someone as experienced as him, it was impossible to tell at first glance.

Magical Resources would always be bought away by one of the three clans' upper-tier citizens as soon as one appeared in the city.

Among the crowd, Han Li just walked and glanced by each shop's ware without stopping. His movement was slow and unhurried, often checking some of the products after a short conversation with the merchant.

But neither his Soul Sense nor gaze caught anything unusual or interesting. Everything there were normal non-magical goods and he had no use for them.

As long as they were magical, no matter what type, he would have gotten a great bargain. One shop after another, he went in and soon went out.

Magical Resources could be told apart as long as one knew what Energy potency was. If you hold a resource like that in your hand, your Spark will tremble.

Something seemed wrong.

The fruit looked like a blue apple, but its stems were spring-like and it had this blue shine. Some of the rare Magical Resources that people could identify at one glance.

Even though he had paid the system its price, it seemed a bit far-fetched for someone to give him this for free.

Shaking his head with a chuckle, he said, "All kinds of people in the world. I thought for a second he was eyeing my ugly mom. Didn't have to soil my hand with blood."

But still, he was far more intrigued about the System than the small thoughts of a small man.

This was a fruit that Li Ming had worked hard to earn and the System still influenced his thoughts to make him give it up. And had he not used the system, would he still have come?

And why did Li Ming buy the exact fruit that he was vying for?

As they reached back the roof and he once again laid his head down on her lap, he couldn't help but look at the fruit with a scrutinizing gaze.

Yue Li giggled after staying silent for a while and Han Li pushed the complicated thoughts to the back of his mind. He finally had a resource to improve his body.

That was one down the list.

Chapter 62 - 62: The Seven Great Daos
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li stood in his empty home, sword in hand, feeling the warmth spread through his body as the coldness of night tickled past his naked torso.

He had just eaten the fruit but he could already feel his body transforming slightly. It was as if his bones were becoming mellow, or as if his body was being squeezed gently by waves of water.

But that sensation was slowly fading and in his opinion, should vanish in an hour or so. It was just an after-effect of eating a Magical Resource.

Just like the nasty fishy taste in his mouth.

"Mom," he said to Yue Li who was observing him curiously, "There are seven Great Daos and among them, the resources easiest to get are from Water, Earth, Air, or Fire. Getting a resource blessed by these four Great Daos is much easier than getting one of the other three — Darkness, Light, or Life."

Yue Li looked at him with a focused face, she had to remember these things. Han Li breathed out a wave of misty air and clenched the knife's handle. "Each Great Dao's Magical Resource has a specialty in a way. The fruit I ate earlier was a magical resource related to the Water Great Dao — Water Spring Fruit.

And it is one that allows my body to grow stronger instead of something poisonous like the Blue Raising Vulgar Lotus, which was also a Water Dao resource."

Yue Li nodded after a bit of thought.

"Since the fruit is a Water Dao material, it cannot improve my body's strength or endurance. Water Dao resources can only improve a body's Flexibility."

With that, Han Li waved the sword around and Yue Li looked at him enviously at first before noticing how his wrists were bending unnaturally.

"That doesn't hurt, right, Xiao Li?" She asked with a frown.

Han Li smirked, and putting the sword down, simply stretched his body. He contorted it in weird angles and his limbs bent and twisted with ease.

"A single fruit is that powerful?" Yue Li said in disbelief, "A yoga practitioner will need years to get to that level."

Han Li stretched some more and felt a phantom pain all over his body. He contemplated inside, 'My body is very flexible, but it isn't completely functional flexibility. I should train a bit more to get used to it.'

"Mom, no. One random fruit can't get you to be as flexible as me and even normal low-level Magical Resources can't do it. But a Water Spring Fruit is different. It's a resource that you can rarely find, even one every year is a good amount. That fruit will take your body's flexibility straight to best mortal levels."

Suddenly, Yue Li asked curiously, "So, you can now attempt Evolution sooner?"

Han Li went closer to her and gave her forehead a flick. "Flexibility, Strength, Endurance, and Agility. One good fire resource, one Earth resource, and one Air resource. My body would be sufficiently strong for a mortal then."

Old Man Book looked at him with wide eyes and then sighed. "What a lucky man you are to marry a useful wife. Mine was so useless when it lived. Ugh, bad experience, bad experience!" He cursed and went behind a curtain to take the said resources.

Luckily, everything mentioned was in stock.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 66 - 66: Top of Mortal Physiques
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the end, the savings in Han Li's hand could barely buy 2 Flame Burst Fruits and 2 Air Soft Berries even with discounts that a clan member receives. But with those in hand and Liyue on his back, he returned home.

Without anyone waking her up and due to yesterday's tiredness, Yue Li was deep in her sleep. Han Li didn't wake her up, Leaving Liyue home, asking her to prepare breakfast, and taking something from his room, he left — the resources in his hands.

Liyue was curious but she didn't ask where he was headed. The snow outside was growing heavier and she was craving for something hot. She also wanted to tell her mom how she was now an Inner Circle Citizen of Starlight Zone.

Han Li might not have seen it as something grand but for her, it was grand. Something that herself a month ago couldn't even think of. Even now, she was reeling inside and even the disgusting taste of the Earth Berry couldn't make her think otherwise.

Although the pain and discomfort in all her movements due to the improved endurance did make her groan slightly. It was just hard to move, not really too painful or completely unbearable.

***

Heading deep into the forest surrounding the Southern Lust City, Han Li moved forward with a clear sense of direction. After about fifteen minutes of walking through the thick woods, he reached a small pond; its surface frosted over by the climate.

He took off his clothes and let out a satisfied sigh as he dipped his leg into the water. The ice gave way with a cracking sound as his leg touched it, and the intense cold made his body shiver involuntarily.

Not minding that, he took a small, glass-like berry from his clothes and popped it into his mouth. It tasted like metal and was extremely disgusting, but he didn't mind that either.

As the flavor settled in and the berry went down his throat, he took another orange-colored fruit and bite by bite, devoured it too. It tasted like charcoal.

These were the Air Soft Berry and Fire Burst Fruit.

Both were Mortal level resources that can help a lifeform reach its top potential in a specific aspect of their bodies. Eating a single Air Soft Berry in their lifetime would make a human already stand out amongst his ordinary peers.

Han Li could feel his body growing lighter, and more agile. His reflexes became as sharp as a housefly. With his experience, he could tell his body had become as nimble as an ordinary cheetah. Just after that, he felt his body bursting with strength.

It was as if his blood was bursting after the Fire-type fruit got into his body. Each cell, each bone felt full and explosive with might, had gorillas fought him, he would have probably grounded them in battle.

A deep sigh escaped his mouth and transformed into snowy mist. All his body started violently spasming and black filth poured out of his pores in an excruciatingly painful manner. He didn't even groan and his eyes only grew sharper.

Only a minute or so did the torture last and smelling the disgusting scent oozing out of him, he jumped into the pond, to the cold harsh water. His dirty body was cleaned thoroughly and he washed himself in all areas for about an hour straight.

Floating above the pond, he then looked around at the wilderness. "Strength of an Elephant, agility of a housefly, flexibility of a cat, and the endurance of a bull," he muttered softly.

Those were the actual representations given by the sects at the top of this continent to confirm whether someone had a perfect mortal physique — he now fulfilled all the conditions. All the inherent potential of the three magical resources had now completely melded into his body and modified it for the better.

He was now among the Elites of the Awakened Stage even if he didn't use any of his future memories. If he fought in his current condition even without a weapon, he would be extremely dangerous — a beast among men.

"But still just a bigger ant in an Evolved's gaze."

He could only die willingly if an Evolved wanted to kill him. Of course, if he used all of his means, he might be able to endure one or two direct blows but that was about it.

As the coldness kept his overheating body calm, he swam to the edge of the pond and got out. He could now attempt Evolution any time he wanted and if someone in the world had a chance to succeed, that was him. But he had no plans to do that for now though, after all, his talent was still barely 'High' and not Sacred yet.

Smiling, he sat to a side and took something out of his robes that were put to the side again.

It was a mirror. Covered with a golden frame of archaic design. It reflected his slightly changed appearance and body that would make any normal girl swoon, but he didn't care for that, he looked at the mirror and with a deep breath, let his spark burn on it.

This was the Heart Reflecting Mirror — a miraculous object that was rarely ever seen. It was said that only a hundred thousand of them existed in the continent at any given time and once used, it will vanish. Only to reappear somewhere a century later.

Before having a breakthrough, it was always better to have a clear heart and this mirror was said to be one of the best things that helped people do it.

It had been a real stroke of good fortune for him to find it in the city in some random girl's hand.

The mirror suddenly started cracking as a profound sensation of being gazed at spread through him. It was as if a pair of eyes roamed through his body. His heart clenched at the foreign sensation but he remained seated with a focused, red gaze.

With a resounding crack, the whole mirror cracked and a broken image of himself briefly reflected in his eyes before the mirror vanished from his grasp like smoke.

"You have disappointed me, Han Li." said a voice from behind him, filled with a profound loathing. Making Han Li's scalp tingle.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 67 - 67: Reflection of the Heart
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You have disappointed me, Han Li." said a voice from behind him, filled with a profound loathing. Making Han Li's scalp tingle.

He swiftly turned around like a blur and glanced at the man standing behind him. His heart immediately started beating heavily.

It was a man wearing a black martial robe, his hair a long fall of white, his skin a pale caramel and his hands bloody. His eyes, as red as crimson blood.

"Surprised?" The man asked, his voice barely suppressing his fury, "Were the 500 years of grueling training just so you could be surprised at a disaster you called out yourself?"

Han Li's eyes trembled slightly. The man in front of him was familiar to him. His robes which were once white but had turned black due to the tremendous amount of blood he had washed the lands with were nothing new to him — it was he himself.

The him just after the Transcendance War.

The Red-Eyed Demon, Han Li.

He frowned and looked at the black-robed version of himself. "So the mirror directly reflects my own self-image? Is this my heart?"

The man looked at him with dead fish eyes. Not replying to such worthless questions.

"Let me tell you another of the mirror's attributes instead. One that is not as widespread as the other details. If you fail in this reflection, you will have at least 10 times the difficulty in raising Cultivation Stages." The man chuckled, his overbearing voice making the birds scatter.

Han Li only stared coldly at him. Himself or whoever, no one was allowed to barr his path.

"Look at that face." The man walked closer and Han Li didn't try to walk away. "Those cold eyes, I know those. Determined, and faithful to the hard path you have to tread. Those are my eyes but they no longer fit you, Deep down, you know it too right?"

Han Li's eyes never left the man's gaze. The reflection continued, "The man I was is not you. I am not one to accumulate a bunch of trash and burdens that will only make my path harder." The gaze burned into him.

"You're growing mellow Han Li, you are growing away from what you are meant to be. We're Demons, people who will kill their own parents to achieve their goals. Don't you remember the days when I — ah not I — YOU! Don't you remember the days you killed little kids, barely at the age to talk, in cold blood to reach your goals?"

Han Li didn't even blink. He had killed innocents before and he would still kill if they were harmful to him. It was the basic law of survival, there was no place for emotions there.

The illusion snickered and continued, "Yet that you are here, putting more burdens into your heart. You started to have affection for that Liyue girl too, right? What a joke! Yue Li was one thing I could understand, but you came all the way to the past to actually add more burdens instead of finally improving your chances of reaching that sacred goal?"

Han Li's eyes grew deep and thoughtful. His hands clenched slightly.

"Ah! HOW embarrassing, you always say you want to control life and death and achieve Eternal Life, and that's how you do it? Laughable! Do you think that kind of goal is something you can achieve…" its voice turned as cold as ice, "without sacrificing everything, prove your heart to me Han Li, is your heart clear?

Is it still the same Heart of Cultivation that strived to achieve your goal while keeping your mother at your side? Have you not… strayed from the path?"

Han Li's face darkened a bit as he glared at the reflection. Indeed, he had strayed from the path he had once sworn to walk. In his past life, the number of people he felt affection for was numerous.

The girls he felt he had to protect were not just his mother. But he had cut all affection away from his heart and sworn to revive Yue Li and become an Eternal being with her before once again letting his heart free. But had he become an Eternal Being?

Had he made sure Yue Li or even both of them did not die this time?

He didn't even have another chance. And still, he had chosen to bring more trouble to his life by allowing a budding affection for a girl to build up in him?

"Pathetic, right?" The Reflection faced him an inch away from his nose. "What will you do if Liyue dies this time? Cut affection for Yue Li to become a demon so you can once again attempt reaching the place where even your more resolute self couldn't?"

Han Li looked at it and sighed deeply. "I never thought the mirror was that useful. It really showed the fault in me. It really is a rare and beautiful treasure that is worthy of its renown."

The reflection raised an eyebrow. "You will cut your affection for her? Return to the path moving towards greatness?"

"Maybe that too is a good choice…" Han Li sighed once more. Finally, he moved his gaze away from its eyes and to the direction where the distant sound of footsteps could be heard in his enhanced senses.

"So you are not choosing to cut away emotions for others this time?" The reflection frowned deeply, a profound sensation of disappointment on its face.

Han Li smiled. "I killed many in my past life and maybe, unknowingly, I killed myself too. Do you know how lively my heart could be? Ah, you can't. Because I know you, your heart never beat when you saw cute actions from a woman after mom's death after all. But mine does now, maybe, I don't want to kill myself this life."

The reflection looked at him with an even more disappointed gaze. "You really have disappointed me, Han Li. Are cute women more important than your goals? Your cultivation?"

Han Li chuckled. "I never said that, did I?"

He spread his hands apart. "I just have to prepare more than I did the last life. I just have to kill more than I last did. I just have to be much more protective of the ones I care about. I just have to be… much, much stronger."

"Do you think true strength comes without sacrifices?" The reflection snarled.

How naive was he? Had he not seen the world at its core?

Han Li chuckled. "I am a Demon, what do you think true demons are supposed to do?"

The reflection turned silent. That was true, he himself couldn't call the himself of his past life a 'true' demon.

"Speechless?" Han Li chuckled. "Yes, true demons follow their heart and do what it so desires."

The man looked at him, profoundly stunned. "Do you have that much belief in yourself?"

"Just as much belief as you have in yourself."

The reflection started fading away and the man looked at him deeply. The path of a true demon… "So you — No, I — chose this path this time. It will be much harder."

With a sigh, the reflection vanished along the snowflakes in the clearing.

Path of a Demon was easy. Path of a true demon? It was harsh.

A Demon can be ruthless to all to achieve his goals. But a true demon will always have burdens in his heart that will only accumulate over time.

In the true world of reality, such burdens will be used by your enemies against you. They will be your fatal weakness.

Nevertheless, to willingly take it on and believe in oneself to be the final winner — that was a True Demon.

Han Li ignored the vanishing reflection and frowningly looked to the west. Footsteps grew closer and closer, and his face fell as a man came into his vision.

His heart dropped as the man noticed him.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 68 - 68: Meeting a Disaster face to face.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The man had a calm face, he wore black martial robes and on his head was short, curly black hair. His black eyes gleamed chaotically as they met Han Li's deep red eyes.

They were both about 6 to 7 meters apart but both of their senses were sharp enough to easily see each other. The black-haired man frowned at the naked Han Li and moved towards him.

Han Li casually took his robes as his mind spun in all angles. The man came in front of him exactly when he got loosely dressed; his nose scrunched up at the fishy smell around the area.

"Physique cleansing to achieve the perfect mortal body, you are quite capable. Young man," the black-haired man said with a chuckling voice.

Han Li tightened his white robes and looked at him. He had probably been misunderstood to be an outsider due to his not so alike appearance to the other Starlights. Especially, his eyes. But that didn't make him relieved.

"I was just lucky to have entered my clan's inner circle and bought the 4 essential resources," Han Li said, his face reddening shyly.

The man raised an eyebrow but then asked what he was there for, "Did you see someone with a similar appearance to me somewhere? He has feathers for hair instead of the ones like mine."

Han Li's heart turned tranquil as he showed a confused expression. "I haven't seen anyone like that. And I came all the way from the west today."

Even as he casually replied, Han Li's mind was spinning. The person in front of him was no normal man, he was the Clan Head of the Ravens. Someone who was one of the three most important people in the city. A Power Born.

He looked like a youth but Han Li knew not to underestimate him. He knew this man was as ruthless as Kevin was — and much stronger. There was a chance he would be killed just for breathing in front of people like this and even though no pressure was released, he felt taut.

His life was in a thread and any time the man so desired, he would be dead without the shadow of a doubt. Power Birth Cultivators were disasters in human form, but Han Li tried to act as ignorant as possible about the man's identity.

That was the best for now.

He also implied he wasn't from the Starlight clan with his words about him coming from the west. Thankfully, today he hadn't worn an actual clan robe but just ordinary white robes too. Or he might have been killed for just being a talented Starlight.

Luckily, he didn't look like a Starlight either. He had been exceptionally lucky that day. But that only made Han Li seethe inwardly.

"He was not there all the way till west?" William Raven, the casual-looking man murmured.

"But I did see people like you who had similar eyes and hair, but none of them had feathers for hair," Han Li said with a thoughtful expression.

William shook his head. That must have been junior members of the clan. Evolved all had feather-like hair or something else distinct about them. "I heard loud speaking from around here and came here to check it out, was that you talking to yourself?" he asked as he turned to leave.

Han Li shyly scratched the back of his head.

"What an interesting young man." William smiled and with a chuckle, left towards the south of the forest. Going further west was useless for now.

Han Li just watched him leave and only after he moved into the green forest and vanished did he sigh in relief. But then he clenched his hands.

He had been caught off guard in this encounter. Indeed, even though these people were nothing to the old him, to the current him, they were threats. He should have cared more, and prepared more.

This encounter was not under his control. And that, was something that shouldn't happen. He breathed out and walked back home, his mind spinning with all sorts of calculations.

There was a good chance Kevin's body would be found in a short time since someone like William was out in search, but even if it was found, it was okay. There was no way an Evolved's death would be associated with a mere Awakened like himself.

No roots were left behind as a cause of trouble. Han Li walked into the city and moved towards his home. But he then thought about his current condition.

Almost no Awakened could stand against him at this point, his weapon skill was top notch, and his body was top notch too. Only his Energy Limit or in other words, talent was weaker than the best as of now. He should quickly get that needed Soul Energy from Li Yao and Evolve.

As he moved through the forest, he suddenly remembered a poem one of his women had recited in his past life.

"Living in a land of peace, you grow lethargic. Living in a land of death you grow lethargic. I wonder then, where the hell was, I wonder then where the heaven was…" He smiled. "But I know, in all hells there is heaven, just as in all heavens there is a hell."

As the morning sun moved, it was almost straight above their heads. Time was nearing noon and way past breakfast. Han Li shook his head and smilingly entered the Starlight Zone.

The guard, the women on the way, some men, all of them looked at him as he passed by them. They didn't know why, but Han Li especially made their eyes move towards him.

A physique so shapely that women felt their throats dry up at first glance. An eye so deep men felt their hearts tremble at it's depth.

Nevertheless, Han Li didn't care and just walked past with a humble smile.

Reputation was a good weapon to build. People's voices might help him down the road. No reason to act arrogant and lose the hearts of others. Soon, he was back home and smiled at the sight of Yue Li and Liyue training outside without him.

They smiled instinctively in a sense of smug satisfaction at his smile.

It was never the situation or predicament, not hell nor heaven, it was the person in it that mattered. One could be in heaven and still feel sad, one could be in hell and still feel happy. It was never the situation, it was always how one used it to their advantage that mattered.

Even now, he had many advantages like 500 years of memory or his mother's enigmatic system, but only if he took advantage of all of the good things would he stand a chance.

…

Suddenly, Yue Li and Liyue blinked in amazement. "Xiao Li…!"

They were stunned.

He looked so much different from the morning. It was the difference of stone from clay, he was still evidently Han Li but his face had no flaws. It was fair and healthy while his body emitted an innately athletic aura.

"Amazed? Surprised?" Han Li laughed. "This is how a mortal perfect body looks like, too handsome, right?"

Yue Li and Liyue moved beside him and his mother touched his chest. "So thick."

Liyue was too curious about one thing and looked at his hands unblinkingly. Han Li offered it to her and she blushed slightly as she took a hold of it. 'So firm...' she said in her mind.

"As strong as an elephant. Your husband could totally uproot trees and break down walls." He looked at her with a smirk. She looked at him confusedly, not understanding what he meant.

Smiling, he whispered something to her and she immediately stood rooted. A blush slowly coloring her face a cute red.

Yue Li saw she was looking away and checked her son's important organ. Then sighed relievedly. "It didn't grow any bigger."

Han Li pinched her cheeks. "It did grow stronger. If I was bad at using my strength, I might even hurt you accidentally by pinching like this."

"Will we also become more beautiful if we eat all the four fruits?" Yue Li asked with bright eyes. Not minding him pinching her cheeks non-stop.

Liyue also blinked and looked at him with a red face. "Of course, you guys will grow more beautiful. But of course, I don't plan to give it to you any time soon."

Frowning and holding his pinching hand, Yue Li snorted. "You are not the one to buy it anyway. It's my daughter-in-law who decides if she gives it to me or not, right, Yue'er?"

Liyue looked at her with wide eyes. That was what her mother called her. She had the profound urge to say yes, but then embarassedly looked away. She didn't know what to buy, nor did she have any money.

Nor did she want to go against Han Li's wish.

Yue Li sighed in disappointment, her eyes a bit wet. "Look at this poor mom, she doesn't have her son on her side and neither does the daughter-in-law stand up for her. What a cruel world."

Han Li ruffled her head. "You need at least High Proficiency in a weapon to have Top Level Mortal Physique while having full control over it. Or you will just have a lot of power with no good control. You will just break stuff with that kind of strength, it will be nothing good." He paused. "So become a High Proficiency knife user first.

Or just reach my level of body with natural training instead of resources."

Had the librarian known that he had already eaten the Earth and Water type resource, he would have never given the Air and Fire type without making sure Han Li had reached 'High' proficiency.

Liyue and Yue Li looked at him with awe. That meant he was already at the High level then?

Yue Li was surprised but thinking back to the way he handled his sword and taught them, it was only normal his skills were better than theirs.

But he rarely ever trained with his sword. He only teaches or exercises his body…

My son is so talented! He reached that level with only this much training.

Had she known people at Han Li's age with Awakened High level were considered fiendishly talented people who were training at the weapons even before Awakening, from a tender age; she probably wouldn't have thought like that.

Or maybe, she still would think the same even if it was revealed he had more than just 'High' proficiency.

"Anyway, is Mother-in-law awake? I have something to do with her."

"Mother? Yes, she just had breakfast," Liyue said while pushing a stray hair away from her face.

"You guys stay here and practice, I'll come join you later."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 69 - 69: Meiying, his beloved!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li walked towards Li Yao's room with a faint smile on his face and a warmth in his eyes that hadn't been there before. No matter how foreign the illusion had seemed from himself, in the end, it was his own heart's reflection.

In the deepest core of his heart, he knew Liyue and Li Yao would only add to the weight on his back as he climbed this mountain of life to reach that far-away peak. And he had naturally only kept the budding affections in his heart distant.

He didn't want more weight instinctively. But now here, his heart was a different one from the one he had yesterday. His heart of cultivation had changed — It had broken through.

Previously, his Heart of Cultivation was based on two things: Protecting his mother and reaching a level of might where life and death are under his control. It was an extremely solid Heart based on the demonic path.

In this path, to achieve those two goals, he could sacrifice anything else. He could kill infants, burn the world down, or sacrifice anything else in existence. It was the epitome of demonism. A man going at his goals.

But that sacrifice included his own happiness and his own everything. He would not let himself feel love for others, he wouldn't let himself do anything that Is in any way detrimental to this goal. But after the reflection, it had changed, and deep inside, Han Li knew it had changed.

Now, his heart was the same, but it also had one more goal — to live a human life that also gave his own human desires some space. As he stood outside Li Yao's room, Han Li stopped for a second and thought of it.

In this life, he wanted to live like a human. He wanted to do good for all of the women he had cut off from his past life. There had been some people whom he had betrayed in his past life.

Even now, he could remember his beloved Meiying's face as clearly as the day she died for him. The girl had loved him so deeply, she cut her own tongue off instead of revealing his whereabouts. She had been surrounded by the righteous scums who were in search of him, but for his goals, he abandoned her with the same cold gaze he had adorned for years.

He was cruel. Ruthless.

Ruthless to others but far more Ruthless to himself. He had actually loved her just as much. He would have been ready to throw his head away from her without a blink. But no, his goals were more important.

That was the more logical step to take. If Meiying was saved and he died, that would be the end. But if he successfully ended up achieving his goal, he could revive his mother, the dying Meiying, and everyone he cares about who ends up dying.

So, he had cruelly abandoned her as she looked on with a soft gaze as he hid just above her in a hidden corner. He had witnessed her being killed right infront of him with lips bit and blood flowing.

He had been far too weak to fight against the righteous group then. That was the day he began to cut off affection before it could take root in his heart. Many came after who loved him just as much as Meiying, but never again had he loved them back.

He always cut off the bud of affection right at the beginning. "But no more." Han Li muttered in his mind. No more will he let the world walk over him as he waits to achieve his goals one day, he will not allow a situation where he is not under control to happen.

He will let himself do whatever he wants. No longer will he bind his own heart in chains. He will let it roam free and live to its content, and this time, no one will surround him. This time, he will be the one who knocks — he will be the danger.

And to put a start to this, he would let himself love Liyue. He would let himself care for Li Yao. He wouldn't mind having affection for them.

Most True Demons let their heart lead the way no matter how hard the path seems, their bare greed would let them walk through thorns and nails. But he was different from those ordinary true demons.

Their hearts are based on their desires, if they felt greedy, they steal, if they felt lustful, they rape, and if they felt sloth, they sleep. But he was not like that, he was above his base emotions — he didn't mind losing worthless things like satisfaction.

As their hearts were based on desires, their desires controlled them. But he wouldn't let himself easily fall for such

He will kill, only if it benefits him. He will have sex, only if it actually makes him feel fulfilled or if it is useful. He won't just follow his instincts like a dumb idiot. He will be a true demon who will sacrifice anything for 3 things now.

To be under control of his own life and death. To protect his loved ones. To protect his own Heart of cultivation.

'My heart of cultivation became much more complex, but I should be much more powerful when I Transcend. Far more powerful than I previously was.'

Transcendence mainly focused on the heart after all.

Spark Acquiring, Awakening, Evolution, Power Birth, Transcendence, Demi-Sacred, and Sacred. The seven stages of cultivation were so, and at his peak, Han Li had looked down on all below Transcendance like ants.

And he himself?

He was one of the strongest people on this continent. A Transcendent. And not merely that, he had complete Mastery of the Sword at the Transcendent Stage.

His eyes brightened like a torch as he entered Li Yao's room. Yes, he was accumulating more burdens on his path to the top. But he was sure he would be able to carry it. He was confident.

Unlike his past life, he had experience. And once he transcends, it will only be more profitable. And he didn't want there to be another situation like Meiying.

...

He smiled warmly as Li Yao's eyelashes fluttered open in confusion. She had made it a habit to doze off around this time to make her crippled life less lonely when her Yue'er or someone else was not around.

She blinked as Han Li's face came into her sight. He smilingly came beside her and asked, "Liyue told me you were awake and I came here to see you, how's the pain?"

Li Yao instinctively held her hand out for him and Han Li took it. "My pain flared for around a minute yesterday night, but it wasn't as severe as it used to be and it was not the major flaring."

Han Li sat on the bed and started massaging her hands. He didn't speak and just kept massaging. It was the usual massage but with a smile, Han Li decided to make it a bit more sensual.

His hands glided smoothly through her skin and she could feel her heartbeat rising. But she didn't think anything about it and just closed her eyes like she did usually. A while later, Han Li put her hands down and put his hand above her stomach.

She opened her eyes.

"Mother-in-law, I found a way to cure you," he said, gently caressing her stomach in a way that wasn't uncomfortable.

Li Yao blinked. "Cure?"

"Yes, cure. A one-time thing that will solve your issue once and for all," Han Li said, emphasizing one-time.

"What is it?" she asked, half curious and half disbelieving.

"I want you to let me do whatever I want to do with your body for once. And I will surely heal you. And believe me, it's done with pure intentions."

Li Yao looked at him with wide eyes. Her first instinct was to believe Han Li was after her body but she quickly scrubbed that off. He hadn't even done anything with Liyue who had already shown her willingness.

He was not such a lustful man.

"What will you do?" she asked, filled with misgivings.

Han Li kept petting her stomach slowly as he spoke. "Not something bad. It will heal you."

Li Yao looked at him with a puzzled gaze. "If it was just pleasure that you wanted, I am pretty sure your mother or Liyue would do it for you. Yue'er might not have noticed but I know you guys have a deeper relationship."

Han Li smiled awkwardly.

"…So, it can really heal me, and you won't do something really bad?"

"Yes." He could see she had misgivings and he also knew how to get rid of them. "We can get Liyue's permission if you want."

Li Yao hesitated but nodded, saying with a confused tone, "If Liyue allows you, you can do anything you want with me."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 70 - 70: Healing the diseased
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What will you do?" she asked, filled with misgivings.

"Yes." He could see she had misgivings and he also knew how to get rid of them. "We can get Liyue's permission if you want."

Li Yao hesitated but nodded, saying with a confused tone, "If Liyue allows you, you can do anything you want with me."

"Liyue, come here!" he shouted.

It only took a minute before Liyue walked into the room, confused.

"Liyue, can I heal your mother?"

Liyue looked at them with a pounding heart. She asked incredulously, "Bi..Big Brother Han, you found a way to heal her?"

"Yes, I did. And it's a surefire way to heal her."

She looked at them and nodded without even thinking. It was her lifelong goal to heal her mother. She clenched her trembling hands.

"Yes, heal her. Please, I beg you to heal her!" she asked as she looked down. Hiding her moist eyes.

"No need to be so dramatic. She's my mother too. Come here." He beckoned her and she confusedly came beside him.

Han Li said, making her sit beside him. "In healing her, I might have to touch her inappropriately. Is that okay?"

Liyue looked at his hands placed on her mother's stomach and finally understood why he asked her permission. It was to do the same thing he did with her earlier but this time with her mother.

She looked at her mother who was looking at her encouragingly.

She knew her mother from the day she was born and knew, she wouldn't hate her even if she said no.

Yue Li was just about to answer when Han Li's eyes shifted to his right. A frown forming on his face. With his senses, he could hear all kinds of sounds and he had just heard a footstep. But it vanished as quickly as it appeared.

Had it not vanished and walked somewhere, it was fine, but what was this?

His red eyes shimmered and his soul sense spread out to cover ten meters around himself in all angles. Liyue and Yue Li also got tense at his sudden silence.

"Fellow Daoseeker, there is no reason to hide, why don't you show yourself up?" Han Li's casual and calm voice asked.

There was no reply except the cry of a crow from somewhere else. And Yue Li and Liyue breathed out the tense vibes. "What are you so tensed for all of a sudden, Xiao Li, is someone here? Is it Li Yao? Didn't she say she won't come?

Han Li didn't reply and his eyes started to sharpen. This type of sneaky game, he had no real influence in the world to have enemies yet, was it truly just a mistake?

He refused to believe so. "Akarsanaya Swaha—"

"Stop! Fellow Daoseeker! Stop!" came a fear-filled voice as a young boy walked infront from behind a tree. The boy had faint unhealthy black hair and a sallow face, his eyes were a pale grey all over. But the moment Yue Li and Liyue saw the young boy, they gasped — He was full of wounds from head to toe, and even his cheeks were cut and bleeding.

"Big Brother Han!"

"Xiao LI!"

Yue Li got up from the ground and Liyue also held Han Li's sleeve in worry. He on the other hand just looked at the young boy with a frown. He rarely ever came out to train outside in the wildlife, the episode with the Raven Clan's head was enough of a wake-up call for him.

The only reason he was out today was to use his Magic Power — Low-level Dao Magic, seduction 9 times on some beast to see what happens once it is completed. To encounter some strange Evolved with wounds all over his body was not part of his plan.

'I told you two to not follow me…' Han Li coldly glanced at the Evolution Stage 'Young' boy. He had asked his mom and the rest to stay at home, but they had followed him secretly. Even though he knew it was happening, it was cute, so he ended up letting them come.

He knew the Clan Head of Ravens entered seclusion too. After all, tomorrow was the Raven's turn to host their Awakening Ceremony after all. He knew the Clan Heads would become very busy at this point.

Mainly some rituals like thanking the ancestors or teaching the people about attempting Evolution some good tricks. That was how it was, but even though he was cautious, trouble seemed to have come looking for him.

All these thoughts flickered past his head in a second and he asked, "May I know why you came here, fellow daoseeker?" His voice was rather unfriendly.

If the boy infront was wounded, someone or something should have wounded him.

"Don't worry friend," The boy said, his face a tired wreck. "No one is after me, I already…" He stopped and looked at the ladies behind Han Li. "Anyway, you get the gist."

Han Li looked at the boy deeply. "Are you trying to implicate me?"

No matter what, Han Li already saw the young boy as dead. Nobody except those whom he accepted was allowed to learn that he was an Evolved and since he had spread his Soul Sense earlier and tried to use a magic power, it was sure he knew Han Li was an Evolved.

"I have no reason to implicate you, my friend, in fact, when I felt your presence, I was about to head out when you called out. I just don't want to get hit again in my condition, you know, right?"

Han Li glanced at him seriously. If the boy had not been an Evolved, Han Li would have already used his Eyes and Soul Sense to scan him. But scanning using soul sense an evolved? That was blatant rudeness!

"What are you going to do now?" the boy asked, cold sweat and blood oozing from his cheek and neck.

Liyue and Yue Li cringed in pain at the mere sight of it. It was too horrendous, they couldn't even bear to think of the level of pain.

"I will not use magic and we never met, you can go." Han Li's calm tone didn't betray his thoughts at all. But the pale grey eyes of the boy shimmered and he nodded before walking in a certain direction away from them.

But before he reached far away, Han Li's soul sense covered him. "Sorry, I just have to check something. It is said our rival clan's Evolved was murdered, so just for security's sake, I have to check you."

They were some distance away already and Han Li now had the opponent in plain sight, he had not much fear of a random Evolved. But the boy trembled as he felt the foreign soul sense briefly scan him.

"Okay, you are clean. You may go." Han Li said, his tone much more friendly.

The boy nodded and sprinted away limply. While Han Li stared at him, deep and hard.

[Yang Wei] [Age: 29 Years old]

[Current Cultivation: Evolved - Minor Bone Dao]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Awakened]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Transcendent, Life Dao.]

'Bone King Lifestealer of the Bewitching Beauty Sect. To think I would meet someone like you here…' Han Li's eyes deepened several degrees at the reminder of this person.

Han Li and he had no real connection but he knew who the person was. And that made his heart skip a beat.
