Chapter 85 - 85: War Future, and Bone King.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

'Bone King Lifestealer of the Bewitching Beauty Sect. To think I would meet someone like you here…' Han Li's eyes deepened several degrees at the reminder of this person.

Han Li and he had no real connection but he knew who the person was. And that made his heart skip a beat. In every 400 to 600 years or so, a battle, a war happens. This battle would always be extremely important — it was called the Transcendence War.

The next Transcendence War was merely 15 years away. In this war, Power Born individuals—people at the level of clan heads—would all join in an attempt to claim a unique magical resource that is essential in breaking through from Power Birth to the Transcendent Stage. He had lost his precious 7 years of life in that war as a pawn of the Wisdom Seeking Villa.

Every Continent among the 7 great continents only had a set number of this 'Transcend Mortality Orb' and it was an absolutely important thing for the Power Born. Since if they didn't get it in their generation, the next time the war happened, they would be nearly dying due to age after all.

He was unsure about the other continents, but, Sacred Lust Continent only had 4 Orbs every generation.

So, the wars were absolutely savage with almost all the clans getting involved as long as the Power Born had even the slightest hint of ambitions. In his past life, he had been nothing in the war other than one of the thousands upon thousands of Evolved.

Fighting for months on end, tooth and nail to ensure he survived to meet his family again.

Only in the war after that, 463 years later from today, he had fought on his own will and then won one of the fruits for himself, becoming a Transcendent. But that was not the intriguing part. To Han Li, that was just a memory. What mattered was this Bone King Life Stealer.

He was a legendary person. As all four of the Orbs were taken by some Power Born — one of them being in Han Li's hand — and the other Power Borns were completely out of steam to fight back. The war of Transcendence ended and the next generation's Transcendents were decided.

Transcendents usually had 600 years of Lifespan at best after all. And among those four, Bone King Lifestealer was not one of them.

But as the four people started to get ready to settle back and inherit one of the 4 sects, something happened; A fifth Transcendent appeared out of nowhere and inherited one of the 4 sects' mantle, Bewitching Beauty Sect — The sect that owned all brothels throughout the continent.

And that Transcendent was the wounded 'Young' boy who walked away, who was in fact 29 years old at the moment. Han Li also noticed in his 'Life Reading Gaze' one thing, unlike most others, this person had not died in any of his 2 past lives. He was even alive after Han Li died.

In fact, as Yue Li and Liyao watched the young boy's shadow move into the shadows of the forest, Han Li was looking at him with deeply intrigued eyes. If he had to say why he ended up dead in his past life, and who was responsible for it, Bone King had no direct part in it.

But with how clearheaded he was at the moment, Han Li knew he was one of the most pivotal reasons. In fact, he should be THE reason.

In each generation, there existed 4 Transcendents and at the time of the Transcendence Wars, the previous generation Transcendants would be at their weakest and once the war ended, they would soon die of age. Treasures like Lifespan Increasing Dew didn't work on them.

They were like gods after all. If Power Borns could still be barely considered mortal, Transcendent were true godly beings. How can mortal resources work on them its wonders?

So, once the 4 current Transcendents die, the next 500 or so will be peaceful times. There would be no wars and there would be no need to fight — even demons who killed millions won't be considered like one after they Transcend.

There would only be four of them at a time in the continent, why fight and destroy peace for no reason? All of them would naturally don't want that.

But the 5th one's entry changed this. He was a threat to the power they held over the 4 clans. The power of the 4 Sects was more than what met the eye and no Transcendent wanted to let it go.

So, since the Bone King already inherited one only three more were left and Han Li hadn't bothered caring about it, thus, he lost the chance to win it for himself. And thus, in the peace of the other 4, a potential danger appeared. And that was Han Li.

What if he one day coveted one of the Sects after knowing its true purpose? What if?

They feared such a possibility and teamed up in order to kill him. And then, Wang Ren — Transcendent of the Demon Restraining Temple, killed him after stealing his Sword. That was how he died.

"Who is that person, Xiao Li?" Yue Li asked, nervous. "Shouldn't we bring him to the hospital inside the city? He is too wounded."

Liyue was pale in the face and said, equally nervous, "I think that person is dangerous, Mother. It's better if we don't get into his business… right, big brother? He was even talking as if the wounds were nothing to him."

Yue Li was startled. "Acting with that kind of wound?"

"That is why I said he is acting. He might have thought I couldn't tell but I know him. He is not someone small as you guys think, he is an Evolved."

"An Evolved?!" Both of them exclaimed in fear. "An Evolved was wounded to that degree?"

"That is not the focus here my ladies. The focus is on this person himself. He is Yang Wei, and do you know what his Spark—hmm—Minor Dao Soul is?"

"What is that?" Yue Li asked, feeling weird that Xiao Li knew so much about some random person.

"Your Spark will evolve into a Minor Dao Spark if you Evolve. Anyway, let me explain more. That kid. His Dao Soul is, Minor Bone Dao Soul. And it is not an Ethereal one like ours but a Transformation one instead."

Yue Li and Liyue blinked.

"That means his body is far stronger than normal. Especially with a Dao like Bones under the Life Great Dao, his abilities of rejuvenation could be called almost top-notch already. The only thing he is lacking would be Ethereal Magic Powers at this point."

"So he is strong?" Yue Li asked the cut-to-the-point question like usual.

"Yes, Strong. In terms of our city's Evolved, he should be among the strongest. And if he goes close to them, just forget about their lives. His body is absolutely, horrendously strong. To the point, once he is about 10 meters close, dying is a guarantee."

That was Transformation Path's advantage in the early stages. Once one breaks through, their bodies will immediately become perfect for the Stage. But how strong was this perfected body?

That would be determined by the Cultivator's talent. If he was a Sacred Talent, his body is how strong a body can possibly be in that particular Stage. And Han Li knew this person was indeed a Sacred Talent.

Yang Wei's body was a menace. To the point, even if Han Li completed [Demonic Transformation]'s first 3 Transformations that he can cultivate for now with his soul, he could only barely stand as an equal in the body department.

That was the level a Transformation Path Evolved could be. But of course, Han Li had his own advantages.

"Our people will die if he is close?" Yue Li blinked. "So what if he is in a distance?"

"He will have no good countermeasures against our good Magical Powers. He could only be a sitting duck and before he arrives close, he will be, as you might say Bam! Dead."

Yue Li nodded, having completely forgotten her earlier misgivings and also the irritation against Han Li's harsh training.

"So, no matter what we do, we can't be a match for him in close combat?" Liyao asked, reluctant to admit defeat after this much training. It had already been more than months of grueling after all.

Han Li looked at her with a praising and emotional gaze. "Look at my little Liyue, your husband's effort hadn't been in vain. Far better than mom's laziness. That's what a spear user should be like, they mustn't waver even in front of the sky."

Yue Li glared at him with grievance again. This! This was favoritism!

Liyue on the other hand blushed as a red tint washed her face hot. Even though she had already gotten used to Han Li groping her, his praising her was rare. If not ever.

"Yes, it is almost impossible for an Ethereal to stand toe to tow against Transformation path in close combat. And that logic applies vice versa too… but…"

"Yes, it is almost impossible for an Ethereal to stand toe to tow against Transformation path in close combat. And that logic applies vice versa too… but…"

Yue Li and Liyue nodded, having already put the strange boy earlier out of their thoughts.

"This Tree guy had focused not only on his spark but also bothered about his body like I did by eating all four fruits. He also cultivated a body strengthening technique once he reached Evolution."

Yue Li and Liyue's eyes brightened.

"And take me as a same stage cultivator but I am of the Transformation Path." Han Li clenched his fists, pulled back his arms, held his breath, and as a powerful aura of might build up, smashed the tree with all his might.

BAM! A heavy hit was heard and splinters exploded all over the surroundings.

The girls stepped back in amazement. The tree hadn't completely broken apart but had lost a significant amount of bark and the flesh of its stem was now visible.

"He got severely damaged, but not dead. This is because he cultivated his body and is just as talented as me. This is the best level a person can achieve through cultivation."

Yue Li looked at his hand worriedly. She only calmed down when she saw no bruises. Then she focused on his teachings like a humble student.

Whatever she said, she hadn't ever honestly went against his training routine.

"So, in essence, Ethereal Type cultivators can never have a body equal in might to the Transformation Path?" Liyue asked, her eyes bright as she glanced at Han Li. At this moment, he seemed different.

Like a unique breed. So casually mighty, like a volcano, that could erupt at any time he so desired. She was once again reminded, her husb… fiance was a strong and capable man.

"Sadly, that is true in almost 100% of cultivators," Han Li sighed. "Transformation Path cultivators will always have a stronger body, while Ethereals will always have stronger Magic Powers. It is almost set in stone, but 'stronger' doesn't mean the other part will be vulnerable. Ethereals with great bodies are harder to deal with for them since they can't be killed in one or two punches.

"And once the Ethereals get away from them and get the chance to use Magic Powers, a fight will be to the Ethereal's advantage."

Both girls tried to imprint this knowledge deep into their brains while Han Li contemplated. That was true for 'almost' 100% but not 100%. Sacred Demon Inheritors for example should be people capable of equally standing up with both Transformation and Ethereal Path's top-notch cultivators as equals.

He also knew some weird freaks of nature like the bone king who couldn't be seen with common sense.

A silent calm settled in the area and the three didn't speak for a while. Han Li didn't bother moving in search of the Bone King — Yang Wei either. Even if he fought, there was no true guarantee he could win. He was not some ordinary Evolved in Han Li's mind the moment he understood who that person was.

Han Li didn't even know how long it had been since the Bone King Evolved. If it was recently, he would only have a strong body. But what if it had been a long time? Then he would have already gotten acquainted with a magic power.

"Be aware Mom, Liyue. Just like Ethereal can cultivate a stronger body, Transformation path cultivators can also gain Magic Powers. It is what they need to cultivate themselves instead of gaining after a breakthrough. We didn't get strong bodies, they won't get magic powers either.

But beware, the ones who learn one are not rare, if you ever are in a situation to fight someone, always consider them as superior to you in some aspect."

Liyue and Yue Li nodded seriously. They didn't consider Han Li a wise cultivator who had fought thousands of battles, but they could hear the sense in his words. And in their mind, Han Li… was an unparalleled genius.

His words should be true. If they didn't see him like that, why would they care about his training?

"Anyway, let's do what we were here for…" Han Li stretched his muscles and looked around as his Soul Sense expanded. His gaze was like electricity as it passed around the area to finally settle on a tree cavity some distance away from him, there was an owl nestled inside it.

He became silent and raised his palm to let the other two know not to make any sounds, and then he silently walked closer to the tree and just as gently, started to climb it. Even his breathing became nonexistent as he soon reached up the cavity and held the owl with its fluffy feathers.

It immediately opened its closed eyes and screeched like a startled bird. But Han Li held its beak and with his inhuman strength and control, it became mute and frightened, it couldn't move or screech. He then casually climbed down the tree.

The bird still flapped its wings and tried to struggle out of his grasp with its claws but it was useless.

"Poor bird, Xiao Li, why are you catching it?" Yue Li asked in slight shock. Liyue also gulped and looked at the bird with a fearful face.

"Let's see." Han Li chuckled and sat down. "Don't worry, I have no plans of harming it."

They still looked at the bird and stayed a bit farther away from him. Both of them were not the type to pet any birds or animals. "Doesn't it bite Xiao Li? And doesn't its claws hurt?" she gulped and exchanged glances with Liyue.

Han Li didn't bother answering for now and looked into the bird's eyes coldly. And silently muttered, "Akarsanaya Swaha, Vilasamantra!"

Nothing really happened but Han Li saw the bird's eyes reddening slightly. His magic power [seduction] was working. Putting the bird into lust. But he didn't mind its eyes growing frantic and chanted the power once more "Akarsanaya Swaha, Vilasamantra!"

Both of the ladies looked at him calm and cold face as the owl's struggles lessened.

"Mother, what is he speaking about?"

Yue Li held her own chin and contemplated. "I don't know but with my brain power of one million and knowledge of a chuunibyou, I can give you some intelligent guesses." Her eyes brightened like a torch at the thought.

"Chunibya? What is…" she asked.

Suddenly speechless, Yue Li remembered the warning Han Li had given not to tell anyone about her origin and cleared her throat. "The words he is saying must be the chants that you have to say when activating a magic power."

"Chants?"

"You don't even know that? Liyue, you are so ignorant. I am ashamed to call myself your mother-in-law."

Liyue blinked with wide eyes.

"Anyway, Chants are words that you speak to bring forth the power of the world." Yue Li smugly looked at her son repeating the same words and smiling.

"Why do words bring out power?" confused, Liyue asked.

Yue Li looked at her with wide eyes this time. "So ignorant!"

Liyue ashamedly scratched her chin. She waited for an explanation but Yue Li never gave one. "Mother…"

Yue Li put on an arrogant face like the one Han Li had when training them, and said seriously, "It is not yet time for you to learn that. Wait till the time comes."

Han Li's eyebrows twitched and he looked at her in irritation. But then he shook his head and looked at his own hand. He had already used the Magic Power 7 times and only one more was left before the bar should reach 100%.

The unique power of his eyes, which he had decided to call Life Vision, activated, and his details streamed into his mind, as clearly as any kind of test could show.

[Current Cultivation: Evolution Stage]

[Body: Top Tier Mortal Physique]

[Skin Building of the Demon Transformation: 1.01% complete]

[Magic Powers]

[Low-Level Dao Magic — Seduction, (90% integrated into the body)]

Weapon Mastery:

Knife (Transcendent — Average Proficiency)

War Blade (Power Birth — Mastery)

Spear (Power Birth — Mastery)… and more.

Feeling the need to know something else about him that didn't show up immediately in his mind, he thought of it and the immediate information in his subconscious expanded. He wanted to know everything about himself before he completed 100%, so he could easily figure out what changed.

[Lifespan: 300 Years]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 39]

[Talent: Sacred]

Han Li looked into the glazed eyes of the owl and once again used the Seduction spell to shake its lust.

Its eyes turned a weird hue of red and weakly trembled and fell unconscious, its lust reaching a level its body couldn't handle.

[Low-Level Dao Magic — Seduction, (Integrating into the body)]

Han Li felt an unstoppable thrill going up his spine as his vision grew blurry for a second.

Chapter 87 - 87: Seductive, for real.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Low-Level Dao Magic — Seduction, (Integrating into the body)]

Han Li felt an unstoppable thrill going up his spine as his vision grew blurry for a second. It cleared soon enough and he looked around with a questioning glance. He didn't feel much different for the starters, but there was something, something at the tip of his tongue that he was missing.

As if something was added into him, something mysterious and profound. Yue Li and Liyue curiously looked at his confused and somewhat eager gaze and couldn't tell what was up with him. He looked at them for a second and realizing they were just confused and not stunned, came to a conclusion.

He didn't receive some kind of mutation, or difference in appearance from earlier either. Curious to the max, Han Li looked at his hands using Life-Vision.

_

[Han Li] [Age: 18]

[Lifespan: 300 Years]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 38]

[Talent: Sacred]

[Current Cultivation: Evolution Stage]

[Body: Top Tier Mortal Physique]

[Skin Building of the Demon Transformation: 1.01% complete]

[None]

[Low-Level Seduction]

Weapon Mastery:

Sword (Transcendence — High Proficiency)

Throwing Blades (Transcendence — Average Proficiency)

Knife (Transcendent — Average Proficiency)

War Blade (Power Birth — Mastery)

Spear (Power Birth — Mastery)… and more.

"What happened, Xiao Li?" Yue Li asked curiously after seeing his eyes brightening.

Han Li looked at her with a thoughtful glance and waved his hands gently as a sign to tell her to calm down. He didn't want to break his thought process.

'For the first thing, Seduction vanished from the list of magic powers…' he analyzed his situation. 'And for the second, a new list appeared called Talents and Seduction is now a part of it.'

That was the only difference in everything about himself before integration and after integration. He looked at the unconscious and dying owl beside him and ignored it. "If I test it on this guy, it will die. Come, let's go find something else." He stood up swiftly and went in search of the next animal.

Yue Li and Liyue followed him curiously like his playmates and they soon found a small crow deep into the woods. Han Li caught it and sealed its mouth just like he did earlier.

"Akarsanaya Swaha, Vilasamantra!" he muttered the ancient chant for the seduction magic and allowed a Minor Dao Crystal to vanish out of his Empty Space—or so he believed—but the crystal never vanished and the power didn't activate either.

'What is going on?' Han Li frowned heavily, his thoughts churning. But there was no one to explain things here for him at the moment either.

"What happened Xiao Li, why are you frowning so much?" Yue Li asked with raised brows, huffing and puffing slightly. She didn't get to rest after her training.

Han Li finally broke out of his contemplation and sighed, saying with a thoughtful tone, "Something seems to be amiss mom, my chant is not working anymore… and I don't really know why."

The forest area here was quite windy and cold, dark and creepy too. Yue Li wasn't very much stunned by what he said but Liyue's eyes widened in disbelief. Han Li sighed once more and let the crow free. It cried out in fear, flapped its wings in panic, and fled.

"Why does it not work though…?" Yue Li asked with squinted eyes. The urge to be the deep dive scientist briefly flickered inside her mind before she delved it away, Xiao Li would make fun of her.

Han Li looked at her with a silent gaze, and asked, "Isn't that what I asked?" he shook his head and looked at Liyue with a smile. "Why are you, so surprised, though?"

Looking at Liyue, Yue Li blinked, even she could see how wide the girl's eyes were and how stupefied her face looked. "What happened to you all of a sudden?" she asked with surprise.

A cold wind whooshed past them and an audible gulping sound was heard as Liyue exclaimed, her voice full of shock. "Evo…Ev… Big Brother already Evolved?!"

Yue Li looked at her in confusion for a while and then blinked. "How is that possible, he didn't even go into the Awakening ceremony yet—" she stopped speaking as she saw the casual and cool smirk and brightening red eyes of her son looking at her teasingly.

"You really Evolved?!" she asked in shock. Even she knew how rare and hard to become these Evolved were.

"This is why I call ignorance out when I see it." Han Li sat on a nearby boulder and sipped some of the alcohol from the flagon in his hand. "I have been showing you magic powers and I even used it against you, yet you ask me if I evolved… after all this time."

Han Li shook his head as the alcohol warmed his insides. "Magic Powers are not something Awakened could use, Mom, listen. These chants were the language the Sacred Demons used to speak at the beginning of the world, and when we spend our Dao Crystals, alongside having a Dao Soul related to that Magic Power, and say these words, the actual power happens. Only Evolved or higher can use such Power.

"And good one there Liyue, I like how you analyzed my words. People could have thought I was Awakened and ignorantly attempting to use chants and failing instead of thinking it used to work and suddenly doesn't anymore."

"…No, Big Brother," Liyue smiled slightly as her eyes shined thoughtfully. "Mother told me earlier about the Magic Powers and I was already having such thoughts, but it just seems impossible, though…"

"Because I am just eighteen?" Han Li chuckled, looking at her approvingly while Yue Li looked at him with a stupid look.

"Xiao Li, did you really evolve?" she worriedly moved beside him and caressed his cheek and then neck. She then touched his chest and finally, fell into his embrace. He tightly held her.

"I did really evolve. It was better for you guys not to know it than to know. Could have resulted in me failing if you knew. Sorry… I didn't tell you."

Yue Li looked up at his face from his embrace and he looked at her. Liyue looked at them from the side with a confused face this time. But for now, in Han Li's mind, only his mother mattered.

"Mom…"

"You never do what I say…" she sighed. "I told you to tell me if you plan to do something momentous…"

"You told me to tell you when I plan to bed a woman… though…"

"You got the meaning. Don't act dumb!" She fisted his chest but then stopped. Becoming silent. "I got it, Xiao Li."

Han Li frowned slightly and sat up straight, comfortably moving her body to bring her eyes right infront of his. "What did you get?"

"You were so busy making me work so much…" She blushed at Liyue's stunned gaze and said, her voice a bit weaker, "You were busy making me work so much to cultivate faster. Right?"

Han Li looked at her lips as they moved to speak. "Give me a kiss and I'll tell you."

She looked at Liyue on the side who was already looking at them with disbelief. Her eyes were wider than when she learned Han Li had evolved.

Then, she shyly leaned her head higher and exchanged a kiss with his lips. Han Li looked at her and just as the small urge to seduce his mom budded in his mind, Yue Li clenched his robes. Her sexual urge started to rise out of nowhere. Her mind started screaming to let her body free for her Xiao Li, to let him dominate her.

But feeling something wrong, Han Li immediately stopped and looked at his mother's face which had become completely red. He could feel something clicking in his mind.

He looked at Liyue who was looking at them with a disbelieving gaze. "Come here Liyue, let your big brother hug you."

Liyue was too stunned but Han Li gently took her palm and brought her into his embrace alongside his mom. 'Bi..Big brother and mother are not in a normal relationship?!' she exclaimed in her mind.

Han Li looked at her and said, "Don't worry about it, now let me ask you something instead, am I allowed to seduce you?"

She looked at him with a stunned and still surprised face. Once in his embrace, she wasn't even in the mode to have a reticent expression anymore and blurted out. "You…you already seduce me every day and just won't do it all the way, you… you even did it with both of our mothers! That's so unfair! I am your wife and not them after all...I..I..."

Han Li looked at her surprisedly. "Aren't you the one who wanted a marriage first?"

Liyue blinked and her face started to redden crazily. No way! she really had blurted it out.

Yue Li, on the other hand, looked at her with a hint of dissatisfaction. Why was her son's chest being shared with someone when he was hugging her?

A technique that only a Transcendant Stage cultivator could use, the ability to overpower or confuse other Hearts of Cultivations. At its best, it could destroy someone's mind and make them a cripple as long as the heart was weaker than the caster's.

He knew it; he once possessed the same level of powers, after all.

'Even though it is relatively weak for some reason.' Han Li's mind was cold and calloused, unworried too.

There was no way an actual Transcendant, one of the leaders of the Great Sects, would come here to mess with a junior like him for no reason.

That could only mean one thing, and that was: Yang Wei had methods that only Transcendants are supposed to have.

'Oh Bone King, is this the secret that led to you becoming a Transcendent without an Orb? You certainly are sending yourself to my lap.'

He was unworried.

If the person was that capable, such negotiations wouldn't be happening.

The only thing that worried Han Li was how Yang Wei had gotten inside the Starlight Zone without anyone else catching onto him. An Evolved was protecting the peripheries at all times, after all.

"What do you want to do?" Han Li asked, sounding worried, "I don't want to involve my family in whatever you're trying to pull me into. What about we go some distance away?"

Yang Wei's pale eyes shined.

'I have a bad feeling about this, Yang Wei; always be 100% focused when dealing with someone like this. He is probably acting naive.'

Yang Wei nodded and walked on the roof. Han Li's cold gaze roamed as the footsteps moved to the roof's edge.

'He is about to jump,' Han Li thought, walking towards the room's door. But he didn't go out; breaking the thatched roof to enter this room wasn't impossible.

Even though if Yang Wei dared to do that, the commotion would become obvious, and soon, Evolved would be coming from all sides—Han Li didn't want to take risks if by chance Yang Wei was going to do that for some psychotic reason, Yue Li would get into danger.

But his eyes kept shining mysteriously.

In some people's case, only considering their rationality would be a stupid act. Especially when the option of taking his mom or the other two women hostage remained.

In his Soul Sense, Han Li saw Yang Wei's figure land on the ground without a sound like a small piece of bone and his heart slightly tightened as he walked out of the room and sprinted towards the outside. His soul sense was locked onto Yang Wei and Yang Wei knew it, thus he didn't move and waited for Han Li to come out.

The door to the house opened with a click and Han Li closed it behind him as he finally had a line of sight with the enemy. His cold eyes deepened a layer in its coldness.

Yang Wei smiled mysteriously.

"Heart of Chaos," Han Li remarked.

Yang Wei's smile faltered.

Han Li's tone was rather calm but he was cautious enough to ask the people inside whether they were all right using the 'Sensational Barrier.'

He was confused, if so, he had to confuse his opponent even more. That way, in the chaos amidst both of their confusion, he would have a chance to analyze what was going on.

Yang Wei's pale eyes became deadly. "You know what a Heart of Chaos is?"

He had expected the opponent not to know.

Even normal Power Borns didn't know it, how did an Evolved?

That was why he boldly went atop the roof and attempted to confuse Han Li's 'Dao Heart.' If he succeded, he could either kill Han Li outright without any noise or touch Han Li once to trigger The Spirit inside his mind's mirror's power.

He would have a 24/7 eye on Han Li if he could do that.

But Han Li's heart seemed to have grown even stronger in the last few days after he used the mirror and her confusion power didn't work on him at all.

Instead, the only one affected was his mother, who had a much weaker heart.

He had expected to run away easily if the trick didn't work but Han Li's soul sense was larger than he expected and immediately surrounded him—but that also meant Han Li was as talented as himself; a sacred talent.

They glared at each other and a deep silence settled on them.

"Why are you aiming at me?" Han Li asked.

"The last time you scanned me with your Soul Sense without my permission, I didn't like that. I am here for revenge."

Yang Wei smiled again.

At least, Han Li wasn't bold enough to bring the people of Starlight Clan into this, which meant he was adamant about staying hidden. His guts were right, it was the best choice to come here.

Being cautious all the time about some random Evolved was stupid.

If he could touch Han Li, it would be even better.

Both of their Soul Senses focused on each other and contracted to focus only on each other, anything outside was pointless at this point. Only both of them mattered, one can die or kill anytime.

Han Li suddenly sighed with a smile and asked, relaxing his seemingly taut self, "Why didn't you take my family hostage?"

"Why didn't I take them as hostage?" Yang Wei's eyes suddenly widened. At the same time, his heart skipped a beat.

It had been some time since The Spirit spoke anything.

'Something is wrong…'

The black tip of a sword penetrated his back and gently went through it as he was startled. His skin that was so tough seemed like mere wood as it went in.

Blood leaked from the side of his mouth and he gritted his teeth.

He looked back to see who was using it but no one was, it floated in the air without anybody's help as the sword shifted in his stomach.

"How?! You didn't use any magic…" he said but stopped and breathed heavily. His glare grew menacing as his skin transformed into the color of bone.

Han Li's cold eyes looked at him indifferently.

[Low-Level Magic Power, Fantasy Inducement: An ability to make others briefly forget something by a lack of attention by uniquely inducing their lust.]

He had used that to make Yang Wei forget about the hostage thing altogether.

It was a small trick that always worked in these kinds of scenarios, even though he had no control over what the enemy forgot briefly.

Along with the 'Sensational Barrier' talent that completely sealed Yang Wei's senses and made contact with anyone else impossible without him moving out of Han Li's soul sense or using his own soul sense to act protectively.

"I knew somebody was talking to you," Han Li smirked calmly as Yang Wei pulled out the sword from his back with gritted teeth.

They were both now exactly about 7 meters apart.

Soul Sense could neither sense the sword nor could its sharpness aided by a Dao Crystal's energy be blocked with Evolved people's skin, even if they were Transformation Path Cultivators.

But as the sword left and left behind a hole, Yang Wei straightened his back and the hole was closed by a bone-like membrane, soon enough, healing.

Han Li's face became rather disappointed. "Transformation Path bodies are quite something," he said.

Yang Wei on the other hand had a really bad expression as the woman in his mind spoke finally, filled with disbelief, 'He seems to be able to use Magic Powers without any chants, moreover, it is fluid enough… it almost seems as if he is using another of his limbs. Be cautious, this is growing out of hand. Cover your body with your Soul Sense, and act as if he is always attacking.'

Yang Wei was also a bit frightened in his heart. 'The sword in my hand is nothing ordinary either, it is so sharp.'

Han Li looked coldly at the sword, which suddenly twisted powerfully to one angle, breaking the bones in Yang Wei's wrist as it broke free and flew back to Han Li's side.

"I am in a position I can't kill you," Han Li said indifferently.

Having a Transformation Path Sacred Body…

If Han Li tried to kill him, it would create a lot of commotion and it was even unsure whether he could kill him easily when only one time Yang Wei coming at least a meter close to him would spell the end for him.

It was the problem with having a weak body.

"Why don't we negotiate?" he took the sword in his hand and put it on his shoulder.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 96 - 96: Show me your little trick, Life.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Why don't we negotiate?" Han Li asked.

"Negotiate?" Irritated at not being in an advantageous position, Yang Wei sneered, "Why should we negotiate when I have nothing to lose?"

Han Li's red gaze shined.

It was true.

He was in a situation where his secrets were out and about; if someone knew it, it could even endanger his life and might even lead to one ultimate truth—he was the owner of the Lust inheritance.

Once his secret is out, he would only have two paths left to take in life: either being enslaved by one of the Great Sects or dying and becoming an interesting test subject.

He knew the Sects had more information about the 7 Sacred Demons and probably even the 7 Sacred Demon inheritors. Maybe they even had the power to steal his eyes for all he knew.

In a sense, only he was the one at a disadvantage.

At first glance, at least.

"If we don't negotiate, I will just yell for help either way and once MY clan's head is out here, you would only have one choice: to die. You don't want it, do you?" Han Li sounded indifferent, as if his secret reaching the clan head and all of the people around wasn't of his concern at all.

Yang Wei became silent and cautious, but he was still quite arrogant. "If your Clan Head comes out, don't worry. I have ways to get out of here and survive."

Han Li looked at him with scrutiny.

Did he really, or was that a bluff?

"And red-eyed freak." Yang Wei smirked. "Once, you made me forget about your family and stabbed me in the back. What about twice? I can still attack them, and with you, Ethereals, aren't you just going to be running around in an attempt to exhaust me?"

They never had those one, finishing attacks like the Transformation Path did—which was to go near and bash the enemy to death.

Han Li knew it clearly and didn't have that one ultimate power either. The laws of Ethereals and Transformation also constrained him. Only if he cultivated the technique could he consider himself different.

Sighing inside, Han Li decided to give away one of the most significant pieces of the puzzle.

Something that he was absolutely unwilling to do.

"You know I have a secret." Han Li's gaze condensed like ice. "Don't you have one too, Yang Wei? Something much more enticing than my ephemeral 'something' that people might want or not. Don't you have it, something that will make all the Power Born cultivators in the entire continent go wild?!"

Yang Wei's heart skipped a beat, but he sneered, "Hah, something that all the Power BOrns would want? From a little Evolved like me?"

Han Li chuckled. "You might have heard of these miraculous things. In fact, you might even have such a miracle, but let me ask you, Yang Wei. Have you heard of anything in this world that most wretched Power Born would kill their own family to get?"

Yaang Wei's face paled.

"It seems you remember the name, yes, a Transcendent, ORB!" Han Li's cruel and cold voice penetrated his heart and spoke straight into his soul, "Don't you have it, or something that serves the same purpose?"

Frightened for a second, Yang Wei didn't know what to say. His pale face paled even further, looking exactly like bones.

'Spirit, how does he know?!' he screamed.

But no reply arrived.

'SPIRIT!'

Han Li smirked at the bony-faced man and removed the Sensational Barrier. Unlike other Ethereals, he could use the power forever, but he didn't want to show that and wanted to seem as if he was using up his Dao Crystals after each use.

Immediately, the Transcendent Spirit spoke again: 'As I expected, coming here without you practicing a Magic Power to act as your shield was stupid. You have no protection against his abilities. Lucky that he doesn't seem to have anything outright dangerous, or you would be dying here.

But one thing is sure now: since he has Sacred Talent like you and doesn't have any powers that seem to be offensive fundamentally, it means he most probably had a Life Dao Spark and now a Life Dao Soul.'

The transmission was immediate.

'Not that! Tell me why he knows I have something like a Transcend Mortality Orb.'

The women grew silent, and then a frightened voice mumbled in his soul. 'His Instant use magic powers seem to be affecting me too.'

Han Li looked at the silently squirming man curiously. He had just used his Talent 'Fantasy Inducement' to make him forget something important and make stupid decisions, but he didn't know if it worked.

'Yang Wei, no matter what, do not let this news go out. If it does, we are dead! Let it be, even if you have to lose a hand or eye to do it. You were too hasty. Let this be your reminder.'

Yang Wei knew when to be serious and calmed down.

"What negotiations and on what would we trust each other? For your knowledge, I know Power Borns who could go just as crazy as they would for me for your sword and the secret about Evolving at the age of 18 that has never happened before."

Han Li frowned. Yang Wei was no longer irrational.

"If you can cast 'Heart of Chaos,' you should also be able to cast the 'Binding of Hearts' that Transcendants can. Bind our Hearts of Cultivation together, and let's take a vow on it. We keep what we know to ourselves."

Yang Wei asked inside, 'Can you do it?'

'I can, but this is our opening, tell him you need to touch him to activate it.'

"I need to—" He spoke, but Han Li smirked.

"If you can cast it, it is cast from the heart, so there is no reason to come closer. Why don't you start?"

'His knowledge about Transcendants is nothing shallow,' the spirit warned.

'So, do I go through with the binding?' Yang Wei asked.

'Yes, that is the only choice at this point…' The spirit sounded calm. She had many ways to go through with this ending.

For now, this was okay.

"Let's do it," Yang Wei coldly spat.

Han Li looked at him and nodded.

But this time, he was prepared to check the Heart that made his mom's heart of cultivation chaotic—not with his soul sense or something, but with Life Vision.

His red eyes brightened as a binding aura slightly established with him and Yang Wei; it was ethereal and non-existent. He couldn't even feel it pass through him, but the caster themself allowed him to sense the unique power of Transcendents, and he immediately sensed it.

Along with his Life Vision roaming around.

Sadly, though, he didn't see anything.

Nothing.

But he wasn't worried.

He sensed the aura with his heart, like feeling the wind, and let it connect with his heart. He wasn't worried about a Transcendent Heart playing tricks on him, not to mention this washed-out version of it.

"You first, why don't you vow?"

'What do I need to do, Spirit?'

'Vow that you wouldn't speak the boy's secret to anyone else. Nothing more, nothing less. He will be ours to get once the time comes.'

"Vow that you would never get into the Starlight Zone, and you will never, in any way or form, reveal even my name to someone else."

Yang Wei laughed mockingly.

Han Li's cold glance stopped him.

'Tell him not to come to the Bone Clan's city for safety sake, just in case he could hide like us or something, and we will be in equal steps. But you have me, so we are at an advantage.'

If they vowed so, both of them couldn't attempt an ambush, and once one of them was stronger, both of their secrets would belong to one of them. It would be a matter of who grew strong enough first to claim the other's life absolutely.

Even though the home base would remain a forbidden zone.

According to their vow, they aren't allowed to seek help to kill each other either. Both were smart— Han Li and the Spirit woman—so neither could play any tricks.

Or so it seemed.

"You will vow not to come a hundred miles near the Bone City and to never divulge a word about me in any way."

Han Li nodded coldly.

It was okay.

Then they both spoke the vow aloud, and a lock was cast on their hearts, making the promise an unviolable one if they didn't want their hearts to explode.

Neither of them could speak about each other's secrets, nor could they go close to each other's headquarters.

Yang Wei didn't spend any more time there and glanced at Han Li for a final time before running away and melding into the darkness. Hiding into the buildings and vanishing from Han Li's gaze.

Han Li looked at him with a cold smile and spoke telepathically into Li Yao's mind: "Did you hear everything just now?"

Li Yao nodded from inside her room, her back covered with cold sweat.

"I am bound by chains, so I can't give you any guidance. You know what to do, right? Don't get your identity out, and get this information across to all three Power Borns of the city by tomorrow morning."

He didn't care about the Transcendent Orb or similar things the man had. For him, such a dangerous addition of someone knowing about himself this early was too big of a risk for something he would get one way or the other.

"Or never mind, Li Yao, we will keep it as an option, but maybe getting that treasure for myself is the best route."

Sigh…

He had almost forgotten that Yang Wei would be quite easy to kill off soon enough. The only trouble would be bringing him out of his home ground.

"Let's have a fair competition," Han Li smiled, and his sword shined slightly.

Of course, he had not used his sword's shining power earlier.

'Oh, Life Sword,' a crazy demonic smirk appeared on Han Li's face. His eyes seemed to drip with maliciousness. 'Show me your little trick.'

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 97 - 97: The Boy of Bones
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Back in his room, the sword in Han Li's hands shone like a red pearl, as if it was burning with blood inside its ancient physical constraints.

Han Li sat there as a large amount of information streamed into his head.

Yang Wei.

He was a very normal kid of the Bone Clan.

A very, very normal kid—of the Bone Clan.

Bone Clan was only some weeks away from the Southern Lust City, situated under a gigantic gorge in a gloomy place called the Bone City—which was solely ruled by the said clan.

Having been born with the usual Life Dao Transformation Path Spark that all of the Bone Clan members had—The Bone Spark—he was treated like any other kid of the clan.

Which even made Han Li raise an eyebrow.

At the age of three, the pale-eyed youth was thrown into a dark room filled with the bones of his ancestors and required to get an appetite for them. And then, like all the other youths, the boy ate the bones daily to survive.

2 years he spent in the dark room. Only left out once every week to let his body be bathed by the sun so as not to let him lose health.

Or so the shadowy people had said, who the tender boy couldn't remember the face of.

After that, he was let out, alongside 30 other kids of the same batch as him. He, alongside the 29 others, was then told to walk through the 'path of hardship' by the age of 13, before they were to have their first Awakening.

A path laid by needles, one through hot oil, and the last one, a path that walked through a horde of centipedes.

Yang Wei walked past all of them in merely two more years.

He was a cold young man who didn't care for pain.

Only the first path took him time; most of the time, he fainted. But once he could endure it and all the last attempt's wounds were mended, he walked through the trials like a breeze and settled his name.

He was allowed to spend the rest of the years in normal training.

Yang Wei, named the beautiful hollow bone by the blurry-faced adults, was not allowed to eat the four foundational Resources to build a top-tier mortal body, and worked, wetting his bones, drinking his own coughed blood back—he trained.

Mostly, it was easy training. Just enduring the hot burns of the oil and so on.

He attained the perfect body for a mortal by age twelve, even though he had lost his ability to speak at this point and become a 'beautiful hollow bone' in all its meaning. He was hollow, the killing machine that the clan was slowly building up for a day to come.

At age 13, he was made to eat a weird-looking worm, and his Bone Spark Awakened for the first time. Like most of the Life Dao Sparks, it was just of Low Talent.

But eating the bones of the living was enough for him to improve his talent. He fed on the animals the family provided him—he ate their hot and raw bones as two men held the wolf or dog at bay.

It was the ability of all Life Dao Sparks, unlimited life potential. But for Yang Wei, it was a curse.

At age 15, he was a Sacred Talent of the Bone Clan. He was thrown into the city's brothel to learn resistance to seduction.

Twelve dead girls did the deed.

He no longer was prey for seduction either.

He also started to train in the path of spears. Spears made out of bones could jutt out from an Evolved Bone Clan Cultivator's body; it was to be taught early to stabilize their evolutions.

Their path ahead clear.

At age 18, he Awakened for the third time, and his vision expanded.

He became a Mortal Master at the way of Spears by age 21. His spear could react to anything and everything at his reaction speed, and he was like a feral beast as someone pushed him into the wolf den near the city—left there to train youths like him.

He ate 20 little wolves that day.

Age 25.

That was when he was called into a dark room inside the Bone Clan and given a dark piece of rotten flesh to eat. He didn't hesitate and ate it.

It was then that the Spirit of the clan's ancient ancestor, a woman with a voice bewitching enough to leave men moaning, entered his mind.

What was she?

He didn't know.

Why was she inside him?

He didn't know.

But the more days passed with her inside him, the more emotions that started to build up in his hollow shell. He ate many things from then on.

Especially, the Bone Clan's amount of new infants reduced day by day.

The Spirit taught him the way of cultivation and guided him from inside. He became more proficient by the day. HE ate the four magical resources meant to improve the body to complete that last minor itch.

Since only then could he be entirely sure his body didn't lack.

At age 29.

He Evolved. He had a sound mind now, one unmarred by the devouring hollow deep within him. He was a boy full of confidence.

And the Spirit inside him?

It could only grow silent by the day.

It was being assimilated into the boy instead of the boy assimilating into it as was planned at the start. Someday soon, the boy would eat it.

Like he had done many before.

The Spirit was desperate, so it asked the boy in one mind the moment the boy evolved: "If you do not assimilate me and help me revive, I will give you the Transcendence Orb that I absorbed at my time. Nobody can take it out from others, but mine is different. I am not a human like you or the other Transcendents, I am of a different race. I am a Spirit Being. I will give my Orb to you!"

That, along with the mirrors the women had left behind all over the world before her death, alongside the rightful ownership to the masterless Bone clan that was 100% loyal to the spirit, was enough to convince him.

There, they became a team.

And on the same day…

Han Li's red eyes shone with a profound glint as the information about the mirror and his other facts that led them to believe his weirdness was shown to him alongside the legendary beginnings of the Bone King Life Stealer Yang Wei.

"So that little mirror was the reason…" he murmured.

The anxious mother-in-law, sleeping Yue Li, and the rather tense Liyue who was looking at him with worry. All of them were wondering about the earlier drama.

Han Li looked at them as the final bit of information about the so-called Spirit and Yang Wei flashed in his mind.

'No wonder he was trying to touch me…' Han Li's smile grew casual.

An unknown enemy and an enemy he knew everything about was different.

His heart calmed down as all of Yang Wei's little weaknesses flittered through his head. It's like how he liked fighting without a spear taken out unless he was pushed to the limit.

Like how it was just a bluff that he could escape a Power Born.

But the information he gleaned was still from a third-person perspective instead of delving into the boy's thoughts and it was rather fragmented and only showed the important moments that changed his life's path.

So, he couldn't tell all of the information about him. But for someone as experienced as Han Li, and the frequent mumbles he heard in the records. Of the boy muttering Spirit that and this, it was enough for him to deduce.

But unfortunately, he couldn't tell what kind of Magic Power he was training.

Unlike Ethereals, Transformation Path cultivators could only have one Magic Power throughout their lives. And that Magic Power could only evolve with the cultivator growing to higher Stages but it could never change.

If Yang Wei chose one Life Dao Magic Power at the Evolution Stage, that would be the power he would have all throughout his life.

'Now that I think about it, Yang Wei's Magic Power was never revealed last time. I wonder what it is… moreover…' Han Li began to grow serious.

The trials and tribulations the boy had gone through might have been impressive for a youth, even more than he had endured at that age in any of his lives, but for the current Han Li, those were child's play.

But one thing made him worried.

What was this… Spirit?

He had never heard of anything like that, ever.

And what is the Bone Clan creating such an army for when it was obvious that the world was currently in a peaceful era? And if it was for something later on, why hadn't he heard anything significant about them in the future?

They wouldn't be making an army with this level of rigidness to gain one or two decent evolved, right?

What was this mysterious Spirit's actual goal?

'Oh, Yang Wei,' Han Li smirked as the red light in the Life Sword vanished. He could feel Yang Wei was nothing normal.

But he smirked. He was quite an interesting boy to test his own talents against.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 98 - 98: 10%
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The night's shadows grew deeper still.

Moon, in its gentle light, shone on the lands a beautiful glow. Amidst the silence, cicadas chirped.

Han Li and Li Yao were outside their home, seated on a boulder while sharing a jug of alcohol; their faces reddened by the drink.

"Fuu…" Han Li breathed out a puff of smoke. "This is really something."

Li Yao smiled. "This should've been given to you earlier, though."

Han Li shook his head and drank another hot mouthful of the drink. It felt as if his throat was searing as it went down.

"Mom never made one for me," he said as he felt his chest warm and his body heat up.

"Really?" Li Yao shook her head. "Your mother is quite the airhead, but even for her, isn't that a bit too much?"

Han Li pondered.

In Sacred Lust, once a baby is born, people would store a jar of wine in the baby's name away and wait for the day the baby's age for marriage to arrive. It was a usual thing; almost every parent would store a jar.

One for the marriage and the other for the first birth of the new couple's child. The final one for the first Awakening of the child.

It was a tradition of sorts.

Li Yao, for a woman who loved alcohol, had kept four jars of strong alcoholic wine for Liyao's. The current one Han Li was drinking was one of them, which he had just uncorked.

It had been sealed for 18 years and was quite the fermented delicacy.

"It's not her fault…" Han Li sighed, his mellow red eyes shining with serenity.

It had been an hour since the 'Yang Wei surprise attack incident', and his sleep was quite clearly ruined. He was stuck with explaining a lot to Liyue and Li Yao, too, even though explaining that he couldn't speak much because of the binding was enough in the end.

Yue Li was sleeping all throughout the ordeal, and Han Li was quite worried about her but didn't wake her up in the end.

Heart of Chaos.

That technique could really mess up people's Heart of Cultivation.

He hoped she would be back to normal once she woke up.

Then, it was Li Yao who suggested they have a drink of alcohol that she had kept hidden for a long time. Han Li nodded, of course.

And thus, he was now here, drinking the spicy alcohol.

Liyue was, of course, beside his mom just in case she woke up startled or something. The presence of someone familiar could help in that situation. But it was nothing that serious.

"You really are forgiving in all her antics," Li Yao smiled. "I envy her slightly."

Han Li shook his head. "You misunderstand something, Li Yao—"

"Mother-in-law," she said.

"You misunderstand something, Mother-in-law. She just had no idea such a thing existed, and neither did I care enough to ask her." Han Li laid on his back on the huge boulder, and Li Yao sat by the side.

Li Yao nodded. It was not really like a must-do tradition anyway.

In silence, they just calmly enjoyed the night.

No lust, nothing.

Just thoughtful silence, alcohol, and the gradually warming winds of the 6th month as their companions.

Now that she thought of it, Li Yao once again thought of the earlier occurrence and gulped.

"To think people can really have enemies and such, unbelievable," Li Yao said, taking the jug from Han Li's hands and carefully taking a sip before blissfully sighing: "I really don't want to use this up…"

Han Li smirked. "There are three more there. Why won't you just use them too?"

Li Yao rolled her eyes. "That would ruin the meaning of this alcohol. Even this one tastes unique because it serves a purpose. I put it away to drink with my son-in-law, and it is indeed doing the work. Be thankful you got any. If you want more, why don't you just go through with the marriage already?"

Han Li snickered. "I don't think you would've given even this to me if today's incident didn't happen, would you?"

"Questionable, really." Li Yao chuckled. "This 4th Jar was just for my own little satisfaction. In truth, you can't blame me, can you?"

Han Li looked at her flushed face and could see she was reluctant to give away the liquor. "Alcoholic women do look enticing, I have to say that."

She looked at him with a side-eye.

Shaking his head and analyzing everything about the enemy for the last time, Han Li looked at his hands using Life Vision.

He had a lingering doubt.

Something he had dismissed till now because of the lack of use the thing had till now.

_

[Han Li] [Age: 18]

[Lifespan: 300 Years]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 121]

[Talent: Sacred]

[Current Cultivation: Evolution Stage]

[Body: Top Tier Mortal Physique]

[Skin Building of the Demon Transformation: 1.01% complete]

[Magic Powers]

[None]

[Talents]

[Low-Level Lack of Lust]

[Low-Level Fantasy Inducement]

[Low-Level Sensational Barrier]

[Low-Level Seduction]

_

As he expected, there was no information about his Heart of Cultivation in his whole [Life information].

It had already been over a month since he Evolved, and his Lust Dao Crystals had grown to 121 in the meantime. He hadn't added any extra Talents either.

But as his mind roamed through the info, he understood how the thing lacked anything about his Heart of Cultivation.

Even though a true Heart of Cultivation is not born until the Transcendance Stage, he was supposed to have it there.

He was once a Transcendent, after all.

Even though the uses would be extremely minimal, in theory, he should have the unique power of the Heart, 'Heart Qi.'

But he didn't.

'Now that I think about it…' Han Li suddenly closed his eyes, and his mind delved deep into itself.

In the depth of his insides, where the empty space was, he could feel his Dao Soul resting calmly inside, a red shine still there in its humanoid visage.

When people reach the Transcendence Stage, the difference between Ethereal and Transformation vanishes. Even though there would still be some inherent limitations, it mostly changes.

That was a Stage where people walked past the mortal boundaries.

And at that point, inside the Dao Soul—which would become a Minor 'Great' Dao Soul once a cultivator Transcend—there would be organs like his current Dao Soul had skin.

It didn't have the skin-like material in it before he started transforming his skin. He knew that if he Transformed his organs, he would gain organs in his Dao Soul, too, including the Heart.

This was the uniqueness of [Demon Transformation]. It didn't care about Transformation and Ethereals.

'So if I cultivate all my organs, maybe I might be able to use the Heart Qi like Yang Wei—or the Spirit—is using. Maybe mine might even be a bit better than its.'

He shook his head. 'That thing is weird, I should be cautious.'

He then smiled as he looked at the hundred and some more shining stars in his Empty Space.

They closed in on the Dao Soul and started to meld into it one by one. It accepted them as they came, and as the crystals fused into it one by one, the skin that was once a paper from the [Demonic Lust Sutra] became more and more physical and solid in the ethereal frame of the Dao Soul.

He opened his eyes as his skin emitted a faint hiss and warmed up. At the same time, his red eyes shone with a calm glint. Power coursed through his body like blood.

[Skin Building of the Demon Transformation: 11.01% complete]

Han Li smiled.

He could feel a sense of security wash through his as his soft and humane skin became more solid, more firm. Like leather, but more rigid and much more flexible.

'Even though it is far from the level Yang Wei's skin was when he became that bone form.'

But still, it was better than earlier.

He should test it when he gets the chance.

But his skin felt slightly itchy and made it harder to focus for now.

He shook his head and snatched the alcohol from the dazed, red-faced Li Yao, who was already way down in her alcoholic trance.

Taking a sip, he sighed and carried Li Yao on his back. With a calm heart, he pushed everything about Yang Wei to the back of his head and walked into his room.

He was surprised to find his mom and Liyue sleeping soundly like dolls, hugging and covered in thick blankets to help the cold.

With a smile, he laid Li Yao, who was still conscious, in the bed and squeezed in beside his Mom. Bringing the gentle woman's tired body atop his chest, he protected her in his embrace as he looked at Liyue and Li Yao, who both looked at him with blurry gazes.

One sleepy and the other alcoholic.

"Go to sleep," he muttered, and without waking up the baby-like Yue Li atop his chest, they all went to sleep.

Since his mom asked him not to do anything lustful for the night when she fell asleep, he didn't play anything of that sort.

She was even clothed well now.

His hands just gently and protectively hugged her.

Soon, only gentle breaths mingled as the family of four slept, their hearts calm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 99 - 99: Absolute
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The majestic sun peaked from the canopies, and the misty lands brightened up. The verdant canopies of leaves trickled down gentle drops of water while some birds flew off their nests in search of some morning prey.

Southern Lust City was as calm as any other day. In the city's heart, the vendors got busy setting up their stalls early in the morning, while in the Starlight Zone, a similar thing happened.

The children were still asleep in their homes under their thick blankets and certainly were not yet playing tag like usual. It was way in the morning. It was five o'clock!

The lazy bums of the city and the young men who were looked down on and called lazy bums for being unable to find a good job were the same, sleeping comfortably in their beds while deep in the embrace of their comfy blankets.

Han Li, one of the Lazy Bums of the city for the past few days, almost more than a month—as spoken by the aunties in the area—yawned as his eyes blinked open. His mother's supple body was lying atop him still, drooling on his chest while deep in her sleep.

And on his left was Liyue, who was hugging his head tightly. On the other hand, on his right was Li Yao's foot. It didn't stink, luckily.

'She really can't sleep without tossing and turning at least ten times every minute…' Han Li's sleepy eyes twitched.

She was about to fall off of the bed, to boot.

The bed was quite small for all four of them, but it wouldn't have mattered if she didn't toss around much.

He blinked, and his blurry mind cleared up and escaped its sleepiness. Growing thoughtful about whether or not to wake them up for training today, he shook his head inside and, with one of his free hands that did not have anything on it, unlike the other—which Liyue was using as her pillow—pulled Li Yao back into the bed.

She comfortably tossed around and soon snatched more of the others' blankets.

Han Li sighed with a smile. 'You didn't move till now, and you move so much even in sleep now. It really must have been quite uncomfortable being unable to move.'

She kicked his jaws slightly.

His eyes twitched again, and he pushed her legs aside and took a deep breath of the cold air permeating the room. His whole body trembled as he woke up entirely.

Then, without waking any of the three women, he gently moved out of their embrace and sighed as he saw the tight hold over his clothes that both Liyue and Yue Li had. It was his first time ever sleeping on one bed with Liyue, so he was pretty surprised.

Gently taking their hands, he did a little trick on them, and their hands loosened.

It was his Magic Power—now Talent—that hadn't been used so far since its birth.

[Lack of Lust: The ability to reduce other people's desires over things.]

He had made this power into a Talent so that he could make other people have no Lust or other covetous emotion over his women; he could use this power on such arrogant people to avoid a probable conflict.

He knew beautiful women could be deadly, and since he had decided to accept such a deep burden like Love, it was only natural he prepared enough for it.

'To think I would use this power like this now…' He smiled and stretched his body as small cracks echoed out. His whole body felt refreshed and enthusiastic for training.

Since yesterday was quite a special day, he didn't mind if the three didn't train today, but he was not them, and thus, he couldn't slack.

He briefly looked at his hands with Life Vision.

[Skin Building of the Demon Transformation: 11.01% complete]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 21]

Since he used up 100 crystals yesterday, his first transformation, 'Skin', had progressed, and it should be much more harder to kill him now than before. Even though it would still pale in comparison to the Skin of a Transformation Path Cultivator.

'30% completed Skin should be as tough as an Averagely Talented Transformation Cultivator. 60% completed, and my Skin should get as tough as a Highly Talented Transformation Cultivator. And if I cultivate it to 100%, my skin should be as good as Yang Wei's with his Sacred Talent.'

Smiling, he walked over to the kitchen and took a knife.

He placed it over his palm and tried to cut his hand. It was hard even to move half of a half of an inch in as if he was attempting to cut a taut rubbed with the solidity of something beyond rubber.

But he closed his eyes and wondered slightly.

If he moved the knife good enough, to the level an Evolved Master of Knives could…

He changed the pressure on the knife ever so slightly, raising it and moving for a cut…

A red line of blood appeared on his palm.

'It is at most the level of High-level Proficiency that I just used, and in quite the bad way too.'

That meant he could be hurt by even Average Proficiency Evolved using normal weapons as long as they used their strength on it well. Of course, they also had to have a physical strength similar to him for this to be the case.

'But weapons are rarely part of a Cultivator's base strength. It is always their Magic Powers or Body that comes first.'

If he could cultivate his skin to be tougher, it would be impossible for the average Joe to hurt him from then on. Stretching, he smiled and went to the bathroom to finish some normal routine.

Brushing his teeth and such.

…

Han Li spent the next two hours deep in training.

His body was already used to its strength, and his Proficiency had no way of rising higher at all at this point.

He was a Transcendent being playing with simple weapons, chopstick-like things, so there was nothing for him to learn from it much to improve his Proficiency.

As he was finally feeling some sweat appear after two hours, he sighed and looked at his hands with Life Vision.

His Proficiency was higher than his Cultivation Stage.

He had just thought of this while completing the 1200th push-up today.

Even though his Proficiency in Swords was: Sword (Transcendence — High Proficiency), he obviously couldn't use the sword that perfectly at the Evolution Stage.

If he had to use that level of the Sword, that level of senses and that level of body was an absolute must. Only Transcendant could have Transcendant Proficiency in the first place, so his situation itself was quite messed up.

He was in the backyard of Liyue's home, and as his mind called, a black sword flew over from inside while evading the walls like a dirty little mouse. It was fast and precise as it soon flew into his hands.

Han Li held it firmly and started to do some basic moves.

One side arc.

Thrust.

Slice.

Chop.

Swift shifts.

The air seemed to have been slightly parted as whooshing, ice-cutting-like sounds echoed.

'It is better than Evolved Mastery at Sword, but not even nearly as good as Power Born Average Mastery,'

He contemplated.

Having fought Yang Wei—almost fought—Han Li had also gotten much more serious in his immediate battle strength.

Till now, he hadn't cared much about it since that wouldn't have had much use, since his first conflict—if it all went as planned—would be months from now in a place far from here. But Yang Wei's existence was like a drop of ink poured into white rice.

It ruined the simplicity of his plans.

So he had to care about his immediate battle strength, too.

'My Proficiency is like a barrier in between the two Major Stages of cultivation…' Han Li blinked. That gave him much inspiration about the world as a whole and also made him quite contemplative.

'I shall name this level of proficiency as Absolute Proficiency,' he chuckled as the sword cut the air again.

His heart was beating a bit more vigorously today.

No matter what, he was also a cultivator deep down.

He was proud of his achievements.

Han Li looked at his hand with Life Vision for one last time and was a bit startled, but also having expected this, took a deep breath.

[Absolute Evolved Proficient in more than a hundred different tools, ranging from sword, knife, spear, blade, kunai, and more.]

That was his Proficiency now.

It had always been the same previously but now that he changed it to have a better understanding of himself in his mind, the way Life Vision showed it to him also changed. That meant the information Life Vision gave was clearly based on his own thoughts and understanding.

'This power of my eyes is far too profound for the current me…'

His ears suddenly twitched as he heard some bed rustling sounds.

'Liyue woke up,' he thought.

Chapter 100 - 100: Sweet Candy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was also a sore feeling all over her body. It felt rather rigid.

'Ah, it's true, I slept with Mother Yue since Mom and Brother Han went to drink…' A yawn escaped her mouth, but it suddenly closed as she realized something.

'I didn't wake up in time to train…'

Through the window's curtains, she could see trickles of light passing into the room. It wasn't supposed to be like this at the time they trained.

She walked out of the bed while rubbing her eyes. 'Is my body so sore and rigid cause I didn't train?'

There was even some pain all over her physique. Her shoulders felt loose, her back felt weighed over, her legs tired, and her mind a bit frustrated at missing her training streak.

Just as she was about to check the backyard for Han Li, he came through the backdoor, his body sweating slightly and the black sword flying behind him. He was naked above the waist, and his well-cut body was great eye candy.

Liyue was still pretty dazed but felt sweet at his caring look. She nodded.

He had never cooked for them.

She had always wanted to taste what his so-called famous-all-throughout-Starlight Zone-cooking was. She wanted to know how it compared to Yue Li's cooking.

Honestly, Yue Li's cooking was balanced, with spiciness as a unique selling point, which she used in almost all of her products—Liyue wanted to eat something sweet today.

"Big Brother Han, ca..can you cook something sweet?" Heart skipped a beat as the words came out of her mouth involuntarily.

Han Li smiled and ruffled her hair a bit more. "That's the plan."

She nodded and walked towards the bathroom, a sweet sensation all over her body the first thing in the morning.

Han Li got himself busy in the kitchen. Washing the wares was the first thing to do.

In Starlight Zone, there was nothing fancy like the ones in his mom's stories, but instead of tap water, and instead, bamboo pipes were laid out to create a network that allowed water to flow from higher elevations to lower ones, ensuring a steady supply of water for irrigation and other purposes.

And the water source, of course, came from the houses spread throughout the Zone. Since these houses were built using a great treasure like the House Builder Seed, they had some passive powers that were very useful.

(House Builder Seed was the treasure Yue Li traded using her Talent at the start, and it made houses pop up in the whole zone, see chapter 19 for the full details.)

The houses could accumulate the water content in the atmosphere, turn it into water, and collect it in the root of the central part of the whole thing. The clan had some people pull the water into a huge bamboo storage container at a high elevation.

So, people in Starlight Zone had similar but less comfortable ways of getting water.

As the wares were cleaned, a fire was blazed using two stones that seemed to be shining silvery. Then, Han Li put the pots and such into the fire and began his cooking.

No matter what, he had Absolute Evolved Proficiency with the knife, so was his attention to detail absolutely monstrous.

The corn syrups he had bought from faraway merchants, sugar, flavoring, and finally, some little fruits to add to the color; of course, taste too. All of them mixed to form a mouth-watering smell that permeated the surroundings, while with a chuckle, Han Li started to make some tea on the side.

The mixture of things heated up and began to turn a sweet red color. Han Li's eyes brightened up, and he added some unique honey.

The honey in itself looked normal, but this was no normal one. It was the fruit of him rarely visiting the Trade Area in the city's heart; it was a Magical Resource.

It was the expensive sort.

Water Type Resource—Sea Serpent Honey.

The thing mixed into the red mix, and Han Li sighed as the whole bottle emptied itself. A smell as enticing as the sweetest things but not the sort that made people cringe emanated and spread throughout his home, and then it spread into the outside, too, making some people passing by salivate.

An old man was outside his home, some distance away, in another house's yard.

"Damn, my boy Han Li is cooking!" he said, wiping away some saliva at the side of his mouth as another old man snickered some distance away from him.

"Yeah, I know that guy's way with food. Almost all of his makings smell sweet."

"But I heard he stopped making those makings?" the first old man asked.

"Yeah, some say he inherited Pang Li's inheritance after marrying his daughter. They say he no longer needs money since Pang Li hid a fortune."

"Lucky brat, what about that fatty?"

"He moved away, I heard, signing the papers to his store in the boy's name before going away. It was done in a hurry, and the gatekeeper Evolved was pissed I heard."

"…What about that beautiful woman, Li Yao?"

The second old man squinted his eyes. "I heard… she is still here. Some people even say they saw her walk out of her room at some point."

"How is that possible?!" the first old man's eyes widened.

The other old man shook his head, "There is a rumor going on now, but no one is sure. There is one even wilder one, too, about how the boy healed her; if it was true, though, we should have seen her walking out. But that never happened."

The smell made their noses twitch, to the point that they didn't mind being shameless and going in for a 'friendly visit.' But sadly for them, at some point, Han Li's house had become the scrutiny of the neighborhood for the past few days, and they were people who loved their loneliness.

Yue Li looked at the sweet-looking candy and said with agitation, "He is better because of the honey thing he brought in the other day. Humph, I am and will be the best of this household in cooking."

Han Li smirked. "I am not so sure about that if I tried."

'Xiao Li is too strong, Argh!' she tried even harder but to no avail.

Han Li's voice echoed: "If you want it, go brush and come to me with the bottle, wash too. Both of you stink! Don't act so rushed. The more time you wait, the tastier it will get since it is still being cooked in the bottle."

He had enjoyed many different tastes.

None had ever been so… beautiful.

"Wow, that… what is this big brother?!" she exclaimed.

But she was, of course, still in his embrace.

Han Li smiled at her question and looked at her body, which was visibly relaxing. He pointed it out, "The Sea Serpent Honey is a powerful resource even for people at Evolution Stage; it can wash away the hidden fatigue in the body like a serpent shedding away its skin.

But there was a recipe in my hand that could help me get more of this honey, so we now have a lot of these candies hidden in that bottle, and all of them are, in a sense, able to remove the stacking fatigue after training. It also has minor healing factors to it, too."

This recipe of using simple fruits and such to make a liquid and adding the unique honey into it later to get more honey was of course a method from the Demonic Lust Sutra.

Liyue looked at him with a studious face and nodded. "I will remember about this treasure, Big Brother."

Chapter 102 - 102: The little creature that flew high
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li looked at her black eyes and took a deep breath. "What do you want me to do?" he asked.

Once upon a time, long lost in the pages of history was an ancient tree.

It existed in the desolate world where no wind, earth, water, or fire had yet come to.

In nothingness, it existed as the sole something.

It looked vast, way bigger than anything a man could ever hope to see.

When the first signs of life started to appear in the world; the emptiness was filled with land, water, fire, and wind. The tree witnessed and withstood all of that chaos and stood watch over the then, birthing world calmly.

It wouldn't budge when the oceans at one part slammed into its humongous branches like puddles slamming into a wall when disturbed. It wouldn't flinch when a part of the new forest on one side burned atop its roots. Its leaves, big enough to let giants take cover under it, were seen throughout the world.

In this ancient world covered by the Tree so colossal—a creature walked.

Not many knew how it looked, but the stories say the creature? Beast? Whatever it was, the stories said it had a pair of abyss-like crimson eyes and supposedly a face worthy of mortal nightmares that could steal lives.

It was a creature of horrors.

Maybe the word suited for it was neither beast nor a creature; that was too disrespectful of its divine Sacredness—it was a Demon.

One of the original life of the new world.

It stood mightily under the shade of the Ancient tree, looking up with wonder in its eyes. Below its feet, it could see the waves of the once fearsome ocean slamming hard. In a different direction, there was a desert that stretched long.

It didn't know in which way to walk, was it the hot desert or the deep sea?

Should it even walk?

It didn't think much.

It chose to go with the more comfortable and cold deep sea rather than the irritatingly hot desert. It walked above the sea with only its waist under the water. His back almost seemed as if it held the world secure, akin to a pillar holding up the sky.

As it walked through the rainy days and got washed by thunder and storms, it bellowed like a child with eagerness. It was happy and interested in these small movements happening around it.

The Tree shook, it was a gentle and small shake; a tiny tremble—

Stop.

—and the lands, deserts of ice, volcanoes, and everything suddenly came to a stop. The falling little leaves of the small trees around their original ancestor were stuck in the air. The erupting volcanoes were the same, while the Demon was no different.

It was frozen in place. It didn't move—it couldn't move. It stayed frozen like that. But the Demon, for some reason, could still see everything hundreds of thousands of seas away from it.

And in that distance, it saw a small fruit, the color of blue so vibrantly beautiful, shine as it grew from a small light at the intersection between the desert and sea.

The Fruit was at the top of one of the heaven-arcing branches of the mighty Tree, but the creature was no longer so tiny as it had been when it wanted to eat the red fruit, if it tried, it should be able to reach the fruit in only some time.
