She nodded and then looked at him seriously.

"So the next thing, son, is for this jade beauty to get you ready."

Han Li didn't understand why she was saying all of the things she said, but he had long since begun to ignore such thoughts she had or words she said. Whatever that makes her happy.

He smiled.

"Mom, kiss me."

Yue Li smiled and skipped to stand in front of him; with her hands behind her, she stood on her tiptoes and closed her eyes.

His hands slowly wrapped around her waist. She could feel the giddy feeling again—

Cough, she was not horny or addicted, one day cannot give her withdrawal symptoms. No way.

—She anticipated a warm and enjoyable kiss from the great Xianxia Mc of a son she had, but she was horrified as instead of her lip, his lips gently brushed against her neck, and his teeth grazed her skin lightly, followed by the soothing touch of his tongue. The sensation sent a ripple of shivers down her spine, her fingers clutching the fabric of his shirt.

This bastard, did he just…

Trembling in anger, she opened her eyes.

He looked at her with a smile.

"You! Xiao Li!" she resentfully muttered.

Han Li looked at her neck with a faint smile.

It was a small red mark, a testament to their shared connection. Yue Li touched the mark gently, an angered frown playing on her lips.

"I am not really against you being possessive and wanting to show them I belong to you, but this would totally be everyone's talk in the marriage if they see it. Are you trying to ruin the moment for poor Liyue? She was so anticipating this, you can't be serious!" she said in a suppressed voice.

Han Li spread his arms wide without talking.

She looked at him without moving, filled with anger.

But a while later, they sighed and began undressing him. She didn't say anything, though, and Han Li could tell she was really mad.

His clothes soon fell from his body, and he was naked in front of her, but she didn't do anything and just took out a black robe from a small cloth pack beside her and began dressing him.

She even straightened out his underwear when she was at it.

And after a while, he was neatly dressed.

A tight but comfortably loose robe, white in color, and a royal vibe of flair designs on its edges aided with his remarkable beauty—he looked like the prince charming of dreams.

She pouted her lips and shook her head. "Because you, I will have to hide it all day today. But don't worry, I also had some urges. You just couldn't hold it in. But yes, I am mad, but for Liyue, I will not make a fuss today with you."

Han Li looked at her. She was wearing a beautiful yellow robe accentuated with simple gemstones and then flower designs that were barely visible.

It was an expensive cloth.

But it looked good on her.

"Don't worry about the mark I left. It will disappear soon. It was faint in the first place."

She looked at him dubiously. It hurt just as much as last time, and last time, it remained for three days.

"You better not be lying, but I do have methods to hide it. Makeup is my weak point, but Li Yao can do it for me."

"Well, then you can do that."

There were many going around after that, and almost an hour later, Han Li and Yue Li walked into the middle of a clearing in the forest with smiles.

The place was not at all silent like the other days and was filled with people.

The old librarian, many beggars, and many of the young people whom Han Li had cooked for in the past. Some Evolved protectors of the clan, too.

Among them was Li Ming, looking at Han Li's handsome appearance with glazed eyes. "That ugly woman doesn't deserve you; she doesn't deserve you," he muttered under his breath and swallowed another cup of wine in his hand.

Beside him, one of his boyfriends poured another cup. "Don't worry, my dear, he just didn't have enough taste."

Li Ming shook his head listlessly.

Han Li's gaze briefly scanned around, and he paused at many of them. Especially some of the Evolved who threw supportive signals at him as his glance moved past them. Among the eleven, four of the Evolved had come.

Many of them were looking at him, sizing him up. While not a few gazes among all also looked behind him at his mother and hidden deep within, he could sense the carnal lust in them.

At least a quarter of the beggars looking around to get fed by Liyue's sympathy were looking at his mom with eyes he would have gouged out if it hadn't affected his greater plans.

But of course, to others, they must have seemed natural and calm gazes, but to him, they were just irritating. Most of them.

Even some young kids in the almost hundred-man crowd were looking at her like that.

'Wherever there is man, there will always be some kind of conflict. It just means Mom was lucky she had been mostly in the clan and not out. As much as the rules bind me, it protects the weak.'

But silently, he moved towards the 4 Evolved—3 men who seemed almost like triplets born from the same mother and a young woman in an iron mask—while under their gazes, he slowly activated one of his Talents.

He already had no reason to worry if the Evolved would sense him.

What to say? He even blatantly used his Soul Sense and Life Vision on them.

That was just one simple application of Heart Qi.

Just like how he couldn't sense it when The Spirit and Yang Wei came to mess with him and his mother using Heart Qi, he could also now do the same, just that he couldn't hide his own body in shadows like them.

But he could hide his own Evolved Aura and Soul Sense, and even the activation of his talents wouldn't be sensed by anyone other than people with Heart Qi.

It was a mysterious power only Transcendents had for a reason.

Talent—[Low-Level Lack of Lust]

Immediately, all the men lusting after Yue Li, and some women around lusting after him, all lost their lust as if it was drained of them. Some looked around in confusion for a second before they left the faint feeling of loss behind them.

He no longer had to worry about those coveting ruffians.

Han Li, on the other hand, humbly greeted the Evolved. "Seniors, I didn't expect you would come to this lowly-ones wedding."

They looked at him, and a buff man among them patted his shoulders. "Great job, young man; I am here to get your food, though, so that counts. I heard it was as good a food as you could get without making our inner circle chef cook."

"Oh, if so, then you are up for a surprise," Han Li jovially bowed and left them alone.

He actually felt a bit of respect for them.

Quite strong at heart.

They were much more stable in their emotions than the inferior ants around. They had no lust, or they were rather indifferent in a sense. Whereas the others were just insignificant ants.

With a smile, he looked towards another shelter-like tree covered in vine and decorated like a room.

Inside it, his bride was getting ready.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 121 - 121: Red clothes, auspicious.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside the tree that had been made into a dressing room, it was dark, but faint red candles hung beside a mirror set to the side, briefly illuminating Liyue's face as a woman stood behind her, gently combing her hair and making it with a golden ornament.

"To think Aunt Li Yao would walk again, it is news delightful on its own that I had yet to congratulate you. And to think the next time I end up seeing you, I would be marrying off is rather curious, too," the woman said with a smile, eliciting a nod from the seated bride.

"So, Liyue, who are you getting married to? Your cheap father didn't put you up with some miscreant, did he?"

Liyue was silent for a while before she nodded. "He did put me up with a miscreant."

Surprised, the woman gasped.

Liyue giggled.

"But the good sort of miscreant."

The woman was curious now.

She brought her face in front of Liyue, filled with questions.

"Is he handsome?"

Liyue blushed.

"Very."

"You struck gold then, little girl, good for you." The woman sighed. "Of all the people I have cared for in recent marriages, you are the most fortunate for the past decade. Honestly, I half-expected another woman crying to me, saying she has no meaning in life."

Liyue was startled. "There are many like that?"

"Of course there are," she said, looking at her own reflection in the mirror. "In your Starlight Zone alone, I know many who had been married forcefully. Of course, not all of them were against it."

Liyue grew thoughtful and then nodded. "Indeed, I am lucky, but do you know what?"

The sapphire eyes of the woman shimmered. "What? Boasts about your love story now?"

"Yes," Liyue put on her lipstick, made from pomegranate, roses, and sappanwood. These were ground into powder and mixed with oil to put a blush on her lips.

"Big Brother Han chose me from the start; my father didn't go to him, but he came to us saying he wanted me," Her voice was rather eloquent and proud, making Hua Feilan wonder behind her.

"You really like this man. Now I am quite curious."

Liyue looked in the mirror.

"I wonder what he will think of my clothes."

"You look splendid, to be honest. Even your mother will have to stand a step behind you in terms of appearances today. No jokes!"

Liyue looked at the woman with bright eyes. "Really?"

"Really."

They then once again began to prepare her for the show.

…

Han Li met the guests one by one like the humble village boy he was.

He showed respect to the elders old of age while also keeping a small eye on the Evolved at all times.

Yue Li walked behind him with quick steps like a tail before she had to go away at a call in the back of the clearing—there was smoke rising from there alongside a nice fragrance.

"Hey, Han Li!" someone patted him on the shoulder after a while.

He looked calmly at the person. "Thank you for coming to the wedding. I appreciate it," he said.

"You don't seem like you appreciate it," Li Ming coughed awkwardly. "Well, she was someone I couldn't make happy, so I hope you don't let her down."

Han Li looked around at the forested area that seemed so calm and auspicious with a smile.

"But man, you should have conducted the ceremony in the clan area. It just doesn't feel like a wedding without red lanterns flying in the air and some beauties dancing. I could've given you money if you didn't have it, you know, we're buddies and all at heart after all."

Han Li threw him a sideglance.

The man blushed and coughed. "No need to look at me as if I am some mud on the ground. Friends, nothing more than that. I am an elite of the clan, you know?"

Han Li patted him on his shoulder. "Yes, we will be friends now. Protecting each other's interests even at the cost of death."

Li Ming felt as if his heart skipped a beat, and before he knew it, Han Li was gone and talking to another person in the distance with a humble smile; it was the old man from the library who handed out Magical Resources.

"Was that a joke…?" Full of confusion, Li Ming walked towards Han Li in a daze.

"Eh… Han Li, you are cruel," He said to the accuser with a sigh. "But you are way more popular in the clan than I thought you were, even having Evolved come to your wedding. You really are amazing."

Han Li smirked. "Of course, they'd come. In fact, I am curious why the Clan Head himself didn't come."

"Oh?" Filled with curiosity, Li Ming asked, "So, are the rumors true? That you have a great backing behind you who is in hiding as of now and that you are respected secretly like a secret master of sorts and are just hiding your powers in the confusing mask of being a Spark-less?"

"You overestimate me," Han Li coolly replied. "It is just that the higher-ups want to know how my mother-in-law is walking and even cooking in the back while she was not supposed to."

He coldly smirked inside.

In fact, he had been anticipating the day for months now.

He had brought Li Yao outside on some occasions, especially the Awakening Ceremony before the last one. But other than basic rumors, nothing had happened until now.

But he knew it and was prepared. Honestly, he had expected Li Xingguang, the Head of the Starlights, to come here himself to interrogate him on 'friendly' terms, and he had cooked up an explanation, too.

With his Heart Qi, he didn't have to worry whether the Power Born would sense his hidden cultivation after all—as long as he didn't check him in-depth.

But it seems not to have picked the exalted Power Born's interest.

"So, Han Li," Li Ming pushed back his sleek black hair and said with pride, "No matter what, I see you as a friend, and a friend in need is a friend indeed. I booked some people to set up the hanging lights and such for the night. It will all be on me."

Han Li nodded.

"It is about time," he muttered.

"For what?"

"Of course, for my beautiful bride to come out looking like a goddess descended to the mortal world. And you brat, go sit somewhere else, don't ruin my image. People will talk."

Li Ming was horrified and looked at Han Li with a pale face. "Han Li!"

Han Li's eyes twitched.

But suddenly, his gaze shifted towards the bridal room.

Red qipao adorned her curvy and goddess-like physique with net-like coverings on her hands, and her hair was tied with a golden ornament while her eyes were those like the night sky itself; with graze, she walked towards him, and he walked towards her.

Most of the venue was immediately dazed on the spot.

"Shan Liyue was… this beautiful?" Li Ming blurted out in a dumbfounded expression.

The graceful woman wore a veil but somehow, she seemed divine.

Yue Li and Li Yao walked back from the cooking area while speaking about something important as Han Li and Liyue stood face-to-face. Finally.

'I am not nervous, I am not nervous, this is not scary, not scary," she murmured quietly while trembling slightly, and Han Li heard it and smiled.

In the bottom of his heart, a fire blazed—

She looked up at him with round, somewhat teary eyes filled with nervousness as she moved with a fearful pace towards him.

—a fire of protectiveness.

This was his woman, Liyue. Rather headstrong, somewhat of an airhead who thinks all the time, a hard worker, a rather fearful girl.

He gently held her hand, and time seemed to have stopped there.

It was like a curtain of silence hung in the air.

"Junior, you dare!" an old man screamed with a face full of venom.

Han Li was stunned and looked behind him to see the old man from the library look towards the sky with a face that was drained of the old calmness he had seen so many times.

Taking the cane into the air, the old man vanished only to appear in the air above the forest.

Coldly, he gazed towards the city, and the people below in the forest looked at him in awe and disbelief. Even Han Li was looking at everything with a nervous frown.

The old man coldly snorted, and his appearance slowly shifted.

From the old and haggard nature of his body, he turned into a cold-looking young man in white clothes; his face radiated the energy of youth, and his long black hair flew in the wind.

At the same time, Han Li looked around him.

"It is the Clan Head," he muttered to his bride and, taking Liyue's hand, shouted coldly to the people around him, "Do not worry and forget what you just saw. It is the Clan Head who just had some emergency. The event continues!"

Han Li's gaze, full of hidden coldness, moved straight towards the Evolved, who all looked up at the sky and, one by one, transformed into starlight and then vanished.

The iron-masked woman said before vanishing, "Do not worry, we will take care no trouble reaches here whatever the issue was."

And after mumbling something—

Low-Level Magic Power: Movement of Starlight.

—She also vanished with a yellowish-white light in her wake.

Han Li's gaze shifted around for a bit before it came back to rest on Liyue.

"Don't worry."

His worried glance then focused on Li Yao and Yue Li, who were staring around in nervousness.

"Everybody calm down!" Li Ming yelled from around them and said with a laugh, "As long as the Clan Head is here, what have we got to worry about!"

Han Li nodded, but deep down, he had a bad feeling.

This was not in the script.

Something had changed.

Again.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 122 - 122: Hua Feilan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li, Liyue, Yue Li, and Li Yao got together in the dressing room alongside Hua Feilan—the marriage-handling woman, and sat in silence.

The candles on the side flickered, lighting Han Li's contemplative face cupped in his hand.

'What had changed? And how did Li Xingguang transform his appearance so well? Moreover, does he know something I missed? Why is he acting like the librarian when he has no need to?'

His mind was full of thoughts and ideas, but none seemed coherent enough to be the puzzle piece he was missing.

'I don't have to worry about it as long as it doesn't affect me.'

He cleared his mind off the questions and, with a sigh inside, looked at the woman in front of him.

"Miss Hua, thank you for the assistance throughout this event, and sorry for the trouble at the moment," he said with a short bow of his head.

Hua Feilan quickly shook her head. She was seated on a chair, and her sapphire eyes looked at him curiously while flickering with candlelight; her hair was a brownish black tinged with orange specks here and there, and her skin was fair.

"You guys paid me for the event, and even if you hadn't, I would've come solely to help Liyue marry the man of her dreams," she said politely, smiling at Liyue, "Right, girl?"

Liyue blushed and looked at Han Li while he looked at her. Her pearly hands were now held in his hand tightly, and they hadn't yet let go of each other—the wedding was far from over.

Looking at Liyue, Han Li sighed.

"I always knew you were much more beautiful than you seemed appearance-wise itself, to think that you would be far more deadly when you use make-up and such. I was dazed there for a bit."

Liyue smiled nervously, "I was just super nervous. I thought I might faint at some point when that yell came."

Yue Li was on the side, and she said with curiosity, "And the Clan Head even said, Junior, YOU DARE! I was so startled… cough. And before I knew it, he was flying in the air too, so cool!"

Li Yao ignored them and looked at Hua Feilan. "So, Feilan, how is your marriage service going these days? It's been so long, and nobody even came to visit me after I healed."

Looking embarrassed, the woman replied, "I am too busy. In fact, you walking again seemed more like a ridiculous rumor than anything. And you know what we are like… sigh…"

Li Yao nodded with a sigh, too.

Starlight Zone had many women she knew, but at this point, some of them whom she considered friends in the past were long behind her—they had revealed their true faces when she became crippled—and the rest of them had drifted apart from the city.

Women were not the sort to have many friends in Starlight Zone in the first place since mostly, it is a patriarchal rule there, and most of the time, women are just tools for the men.

That was why all women of Starlight respected 'Fang Li of the Iron Masked.' The only Evolved woman of the Starlight Clan who was so young and vigorous. The pride of them.

Of course, it was not an absolute fact that the women were always the ones in the lower stage.

Han Li's and Liyue's relationship was a prime example.

In other people's gaze, Han Li was just a Spark-less brat while Liyue, in essence, was an inner circle clan member who had passed the ice flag test. So, of course, the dominant one was probably Liyue, with Han Li being her obedient husband, at least in people's thoughts.

So, usually, women having much of a friend circle was rare if it was not laid by a capable lead.

Even if they had some circle going, the narrow-minded husbands usually cut it off. So, Li Yao's friend circle was low in number, if not nonexistent, just like Yue Li's was. But in her case, she did know many people, but she wasn't friends with any. When young, she was afraid of her secret Transmigrator identity being spread around, and when old, she had all her friends in Han Li.

And, of course, if it came to it, she did have people with whom she could go out on something like a shopping trip. Yes, and that was why this woman Hua Feilan was so famous around, not only in Starlight Zone but the whole city.

She was a big shot.

Nicknamed The Friend of all Women.

In fact, even if the woman looked and acted young, Han Li's [Life Vision] showed him the truth very simply.

[Hua Feilan] [Age: 51]

[Current Cultivation: Evolved]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Evolved]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Power Born, Fire Great Dao.]

[Death: (in the last premonition) At age 399 in a fight with the Demon Han Li, killed in cold blood with one slash of the Life Sword.]

A gentle smile stretched across Han Li's face as he spoke some simple things with Liyue and Yue Li while Li Yao conversed with Hua Feilan.

In fact, Hua Feilan and Li Yao weren't really great friends, but Hua Feilan liked to act as if every woman in the world were actually her friends, and subconsciously, they end up acting that way.

Even Liyue just had two days of familiarity with her and was speaking with her like they were long-time friends. But Han Li wasn't worried about her intentions. He was the one who went to her Marriage service asking for her to handle his marriage; he had also paid heavily.

Thus, he didn't actually worry about any bad intentions this woman might have; if he knew someone who would die for righteousness—this was that woman.

She had died against him in his past life for righteousness alone.

"So, Miss Hua, how does the marriage ceremony continue from here?" he asked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 123 - 123: When my big brother gave what I asked for.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"So, Miss Hua, how does the marriage ceremony continue from here?" he asked, and finally, all four women's gazes came to rest on him. That was a serious question.

Liyue held his hand tighter and said with worry, "We really seem to have chosen a bad day, Big Brother; I hope there are no big issues."

Han Li didn't reply to her, and his gaze focused on Hua Feilan.

"Young Master Han, we do what we are paid for, and you don't have to worry," Hua Feilan said with a graceful smile. "I have by now managed 3998 marriages, and none of them had failed. What to say, even though the Hua Matchmaker Association is just budding its roots in Southern Lust through the last decade, we have been doing business outside for years."

Han Li nodded. "Miss Hua and her association have great prestige; I have often heard of its great stories."

Pleased, Hua Feilan nodded and looked at Liyue. "I might seem like a simple woman because I am your close friend, but don't think so simply of me. I am an Evolved, and I have also called for backup just in case to my Association manager; he is also an Evolved. So, you have two Evolved protecting this place from here, and then there is also the Starlight Family Evolved who would protect you, too.

There is nothing to worry about in that regard."

Liyue nodded, feeling less worried. "But does such things happen often, Big Brother? This seems so sudden and so out of nowhere; I wonder why the Clan Head was so angry."

Han Li shook his head.

"Something significant must've happened for the Clan Head to lose his temper and reveal his appearance, but I don't think it will affect the ceremony."

Hua Feilan noted how Liyue seemed reliant on Han Li a lot in her mind and said duly, "The next part of the ceremony would be the blood oath event, and after that, the groom and bride will kowtow to their parents and get their blessing. If it is completed, that means you are married. Then we can serve the dishes to the guests. It is all set."

Han Li looked at Liyue's veiled face. He could feel how tightly she held his hand.

She was really anticipating today.

"Shall we begin?"

Liyue nodded like a bird pecking race.

Han Li held her hands and looked at the mirror for one final time.

He silently connected telepathically with Liyue. "Are you nervous, Liyue?"

Liyue was a bit surprised, but she held on to her big brother's hand and just tightened her hold to show her response.

"No need to be nervous, Liyue; your big brother is with you. Just leave everything to me and follow in my footsteps. I'll lead you."

Liyue looked at him.

Then she looked around at all the people around.

They were each looking at her with supportive expressions. She smiled under her veil.

Then walked in front of Han Li and hugged him.

Han Li was a bit surprised but gently patted her head.

"Liyue, have some confidence in yourself now. For me to show off at least how great a wife I had gotten," Han Li said to her calmly. But to Liyue, she could hear how much confidence he had in her.

Honestly, she was an unconfident woman to herself. Incapable of much greatness. But her brother Han had far more belief in herself than she herself did.

Images flickered in her mind.

He had always cared for her like a younger sister, a baby of his. He was so caring and gentle that she sometimes wondered whether he was really her… fiance.

Was she that lucky?

She had seen and understood that he had another part to himself that she hadn't seen. A crueler, more ruthless part that was not anything that she or her mothers understood or knew of. But she had no doubts. In her life, her only man was Han Li. Her Big Brother Han.

Whatever he was, she had decided.

Of course, there were little ambitions in her mind, too. She did want to make her big brother more of a good, righteous man. For that, though, she had to learn more about him and understand who he was.

And she was ready to dedicate her life to that.

She said with a soft tone, "Let's just complete this and go back to our usual simple home. That is far better than here with all this."

Han Li nodded.

"Miss Hua, let's begin."

…

Han Li and Liyue stood in the middle of a crowd of people. Men, women, rich, poor—it was a mix of all that filled the forest clearing. In the birth of noon, the shadows in the forest made the whole place a comfortable area to stay at while all of them looked at the two about to become one with interest.

Even though all sorts of people were there, it was properly divided in standing.

The poor but clean beggars stood to one corner while the proper people stood to another side, and around another corner were the Clan people of the Starlight Family.

As the people bustled with excitement at the delicious smell permeating the area, Hua Feilan stood in front of Han Li and Liyue, asking, "I ask in the name of heaven and earth, standing as a guardian to your union. Say, Han Li of the Starlight Family, Son of Yue Li, do you wish to marry Liyue of the Starlight Family, daughter of Li Yao wholeheartedly?"

Han Li looked at the red-veiled, beautiful woman whose hand was now sweating in his grasp and nodded. "I wholeheartedly agree to the marriage."

Hua Feilan nodded seriously and asked Liyue next. "Do you agree to the Groom's proposal?"

"Yes, I do." Liyue nodded.

"Please conjoin your blood."

Hua Feilan then cut a small wound on their thumbs, and Han Li and Liyue gently touched it together.

A long silence followed before Li Ming started to clap from the side while one of his many boyfriends rubbed his wet eyes.

"Both the wife and husband may now kneel to their parents or guardian."

Han Li and Liyue moved to stand before Yue Li and Li Yao, who were standing together beside Hua Feilan.

Then, they both gently kowtowed on the grassy ground, and with moist eyes, their respective parent pulled them up and gave their blessings with pure love.

"Serve the dishes!" someone yelled, and it was soon followed by many uniformed men coming out of the forest with trays of food and tables.

It was men of the Hua Matchmaking Association.

In Liyue's mind, Han Li gently whispered, "Little Wife, there are many things that follow marriage, so be prepared. But for now, let's just eat."

Han Li smirked as her hands tightened his hand.

She leaned towards his ears and asked with a smile, "I was thinking I needed something as a wedding gift. Can I get something? To remember today…"

"Oh, when did my Liyue start to ask for things herself?"

"When my big brother began to give her what she asked for."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 124 - 124: Interrogation by the sister. Leave him.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The beginning of the marriage was rather tame in comparison to the grand weddings the rich men usually held, but as the food was served, the people from all walks of life slowly began to settle onto the ground and just eat.

Many had tears going down their eyes as they ate; many were just children who were being given something good, something really good, to eat after days if not months. And to such people who struggled to make ends meet, the food they ate today was well and beyond something heavenly.

Not all people of the Seven Continents hailed from a clan like the Starlight Family, Ravens, or Rocks. Such clans came to be because of strong Power Born individuals like the Clan Heads. If they were not there, such clans wouldn't have existed.

But they all had a Spark in their soul from birth that they could Awaken to grow stronger. They also had the right to dream.

But as Liyue's gaze moved from one beggar eating Yue Li's special dish that was served with teary eyes to another, she couldn't help but feel bad for them; she couldn't help but pity them. She was eating such things on a daily base, and she had never even stopped to appreciate it.

She couldn't help but see another side of life she hadn't seen yet.

Maybe her big brother being cruel—at least in his own words—might also have reasons she didn't understand completely.

Among these beggars, a vast majority were crippled. Some had immovable hands, some had actual physical deformities, while some more had issues on par with her own mother, Li Yao, and were being dragged by children to sit and then fed the puffy white race and the side dishes.

'As Big Brother said, the world is cruel, and we could only attempt to have some semblance of control… with what I am given, if I don't do my best, it is a sin on its own.'

Her husband was not beside her; he was on another side, talking with some of the guests and inquiring around to get a sense of what had happened earlier, but she didn't mind; he had promised to give her a wedding gift, and she was anticipating what it would be.

She could also see some children beginning to hang lanterns around the area.

"Liyue, what are you thinking?" Hua Feilan, her friend, asked from the side teasingly, "About how tonight would be?"

Liyue shook her head. Somehow rather comfortable around this woman. Like a person she had been familiar with for years.

"I am wondering what our children would be like. Would they be like him or me or my mothers…" she wondered really curiously.

Hua Feilan chuckled. "Fantasizing about children even before you bed him. Gotta leave it to you for having found someone you liked. And I did research on him on my own, too, for your sake if you want to know."

"Oh?" her interest sparked; she looked at Hua Feilan curiously.

"Han Li, son of Yue Li and some mysterious person. He had no Spark from the day he was born, was ostracized by the children his age, and had many weird rumors going on around him and his background. Even his mother was a source of rumors, too."

Seeing Liyue was listening, she nodded and continued. "But he is known for his uprightness in relationships and his cooking in the Starlight Zone, and now known for something rather weird, which I would like to confirm from you, if I am not rude, that is. Of course, I like to believe there is nothing like rudeness between friends."

Liyue tilted her head. "Why are you doing such research, though, Big Sis?"

Hua Feilan patted Liyue's head without roughing up her hair. She was tall enough to do that.

In fact, Han Li was a bit shorter than Hua Feilan.

"Liyue, this world is vast and everyone is not the way they seem. Especially men. They can be animals, demons even. How can I hand a cute girl to someone I couldn't trust? My conscience would kill me if something happened to you because I was too lazy to research."

Liyue smiled wryly at Hua Feilan through her red veil. "Brother Han won't harm me. In fact, I can guess what you are about to question too… but go on, I will answer."

"What happened to your Father, Pang Li?"

Liyue shook her head. "I had thought till today that he had just gone up and walked away from our lives one day, but supposedly, that was not the case. But only Big Brother knows the full picture, and he has yet to tell me. But he did say it was not as it appeared, though. And he also told me father's current location, which I would like to keep to myself."

"What about…" This time, the ever-heroic voice of Hua Feilan seemed a bit softer: "What about that man's relationship with your mother? There are these weird rumors going on that he also uhm, has your mother under his wraps."

Liyue blushed furiously under her veil and looked away, muttering inside herself, 'How shameful… I should have let Big Brother Han handle her.'

Hua Feilan squinted at Liyue and nodded. But asked in a faint voice, "Are you against that relationship? Are you being forced in any way or form?"

Liyue looked at Hua Feilan like she was looking at a fool.

"Even I could see how happy I look at marrying him. Is that question even necessary? In fact, my brother Han and I had been living together for months now. This marriage is actually just a formality and not anything like you think. So no need to worry about me."

Sighing, Hua Feilan nodded.

"He did seem like a decent person; now, my last question, Liyue, and the most important."

Liyue was curious now.

"Liyue, do you know the consequences that may follow by marrying a Spark-less?"

Liyue was startled by the question, and Hua Feilan could see right through it. She frowned.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 125 - 125: Deal or no deal?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Spark-Less are those hated by the heaven or, for better terms, those not loved by it."

Liyue looked at Hua Feilan's attractive and beautiful face, which was full of sighs.

"If your father, Pang Li, were to be here, he would have never let you marry someone so useless; not to be rude, Liyue, but the truth is the truth."

Liyue was a bit flustered and looked away. Her big brother hadn't taught her what to say in a scenario like this.

Hua Feilan, on the other hand, continued seriously. "I do accept that he looks handsome and has all the redeeming features you would want in a husband. But as you said, your children… what assurance do you have that they won't inherit his traits and be Spark-Less, too? In fact, I just came to know this, or I might have acted rather differently to him earlier in the dressing area."

Liyue asked, a bit nervous. "But isn't not having a Spark and having failed Evolution the same… don't people marry after failing if they don't have some really bad crippling issues?"

"No, Liyue," Hua Feilan said even more seriously but faint enough that the people near them didn't hear her. Luckily, the bride won't be disturbed much before the night ends as that was considered disrespectful to the husband.

That was the reason she was wearing a veil in the first place. It represented her maidenhood—of course, her maidenhood was quite unique since she had experienced the forbidden fruit (only in the back) already.

Hua Feilan spoke, "Sparks are most of the time inherited from the parents, and usually, your Starlight Spark and your previous fiance Li Ming's Starlight Spark becoming one would have given birth to an even more talented Starlight member. But this one… maybe your child might be born with a Starlight Spark, or maybe none, like him."

Liyue took a silent second to ponder it.

Hua Feilan rationally told her even more of what she was missing.

"I know people like Han Li might look pitiful to you and you might have already grown close to him. But don't be blinded by how he supports you, he is not worth it. I will find you a better man, in fact, I know someone who will fit you perfectly. Don't mind the marriage since I have ways you could undo it just as easily as you were bound."

Liyue sighed. "What can I do at this point… I am already pregnant with his child…"

Hua Feilan was stunned and looked at Liyue with a pale face.

Liyue sighed once more. "I am becoming more shameless, and that was a joke…"

And she really did feel very comfortable around this person to the point it was becoming slightly suspicious. She wondered if she was being played around with some Magic. But that shouldn't be, not when Han Li was around.

Hua Feilan breathed out in relief and looked at Liyue.

Liyue smiled. "Sorry, but you will have to talk with my Husband before introducing another man to me. I think he is the sort that gets angry when other men approach his women, and well, it is hilarious to think of, though."

"What is hilarious?"

'That my Brother Han could be even considered inferior,' Liyue said in her mind.

She then smiled and looked towards Han Li who was talking with a deep frown with his two mothers at a distance. She could see the faint sense of anxiety on the two women's faces. She furrowed her brows.

"And Big Sis, were you ever in love with someone?" she asked, a bit worried.

"Me?" Hua Feilen sensed how she wasn't being taken seriously and stopped bothering—she could take a clue. "I do not love, and I would not love. But you are far too young to understand my dilemmas, so well, since you do not mind any issues I mentioned… Liyue, I can only bless for you a great life full of luck and fortune."

Just as they were about to continue speaking, they saw Han Li coming towards them while Yue Li and Li Yao followed behind him nervously. Many people's gazes were on Han Li and the core part of the family where the marriage was happening, but now, Han Li didn't care.

He walked with them and told them to gather in the dressing area once more.

Hua Feilan was curious and Liyue nodded worriedly.

…

Inside the Tree Hollow.

Han Li took a deep breath and then breathed out.

"Miss Hua, did you receive any intelligence about what is going on?"

Liyue was sitting at his side; today was her day, so Yue Li wasn't one to be stubborn—even though she was already wishing for the day to end already, she was growing uncomfortable and tense; what Han Li said earlier didn't sit well with her.

Liyue muttered in Han Li's ears, "She did gather information, but not about the situation but about you, big brother."

Hua Feilan saw it and felt as if she was betrayed, but she sighed. "I have no guts to go investigate a clan; I value my head quite a lot, you see. So, any way that I could learn something about it?"

Nodding, Han Li leaned towards her and looked into her eyes. "What do you say about a deal? A deal that would net you ten times the amount that you earned through this marriage?"

Her sapphire eyes sparkling, she looked back at him, and an intimidating Soul Sense surrounded Han Li. "That would be nearly twenty thousand Sacred Coins. Are you sure?"

Han Li smiled coldly. "I am sure, and in fact, I am sure it won't be much of an issue for you to complete the mission, too."

Curious, the Soul Sense vanished, and she looked at him with a tilted head. "What is the catch? What is it that you want the matchmaker association to handle?"

"I want you to protect my family for the next three days. It might be less days, but not more. And for just that, you earn 20000 Sacred Coins."

"I have no proof that you own such wealth," Hua Feilan's orange-specked hair seemed to brighten as if it was a flame; her sapphire eyes, on the other hand, seemed like molten rock.

Han Li put his hand to the side and brought out a small bag. It was all the money he had collected from around the area with his future knowledge, and it contained five thousand coins.

He dropped it on the ground, and the coins made jingling noises.

"I am willing to pay before I go, but I would want you to take a vow."

Hua Feilan scanned Han Li from top to bottom. "You seem like the other rumor of you more than the one I believed in."

Han Li shrugged. "All of them are people's pastimes, so say…"

He paused.

His eyes grew a calmness that not even his mother noticed.

"Miss Hua, what do you say?"

Pondering, Hua Feilan nodded. "I can see something is going on, but I have no plans to waste such good money. You have my word."

Han Li's eyes grew even calmer.

"Can we talk somewhere else private?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 126 - 126: Han Li had a guess
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What is going on?" Liyue asked nervously to Yue Li and Li Yao, who were in the tree hollow.

Both of them looked at each other and then back at Liyue. Han Li and Hua Feilan had left earlier and were supposedly going to someplace private to speak about what was going on now. They had been talking with Han Li regarding it anyway; Liyue was the only one in the dark.

"Xiao Li got some information earlier from your previous fiance, Li Ming," Yue Li said, worry etched on her face: "He didn't tell us exactly what is going on, but Li Yao heard a bit of what Li Ming said, and we just confronted Xiao Li to tell us what is going on, but he didn't tell us at all."

Liyue could see the worry, and she had a bad premonition.

"What did you hear, Mom?" she turned to Li Yao, hoping it was not as bad as the places her mind was going.

Li Yao chewed on the thought for a while and sighed. "I think we should wait for your brother Han to come and explain; it is not good news at all…"

"Tell me!" Liyue said, calming her nervousness.

A long silence hung in the air, and Li Yao finally relented, "Deeper into the forest, a caravan full of traders were attacked and then killed, a river of blood was left behind.

Supposedly, for a while, there were sounds of earthquakes echoing out from around there… that meant two people at the Power Born level were fighting, and the next news I heard was that, currently, the entire Southern Lust City is in a lockdown; no one is allowed inside, and no one is allowed outside."

"Lockdown? Who is locking it down?" Liyue asked with a frown.

"It is the Rock Clan. They erected huge rocks across the entrances of the city, and no one is allowed in or out now. Moreover, each area has an Evolved guarding it."

"…Why would they lock the entire area down? And… it had to be today…" Gritting her teeth gently, Liyue held back the desire to curse and asked, "What did Big Brother say exactly?"

Yue Li looked at her and said word by word, "Don't annoy me for a while, something interesting is happening, and I am wondering how I am supposed to not piss off my wife if I go away now. And then he came towards you and talked about that deal with Hua Feilan."

Liyue frowned and then gloomily looked down at the ground. "So he really is going to go away somewhere… today…"

Yue Li coughed awkwardly. "Don't tell him I told you that."

Li Yao sighed. "Liyue, those dead traders of the caravan also included children of tender ages, and moreover, there were even pregnant daughters inside… someone came and murdered them all in cold blood. They were all people like us, alive, breathing, hopeful of something, and they died that simply. It is not time for you to be selfish. I think he is as reluctant to go away as you are.

He must have his reasons."

Liyue nodded and then paled as the realisation struck her.

It was hundreds of life that was lost there.

…

The shine of the sun streamed in through the canopies of leaves and was then reflected off of the still pond's surface. Looking at it, Han Li was silent.

"May I know what you wanted?" Hua Feilan asked.

She would have acted coldly earlier, and even now, she still felt a bit that Han Li was taking advantage of Liyue when he was a good-for-nothing.

Han Li was silent for a while and chewed on many considerations. Then, finally, he asked, "My deal stays, and I would like to bind the contract with you. But not some verbal contract, but an actually binding, Magical Contract."

She frowned.

"I don't see what you are talking about."

"Hmm, have you heard of Heart Qi?"

"…Heart Qi?" confused, Hua Feilan asked.

"Yes, it is a power only those great Transcendents have," Han Li looked on distantly, filled with desire. "Someone among them once gifted me a jewel of Heart Qi. Only they know how such powers work and all, but with this jewel, I could bind people's agreements. So much that if you go against it, you will die on the spot."

Hua Feilan's sapphire eyes shone with surprise. "Such things exist?"

"I was also surprised, but sadly, it only works once." Han Li took out a blue gem from his pockets. "Well, I want to use it on our agreement today that even if hell is let loose, you will protect my family at all costs till I come back."

Hua Feilan frowned and grew thoughtful.

"Such precious thing, using that would be a waste…" she muttered. "You can actually just believe me and give me that gem instead of the sacred coins as payment. I am a forthright person; I have never gone against any of my promises."

Han Li coldly looked at her, but she didn't notice it.

If she wouldn't accept to bind a contract with him, he would kill her here and now. There can be no one who knows he had Heart Qi without being bound by that knowledge itself.

Not everyone was Yang Wei. This woman just had Average Talent as a Transformation Type Cultivator. Her Dao Soul was Fire Peacock, and she could transform into one.

But with her Average Dao Soul, her cultivation was as good as none against him. His body was stronger than hers, and he could see the tender neck clearly—one thought, and she wouldn't even have a chance to resist.

He smiled.

"This is the final deal. Let us vow with each other using the gem as our intermediate, and you protect them for the next three days. As simple as that, and for your understanding, let me also tell you this: the chance of something dangerous happening is almost zero, but I just don't feel comfortable with the city the way it is now."

She looked at him with a sigh. "The gem is such a waste, but I can see you might be suspicious… I would be too… but before using it, may I know what is happening?"

Han Li nodded. "Demons. A horde of them. Evolution Stage mainly, and some below it too, are joining together in a hunt of young children of all three clans of Southern Lust City."

She frowned heavily. "You mean?"

"Yes. So many evil men," he chuckled inside at the wording, and then continued, "so many of them waited inside the city and outside it, capturing Starlight, Rock, and Raven Clan children one by one when they got the chance before running away."

A silent second later, she said through gritted teeth.

"Magical Resource refining using children as the base. Repulsive!"

Han Li nodded solemnly.

Now that he thought of it, he had also seen someone like that in the city, but to think there were tens of them all in cahoots was news to him. Something that he was sure had not happened in his last life.

"These demons seem to be aiming at our city mainly, and the most fearsome fact is not that. There is something scarier. Something I can't figure out why."

She gulped. "What?"

"There is a Power Born behind them. And this guy is strong enough to keep escaping the three Power Borns of all three clans while keeping the Demonic Evolved protected. And he is doing this on purpose and leading the Power Borns away one by one."

She paled. "A Power Born Demon? Aren't we supposed to notify the Demon Restraining Temple immediately? And shouldn't you also notify your clan head that you think they are being led somewhere?"

Han Li chuckled.

"They are all smart men. They must know they are being led. And Miss Hua, here is where I need your help."

"In what?" she asked, her forehead covered in sweat now. So many things were happening.

"I want to go out for a while," he said, "but I am worried about those demons entering the city seeking revenge or anything along those lines. I am sure the Power Born backing won't do that openly, but some Evolved might come, and if they do, I want you to protect my family. In fact, you should stay in hiding with them till I come back."

"Where are you headed, and why today of all days?"

Han Li smiled.

"Well, you see, that is not part of our deal. You just have to protect them till I come back."

She frowned and thought for a long time. Then sighed.

"It is deal, bind us together or whatever. I would have gone into hiding in such a situation anyway, and this information is really valuable knowledge."

Han Li nodded and extended the gem towards her.

Releasing his Heart Qi, he swore on it as a deal, and sensing the weird power, she also nodded and recited what he told. It was a simple deal that would expire in three days. But even after that, they were not allowed to expose today's events to anyone else.

Han Li could have tried to take advantage of her, but he didn't. At the same time, the gem in his hand vanished into his Empty Space.

She looked at his bare hand and nodded.

"Three days. I hope you tell me what was special about today then. I cannot use the information against you now, after all. I am rather curious."

Han Li said coolly with a chuckle. "I won't mind if you become my woman. What do you say?"

She shook her head.

"Not possible."

Han Li chuckled.

"Well, consider it a goodwill from an old friend; if you see something around that flies off like a firefly but is the size of a head, catch it and hide it. If someone sees you with it that is not my family, kill them immediately or at least bind them. That thing is a treasure Power Borns would kill for. And now, around here, tens of them are running like fallen coins."

She was stunned.

"So you are going in search of this thing?"

Han Li wryly coughed.

"I am, but I have to speak with my wife first."

"Isn't it… too dangerous for a Spark-less?"

Han Li sighed.

"I have my ways…"

She nodded, and then they walked back the way they had come to the wedding area.

Han Li muttered inside himself, 'Maybe it is finally a chance I will get to have some practical training before I fight Yang Wei.'

Just as he reached the clearing of the marriage once again, he saw the chattering crowd and the people around once more. His gaze roaming, it finally landed on a young man who was being fed his dishes by another mature man.

"Li Ming, any other news you got?" Han Li asked with a smile.

Li Ming looked at him curiously and then nodded. "I just got more news…" he looked at Hua Feilan inquisitively.

"Speak, she is not an issue."

Li Ming raised an eyebrow and then nodded. "That Power Born is a woman. One of my boyfriends, this uncle, just came from that area. And he says he saw a glimpse of her and the Starlight Head fighting in the distance before he ran here."

Han Li looked at Li Ming and asked, "Nothing more?"

"Nothing more," he shook his head.

Nodding, Han Li walked towards the tree hollow with a thoughtful mind.

'Woman, Power Born… at this point in time, who was here that matched all the details…'

Pushing aside the green vines, he entered, and immediately, three gazes focused on him.

Especially intense was one gaze that seemed to look at him with all sorts of emotions.

"Liyue…" Han Li smiled wryly. "Don't worry, I have an idea."

Liyue looked at him with tearful eyes, and he could see they had a lot of questions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 127 - 127: Hidden Undercurrents
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Liyue…" Han Li smiled wryly. "Don't worry, I have an idea."

"What idea? What is happening in the first place?" she asked, seated on his lap now.

Hua Feilan had already taken her leave for now to prepare some things, and their family was the only one currently in the hollow. Yue Li and Li Yao sat to the side, looking at them focusedly.

Han Li pondered for a second and looked at his wife.

How weird.

Even though he had seen such things as mere titles, there was still a warmth to the name. Maybe it wasn't all meaningless wording.

"Let me first tell you what is going on." He stroked Liyue's hair. "And you can decide my actions from here on since I am also a bit hesitant about the choice, and the benefits barely match the risk."

Feeling the warmth of his hand as he glided his hands across her head, she grew silent and listened for what was happening.

"Basically, many cruel cultivators are attacking the city, and all three clans are in a startled state. Some of even the Starlight Clan kids are in their hands, supposedly—kidnapped. And we can't enter the city now; most probably, some of the demons are inside the city, too."

"Why are they attacking?" Liyue asked, grasping her brother's robe. Nervous.

Han Li thought about this question deeply for a second and then spoke out his thoughts, "Detached Empty Spaces. Have you heard of it, any three of you?"

They shook their heads.

"The empty spaces inside us is a mysterious space that holds our Sparks, Dao Souls, and everything of that sort. And when someone at the stage of Power Birth or higher dies, their empty spaces disintegrate into the atmosphere, while their sparks or dao souls rise to the sky—in myths, they are said to one day transform into stars."

They nodded once more.

They had heard of it before.

"But there is a rare chance of an Empty Space not disintegrating, and then it transforms into a treasure. A physical orb that could actually be touched physically. The Dao Soul inside it would still phase through its physical bounds and fly off into the sky once the cultivator dies, but not the Empty Space in itself—that is, if the rare instance occurred in the first place."

"What can it be used for?" Liyue asked softly, feeling him stroke her lips through her veil with his own lips. She could also feel the warmth of his breath.

Han Li smelled the unique scent of his wife and spoke, "It can be absorbed to gain a hell of a lot of energy, and I can absorb it slowly over some months; even one of them would cause my cultivation speed to skyrocket for a few days. And not only that, it can be used to cook some special recipes in my hand."

Yue Li asked, feeling something amiss deep inside her. "I don't know Xiao Li; I don't like what is happening."

Frowning, Han Li looked at his mother. "Why?"

Li Yao and Liyue also wondered about that question. In Liyue's mind, she had already accepted that her Big Brother wouldn't be there today on their own first night, and she was planning just to have a good time before he went away and wait till he came back. As frustrated as she was, he had taught her to seize opportunities when it was before her; she can't contradict him now.

But it would be better if some kind of actually good reason made him decide not to go today.

"Xiao Li, I don't know… something about this whole thing seems very unsettling to me."

Han Li frowned deeply.

"For no reason at all?"

She nodded.

He frowned even deeper.

According to his thoughts, the only treasure that was around here at this point in time that could incite a Power Born level battle was the Detached Empty Spaces that was supposed to be near this city, hidden somewhere even he didn't know, not yet excavated or usable.

The only reason he came to the conclusion that it was these empty spaces was due to one single thing. There was nothing around here worthy of Power Borns taking action except that—rarely would people as intelligent as the Power Born willingly risk their lives. Petty things normally never bothered them.

They wouldn't fight with another Power Born even if their Clan Children were stolen at times. People below Power Born didn't really matter to them at all—it was that simple.

'There were no eternal enemies, only eternal benefits.'

For people the age Han Li had been, revenge and emotional things were never worthy of their life, if they still lived without offing themselves after 500 years, they had a reason, an ambition, worth living for.

Then it could only be that particular treasure which he wasn't sure when it would pop up. Detached Empty Spaces.

'Mom's instincts…' he pondered.

"So here are the losses and benefits: if I mess up, I might get my identity of an Evolved exposed. If I don't and everything goes well, I can lessen tens of months of cultivation into months or even days if luck had it. And I am more than 60% sure everything will go well."

Yue Li and Li Yao looked at each other nervously while Liyue looked at Han Li's face.

"What will happen if you fail and you are exposed?"

"Not at all worth the risk!" Li Yao said calmly. "You would become the complete attention of the whole world around us. Many will come to kill you."

Han Li remained silent.

That was true.

Being too much of a genius was not good.

18 years of age was too young for an Evolved.

But the reason he was even considering it was one fact.

If the Power Borns aren't included, he had a 100% chance of returning as if nothing ever happened.

"Liyue, so, leave that behind you; I was just speaking to clear my own head. But I will leave you the chance to decide. No matter what, I won't die, so don't worry. Would you mind me going out for a while and returning on a day I promised to be with you? When I return, I will devote all my time exclusively to you for three days. Not even bothering with the other two."

Liyue remained silent.

Even if he said it in a joking way, even Yue Li and Li Yao didn't consider this a joke. At all.

He was doing something dangerous.

Yue Li worriedly asked, frustrated, "Don't you usually do such things without telling us? You even evolved that way. Why the change now?"

Han Li looked at her.

"I wouldn't have told you this, but you see, you have to stay three days somewhere else hidden, and knowing everything is better than not knowing."

Yue Li snorted.

"You are not going anywhere. If you do, I am coming with you, and that's it. You have no reason to risk your life when you have me."

Li Yao and Liyue were startled at the words, wondering about the hidden meaning and blushing slightly. Indeed, he could cultivate faster by bedding them after all; that was enough.

Han Li, on the other hand, thought about the [Luck-Chat System] he hadn't used at all the past few days. If he had the right resources, maybe he could trade it for a detached Empty Space, too. He had never tried that in the past life—so maybe.

'Hmm, so stubborn…' He chuckled inside, 'Maybe I should do it another way. In fact, I had been a bit blinded by greed and…'

His eyes darkened.

It was not his mother's instinct alone that had noticed it.

Something was happening that was making him unsettled deep down. Something that he couldn't quite put his fingers on. There was this gnawing sensation at the depths of his heart that just wouldn't go away after the timeline changed this time.

And there was something hazy about everything, as if he was missing something or as if his head was being led astray by some magical force from the actual truth. Something was going on that he didn't know about.

And that, more than anything, made him want to participate in this fight some way or the other. Only then could he know this thing—that might as well be a wrench in his plans if he let it play as it wanted—clearly.

Until he was sure of what it was and he was under control, his sleep wouldn't be peaceful, no matter what it was.

Sigh…

Even his deals were ignored. They really didn't want him to go.

So, more than the tangible benefits, he had to go fight to understand what the change was in the timeline and what long repercussions it might have.

"Moms, Liyue, this wedding is far from over. We would do it once more, and next time, 10 times more luxuriously—and our First time Liyue, I will make it a memorable one that we will remember even eternities later."

Yue Li got ready to block his path. She was a bit agitated, and she was ready to actually fight. "Xiao Li, listen, stop. I am coming with you."

Han Li chuckled.

He wouldn't have normally let her come, but this time, he had a plan.

"Okay."

"Big Brother," Liyue called out from his lap.

He looked at his wife.

"Can I come too?"

He shook his head.

"Mom would be helpful, but you will be a burden."

Her heart shivered.

She nodded.

Han Li kissed her gently through the net of her veil. "But don't worry, I love you. I really do. In fact, I wouldn't really mind staying behind and hiding with you if it weren't for some doubts I have."

"...You love me?"

Han Li smiled.

"What? You still feel that distance with me you once talked about that I need to spell it out for you?"

Liyue sighed.

"3 days... my first night is ruined... what a bad day... my wedding gift better be grand. Or I might get mad at you. And I think I am better at being mad than Mother Yue."

Han Li awkwardly brooded.

Liyue brushed aside her hair from near her nape and showed it to him, her eyes shining gently.

"And also, give me that hickey thing."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 128 - 128: A little hidden truth Han Li was just told.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Yue Li and Han Li were in a part of the forest that not many came to; they were holding each other's hands and waiting patiently. His mother cheerfully said, "I thought you wouldn't let me come, to be honest."

Smiling to himself, Han Li threw a side eye at her. "Want to go back?"

Yue Li pouted.

"I am finally getting a chance to do something interesting, so don't you dare change the plan mid-way." Her eyes lit up while talking, and she murmured in his ears, "I can pay with my body. A lot. You see, a lot!"

Han Li chuckled and shifted his gaze to the great wall in front of him. This was the wall of the Sothern Lust city. In fact, directly beyond it was the Starlight Zone. At the moment, the Zone was protected by 3 Evolved on all sides, and any suspicious movement was being inspected with scrutiny.

Truthfully, if he and his family did want to go back into the city even while it was in lockdown, it was not impossible. They were not ordinary people in this city; they were the members of the Starlight Clan, and the Rock Clan would let them through after enough bargaining and inspection. No matter what the situation was, they had some basic rights that others wouldn't have in their homeland.

But Han Li liked to keep his family off of a possible crossfire. His wife, her mother, and Hua Feilan had already moved to somewhere where they would be completely safe—they had ridden on a carriage to the west, towards a hidden cave that Han Li knew about. Through a way that most wouldn't choose to go. They were safe.

Even if somebody wanted to go find them, it would be impossible unless they had a map or knowledge of the place he had sent them to, and so far in this timeline, he was the only one who knew of that place. And even if, by some abysmal chance, someone found Hua Feilan and the others, the one protecting them, Hua Feilan herself, was no weakling.

That was the Demon he had seen in the city who would go on to murder the seven-year-old young boy named Hou Jing to gain more power. But the man seemed haggard and near death; he was holding on to his left hand, which seemed as if it was on the verge of being severed.

'What is going on?' Han Li's eyes sparkled, and he pushed his mom towards his chest. She silently looked at him, her heart beating wildly with excitement and nervousness.

His full focus then moved to the outside world.

…

Hou Mang breathed heavily and walked a step at a time, and as he reached the Elephant Stone, he leaned on it, his blood marring the pristine stone surface.

"Gr…" he groaned.

"What a bad time. I should have just moved the other route."

He leaned back and took a small pouch from his side. With a further grimace, he pulled out a yellowish flower from inside it that seemed to lighten his body in a star-like essence.

"What a waste, what a waste," he muttered and looked at the sky. "I hope this heals me entirely."

Inside the rock, Han Li grew contemplative as the man got ready to eat the flower.

'Starlight Flower. A Magical Resource that could heal the wounds of people on a surface level. It might not be able to heal everything, but it can heal simple wounds like that and make him at least a bit functional.'

It was a resource that came under the "Light Great Dao."

He gently closed his mother's ears with his hands, and outside the rock, a dark thing slowly flashed towards the old man. The old man was an Evolved of the Ethereal Path, and his Dao Soul was of the Smoke Dao. According to his understanding, the reason the old man was grooming that young boy was to strengthen his body—as in, it was not strong yet.

His sword could hurt such flimsy bodies as Top Notch Mortal Physiques even without him using his own hands proficiently.

He hoped that was still the case as his Soul Sense covered the old Hou Mang from all sides, ready to stun him with one of his Talents if need be. But as the Life Sword flashed forward, it gently sunk into the old man's flesh, into his heart, and as his eyes widened and he shrieked, Han Li's cover over his mom's ears tightened ever so gently.

A second later, Hou Mang gently fell to the ground, as dead as dead can be.

The sword circled in midair, cutting off the small pouch in Hou Mang's hand, and, once more, pierced into the body. Its pure strength was based on his Soul's power; it could easily carry a weak body to around a meter height.

It took the body and flew away for a while before dropping it under a small hollow tree, and then, it came back to rest, hidden under the Elephant Rock.

Han Li sighed inside.

'There is a lot of blood around; if someone checked, it would be easy to figure out something happened.'

But his goal was not to hide things from others; as long as the one who hurt Hou Mang couldn't even kill Hou Mang entirely, Han Li believed they couldn't do anything to him either. But he wasn't one to move without being cautious.

Now, he finally had a way to realize what was happening.

It also carried the small pouch with it, whereas, a hand moved out the rock to grab the flower.

Han Li took hold of it, and a second later, the entry closed with a dragging sound.

Chuckling, he closed his eyes and said calmly, "You can relax, Mom."

Yue Li looked at him curiously. "What happened?"

Han Li sighed. "A secret."

"I also want to use your sword to see your memories someday, or it will be too unfair, Xiao Li."

Han Li saw that she was no longer so aroused and couldn't help but feel it was a pity. He shook his head and said calmly, "Don't worry too much about it now. You are not capable of doing that either."

"Someday?"

"Sure."

"What now then?" she languidly asked, not knowing that a man had died outside. If she had known, she might have been frightened, or maybe disgusted at her son and probably angry too. Even if the person outside was a Demon, his mom would never approve of his action.

She was too naive.

Han Li caressed the back of her head. "Now, I want to do something for some time. I want to check the memories inside the sword that I just absorbed; wait till then, and we will continue."

Yue Li's eyes brightened.

"You wounded our enemy?"

Han Li sighed and took the sword; as it brightened, his senses slowly shut down, and he entered the land of those memories. Yue Li frowned, feeling her gut wrench slightly.

Just before completely falling into the memories, Han Li muttered, "The person outside was a Demon who planned to kill a seven-year-old boy. And probably, also involved in killing the caravan full of people too."

Yue Li frowned even harder. Feeling her skin crawl slightly.

Han Li never wanted to show her the dark side of the world like he wished to do with Liyue. He wanted to keep his mom who she was unless it was her own decision to change.

But she would have to see some things regardless of whether he wanted or not, and today, her being here was important since he would have to use the Luck-Chat System to keep himself safe, so using it to keep his Mom safe was the same as that.

His Mom's presence made both of them safer rather than her being a burden, especially since he had already gotten something as good as a Light Type Magical Resource this early on to trade with. Not that he had thought of this in his plans; it was just a very good addition. With it, he could get something that would help them escape faster. Even if it would only be for one person.

And this would only pile up to be more advantages if he could gain something more; that was why he had brought her here in the first place, especially with the Elephant Rock as a distraction; he was rather sure of his success.

But in the end, bringing her would also mean showing her a bit of his 'other side.' The part of him that was not the gentle son she knew.

In a faraway city existed a small clan, the clan barely had 20 people living in it. The family of twenty named themselves the Smoke Clan, and they were rather prosperous. Some time ago, exactly one hundred and twenty years from today, a baby named Hou Mang was born there.

Chapter 133 - 133: Hou Mang
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In a faraway city existed a small clan, the clan barely had 20 people living in it. The family of twenty named themselves the Smoke Clan, and they were rather prosperous. Some time ago, exactly one hundred and twenty years from today, a baby named Hou Mang was born there.

The day he was born, Hou Mang's father crowned him as the one who would go on to become the next Clan Head—someone who would one day become a Power Born. All of the clan's resources were to be used on him, and all his brothers and sisters could only look on in jealousy.

Hou Mang was born of their father's favorite concubine, and they could do nothing. But at age ten, he awakened his Spark, and his intelligent father understood a great fault.

This son of his had the worst talent.

His Smoke Spark was barely a baby fist size; he could barely sense a meter around him with his Soul Sense. More than that, Hou Mang was not as intelligent as he expected him to be, either. Unlike the cunning concubine, whom he had come to swoon so much.

But still, Hou Mang wasn't treated badly, and he slowly grew up to be a youth of 18 years of age. In the City, he had a great reputation; to build a clan, someone had to be a Power Born, and his father was a Power Born, even though an ailing one.

No reputation was better than that in his city.

The old man was at the end of his lifespan and would soon die. But even a dead camel was thicker than a horse, and his father wasn't dead. With that reputation, Hou Mang began to grow a bit cocky.

He began to take away young girls from their homes and murder husbands wantonly as he wished to, but his father was a righteous man; he saved the girls from him and made him officially take them under his wing. And he was given a strong warning to never, ever repeat it.

He agreed with a bowed head.

He was sure as long as that cunning fox of a mother was in his back, he could do anything.

Then, he slowly grew up to be more righteous. He killed bandits from around their city and saved the woman they stole. Some women among them became his through 'rightful' ways, and this continued for three years when he lost interest in such things altogether and focused on becoming a successful cultivator instead.

And that was when it happened.

No, his father didn't die.

The Concubine cheated on his father instead with an Evolution Stage and even an extremely ugly cultivator who had hopes of becoming a Power Born, and more than anything, his father caught it red-handed.

One greyish Smoke had covered the whole city, and in the man's wrath, many had died. But for some reason, the man didn't kill him and let him escape.

At age 32, Hou Mang became an Evolved and returned to the city. His father was too weak even for a power Born now, and he himself had let most of his wives and concubines take their leave—to do whatever they wanted.

Hou Mang was disgusted at his father's hypocrisy and went to meet him, but unexpectedly, they ended up having a long conversation about the fate of human life and how fickle it was.

Hou Mang's father then gave him an old scroll filled with random details.

"This scroll contains a method of Body strengthening that is far superior to the ones available around us. You can gain a very strong body that can withstand about ten or twenty full-power attacks of a normal Transformation Type cultivator, but it needs you to have children of your bloodline with similar cultivation to you.

No need for them to Evolve, just Awakening is enough, and a half-assed Awakening at age seven would do the trick."

"When had you entered the demonic path?"

"I never did; that was a mistake; for me, kill as much as you can."

And that was what Hou Mang did.

He went around in cities, killing mortals when he felt like it to complete the demonic technique, and slowly, even without any resources, his body became as strong as Top Tier Mortal Physiques, but after that, it got stuck, and he needed a child.

But he was attacked by a powerful, Righteous woman, and then, even though he escaped, he had to be bedridden for 59 years, and when he was able to walk again due to a miraculous Magical Resource, he was more than 100 years old. But he had no bloodline of his own, so he went around, and a child was born to him, Hou Jing.

A young boy who called him Grandpa.

Seven years later, he was walking with Hou Jing all over the cities, collecting materials he could; slowly but surely, he was reaching his goals, and soon, he was near another city, Southern Lust City. The city was at the southern edge of Sacred Lust Continent.

Some distance away were the rivers and ocean; he was rowing a boat gently while telling Hou Jing some stories, slowly nearing that city, when suddenly, a dark-robed woman appeared on his boat, her face cold and her eyes colder.

"Hou Mang, right?" she asked coldly.

Hou Mang shivered and answered, "Yes."

"My lady says to give this to every demon I come across. If you speak of this to anyone else, the trap worm technique I just planted into you will be triggered, and you will die. Do what you are asked to do, and I will come to remove the Trap Worm." She threw him a letter and, with a swoosh, vanished herself. Shivering still, Hou Mang nodded.

Even without him knowing, a worm had appeared in his heart; he could feel it crawl inside him. Warning him not to do something stupid.

'Was that how strong a Power Born was?' he breathed heavily and looked at the oblivious boy beside him; he hadn't even known something like that happened. 'What Dao Soul does this Power Born have? Invisibility? What is that?'

How did she find me?

At one glance, he knew who that was. 'Starlight Clan Head.'

So the boy stolen must have been that 'Li Shuanwei.'

His heart went cold, and with the boy in hand, he immediately left the city. He sighed when he understood that mere minutes later, the Rock clan locked down the whole city.

"This is the location of a Caravan. Kill everyone in there, and the worm inside you will vanish."

Hou Mang immediately did exactly that, and there, he had to fight with another Evolved, but the person was just a youth with not much experience; he easily fought him, and when another Demon came to help, it became even easier—in the end, they massacred the whole Caravan and left with the Sacred Coins.

They also felt the Worm inside them vanish. They joined an alliance to escape together and ran away.

The last thing that happened was a sharp black sword piercing him from the back.

Inside the Elephant Rock, Han Li opened his eyes, sharp and calm.

They were both different people.

He only knew about it because he had experience dealing with similar opponents.

"Mom, we're going back the way we came. I misunderstood what was going on, and we're about to get into something that would be of no benefit to us."

Yue Li confusedly looked at him. "What happened?"

He looked at her curious face, and sighed as he explained.

Chapter 135 - 135: Move not unless you see an advantage; use not your troops unless there is something to be gained; fight not unless the position is critical.
