The Power Born Woman, whom Han Li knew by the nickname—Demonic Ice Fairy, was going after Starlight Clan Head's Son, Li Shuanwei, for some reason while also stirring up the other two clans using her underlings.

And then, there was also that Evolution Stage woman with the Illusion of Dao Soul, for whom he had special consideration due to how dangerous she was to him when he had to protect his mother. So overall, he was in the middle of a quagmire if he decided to move.

And even if he won, he wouldn't have much substantial benefit to show for it. It would be a wasteful way of putting himself in danger—not to mention his mother. So, there was only one thing to do: get out of this situation and get back with Liyue and Li Yao.

Then, he simply had to wait for the storm to calm down.

As simple as that.

Honestly speaking, one choice was to lay low inside the Elephant Rock for some time and go out when it was all over, but that had a lot of issues: the blood outside for one, and the second, he would be in a very passive situation.

He can't make the Elephant Rock fight without it being midnight, so he would be utterly passive. He chose to run back instead, of course, not in plain sight; that would be stupid.

Yue Li was carried on his back, and he gently got out of the Elephant Rock, his heart calm, his Soul Sense expanded and searched for issues and, without stopping, ran away, as alert as he could be.

His mother looked at the blood on the ground around him and hugged him tightly, but she didn't say anything, and before she knew it, Han Li was running at full speed. The fact that his Soul Sense was entirely hidden helped him have a huge advantage in the area of the senses, so he didn't fear an Evolved finding him.

The only fear he had was a Power Born finding him, in which case, he would have to scheme or show them one of his cardinal secrets.

One tree after the other flickered by, and Yue Li wondered how fast they were going.

As fast as a car?

Suddenly, Han Li came to an abrupt halt, and his face paled slightly.

The ground was shaking slightly.

"Power Borns are fighting around this area," he muttered and turned around resolutely. Without pause, he ran towards the Southern Dragon River.

That was the only place he was sure no one would be fighting at. After healing himself, Hou Mang planned to go there.

Soon enough, he reached the Elephant Rock and ran towards its left, thick with rocks and forestry. Jumping down a meter-long cliff, he asked his mom. "Mom, trade the Light-Type fruit in your hand with Rainbow Leg Fruit."

Yue Li could see he was serious and didn't waste time. She turned on her [Luck-Chat System] and wished for a Rainbow Leg Fruit in trade with the Starlight Flower in her hands.

The resource in her hand vanished, but Han Li didn't stop and ran towards the river non-stop. He didn't even bother with hiding his tracks anymore; he knew once he reached the river, he had a hundred escape routes, whatever that may happen.

Just like that, minutes passed, and suddenly, he smelled a faint scent of blood. His expanded Soul sense blasted towards the source, and he halted his steps.

"What is it?" Yue Li asked.

"Don't expand your Soul sense. They can sense yours but not mine." Han Li warned while his Soul Sense checked around what he found with scrutiny.

It was a caravan turned upside down, as tens of corpses lay strewn around it. Dead horses, dead men, dead women. But what he found next made his heart clench. Stuck to the slowly rotating caravan wheels was an inconspicuous fruit, almost crushed but not at all damaged.

He changed his route and ran towards it while saying calmly, "If you love me, Mom, close your eyes."

"What is there?" Yue Li asked, her face paling. "Another died? But you were always with me; how can you kill when…"

She stopped while Han Li looked at her briefly.

He could see she was traumatized by him killing someone, and that was still in her thoughts.

He knew it would be.

It was her first encounter with death, after all. Naturally, it will stick to her like gum.

"Mom, just listen, okay?" the dark night made his red eyes rather scary instead of the gentleness she was used to seeing in them. "We are now in the middle of a quagmire, a shithole.

If we just as much as touch it, it will start to stink, and the next thing we know, we will be in one hell of a lot of trouble, so just know that you can convert me into a good samaritan back when we're home, for now, stay calm and listen to me till we are safe."

"Xiao Li…"

Han Li sighed. "Close your eyes; I will do the rest."

She clenched her teeth and hid her face into his back, closing her eyes.

Han Li walked into the middle of the caravan, full of fleas and a disgusting smell, and gently picked up the inconspicuous fruit near the caravan wheels. He wondered whether it was here before his mom used the System.

Or did it just… pop up?

Then it would have been better to pop up in Yue Li's hand, so it must have been here. Looking at its position, it was just in place to get crushed and was just lucky to have survived by a hair's width.

Immediately after, he shook his head and ran away from there, back towards the river area. He would care about it some other day. For now, he had to get safe first before anything.

Handing his mother the fruit, he said while on the run, "If I end up having to fight, use this and get to a safe distance; eating it will temporarily make your speed even faster than me. Don't worry about me. I have myself to rely on."

In his original plans, he would go to another area where he knew he would probably find another Light Type Resource, but there was no need now; now, it was best to run straightforwardly.

His mom nodded silently, but he could feel her tears streaming down his neck. "Xiao Li, it stunk there," she muttered.

Han Li ran faster, silent.

Chapter 136 - 136: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.

Yue Li looked at it with relief while Han Li calmly looked around his surroundings.

"As I expected, nobody is here," Han Li said, his red eyes glinting mysteriously. His voice was calm as he looked at the flowing water that stretched far into the distance. It was the Southern Dragon River, a river that had no ruler but a lot of resources.

Calmly, he walked into the river, the water climbing up to his thighs in no time. "Xiao Li, what are you doing?"

Han Li smiled.

"Underneath this river, there is a cavern; if we enter it, we can walk through that, and no one will find us. Just hold on to me and hold your breath."

Yue Li nodded and took a deep breath, puffing her cheeks full.

Han Li expanded his Soul Sense into the water and, with a smile, walked deeper into it. Soon enough, his head was underwater, and they were holding their breaths. Both of them had rather powerful Endurance; Yue Li had eaten the Fruit that made it better, while Han Li's body was superior all around.

He swam deeper and deeper, his hands moving like an experienced swimmer while Yue Li held on to him for dear life. Her swimming was far below par, so her lifeline was Han Li under the heavy water of the river.

Even Han Li was cautious inside the river. He had to be.

He was not a Power Born who could fly; if something held him back long enough, he would die, and his mother would die even sooner. But he was confident in his experiences.

While on the surface, a bloody, rusty sword gently jumped into the water along with them. On top, a man sat in the lotus position, his decapitated head calmly held in his hand.

That was the person who had injured Hou Mang. He had been waiting here to ambush Hou Mang, but of course, he could do that in the underworld now.

Deep in the water, Han Li felt his sword sinking towards him and smirked while swimming faster to the center of the river. By now, he was deep in the river, and his cold red eyes found what he was looking for.

A dark abyssal entrance tucked between two obsidian underwater rocks. It was a long distance away, and he could feel his mother growing restless behind him. With a simple move, he snatched the rainbow fruit and quickly devoured it along with some water.

His speed increased quite a bit as if his weight had lessened, but his strength had increased. He moved swiftly, and as his mom was growing even more restless, he entered the cave.

The dark rocks surrounded him in darkness, and even his Evolved eyes found it difficult to pierce, but immediately after, he and his mom surfaced their head out of water in an underground cavern, shortly followed by a dark sword.

Han Li's chuckles echoed while his mom breathed heavily.

"Scary," she said, leaning on him while water dripped from her wet hair.

"Don't worry, I know how deep this river goes, and even if you did grow breathless, nothing a kiss won't heal."

"I would not dare put you in danger. I was not sure of escaping."

"…what if we met the villainous Power Born, that woman Li Ming talked about."

"If that happened," Han Li's eyes flickered. "I would have had to tell her about some secrets. I am sure she is a curious woman, so it won't be that hard to keep her engaged for a while, but it would still have been rather worrisome. So you wanna know what I did?"

"What?"

Han Li showed her the Life Sword.

On its dark surface was a small note written in discernible letters. `Follow this sword to find the Power Born Demonic Ice Fairy.`

"I would stall her while this sword would alert the other Power Borns without her knowledge. They will come for rescue, and she won't even know we were the culprits so that she won't release her anger on us; she wouldn't want to lose her box of secrets to unveil after all. An Evolved at the age of eighteen is far too interesting. So she would protect us if it came to it.

And just in case she planned to threaten me with you, I will tell her you have a bigger secret than me. She would be stalled. In the middle of the chaos, we will escape and let loose the Elephant Rock, then escape to somewhere they can't find us."

Yue Li sighed. She was leaning on him as he floated atop the water, and she couldn't help but think, for the first time in her life—her son had grown up.

For some reason, that realization was rather painful. Especially because he had a lot of secrets.

"But these schemes have a lot of loopholes, Mom. Improvisation was the key, and I am sure I would have been able to, so don't rely on selling your secrets; it is a cheap last resort I like to use that is rather effective once used well," he said and swam towards a rock.

"Xiao Li," she made him pause his words. "Let's talk when we're safe."

Chapter 137 - 137: Han Li attacks in the dark (R18*)

Here, both of them could see nothing of each other even with their great senses unless, of course, they released their Soul Sense. But it was in such times that the true face of a soul sense was revealed, even that relied on the eyes to provide color.

They could understand what was going on with the soul sense expanded, but in a dark where the eyes couldn't distinguish this from that, it was normal they only felt as if shadows were moving around, even Yue Li was just a bundle of warmth in his senses. "We will soon be in a completely different world; after we pass this red mist-like thing, we will be still flying for some time, but then… we will enter there. And after that, we will have to be extremely cautious. Everyone we meet will be our enemies."

"There?" her voice was laced with worry.

"The Empty Space of the Sacred Demon of Lust, we will enter it."

This time, her heart thumped. "That means?"

"Yes, we are up for an adventure that you wished so much."

Just as they were about to continue speaking, the red light suddenly vanished as if they had surpassed a cloud layer. And once above, Yue Li's eyes widened as she looked at the red clouds under her and the blue expanse above. But Han Li's gaze coldly moved around.

Far, far in the distance, he could see a small dot flying up just like himself. The one person in this world who had Sacred Talent and was the closest to himself, and in that direction—Yang Wei.

His gaze shifted to another angle, and there, in the distance, he could see small black dots, too. But in his vision, that was all the Sacred Talents. But he was sure that at least tens of thousands existed with this level of talent across the entire continent—and all of them would fly up today, rising to the heavens.

"When people die, Mom," he said calmly, "they leave behind their Empty Spaces and Dao Souls just like they leave behind their bodies to rot. The Dao Soul floats up to the sky and according to the legends, become stars. Empty Spaces either disintegrate; transformed into Energy that joins back with the Energy of the World.

Or they become Detached Empty Spaces, which are unique resources that other people could use."

Yue Li nodded, hesitant. Unknown emotions were roiling inside her chest.

"But that is for us normal people at lower cultivation stages. As for the Sacred Demons, their Empty Space in itself are different; none of them disintegrated, all of them are Detached Empty Spaces, and all of them float above the Demon's respective continent. Above Sacred Lust Continent floats the Empty Space of the Sacred Lust Demon."

"So we will… enter it today? That Dark place?"

Han Li nodded. "But not in a way you might expect; soon, we will be pulled into it. Even now, we feel static, but we are flying at a speed we simply can't even comprehend; you only have to know this much for now. I will tell you the rest when we reach there.

***

Yang Wei looked at the distant black dots in the sky's expanse, a furrow on his face while his white robes were covered in blood. "How peculiar, huh, to think Han Li's city had two Sacred Talents, amusing indeed."

A voice inside him spoke. "This is no simple matter, Yang Wei. This is the Heaven Rising!"

"Yeah, Yeah, shut the trap." He laughed, his dead fish gaze focusing on the skies. "What will we gain from this? I want to know that. All else is just air."

"Not what you have to gain," the voice of the Spirit hissed, "What you have to lose is more important. Sacred Talents will be here, all the way from new borns to Power Borns, and all of them creme of the crop. And it will be a trial, and the chance you will die is too much."

"What are you telling me to do then?" He laughed, uncaring. "If I die, I die, but if I die, I will first kill too."

"You should allie with Han Li or people like him. I have been having this bad feeling for a while now, and this seemed to be the crescendo of the feeling. What happens here might as well determine the future of this continent."

"You haven't told me what we stand to gain." Was the cold reply.

"Plenty. Magical Resources, Knowledge, and most of all…" she hesitated. "You can see the true essence of cultivation and understand what makes a Sacred Demon, a Sacred Demon."

Yang Wei was about to question the statement when his head grew hazy, and his consciousness faltered; a second later, he was unconscious. He couldn't even last long to realize.

***

A hazy feeling assaulted Han Li's psyche, but he activated his Talent Seduction on himself multiple times, and his body warmed up to an extreme. But that was far from enough to endure the sleepiness, so he gritted his teeth, and his skin vibrated all over.

It was hard to stay awake, but with all his strength and resolve, he held back from falling asleep; he even used some other Magic Powers that he had never used before. Especially the Magic Power—Sleepless Lust that can remove sleep to some degree. Luckily, his will was iron strong after the struggles he had gone through in his previous life.

His Mother, on the other hand, was already unconscious, so he held her strongly against his chest as her head fell to his back. And just as his eyes grew blurry, he saw it, a red light blinking in the sky like a distant star.

It brightened shortly, completely overwhelming his senses with a red hue, and a second later, Han Li, along with all the Sacred Talents under the Transcendent level in the Continent, vanished from the sky.

Into the "mouth" of a Demon, as some old guy mockingly commented.

But then, a silence ensued.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 142 - 142: What the... F*ck? Was I that much of a fool in my past life?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The sound of the rolling waves rhythmically hitting the shore filled the air, accompanied by the distant calls of seagulls. As the sun began its descent, painting the sky with hues of red and orange, the beach was transformed into a canvas of serenity. Yet, stranded on the shore were a young man and a youthful woman—Yue Li and Han Li.

A dark sword laid just beside them.

Both of their bodies were gripped by the sea foam and then released right after as the wave tried to climb higher and higher. But both of them were out cold. A black seagull-looking bird gently landed atop Han Li's body and was just about to peck at his cheek when its feathers suddenly stood up and looked towards the sea as if about to flee.

It wanted to fly away, but it was too late.

A hand wrapped around its neck and squeezed, and before it could even squeak, its whole neck exploded into bits and pieces. Two cold red eyes opened and then looked at the sea—something shivered inside the water, and Han Li could feel it running away.

Shrugging and sitting up coldly, he looked at the dead bird in his hand. Then it moved to his mother; she was also completely bloody. "What a good start," he coldly said and stood up in a single jump.

Taking his mother on his back gently, he moved towards the sea and used the water to clean both of them. Their clothes were already wet, and it would be troublesome if his mother caught something like a cold or something similar—even though Awakened rarely was susceptible to such things.

After cleaning themselves, he looked around and soon found a forest some distance away; without any wait, he ran closer and, soon enough, vanished into the woods.

Even though he had managed to stay awake while on the flight, and even when the red light completely overwhelmed him, he had fallen unconscious right after entering the world. But just because he had not fallen asleep when the red light overwhelmed him, he and his mother were now together. Or they would have both been teleported separately.

He had just woken up some seconds ago and was waiting for the thing in the water to jump at him, but the Black Seagull completely ruined it. And in a rush, he crushed it before it flew away, which made both him and his mom bloody.

That was a mistake.

Running through the forest full of vibrant greenery and life, Han Li was focused to a point he had never been since his rebirth, and soon enough, he ran into something. His soul sense focused on that place, and he coldly shifted his route.

In three seconds, he was standing in front of an unconscious young-looking man.

[Yang Jue Ying] [Age: 97]

[Current Cultivation: Power Born]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Power Born, Great Dao of Life]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Power Born, Great Dao of Life.]

[Death: (in the last premonition) at age 116, died in the Transcendence War in an attempt to steal the Transcendance Orb.]

Han Li gently touched the man's skin, and understanding his sword was sharp enough to cut him, ran away from there. The dark sword flew behind him and, as it moved past, cut the man's head off. Clean and in one slice.

Running back to the place, Han Li coldly collected the blue martial robes of the man, got into it, and then threw the body aside. He didn't even get any loot.

With his mother unconscious behind him, Han Li did this three more times. Two Evolved Cultivators and another Awakened Cultivator. He had no mercy, nor a warrior's shame. Later, they would be his enemies—none of them knew what awaited here, but he did.

And because he had worked hard to stay awake then, he was awake now.

As for his promise to his mother?

This place had no place for the weak-hearted. If he followed that promise, by the end of this, there wouldn't be a Han Li or Yue Li; they would both die 100%.

Having found nothing else, Han Li found a safe place with a wide view and settled there for a while, he had to clear his thoughts. After getting seated, he first undressed his mother; she was wearing wet clothes, and he had to change them.

He made her wear the clothes that he had gotten after killing the Awakened old woman, the clothes were shabby and old, white servant clothes, but better than the wet clothes.

Then, he laid her head on his lap and let her sleep for now.

For the next 30 minutes, no one would wake up. But going around killing anymore wouldn't be wise; thirty minutes weren't much, and if he ended up in front of a startled Power Born, he was done for. Even Evolved here wouldn't be Evolved from the Southern Lust City; each and every one would be dangerous. In one way or the other.

He closed his eyes and began meditating. But his senses were alert, and he didn't plan to stay here for more than 15 minutes.

…

First of all, he had to think about his future knowledge regarding this situation.

Heaven Rising.

It was a major event in the future with continental repercussions.

In his past life, 15 years from today, the Transcendental War would commence, and almost a decade later, it would end. And then, a revival period would begin, with many visits from other continents and so on, and so forth.

Nothing major would happen after that, until 45 years from today, a red veil blankets the whole world. Lives started to rise to the heavens.

Not many traveled to return; most of them who entered ended up dead, and those who didn't die became great men or women who were all feared for their power.

Han Li looked up at the setting sun, the sky, the land, the foliage of greenery, and even the black mud under his lap. He couldn't help but fall silent.

This was an Empty Space.

He was inside the Empty Space of the Sacred Lust Demon.

There was no darkness here, no Sparks in sight, no Dao Souls, No Dao Crystals. This here was a world unto its own. Something beyond his understanding. And inside it, he was just like an ant, nothing more than that.

He breathed out as his mind delved into other thoughts.

According to his future knowledge, the Heaven Rising happened because before the Sacred Demon died, he had set up some kind of mechanism inside this place to make the Red Veil fall down exactly at that time. But today, Han Li could understand there was no way that was true.

There must have been a mechanism, but surely not the kind that was automatically set to activate in time, but something that was supposed to be triggered. The question was, how?

Or maybe… who?

He gently caressed his mother's hair, and his heart started to beat faster and faster. It was in such moments that he was reminded of his significance, of how worthless he himself was—he didn't know everything.

Returning back to time didn't mean he was now the most important person in this world, nor did it mean he was the main character of some story, as someone great once said, everyone was a main character, just like how everyone was a side character.

And in the triggering of all these great events, he wasn't the major character.

A cold shine simmered under his red gaze.

He smiled calmly. As long as he got the greatest benefits here this time, it didn't matter who the one to trigger it all was.

'According to the future,' he contemplated, 'there would be hundreds of Life Type treasures in this world, and all of them are quite easy to get. What I have to do first is get some of them before the actual trials begin.'

This world had some trials set up by the Sacred Demon that the people could complete to gain certain rewards, like Magical Treasures, Cooking methods to create unique Magical Treasures using materials, and much more.

But the Trials would only begin 24 hours after everyone wakes up, which means the next sunset. After that, Power Borns can't kill Evolved, while Evolved can't kill the Awakened, but until then, chaos will prevail.

'One step at a time,' he thought calmly. 'What I want from here is information on why all this is happening suddenly, Magic Resources, and then win the Trials to get the best I can."

Without waiting a second, he smiled, and feeling the eagerness of his Dao Soul, he began to cultivate. But a second later, opened his eyes in shock.

He had barely cultivated for around 10 minutes, and a Dao Crystal had already formed inside. And more were forming even faster. And there waas zero strain on his Dao Soul too.

Immediately, he understood why after using his Soul Sense.

Spread all around this world were not chaotic and unusable energies that the Spark or Dao Soul inside him refines into usable energy. The energy spread around here; he knew what it was—Energy solely meant to be used by those who cultivate under the Life Dao.

"Sacred Demon…" he mumbled in shock, "Of course, his Empty Space would have energy that is comfortable for me to absorb…"

That also meant that all the Life Dao cultivators inside this 'Demon World' would experience explosive growth. His heart thudded inside his chest, and he ran away with his mother on his back.

This was news to him, but it was no wonder. 45 years later, when the Heaven Rising happened in his past life, he was a heavily depressed man; at that point, it was just 15 years after his mother's death, and there was also the death of his wife Ruo.

So, with a heart that had barely any desire other than a faint hope of achieving some ungodly level someday and reviving his mother and wife, he didn't care much when he rose to this place, and that time, the only thing he did was drown in Magical Alcohol and wait till the trials began. Only now that he experienced this today did he understand how much of a great fool his younger self was.

He was borderline ashamed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 143 - 143: Sorry for killing you.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A heavy waterfall, almost akin to the Niagra Falls on Earth, was the first sight that Yue Li witnessed as her eyelashes fluttered open. Then, as the panic started to rise, she felt herself leaning against her son's chest and immediately breathed out a sigh of relief.

But once more, her gaze shifted to the great waterfall and panic once more rose up in her guts. She also realized that they were now high up, on top of a large tree, to be exact. It was so tall, looking up, she felt as if the clouds were not that distant.

She felt Han Li gently kissing her nape and turned back to him, her eyes bright.

"Where are we?" she asked.

"Inside the Sacred Demon's empty space," Han Li replied, a slight smile on his face.

Yue Li looked towards the sky, the sun was still barely visible, but it was already nearing dark.

"So realistic, Xiao Li, it's almost like the real world," she said in awe, full of reverence. "Will your Empty Space become like this too?"

Han Li looked at her curiously, not interrupting her amazement. With a smile, he said, "I don't know, maybe it might. But my theory is that all Sacred Stage Cultivators have Empty Spaces similar to this, a World of its own inside them. Maybe that is their source of god-like strength."

His mother nodded enthusiastically and decided. "When your Empty Space becomes like this, we will set up a mansion in the middle of the world, and then, I don't know… we will do something interesting."

Han Li couldn't help but smile. How cute.

"After I get it, sure."

She squinted at him. "So tell me, what will we do in this world? Explore? Have sex? You were so stiff when we were flying. But you seem to be in a better mood now.

And why are we on top of this tree? Also, how long was I sleeping?"

"In this world…" Han Li grew serious, "we survive."

Her words got caught on her throat and she looked at his red eyes with a frown. It was also then that she noticed her son was wearing something she had no recollection of buying for him or him buying. He was not in those clothes when they came here too.

"Where did you get those clothes?"

"I told you when we were flying here, until we get out, no questions, and you will obey all my words," he replied calmly, looking far west, on the opposite side of the waterfall.

Yue Li looked at him with a disgruntled expression, even a bit pale. "I am not your slave, bastard. Tell me, did you kill once more?"

Han Li looked back at her. "Look west, I mean, there." He pointed.

She looked west, and Han Li said calmly. "There is a Power Born there, and there, inside the cave behind that great waterfall, a Power Born also flew into it a second ago."

"So what?" she asked.

"So, my lovely mom, tomorrow, they will come to kill us. In about 24 hours from now, a Trial will begin, but before that, there will be many fights, and today, at least thousands will die for no reason. I killed, yes, I killed. But due to that, I might not have to see you die, understood?"

Yue Li took a deep breath and, deep down, felt a coldness take hold of her heart. She involuntarily slapped Han Li. He looked at her calmly.

"How many did you kill?" she asked.

"Three today."

"Do you know? They might have a mother like me waiting for them to return home, a family that is reliant on them to survive. How…" she gritted her teeth gently, "could you break our promises so coldly."

Han Li didn't speak; he was far beyond such simple guilt. And as his mother started to tear up and sob, he could only comfort her silently. He knew this would happen. That was why he had climbed up the tree even when there was a chance he would find a precious Life Type resource if he took the chance. But of course, coming here had its use—they were rather safe up here.

It took about half an hour for his mother to calm down even a bit, and even then, her face was still very pale. "…Xiao Li, you killing them is the same as me killing them. I failed to teach you, *sob* I feel sick…"

After that, she fell onto his chest, crying, making Han Li feel slightly guilty for the first time. Not because he killed, but because he made her sob. But she would get through it, he knew.

"Me killing is just that, Mom," he said, "Nothing more, nothing less. You have no part in this."

She shook her head. "I always remembered it when I read fantasy novels on Earth: all of the main characters slowly become like you. Black-hearted and callous, only caring about their own benefit… our world is different from those, but it has its similarities and... I don't want you to be like them! Even Liyue would hate you if she knew this."

"Do you hate me?" he asked.

She looked at him coldly and slapped him once more. She was angry as much as she was sad. Han Li understood that just then.

"I… know, Xiao Li," she said, biting her lips. "I always knew we would have to kill one day; that was always how it had been in those books, and they were all written by people much smarter than me. So, I always knew this would happen. I am more affected that you don't feel sad than even killing, and I feel horrible for saying that, too."

"What about this?" Han Li asked, looking at her calmly. "I will kill anyone I feel threatened by inside this World, and after this event, both of us go to some temple and become monks for a while. Does that help?"

A long silence followed. He looked at her intently, too; he knew her morals were different from just simple things like obeying the laws. Anyone who based their morality on legality was an idiot, and even though his mom was a fool, she was foolish enough not to bother with the rules.

Her morals were simple; she wished to live happily, but in that, she never wished to hurt anybody. It was not that she hadn't taught him her morals well; it wasn't that she failed as a mother; it was just that he had seen the world for what it had to offer.

He didn't mind being a monk, even the most righteous man alive, if that would help him achieve his goal of eternal life and gain control over life and death. But he knew the easier path was being a Demon—and he felt no regret in being a Demon.

Other than hurting her, that is.

It wouldn't matter who he killed or who he saved after he achieved that level after all; then it would be easy for him to change anything. Heck, there might be people out there who could already do that; from a small speck, he had to become a giant; that was the only way.

He loved his mom. He loved her really much. He hated seeing a wound on her fair skin; he didn't like seeing tears on that fair face, and he didn't like how her voice broke.

But he knew.

'No one saves us but ourselves. No one can, and no one may. We ourselves must walk the path.'

"So here is the deal, Mom, I will not kill unless they threaten me, and in return, you accept all that I say. Once outside, I will do what you say."

Yue Li saw the plea in his face, and her own gaze lowered.

"…You will also help, not half-heartedly, but with full heart, every family that you hurt by killing them. If you lose money or even pride, let it be."

"Okay."

"Do you remember the names of the people you killed? Were they unknown?"

"I know who they were."

"Okay, we will both bear this sin together. Remember Xiao Li, now it is also my goal to make you have the power to revive people."

Han Li looked at her, and his heart skipped a beat. Her eyes were sparkly with tears, but her words didn't stutter.

"I had always wished to be a part of that great ambition of yours, so now, it will have one more meaning." She closed her eyes, settling her heart. "You will one day become the controller of Life and Death and will revive each and every one you killed. And I will apologize to them for the mistakes my son made."

This was the difference between a person with a Heart of Cultivation and one without; she could keep calm in confusion; if it was Liyue, she would be jittery now, but not Yue Li. She could atleast process the information and think.

She had at first seen his ambitions as a joke, but she wasn't his mother for no reason. She had seen him train like a madman, and if that was a joke, she didn't know what wasn't.

"You will… apologize to the people I killed?" he asked, amused.

"You will, too. And then only will I consider you a great son. So work hard."

Han Li chuckled with a rather amused expression and gently cleared her tears using his sleeves.

"That seems interesting. We will make Liyue apologize too. It was her husband who killed them after all."

"Then, you might as well include Li Yao, too. She is your mistress? Is that the word? Or Mother-in-law, I guess…"

"Then I have to make Ruo apologize, too."

"…Ruo?" Yue Li frowned, a displeased expression immediately plastering itself on her face. "Who is this wench I don't know about?"

"I haven't seen her yet, either, but she would be in this Demon World. You won't call her a wench once you see her; believe me, she is gorgeous."

But he was a bit worried about her situation in this world. It could totally end up dangerous.

"Mom, it's time to find a Life-Type fruit and trade it. Then we go find our Little Ruo. It would still take hours for Power Borns to begin their rampage. We have to find her before that."

But he had some confidence she wouldn't be in danger at this point—or he wouldn't have wasted time sweet-talking his Mom, and there was a reason for that confidence. ill-placed as it was.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 144 - 144: Legs pause, heart stutter, mind pause.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Exactly 33 minutes after climbing down the tree and running around in search of Life-Type Magical resources, Han Li caught the whiff of something pungent, and his nose felt a stinging sensation. On his back, his mother also frowned.

Without any hesitation, Han Li shifted direction towards the scent, and just as he reached closer, he heard the sound of a fight ongoing. With his expanded Soul Sense, he saw that the fight was just between two Awakened youths, both older than him.

He almost flourished his sword but held back. They were no threat to him.

With a cold gaze, he decided to use Magic Powers.

At the moment, he solely had 33 Dao Crystals inside him, and one of them vanished.

Magic Power—[The Elusive Movement.]

The Power of the Lust Dao induced lust in the two people and made them dizzy; the only thing they felt was that Han Li was too fast for their gaze to focus on him, whereas it was just that their minds were being messed with. A second later, when they noticed the egg-shaped flower that they were fighting for had vanished.

Some distance away, Han Li and Yue Li stopped, and he heaved a sigh of relief. "Something so goddamn rare in the outside world that Power Borns would bite each other's bones to get it, a Life-Type Magical Resource, and puny Awakened are fighting for it here."

"What is this Resource?"

"Life Type Resource The Egg Flower. It is a resource that, if you use it once, will make your youthful face remain youthful till the day you die. Even if you only have one day of Lifespan remaining, you will look just as young as you look today. Just as beautiful."

Yue Li blushed meaningfully. "So what do I trade this for?"

"The Brain Teaser Vine. Trade for it."

Yue Li nodded.

He passed her the Egg Shaped Flower, and as she took it, she opened her [Luck-Chat System]. Clicking on "Luck Trade," she began to wish.

'I wish I get a Brain Teaser Vine, I wish I get a Brain Teaser Vine…'

The Egg Flower vanished from her hand like colorless smoke, and she looked at her son curiously. He seemed to be pondering for a second before he looked down with a sigh.

Below him were a lot of dried leaves, and under these dried leaves was a small, worm-like vine, the same as a palm that had fallen off of the tree just beside him. With a deep breath, he took it.

"What does it do, Xiao Li?" she asked.

Han Li calmly took the vine and placed it on his forehead.

"It is a very unique resource among all Life-Type resources. It can help the user find the living being they are searching for, wherever it is. As long as they are in one continent, it will work. Just one condition: you must know everything about whom you are searching for, their body proportion, moles, and everything.

If they do know it, it will search for that person, beast, or whatever you are looking for and show you where they are as instinct."

"Hmm, so you know everything about this Ruo woman even if you haven't seen her? That is… odd. Are you messing with me, you bastard?"

Han Li smiled.

The Brain Teasing Vine entered his skull through his pores, and his head started to pound in pain, but he calmly stood and thought about his Ruo's body. He was honestly worried about this part the most. He knew how she would look about three to four years later, but at this moment, he didn't know. He hadn't met her at this point in his life.

But he thought about everything he knew about her.

Brown eyes, always curious, with white hair that always fell to his mouth when she jumped to kiss him. But at this point, she should be in the worst parts of her life. He smiled to himself. Due to this event, he could save her earlier. It was better.

She had a mole under her chin, one on her spine, another on her butt, one on her nape, and then there was one under her legs. She had this scar inside her mouth that was harder to find unless you had spent years with her. It was a scar left behind after being forced to chew glass.

Just as his image started to grow more and more vibrant and the fading memories of that girl came to life, a thread seemed to pull him somewhere.

With a heart full of turmoil hidden under a veil of calm confidence, he ran so fast he was close to becoming a blur on his own, just not nearly at that level yet. Nevertheless, he didn't care. That thread was farther away but nearer than it had ever been.

***

A young girl was crouched in a shadowy hole; her eyes were dazed, and her mind was hazy. Suddenly, some kind of pull made her look to her right, but it was the same as earlier. Nobody was near now.

Nobody had come to beat Ruo yet.

She felt her heartbeat calm down.

Regardless, she hid deeper just in case, hiding herself in a place that stank the most always took them the most time to find her, so she was exactly in such a place.

But what was this… unknown warm feeling on her forehead?

As if someone was patting it gently.

***

Han Li's heart beat faster and faster, and he became a little reckless in his run. Usually, he would have checked tracks and anything for that matter to know of possible dangers ahead of time, but his heart was way too uncalm. Nevertheless, as a cold blade suddenly came just in his tracks, an inch away from his face, he didn't even flinch.

A jump.

Everything slowed down.

With his mother behind his back, he rotated just enough to see the man's face and name before a dark fell vertically on his head, piercing straight through him like tofu, splitting him into two clean pieces.

Time moved at its usual pace again, and he landed on his feet, without stopping, he ran as fast as he had been running. His gaze might waver, but the Soul Sense that could see everything without being seen wasn't something any Evolved could take on.

The moment that The Ethereal Cultivator attempted a close-range attack on him, he was dead. There was no other option there for him. Even if he was a Sacred Talent, that didn't mean he could underestimate others.

He must have been dominating the place where he came from, so it was natural he was arrogant, but Han Li didn't care. In fact, if his Mom wasn't biting him on his nape and trembling in fear on his back as he ran, he would be laughing demonically, too.

"It's close, Mo—"

Suddenly, he came to an abrupt halt, and his foot slid on the dry leaves. His cold gaze looked at one man in front of him, and his heart grew cold and ruthless but calm.

The man looked back at him with a smile, a 2-meter saber with a skull handle dripped with blood on his side. His pale eyes looked at Han Li with a smile that was not a smile.

"Han Li."

"Yang Wei."

They muttered.

Then, with smirks, both laughed out, laughter that resounded through the forest like two blades clinging together and ran away in different directions without a single word. Han Li towards his Ruo, Yang Wei, to wherever he planned to go.

"That," Han Li said, "is an Enemy you have to love."

His Mom wondered what he meant.

While Han Li, jumping across a boulder, looked westward.

There she was, just around 200 meters more.

He ran.

The next intrusion was a bald woman with yellow eyes.

Magic Power—Elusive Movement.

A blur later, Han Li and Yue Li were past her.

She turned back with calm, cold eyes.

A sword flashed past.

***

"I wonder why these Ethereals are so confident," a calm youth sighed to himself.

"Are you going to attack him?" An old man asked, standing beside him with a cane and a calm smile.

"Hmm? No? Who wants to stand infront of a charging bull? He seems like trouble."

"Then why are you aiming your bow at him?"

"I don't know, I feel like he is watching me, and it is irritating."

"How scary. I would've tried to steal that sword, though, seems a bit interesting."

"He is stronger than you Old Man, you should know that yourself."

A bone blade sank into a tree beside them and both of them turned to look at a pale eyed Young Man. "And he is stronger than you, too."

The sharp eyes of The Bowman turned to Yang Wei.

"But he is not as strong as you."

Yang Wei smiled.

"Who knows? But hear me out. Want to team up?"

***

Han Li stopped infront of a stinking swamp.

His gaze wavered slightly, but he looked under a boulder, there she was, closing both of her ears tightly, covered in swamp mud to avoid someone's sight at first glance in a place stinky enough to make the woman of the city frown at just the mention of it.

This was why he was so sure she wouldn't be in trouble.

She was the sort to hide like this in fear when he met her too.

And the place hadn't descended to killing hidden people as of yet.

Moreover, even if it did, she wasn't hidden in a place people would want to visit at the start.

His heart almost melted as he gently walked closer.

Yue Li looked around for a while, and only when she used her Soul Sense did she finally find who she was looking for. Shriveled to a corner, slightly trembling.

"Like a stray cat…" she murmured and bit her lips. "And she is just a kid. Are kids here in this Demon World, too? Will the kids also die inside here?"

Han Li didn't speak anything and came beside the little girl.

Tears unconsciously streaked down his cheeks.

"…No wicked thoughts, Xiao Li, she is just a child whatever you say," Yue Li softly said from his back, her heart feeling happy at his emotional face even though she didn't know why he was so emotional.

Han Li wiped away the tears in his face with his sleeves.

"I won't feel such things now even if I want to, Mom, but my hand is currently full of blood, and it's too cold. Go, carry her. Let's go somewhere else and wash her up. It is full of wolves out here."

Yue Li gulped and, pushing away all her emotions, did just what he told her.

A black sword flourished beside Han Li, and he sighed.

It was a bit reckless to reveal himself and his treasures when Power Borns were around, but he rarely ever did anything without a plan.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 145 - 145: Calm before the storm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and Yue Li ran once more, this time, he didn't carry them on his back but instead walked behind them. He often changed his position just in case some other crazy Cultivators planned to attack him.

Demons were sane cultivators, but for them, killing people for no reason when they find themselves in unknown circumstances was an ordinary thing.

Wasn't it logical?

If not, it was very logical for them.

To kill for fun or just for a better sense of security.

Not all of them here would be sane, especially if they had any knowledge of where they were and what might await them. That also went for if people had found any Life-Type Magical Resources already.

If anyone had found it and the news was out, it would only get more rowdy, especially because of a simple fact that Han Li was all too aware of. Sacred Talent cultivators were arrogant almost all the time.

From their earliest ages to this very day, they probably didn't see any other Sacred Talent Cultivator or anyone who could actually put them in their place at their own level. And when they see someone running at them or someone they perceive as "Interesting," they would attack.

He knew how far such idiocy went.

Or there could also be the other type, the type who would lust after his mother. Or even… Ruo. She was a child barely at the age of 13 at the moment, but not all of the people here would see her the same as he did.

Especially Demons. They would, at most, see her as a beautiful tool at best.

And in a place out of sight of forces like the Demon Restraining Temple who would list them as wanted Demons across the continent once they go against the law, it would become all too clear, very soon, how far even the 'righteous' men could go.

Han Li didn't speak on the way while Yue Li held Ruo safely in her grasp and walked forward. No matter what was happening, she felt secure as long as her son followed them.

Even though she felt as if corpses were pulling her leg from the ground, she pushed that feeling away for now. Her conscious felt torn between her son and morality, but her son had way too much of an advantage when it came to her mind.

"There is a stream near us, to your left, walk to the left," came his transmission straight to her mind, and she walked to her left without a word.

They soon found themselves near a stream.

Han Li's gaze briefly examined the surroundings.

It was a small stream surrounded by some meter-tall buffs; Han Li sighed to himself as he told his mom to go and wash Ruo clean. She looked at him for a second and went down to wash Ruo.

At the same time, Han Li turned his focus to his surroundings.

His gaze was deep red; it roamed left and right while his Soul Sense covered the whole area in a 10-meter circle around himself. No matter whether they were a threat or not, they would be killed as long as they entered it.

But he hoped no Power Born would come with his deepest heart.

'I should quickly try to find another Life Type Resource. Only then will I have something to use against the Power Borns if it all goes down. And then there is also Ruo…' He turned towards them but smiled and looked away.

His Mother was covering them with a cloth that they had taken on the way and cleaning the dirty swamp mud from her. He wondered when his own mother had grown so untrusting of himself.

'Or maybe she is doing this to avoid others seeing anything they shouldn't if they come?' he thought of that and felt much better. Overall though, even he could tell he was avoiding thinking about his first conversation that would soon follow with Ruo by thinking about all of such things.

Deep feelings lay buried in his heart, and he knew his first conversations would poke a hole in his defenses. But this was not a place for the weak; he was just one of the small players in this game, and he had to be cautious.

Even if he gained a Life-Type Resource, there was nothing that could outright protect him. All of the Life-Type Resources were utility sort most of the time; at best, potent poison or something similar would be the best choice to trade with, but even that had its limitations.

Life-type resources were rare, but that didn't mean they were the best for all situations.

It was related to Life, and they were life forms; that was it.

Light would have been much better.

After thinking while being just as alert, Han Li thought of what to trade for and just then, his mother called for him. "The clothes you took are far too big for the girl but enough to cover her, but don't go steal them from other children," she said and walked out of the small stream with a dressed-up Ruo.

Just the sight of her now clean face and wet white hair made Han Li remember the last time he saw her. A cold corpse hanging from the tree.

But even now, the girl's eyes were blank; she looked at Yue Li with a rather strange gaze, but Han Li could guess what she must be thinking.

'Why is this woman not beating me?'

"Mom, handle her with care…" he said softly, "she—"

"Don't teach a mother how to care for children."

He nodded.

"Now what?" Yue Li asked.

Han Li took a deep breath and looked at Ruo. He had the urge to say, "It's just mere Life-Type resources that we would lose; let's go find a hidden place and spend the day there; we will complete the Trials tomorrow safely without putting ourselves at risk and go home."

But he knew what these 'mere' Life-Type Resources could do. The lack of one had cost him his Mother's life last time, and he didn't wish to go back without atleast one in hand.

As much as he craved to make Ruo at him, he knew they were just emotions, and he had experienced pushing away similar emotions many times. He knew how to handle it in such situations.

Pulling his eyes away from the dazed girl. He took a deep breath. "I want to find a Life-type Magical Resource, but it is quite dangerous from now on… maybe, some of the Power Borns have already realized that there are Life-Type resources around; if so, it would be…"

Yue Li looked at him with a pale smile.

He sighed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 146 - 146: Getting the fruit without sowing the seeds
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li hesitated a lot, but in the end, decided to take a strategic retreat. Life-type treasures were important, but not nearly as important as his mother's or Ruo's life, his too.

Greed could kill, so he has to be greedy with a plan. And he decided to go with the simplest of plans.

He would let his good brother, Yang Wei, work his skin and bones to collect the Life-type resources, and when it was time for a harvest, it would all come to him naturally. It was not like he would use all the resources immediately, and for Han Li, the resource in itself didn't matter as long as he had a decent quantity.

Once again, Yue Li and Han Li found themselves in a cave. It was such a well-hidden hideout, full of hidden routes and winding tunnels and so hard to find that Han Li was sure he wouldn't find it by himself half as quickly. Nevertheless, he had used a simple cantrip to find this place.

The "Demon World" was not without its own aboriginals. Nonsentient as they were, there were many creatures in this world, for example, the Black Crane he killed just after waking up here. Han Li just found and wounded a sort of rabbit with his Life Sword and used the sword's ability to see its memories.

Soon enough, he found this underground cavern hidden under a rabbit hole.

It was dark, but with their senses, it might as well have been bright. It was easy for them to see through it.

After killing or wounding dozens of animals, this was the safest he could find, and finally, here, Yue Li sat down on one of the hundreds of tree roots spread around and, with a sigh, murmured, "This place is really cool."

Han Li nodded, sitting before her. While in the center sat Ruo, still looking at his mother in wonder. He couldn't help but feel that pang of guilt again.

"Are we safe here, Xiao Li?" Yue Li asked.

Han Li nodded, pushing away the thought of what he would miss due to not fighting outside at this moment with the thought that his mother and Ruo were safe.

"So can I lay down for a while?" Yue Li asked.

Han Li looked at her with a soft gaze. "You don't need my permission for such things, why are you acting… so dramatic?"

Yue Li pouted her lips, unamused. "It's natural. My son killed so many people today, and I am almost starting to feel numb; we're both wrong; of course, I knew it was all wrong, and in a sense, I even approved of it earlier…"

"But because I killed without batting an eye, I was able to save Ruo."

Yue Li looked at the girl looking back at her with that weird gaze and felt a bit smug inside, but she pushed such thoughts away, wondering why she was not feeling so worse now after basically watching her son kill others just half an hour ago. With her own eyes, for the first time.

Han Li pointed out. "Don't push yourself too much. They are all just dead till I gain the ability to revive them anyway."

Yue Li blinked and nodded. "That's true, I forgot."

"And I went out of my way to remember their names…"

"Yeah, I was just too preoccupied with what I was feeling like, you know? It's natural that I forget some things, my head hurts thinking about all this… I've heard about such defense mechanisms that would trigger when you see someone close doing something you don't want them to. Like my love for you is too much; it makes me not see your flaws or something like that."

"…is that so," he could only awkwardly shift his gaze back to Ruo.

She was still staring at his mother. Probably the first person to show her kindness.

"Can I know this girl's story?" Yue Li asked, gently caressing the girl's hair. She had never seen such beautiful white hair. Was it natural?

Probably, she guessed, she herself had red hair when her Spark activated after all.

"What is your name, girl?"

Ruo just looked at her with that weird gaze without any words, making Yue Li speechless.

Han Li gently took out a sweet candy he had made the other day using Sea Serpent Honey from his Empty Space, and immediately, a sweet smell started to spread around the area. Ruo smelled the scent and looked at it with wide eyes.

His heart beating like a drum, he extended his hand towards her to feed her, but her face paled instead, and she moved towards Yue Li with a frightened face, almost making Han Li flinch out of his seat.

Yue Li smiled and brought the girl back into her embrace. Gently coaxing her with her head pats, she could feel the girl's heart, and it was wild, making her wonder what kind of experience such a small child had to have been through to make her like this. So frightened and so… weird.

She was like a cat, in all sense of the word, mind included.

Yue Li said with some amusement, "Children don't like Demons. Especially not a mass murderer like you. Don't come close, bitch, get lost."

"I murder for you," Han Li said with a speechless expression. But he could feel how Ruo was in Yue Li's embrace. She was calmer, less trembling.

'Even last time, you always liked to be coaxed like a child by Mom,' he smiled inside.

He felt a bit of relief for some reason at the fact that she didn't accept it from him. He also realized how truly he felt undeserving of Ruo's trust once more.

"Tell me the girl's story, and I will give you a chance to feed her. I am pretty sure I could help you there," Yue Li said joyfully, feeling like a mother once again.

Instead of a damn motherfucker's wife, that is.

Han Li smiled and snorted coldly. "No need, you old hag; coax her for now, and let me think of how to get the Magical Resources."

His mother's eyes widened in anger. "Wha..What did you just call me?"

"Only you can call me hurting names and slap me, but I can't. Did you think I only know the curse words stupid, dumb, and idiot?"

"Of course, I am the Mother, not the other way around. I have no reason to respect you, but you have a lot of them, damn you!"

"Well, cry about it. Like you always do anyway."

Yue Li glared at him with real anger this time, while he delved into his thoughts.

With a chuckle, he exclaimed as he stretched his back, "Yang Wei, all my hope rests on you, do your best!"

He couldn't wait for the trials to end and his harvest to arrive. 'But for now, let's use the chance to cultivate...'

Sadly for him, his mother didn't seem to be in the mood to let him immediately cultivate.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 147 - 147: Ruo. Not of the Fang Family, but my Ruo.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Yue Li harassed Han Li a lot that day. The word 'Old Hag' seemed to have really pressed some buttons in her. She, as usual, tried to beat Han Li up, only to realize once again that his skin was too thick; then she tried to bite his hand, only to realize her teeth were too weak, and realizing that nothing worked on him, chose just to use Ruo to manipulate him.

This devious woman!

Han Li was, of course, not an easy man to trick into something, but pretty soon, he was talking to Yue Li like he was a vassal and she was a queen. The most amazing part?

His mother almost seemed the part in her mockeries. But when he would be about to reach his 'annoyed' level, when he might probably stop caring about the game and just start sexually harassing his mom, she would stop.

As usual, she was an expert when it came to him. Even though she would be quite foolish otherwise…

Anyway, just like that, Han Li had to waste about an hour of precious meditation time, but he was pretty sure that he couldn't focus anyway with Ruo being so close. Every time the girl's gaze moved from his mother to him, he would feel his heart thump and his hand clench.

He wished to hug her. Tell her it was alright now. Tell her that her big brother had arrived to save her. Feed her the tasty candy. Show her what lay beyond survival. But he didn't dare to even go close enough to touch her.

Her frightened face gave him bad memories.

By the end, even Yue Li realized how profoundly impactful the little girl was to Han Li and grew more and more curious while also thinking about why the girl was so weird. But Han Li seemed to avoid the matter for a long time before he sighed at her glare.

"So, here is the proposition, Xiao Li," Yue Li said, closing Ruo's ears with her palms. "If you tell me her story now, we will end the bad tension brewing between us with a kiss, and that's it. You and me stay the same, fooling around couple of a mother and son. If not, I bet you I won't let you touch me in any way that is even a bit sexual."

Han Li clicked his tongue. "There is no tension between us. What are you talking about?"

Yue Li smiled. "You know, so many hidden secrets, so many confusions, so many doubts. And I don't deserve to know this, too?"

He looked at his mother with a sigh. "Your cheeks have accumulated a bit of fat after eating all the time. So cute."

"No, I puffed it by myself, but don't change the subject."

"Ruo…" Han Li said, and the girl looked at him with wide eyes, almost making his heart jump. 'Damn, I am almost afraid of her at this point…'

Sighing aloud, he murmured, "Ruo is a very pitiful child, as pitiful as they come. You won't believe, or you won't wish to believe her true story."

"Tell me," Yue Li said with a solemn tone. "I expected to see some kind of wounds or something when washing her, but other than her acting meek and fearful, she seems entirely healthy. That is even more unnerving though… and the fact that she lacks the intelligence to even speak clearly makes me worried too."

"There is this tradition in some of the Brothels across Sacred Lust…" he looked at Ruo, knowing she understood what he was speaking of even though she seemed rather unwell in the mind department. "The brothels create some families solely meant to generate beautiful women throughout the ages, and one of these families was the Fang Family in a faraway city from here.

They have all sorts of beautiful women and handsome men they use to gain beautiful and handsome offspring."

"For what?" Yue Li asked with disgust.

"To sell, to use as 'vases,' or to just become usual brothel women."

"Vases?" Yue Li asked, feeling the girl shiver on her lap.

"Basically, they would be the most beautiful of all the brothel's women and would not be allowed to be touched by any man but instead used as an advertisement. Just due to their beauty and attracted by their purity alone, hundreds would visit the brothel, it is a marketing method."

"And Ruo… she?"

"She was born to this Fang Family, and from the day she was born, she decided to be a future 'vase,' her beauty was too powerful, and her talent even more wondrous, as you can see, since she is here, she obviously has Sacred Talent. And even her Spark is special. White Sparrow Spark is a transformation spark of the bird variant. She could fly when she becomes an Evolved."

"I don't care about her, Spark; tell me why she has this kind of mental scars."

Han Li's gaze grew cold. "The Fang Family saw potential in her and began to teach her the true way of cultivation. She Awakened like everyone at the age of 13 about three months ago today, and seeing her Spark, all of them rejoiced and were over the moon. And do you know what her current situation is?"

Yue Li shook her head, a bad premonition in her heart.

"I told you before about Beasts, right? Just like ordinary animals with Beast Cores, there are insects with Beast Cores, too, and they are called Gu Worms. Some people tame these insects or even nurture them by themselves, like the Raven Clan in Southern Lust City, and they can be used to make people act like slaves.

"And they planted more than three of these Gu Worms in Ruo; her heart is currently a worm, her stomach is also currently a worm, while the most problematic thing about her is her wings in case she Evolved, her backbone is also a sort of Gu Worm."

Yue Li felt her own face pale, and her hold over the girl tightened… she felt as if the girl would break at a touch. And there was this frailty in her that just made Yue Li's motherly instinct scream. That made her bite her lips.

Another thought haunted her mind.

'Just like her, there are a lot of kids currently in this Demon World…' her mind muttered, 'and some of them might die somehow. How cruel was the Sacred Demon? can't he keep the children out of this? I have to ask Xiao Li for help…'

"So how did that make her like this? Her eyes are so blank…"

Han Li extended his hand and gently patted Ruo's white hair. She felt warm to the touch.

"They did all that because her Spark was an absolute rarity, it appeared somehow by luck, in a sense, like two chickens mating to birth a phoenix. They wanted her to be the start of another great Clan, White Sparrow Clan.

"But these Gu were not just simple things like changed circumstances; her spine was literally replaced with a worm that now acted as her spine, and the same went to the other two. And moreover, the Fang Family also held the connected Twin Gu of her heart, as in, if they killed it, she would die."

"What kind of lunatics are these bitches?!" Yue Li hissed. "Why do it? To their own child nonetheless."

"This is actually considered a surefire training to achieve Evolution for the Transformation Type cultivators. They could endure the Evolution time's pain easily, but they had underestimated the Gu Worm's lasting effect and overestimated Little Ruo's endurance.

They had believed the pain to vanish and her mind to work okayi'sh after a while, and they didn't need her to be intelligent; they wanted her to be working."

Han Li looked profoundly at his Mother and her pale expression along with her bloodshot eyes. His own eyes, on the other hand, grew calm and callous.

"But only when she became around 16 years old did they realize their plan totally backfired, and by the time she became that age, her mind was entirely broken by the pain, suffering, and pressure. She had become a doll, and they couldn't even use her as a vase at that point. Since the damned Demon Restraining Temple liked to keep appearances clean at the very least.

"Even though they knew it, they would turn a blind eye, of course, unless it is a publically known evil.

And then the only way for them to use Ruo was to do the greatest evil, which I don't want to even speak about, but luckily, her sister, by blood, couldn't bear to see it and, in a nice and sneaky way, went to sell her out to the brothel as a badly functioning 'Vase', but two people intervened on the way and bought her with a high price, and those two people were you and me."

Yue Li looked at him with a stunned expression. "What again?"

"Those are just speculations of how it would have ended up, just that I gave it a happy ending by including us in the story, but the logical part, without including the future, the story is accurate."

Even the future part was real, just that it won't repeat. And just that he had omitted a lot of the story, about how he had lost his mother to longevity and lost Ruo to the schemes of the Fang Clan soon after.

Yue Li felt something amiss, but she couldn't put her finger on it. She just looked at him dubiously.

Han Li chuckled wryly and gently took the shivering girl from his mother. He could feel the girl almost cry out loud in fear, and that made his heart ache.

"Ah, don't cry, it's just your big brother..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 148 - 148: Freebie
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While Han Li and his Mother were inside the underground cavern, one trying to get Little Ruo to eat the candy in her hand and the other deeply in meditation, the outside world was full of chaos.

Yang Wei and some allies he had put together were only one of the many intelligent enough to do something like that. By the time it was deep at night, as the moon hung high in the sky, filtering the land in gentle luminescence, hundreds died every hour.

It didn't take long before everyone understood that the world had Life Type Magical Resources, which was enough seed for all the chaos. At first, it was a battle between Evolved, and then, the intelligent ones slowly slipped away with their loot while those who still fought ended up seeing something interesting before they welcomed the bliss of death—a simple attack by a Power Born.

Soon enough, every Evolved was working like slaves for the Power Born, all searching fervently for a Magic Resource; if they didn't find it in some hours, one of them would be killed at random—that one of them could be them!

But the world didn't lack people who cared for life. There were some Power Born, like the Saintess Moon Girl, Monk Hen Shin, or such people who just ran around saving kids and the powerless from the struggles of the wicked. While some demons on the other hand…

Luckily, among Sacred Talent Cultivators, not many were so pitiful as to need helpless people to push their will on, if they needed to, they were superior enough to bend wills in the Seven Continents. Regardless, the world didn't lack evil, but none of the evil was Power Born, so almost all such ugly faces and weak men died at the hands of the righteous.

When a Power Born decides to do something, it was natural the Evolved would suffer. And Han Li hadn't noticed it, but this world was not nearly as vast as the continents. It was barely a day's work for people who could fly to go around and come back.

But just like in the real world, if they tried to fly above a certain height, their bodies would start to react badly. It would start to convulse, and just like the real world, it was a globe, so they could never find an 'edge' to the world.

Regardless, Power Born were too scary…

Even Han Li was afraid of them.

***

As the world filled up with more and more dead human bodies, in the underground cave, Han Li was seated cross-legged, his back drenched with sweat.

He had taken the risk of briefly going out; his mom wanted him to save at least one child for her, and he tried to, only to end up seeing a Power Born face to face. His heart thudded in his chest, and he took a deep breath. Luckily, that was Monk Hen Shin. A paragon of righteousness.

Yue Li was scared half to death after understanding that she had actually put her son's life in danger and that he was not some invincible god who could just go out and come in.

As a result, she was now clinging to his chest with a pale face and kissing his cheeks like he would be lost if she let him free.

Whereas Ruo was now sleeping to the side with a sweet candy stick still in her mouth, it was nothing bad; it was edible too, so no one disturbed the completely fatigued girl. Yue Li had easily fed her the candy, and its effect of removing fatigue from the body really did wonders for Ruo; it made her fall asleep.

Yue Li covered her with the leftover clothing that Ruo herself wore—it was too big for her, so it was as good a blanket as they could find for now.

Suddenly, Han Li's meditation was once again broken.

Feeling his mother gently kiss his lips once more, he opened his eyes in annoyance.

"Mom, let me meditate…" He sighed, "I can't focus with all this, it is such a good opportunity to get Energy… I am missing a Dao Crystal every minute of attention I give you."

Yue Li pouted. "Am I not worth that one Dao Crystal you lose? You don't like me kissing you anymore, do you? I am an old hag, after all."

Her son just sighed and planted a deep and forceful kiss on her lips, and a second later, he broke it. "I now understand that you are not the type of person you would want to be with when traveling into places like these. You are meant to be at home, in my room, cooking, or just traveling into nice places on vacation."

"Right…" Yue Li nodded, saddened. He had already told her about the Righteous woman who was going around saving kids alongside Monk Hen Shin, and thinking about her made her a bit envious.

She daydreamed in his embrace while he once more went back into meditation. "One day, if I become strong, I will be like her. I will also save people, you know? And by the way, revive all of these people when you become strong enough, okay?"

Han Li smiled, his eyes once more opened, looking at her with a gentle gaze.

"Okay, this is the deal. I will revive everyone dead in the world and create a pure land for them, indoctrinate them with the belief of righteousness, and enslave them to that mentality. Then I will throw them to a world and call it Heaven. How is it?"

Yue Li looked at him, pissed. "I am not joking, you have no respect for me these days, do you? Calling me an old hag, too."

"Are you not one?" Han Li teased.

"No…" Yue Li said, the rims of her eyes wet, "I am young, just thirty-five years old."

"Oh no!" Han Li suddenly exclaimed.

"What?" Yue Li asked with a weird face.

"I have been seduced by a vixen twice my age, and she is now saying that she is young! Oh heavens, someone help me!"

Yue Li giggled and gave a smack on his head. "Don't call your mother vixen. Call me something nice for once."

"Like?"

"Something romantic or something?"

"Darling…" Han Li felt his goosebumps rise, and even Yue Li felt a cringe.

"I'll keep to Mom," he said with a cough.

"Yeah," Yue Li nodded readily and said, "We are not supposed to be romantic. Go be cheesy with some other woman. I am too high class for such stupid things."

Rolling his eyes at her, he returned to meditation, and this time, no matter what Yue Li attempted, he didn't wake up. Seated there, he was like an iron statue; even if she punched him, it wouldn't make a difference.

She tried sitting on his back, pushing him, kissing him, licking his cheeks, and even leaving trails of saliva on his neck trying to leave a hickey, but he was entirely akin to a statue, immovable by lust, desire, and even her pitiful strength.

Bored, she gently brought Ruo beside them and made the girl use one of Han Li's lap as a pillow, and that immediately seemed to have worked as he opened his eyes, filled with annoyance once more. "What is the problem with you?" he asked.

"Nothing," Yue Li coughed. "Last time, no more games. I am also going to sleep."

As he glared with a gaze that said, 'I would ravage you till you go crazy and lose all manner of understanding if you don't sleep immediately,' she gulped and slept on his lap, whistling nervously. She didn't really know the extent of what Han Li could do when there was a child beside them, and for now, she didn't want to test it.

What if he really did that, and Ruo woke up to see something so horrendous?

She would lose all kinds of respect with this future Daughter in the law of hers!

Moreover, she was just a child, and she shouldn't be exposed to such things yet. There was time for everything, but Yue Li didn't know what Han Li would or wouldn't do anymore. He had grown a bit… unfathomable. In a bad way.

She had to crack his shields slowly. But she could already feel that she was at the cusp of figuring out his damned secret already. "Someday soon…" she muttered as she fell asleep, and he sighed, gently removing their heads from his lap. He walked to the side and once again began meditation.

The more he meditated, the faster the gains.

He felt as if he was gaining a crystal a minute at this point.

…

2 hours later, Han Li opened his eyes calmly. They shone like red gems.

"I wish I could stay here forever…" he sighed, "That would have been so great. Hiding with my girls from society till I reach my limits…"

He shook his head and used Life Vision on himself.

[Current Cultivation: Evolution Stage]

[Body: Below Average Evolved Physique]

[Skin Building Completed—Tempered Skin—Vibrating Skin]

[Organ Strengthening: 99% completion]

With a smile, he went back to meditation. He would only level up the rest one percent when his Mother woke up—it was a majestic sight worth seeing.

Till then, he had no reason to waste these freebie Dao Crystals.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 149 - 149: The unexpected benefit
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Yue Li woke up after 3 hours, but Little Ruo seemed extremely fatigued, to the point she was in a sort of comatose state, so she was still asleep.

Han Li calmly explained to his Mom that he was about to have a breakthrough in transforming his organs to those similar to the Sacred Demon, and his mother looked at him curiously. Remembering the last time she had seen his Skin become stronger.

"The skin gave you the ability to vibrate. Will Organs give you something like that, too?" She asked while rubbing her eyes off of sleep, sitting before him and looking at him curiously.

Li Shuanwei. Yes, now he remembered that person once more.

Why didn't Life Vision work on him?

Life Vision told him that person was just Unawakened, even in his twenties. So why was he said to cultivate a different path? Why?

Was Life Vision a flawed ability of his Three Life Sacred Eyes?

Did he put too much faith in this gimmick?

Maybe the people after Li Shuanwei, especially the Power Born Demonic Ice Fairy, were mistaken and just making a mess for no reason.

"He is said to be an Unawakened, but that is bullshit; that person is not who he seems to be. He is a cultivator, but just not the way we know it…"

Two of us from one city?

The Spirit inside Yang Wei made Han Li worried. It was an Unknown.

What was the Spirit?

There were no such things in this world as far as he knew. When you die, you ascend to heaven and become a star if you are a Power Born at the very least. If not, you just assimilate into the surroundings as pure Energy.

Even the Sacred Demons had died and perished.

She couldn't move.

Was handsome the word?

But Transformation Type Cultivators did have hearts in their Dao Soul, but they still can't use their Heart Qi, why? THe question boggled his mind for only a brief moment before he gained an understanding.

Because their hearts were not strong enough.

Only Transcendants had transcended the mortal coil, they were entirely of another level compared to all previous Stages of cultivation. In that Stage, the difference between Transformation and Ethereal blur, with a wave, Transcendents could create a disaster for mankind, even for the continent as a whole if there were no one to stop them.

Even though Han Li's body wasn't Transcendent, his heart was. Till now, the Organs in his Dao Soul weren't completely real yet, but now that they had become real, it was natural he could use the full power of his Heart Qi now. But that also made his heart skip a beat.

If his Heart Qi was already as strong as Transcendants at the Evolution Stage, what would it become when he reaches Power Birth Stage? What if he Transcends?

It would only become stronger and stronger through every level up, as his Dao Soul grew stronger.

The only issue was, he didn't really know how to make the best use of Heart Qi. When he became a Transcendant, he was being hunted down from all sides unlike the other Transcendants, so he didn't have the chance to learn from other Transcendants or records that the Four Great Sects kept, so his use of the Qi was limited to instinctual level at best.

The only ways he knew how to use it were for making binding contracts, hiding his Soul Sense, and using it as an aura to dominate others, even Power Borns would be startled if not cowered to that aura. Yue Li had just sensed a wisp of it.

He wouldn't be so much of a show off to make his mom actually frightened.

In the continent, only four people currently had Heart Qi, the Four Great Sect's highest standing people—the Four Great Transcendents. All of the others wouldn't even know how it felt like to have a power like this. 500 years of experience is a thing everyone would want to covet if they knew of it.
