He would wait till the last hour, and since no one would know that they would be teleported to somewhere else in an hour, even just getting to carry the Magic Resource at that precise time would be enough; he might not even have to fight.

It was a small game of wit and might.

Chapter 152 - 152: He who fights monsters.

The sun's harsh rays reduced in might as it went around the dome of heaven, finally nearing the time of sunset. Slaughter-bathed and death-filled, the forest of the world felt as if it had broken a sense of monochrome that had kept nature balanced till now.

Standing at the top of a tree, his gaze akin to an eagle, Han Li looked around, pondering. Humans were like this, the most chaotic of all races as if a mistake by the heavens. Everything else, Beasts, Gu Worms, trees with a beast core inside, all of them were trapped in a sort of life cycle where their standing was always a constant.

If it was a lion, it was put at the top at birth; if it was a squirrel, it was put at the bottom. But humans had the inherent nature by which putting them in any category would be just wrong; they were neither predators at all times nor were they prey; they were just a unique cog in the whole system of things.

Even Power Borns couldn't have been mighty enough to withstand that waterfall.

Even mosquito meat was meat, he wouldn't reject such heavenly fortune.

Saintess Moon Girl!

Chapter 153 - 153: Luckyly in the Maw
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

None of these foxes were Beasts. They were normal animals. But around the cradle of leaves where laid the baby, they walked around, as if they were protecting the baby. As if they were sentient beings filled with pity at the small lifeform.

Any other person might have jumped at the chance, saving the kid in the process from the bloodshed around. Han Li would have also done that; he would have returned to his mom's side already. He was feeling anxious now, even though he was pretty sure his mom was safe and that no one could find her in merely 40 minutes that remained till sunset. He didn't like putting it to an off-chance.

But no, he didn't move to take the fruit. He stayed still. Just looking at the baby and the cradle, his sense of danger alarmed out crazily. There appeared to be no danger, nothing at all. Yet, his life vision told an entirely different story.

It was the first time he had used Life Vision on a beast like this, and he could feel the lack of a name the thing had. Instead, the feedback he received was the name of its species instead.

[Parasite Bait Snake] [Age: 39 years]

[Current Cultivation: Ordinary Beast—Blood Dao Beast Core.]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Ordinary Animal]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Beast King, Life Great Dao Beast King Core.]

[Death: (in the last premonition) At age 56 in a battle against another Beast.]

'…A snake that releases parasitic venom. It can enslave other beasts for a short time till they die soon enough, and when they die, it uses them as food. It baits others with nasty tactics as if it is sentient, but it lacks any intelligence, yet it has very keen instincts…' he remembered. The snake was stuck to a tree, but it was hard to notice due to its almost bark-like skin.

He was quite sure many had died here already; the snake was good at hiding such things, so it was quite strong. In fact, almost all of the Beasts in this world had bodies at the level of Sacred Talent Cultivators of the Transformation Type even without Transforming, so if they took you by surprise, you'd be done for most of the time.

He also knew this thing was even more dangerous. Unlike Transformation-Type Cultivators, who had to transform, Beasts were strong in their original forms and were extremely strong. Just like him, who had Talents instead of Magic Powers, they also had inherent abilities like flight, venom sacs, flexible bodies, and similar.

Even though they lacked the intelligence to use it well, their instincts were, in themselves, powerful.

Han Li took a deep breath. The Life Sword floated gently closer. If even one of the wolves were harmed, the snake would notice, Han Li knew the danger. He knew it didn't rely on something like Soul Sense either, so even the slightest sound would alert it.

'I have to deal with this before one of the Power Borns who are searching for the kids arrive here…" His gaze locked in on the snake's possible vulnerable areas. Han Li pondered the best route for a long second. What was the best choice here?

A passing breeze lifted his hair slightly, brushing by his cold and calculative gaze.

The beast had a better body than him, but his sword could cut it. His proficiency that Evolution Stage couldn't even dream about was not something it could easily push aside. The stronger your proficiency, the sharper your weapons were.

Even though he couldn't cut through metal like butter now, he could cut through normal-width trees like butter. His strength had risen, and his body could help him better use the sword now. Of course, using his strength alone, he could bend metal.

But even though the snake appeared so brittle, he knew it would have flesh tougher than normal trees. It would have a body powerful enough to crush him if it coiled around him.

Its skin would also be hard to pass through. Even the wolves could end up being a nuisance. Suddenly, Han Li sat down cross-legged and began to breathe evenly. He calmly waited with anticipation rising in his chest

It didn't take long before a long-haired young man entered the clearing. He had pitch-black hair, blue eyes, and black teeth. The young man first looked at the baby in the clearing and scanned the surroundings using his Soul Sense. Han Li took a step back from where he was. The soul sense reached the limit of 10 meters just a step before him.

While at the same time, he looked at the youth with curiosity. 'Mud Dao Soul, Ethereal Type…' he thought.

On the other hand, the youth had his eyes shimmer as he walked confidently towards the baby. A magical resource!

Could it be a life type?

All of the wolves turned and looked at him, their eyes immediately bloodshot.

*Growl!*

They growled at him hatefully.

"What a pity," the youth said, his voice airy, "I would've loved to have you guys back home as my pets. What beautiful fur, what moon-like brilliance, but sadly, I have not the power to leave nor the ability to take you away with me. We could have some fun here if you wish, though."

He said with a weird grin and added with an emotional chuckle. "I really love these animals who care for human life, you know? Better than humans, at least."

The wolves looked at him coldly, slowly surrounding him.

The youth sighed, "My family was like you, wolves. I used to think of them as mighty, you see. But in the end? When they surrounded me to kill me, I was far too strong for them. Just like now."

He walked forward and suddenly chanted something inaudible. The weight on the wolves seemed to have suddenly doubled, tripled. Magical Power: Weight of Mud!

The youth didn't immediately move to the baby, though, making the hidden Han Li frown in irritation. The youth instead walked towards one of the white wolves and ran his hand through its fur with a weird, blissful expression.

Han Li coldly snorted to himself. The fool!

The chance of them both dying to each other was now out of the list.

The snake slithered, gently and silently, moving towards the youth like a silent wind. Even the grass it slithered upon seemed not to have felt it as it suddenly appeared behind the youth and suddenly stood long.

Han Li felt his heart thump at the sight. Seven meters, the whole thing was at least 7 meters!

It was like a sudden spring, as before the youth could even realize what was happening, a huge maw devoured him, and a great tail surrounded him with the fox he was caressing together.

The snake's hold tightened. A small geyser of blood exploded; the boy's body was then devoured whole, soon followed by the 2-meter big wolf.

The snake stood there, blood completely bathing it as it hissed coldly at the remaining foxes. With another hiss, it heavily rolled around in the blood-soaked mud, and the blood was slowly absorbed into its skin. About ten minutes later, the snake hissed and returned to where it had been hiding.

But then it looked with its green eyes at the empty cradle-like structure where neither the baby nor the fruit was visible.

It was silent for a second before it opened its mouth wide in a hiss. Han Li smirked from some meters in front of it. His gaze focused, he spun the sword now firmly in his grip, adjusting his hold until the hilt felt just right. The world seemed to hold its breath as his leg was planted heavily onto the ground, and he hurled it.

The sword spun through the air like a dark flash, a streak of deadly precision. It had crossed the distance in a second, and in another second, it pierced the soft tissues of the beast's inner mouth. It screeched and closed its mouth like a toothpick stuck inside the throat; the flying sword wreaked havoc inside.

It didn't take long for the squirming beast to stop squirming; just 4 minutes after the heart was destroyed inside like tofu, its movements came to a halt. Like a balloon losing air, blood leaked through its pores.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 154 - 154: I had expected him to be a Demon but appears not.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Yue Li was inside the underground cavern, Little Ruo calmly sleeping on her lap. She gently combed the girl's white hair with her hand, her motherly instincts overflowing her. While on the other hand, she was worried about Han Li, too.

'Only about ten or twenty minutes before sunset…' she thought, 'and then, I should be teleported to the place where the trials will happen. Xiao Li told me not to stand out among the crowd when I reached there, so there must be many people there.'

She closed her eyes slightly. 'Xiao Li will be there, what have I got to worry about? I wonder how Liyue and Li Yao are doing…'

In the darkness of the cave, her mind kept wandering. There were many children whom her Xiao Li, or she herself, could have probably saved, but they didn't… she wondered how many children died here today. She took a deep breath.

In her mind, Han Li was beside her, gently comforting her. That made her heart calm down; as long as her son was there, everything would be fine, one way or the other; nothing could go wrong. Suddenly, she heard the sound of footsteps. Her mind woke up from the grogginess. Did Xiao Li return?

Even though the cavern was dark, she could see rather well with her eyesight, and in the distance, she saw a figure. The person appeared rather blurry, but she focused, and her heart skipped a beat; it was not Xiao Li. But he said we should be safe here…

Her heart started to thud as the image of the person grew clear. It was a wide man; he appeared almost like a mountain, and soon, he was close to them. The old man had a bald head and long white beard. He wore the clothes of a monk. In his hand was a prayer bead, and in his gaze was something unfathomable. As he reached near them, he looked down at them, especially at Ruo.

They were about three meters apart. The monk squatted down in the distance to Yue Li's eye level. His gaze was calmly on Ruo, who was sleeping on Yue Li's lap.

Yue Li was fearful; all of Han Li's teachings and all of his words came to her, and her heart thumped in fear. She instinctively hid Ruo close to her side and looked at the old man with a gaze that spoke volumes of her fear. Her hands trembled slightly. Her face was pale.

Knife, yes, a knife… she thought, but somehow, she couldn't move her hand to take the knife Han Li had handed her. It was hidden beside them, but with one glance of the man, her hands paused. It was as if she was looking at a mountain.

His gaze held the weight of a mountain.

The man looked at Yue Li calmly, his pond-like gaze unstirred and calm.

"The pitiful child of the Fang Family, huh? Hmmm... do you know her name?" the Monk asked.

Yue Li stuttered slightly but replied, "Ruo… she is Ruo."

The Monk smiled.

"Where is your son?" he asked.

"..Ou…out," Yue Li replied, horrified.

The old man nodded.

"He might be able to save this girl in this world, but not if she returns to the seven continents. The Fang Family, if she returns there, she is done for…" The Monk sighed. "I had the feeling that your son was no different from other demons, but seeing you, he seems to be a true demon instead (A demon who still cares for others).

Are you his obsession for him to have such a ruthless demonic heart at such a young age, or is it something else?"

Yue Li felt cold sweat streaming down her forehead.

The monk saw her expression and laughed, his voice reverberating across the cave. "It seems correct. Only with an obsession as strong as the heavens can one have the unflinching heart of a demon, yet seeing you, the youth certainly does care for you. So his obsession can only be you!"

Yue Li looked at the old man, feeling paranoid still, she asked, "Ca… can you move a bit away from us?"

The monk raised an eyebrow. "Hmm? As expected of a demon's mother, even paranoid of a monk? How disrespectful." The monk shook his head and walked ten steps back.

"What do… you want?" Yue Li questioned.

"Just answer this monk's curiosity," The Monk suddenly paused. "Ah, I am Hen Shin, Monk Hen Shin of the Demon Restraining Temple. I had forgotten to introduce myself."

A silence hung for a second.

"My…My son is not a Demon like you said," she said, "And he killed only the people who attacked him!"

Monk Hen Shin was briefly surprised and closed his eyes. Remembering what he had seen from a distance in hiding, indeed, as far as he had seen, that youth had only killed those who attacked him. As far as he had seen, that is.

"Do not worry, mother of a son who is not a demon," The Monk smiled. "I am not here to trouble him either way. If I wanted to kill him, he would have already been dead. I am here for the child currently sleeping in your lap, as she is someone I had vowed to save from the grasp of evil, and what better chance than now?"

Yue Li frowned. But deep down, she was scheming something.

There were barely about ten minutes more till sunset, old man. You jabber on like this, and we'll be teleported in a second. Hahaha, you, sonavabich!

"I cannot give you the girl, Monk. I just adopted her as my child some time ago…" she replied, her throat slightly dry from the earlier scare. She didn't want to talk about her son's unknown connection with the girl since that didn't seem like something she should discuss with a stranger.

Monk Hen Shin grew thoughtful. "But there is no way you could bring her out of here. If she comes with you, she will just be teleported back to her abusive family. If you really care for her as her mother, you should let her come with me since I have a way to take her away with me."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 155 - 155: Monk
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

She coughed. Xiao Li also told her they couldn't bring her away without trading for a unique Life Type Magical Resource; she knew he was out there searching for the trade-worthy resource.

"I'd have to discuss that with my son, apparently…"

The Monk chuckled, twirling his beard. "Just tell me this: is your son a bad person at heart?"

"Absolutely no. He is like an angel at heart, just that he is a bit too much of a realist," Yue Li said with full confidence. "And there is nothing he wouldn't do for me. He is a very good child."

"Hmm…" The old man scratched his hair, muttering, "To think that I would rely on a demon to complete one of my vows. The world really is a weird place. But even if I bring her back with me, I would have no place to hide her, and I am not strong enough to fight the Fang Family by myself, nor their backing…"

Yue Li looked at the old man, her heart full of worry.

Was this old man really stronger than Xiao Li?

Doesn't Demon Restraining Temple hunt down demons? Was her son in trouble?

"Look here. Where are you from if you tell me I won't harm your son," The old man said, a weird smile on his face.

Yue Li didn't know whether to answer it or not, but she didn't have to think anymore. A black sword calmly rested on Monk Hen Shin's neck.

"Is it enough if I am the one who tells you that, Old Monk Hen Shin," the voice that asked was cold and violent, but Hen Shin only smiled.

"What an interesting duo you both are: a mother with Sacred Talents and a son obsessed with her who is hiding his cultivation," he said, "Tell me, just out of curiosity, how were you trained?"

Han Li calmly spoke, "You, monk, certainly seem to have a talent for peeping into others' lives. Isn't it a rule of you people to abstain from idle chatter?"

In fact, so good at peeping that Han Li didn't even know he had been looked into.

The monk blinked, his profound eyes filled with curiosity of the young man behind him. "Rules are meant to be broken, my child who had chosen the demonic path. Now tell me just one thing: are you worthy of me entrusting this girl to you?"

Han Li frowned. His sword in this man's neck was nothing but empty threats. He wouldn't stand a chance against him. He hated this. He hated this so much! His guts were wrenching inside; his emotions were roiling, telling him to sink the sword into the monk.

Losing control… he hated that.

He smiled, his calm gaze locked onto the old man's neck.

"Am I worthy?" Han Li laughed.

The old man frowned. Had he misjudged this boy's character?

His belief was that even among demons, there existed trustworthy people, and with his experience, he could see through the facade of a youth like Han Li if he tried to act. He wanted to leave Little Ruo in their hands and threaten them with a visit some months later; he would see immediately once he returned whether it was a good choice and immediately travel to their location.

He had no one else here in the Demon World to whom he could entrust this child, not even the disciples of the Temple who were currently here. Fang Family was dangerous. It was best done under the wraps.

'As expected, leaving them in a demon's hand is not the way, Hen Shin. You are becoming too naive with age,' the old monk said to himself, but then he thought of the expensive medicine that Ruo had already been fed. (the honey candy) and couldn't help but think again. There was also a mother in front of him who seemed confident in her son.

Both the youth and his mother are far too young to deceive me.

"Are you worthy?" The old man asked, his tone a turn colder. If he was laughed at again, he had no issue taking a hand away from the youth as a lesson. It should curb his arrogance.

He had forgotten. All young Sacred Talents were like this, after all.

Han Li stopped chuckling. His red eyes focused on the man who could kill him easily.

"Am I worthy? I do not know Monk. But what I do know is, I'd fight you to protect her if it came to it."

The Old Man looked at him with his shimmering black eyes. "That was not a lie. If it were, you'd be dead."

"Lie detection? A very good Magical Power. And your chanting speed is so ungodly I didn't even notice," Han Li praised.

"Where are you from?"

"Sacred Lust Continent, Southern Lust City, name Han Li, the sole son of Yue Li. I am a man who doesn't like attention, so I'd prefer you not speak of me."

A brown fruit appeared in the old man's hand, and he threw it at Yue Li.

"Use that fruit; you can temporarily put living beings in your empty space. Three months later, I will come to Southern Lust. If Ruo is not treated well by then, I'll kill your son."

Yue Li caught the fruit and looked at Han Li.

The old man looked at Han Li for the last time.

"As long as there is a little spot of compassion in your heart, there is always a hope of redemption. I usually abhor demons, yet I admire the true demons who can sacrifice anything for their obsession. Is your mother your obsession and clinging that you use to love life?"

Han Li looked at him with a bit of surprise.

"Monks should abstain from idle chatter," he calmly replied and pulled back his sword as a red mist started to rise from the ground. A second later, both the monk and the boy smiled as all of them vanished from the cavern and were pulled into another space.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 156 - 156: Unexpectedly found destiny before the trials began
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What is your name, Woman? You look gorgeous," A youthful male said, his face full of eagerness.

Yue Li looked at him with irritation and disgust. She was carrying Ruo like a baby. It had been about five minutes since she was transported into an infinitely expanding white space filled with trees everywhere, and her Xiao Li was nowhere to be found.

She was waiting for her son now but she was no longer worried; from the moment she was transferred here to this weird place with white soil and square white heavens, she was now inside a protective bubble of sorts, and no one could harm them now.

As far as she saw, the bubble was invincible!

The bubble even seemed to be able to tell enemies and friends as she and Ruo were together. But even though other people couldn't approach them too closely and her son had asked her to maintain a low profile, these youths seemed nonstop, one after the other, coming to her and attempting to get her address or flirt. This guy alone was the seventh, and he was the most annoying and persistent.

"Hey, tell me, fairy, what is your name?" the man asked, his voice filled with a romantic flair.

Yue Li had enough and snorted with irritation. "You snot-nosed brat, get lost! I already have a boyfriend!"

The man frowned. "How dare he! Who is this arrogant man who dares to covet what this young master from the great clan—"

His words were caught in his stomach as he felt a gaze. An aura. It was so vast, so unfathomably barbaric. His back started to sweat coldly while his teeth started to chatter. He trembled. An arm comfortably wrapped around his neck as if the protective bubble didn't exist at all.

"Continue," a calm voice said.

The youth mechanically shifted his gaze and saw scarlet pupils looking at him. His soul almost left his body at the sight, and his heart shivered. "Who, who are you?!"

The owner of the red eyes curiously wondered for a second before smiling. "Me? Have you not heard of my resounding reputation? Have you not heard of the majestic title, Red Eyed Han Li? How dare you slight this young master!"

The youth felt cold sweat bead down his back. A title?

Only powerful and mighty Power Borns had titles of their own. Evolved were not worthy of such names of adoration or fear.

'Don't tell me… did I kick the hornet's nest by flirting with the most beautiful woman I had seen in the entire white expanse? Damn me, she was just too beautiful, and I was too curious. And why is my protective bubble not working?' the youth sweated coldly.

Han Li's eyes turned even more curious as he chuckled. "What is a woman doing in the first place acting like a man? Not to mention trying to flirt with my mom at that."

The youth's eyes widened to the limit, and she turned to look at Han Li's face once more. This time, cold sweat trickled down her forehead. "What might you mean, mighty senior?"

Honestly speaking, she had no status nor big clans backing her. She was just a normal village woman acting as a male due to curiosity. No one could attack her anyway with the bubble in place. She just wanted to act like a young master. Uh, that was a bad idea.

She had walked all around and seen many people around here trying to attack the bubble, only to fail miserably. There was even a Power Born there who was injured badly in an attempt to do that, as his own attack was reflected back.

Han Li looked at the woman with even more curiosity. "You might fool other people with your facade, but woman, you can't fool me. I can smell maidens from a kilometer away!"

Yue Li came closer with a curious smile and gave Han Li a slight smack on the head.

"What are you, a dog, to smell maidens from that far?"

Han Li chuckled. His Life Vision was just too useful in such scenarios.

"Mom," he shifted the girl's face towards his mother. "Doesn't she look cute?"

Yue Li looked at her curiously. Now that she looked closely, there really was a feminine touch to the youth. She had short black hair and rosy jade cheeks, while on her head was dark hair, and her eyes were a particularly curious purple.

Even now that Han Li was basically showing her up like merchandise, she seemed more curious than actually afraid, even though she sweated a lot.

"Are you really a woman?" Yue Li asked, filled with curiosity.

Where was the woman's breasts?

"Eh…" the woman blushed. "Apparently, I am. Can you ask your son to move a bit away from me?"

Yue Li looked at her with sparkling eyes. "What happened to your boobs?"

The woman glared at Yue Li. "Of course, not every woman has to be so blessed like you!"

Yue Li chuckled, proud. That was a part of her that her son still adored.

"What a cute girl! Why did you act like a hooligan?"

"Cough," The girl coughed. "I was just curious and didn't think the bubble would fail so easily. Luckily, I was trapped with people as benevolent as you two who quickly let me go."

"Who let you go?" Han Li asked, smiling.

The girl tried to move away from him, and Han Li smiled as he said, "Your bubble is still working, you know? I really can't harm you."

The girl blinked, staying a while longer under his arm. "Oh, what might you mean?"

What a crafty little fox, Han Li chuckled. The girl wanted the information.

"It's the intent that you hold in your heart that matters. If you can channel your heart to feel no harm to the person you are going to interact with in any way, the bubble won't repulse you. As in, see my mom holding that girl; if she felt anything negative of any sort that could be harmful to the girl, she and the girl would be pushed apart."

The crafty girl's eyes lit up, and she moved away from Han Li with two steps. She looked at him with a laugh, "Okay then, benevolent brother, may I go now? My great clan members are waiting for me somewhere else. You see, I have something else to do."

Han Li looked at her, suddenly growing a bit bored. With a sigh, he went ahead and muttered, "I was wondering if I would get another disciple, but it seems not."

"Eh?" Yue Li was surprised. "You want to take her as a disciple, as in like me and Liyue?"

"Of course, she seemed like a good addition. My type of person, honestly."

The woman stopped in her tracks, her ears perking up. From a young age, she had grown up in a village with no one to teach her the ways of cultivation, and she had always wished to have someone to teach her. The four sects were too far from her village, and she knew of no one else in the world with a Spark similar to her own.

So, it was a very awkward situation for her.

Her foot walked back, and she stood studiously in front of her new master.

This person, who obviously seemed like a Power-born old man acting like a young man after eating some Magic Resource, could probably be her best bet at gaining a powerful master.

Han Li squinted at her curiously while Yue Li judged her from the side.

"Is she worthy of you? Liyue might be mad if you get entangled in others even before you guys…"

The girl's heart skipped a beat. Even though she felt there was something fishy and that she was jumping into it too headfast, the forest and the village had taught her one thing: when opportunities arrive, you have to catch them.

"My name is Wei Yuanfen! I am not a lesbian, and I do not love women. The earlier thing was just a joke on my part; please pardon me, Miss Mother!" she bowed.

Han Li smiled charmingly. "Does that mean you love men?"

Wei Yuanfen felt a clump in her throat, but taking a deep breath, she said, "I have never been in a relationship, but I am searching for someone worthy of marrying… I have a very peculiar Spark that would require me to cultivate with a man in my later years. But I have not yet found him."

Yue Li's eyebrows shot up. Her son, on the other hand, chuckled.

What else could it be other than destiny?

Liyue lacked a nice friend around her own age group anyway. She should understand.

[Wei Yuanfen] [Age: 21]

[Current Cultivation: 3rd Awakening, Love Spark]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Third Awakening, Love Spark]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Evolved, Love Dao]

[Death: (in the last premonition) At age 29, she died fighting a Beast who attacked her village.]

Han Li grew a bit wistful.

Love Dao. Another Dao under the Great Dao of Life like the Lust Dao. And since she was here, it also meant that she was a Sacred Talent Cultivator like himself.

"Wei Yuanfeng…" Han Li muttered. "What is your spark?"

She hesitated for a long while before saying, "Love Spark."

Han Li was calm, unlike her expectations. "You needed to love someone or something intensely for you to raise your cultivation talent, too, right? Since you already have Sacred Talent, who did you love so much?"

Yuanfeng sighed. She knew she couldn't keep anything if she wanted to be someone's disciple. Her cheerful face became downtrodden.

"I don't really like thinking about it as I like to keep myself cheery. My mother was a very kind person, but she passed away three years ago, and my goal is to one day revive her. Uh… I know it sounds ridiculous, but I want to try."

Han Li looked at her. He then looked at his mom, who was sympathetically looking at her.

"What do you wish by becoming my disciple?" he asked with a solemn tone.

The girl looked at him with a smile. She looked quite charming.

"I want you to teach me the basics. I am confident in myself to learn the rest by myself. And… I don't mind it if you try to make me fall in love either since one of my goals is to fall in love with someone…"

The man in front of her, Han Li, couldn't be too bad of a person since he had a mother with him. She still had suspicions about whether he was a Power Born or not, but even if he was not, she could still learn the basics. That was enough.

And he was quite handsome, even though he might be older than his appearance, she felt she might fall in love with someone like this. Then, her cultivation speed would soar!

Han Li looked at her and then at his mother. "Mom, what do you say?"

Yue Li blinked. "Seems like a good girl?"

Wei Yuanfei sighed in relief. "I shall bow nine times!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 157 - 157: Thousands of Sacred Talents
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Walking through the white-mudded expanse filled with gigantic trees, Han Li and Yue Li were followed by a very springy Wei Yuanfen, who had officially kneeled nine times to him in order to become his official and first disciple.

The girl seemed to be very familiar with forests, and seeing Han Li leave a lot of tracks behind while walking on their way, warned him: "Master, when you are in forests, you should always remove every trail you leave behind. Even if you don't have any enemies…"

Han Li raised his eyebrows. "Why is that?"

Honestly, if the invincible bubble weren't protecting him, he would have removed such trails as he was very averse to leaving behind clues of himself. But such a young girl's perspective was always good. Maybe he could learn something?

"Donno why. My Mom used to tell me to do that," The girl replied, making Han Li sigh.

Yue Li was beside them, and she was carrying Little Ruo, who was still in her deep sleep. With a questioning gaze, she asked her son, "You told me the trials would begin here, but there is nothing going on… just another forest."

Her son shook his head. Now that she was focused on him, Yue Li could see that he had wrapped his sword in some clothing like a sheath and that it was hanging on his back while his face was, as usual, calm.

He spoke softly, but against the silent forest, it was enough to be heard. "The trials had already begun. By the way, let me tell you something that would make you proud."

Blinking to herself, she looked at him curiously.

"I saved a baby earlier. Put him in a safe place inside the protective bubble. He should be descending back to where he came from when the trial results arrive. If I didn't help him, he would've probably been snake food."

Yue Li smiled, feeling warm. "As long as there is a little spot of compassion in your heart, there is always a hope of redemption... The monk said so. He must be wiser than you, so don't just ignore his words."

Han Li laughed and looked up at the sky. It was a white squarish sky.

What good? What evil?

What redemption?

"The trials have already started, Mom. The current trial, for that matter, is getting to your teammates if you are planning a Teamwork Trial or getting yourself ready if you are entering a Solo Trial. So, what do you say? Do you want to play solo, or do you want me to play with you?"

"Of course I want you to play with me," Yue Li said. "And you did tell me it would be a piece of cake for us to win as long as we play teamwork if it weren't for Ruo… now I can just eat this fruit," she showed him the brown fruit that the Monk had given them. "And I will be able to put Ruo away."

Han Li nodded, muttering to himself. "True, teamwork is obviously much better for us. It will be easy to gain the rewards. Maybe we could even go further than anyone…"

In his original plans, he would be a Power Born, at the very least, by the time he arrives here. But now that the Heaven Rising incident happened quicker than usual, he could only sigh and do what he could with what he had.

If his plans had gone as they should have, he would be about as strong as anyone inside the Demon World, and he could lead his mother all around, dominating the place.

In that state, he was sure he could do much more than what he could now, so his actual goal of completing all 7 of the Trials was now impossible, in his current form, if he played the trials with his Mom as his Teammate, the first three trials would be generally easy but the next three would be harder than if he had played solo.

In his last lifetime, no one went past the 5th Trial. He himself had failed in the second trial, but he knew who had reached the 5th Trial, that was Monk Hen Shin himself, and after returning to the continent, he was called the strongest Power Born of the Era.

"But Master, then what about me?" Wei Yuanfen asked, raising her arms.

Han Li looked at her, even though she looked like a man at a glance. To him, her femininity was clear. Her arms were lithe, her lips too pink, her purple eyes seemingly a cauldron of curiosity, her face too fair.

There was a different charm to cute girls wearing male robes and acting like one. Especially if the cute girl had an issue with her own cleavage being too small.

But even now, he felt as if the girl was sizing him up.

She was somehow a literal wild catch.

"You?" Han Li chuckled. "You going solo is enough. You won't like the team trials; Mom and I could do it because we are very close people."

"What do we have to do in the Trials?" Wei Yuanfei asked with a curious tone.

Han Li sighed. "I don't know, since it is individualized, everyone has a different trial. But for couples, it should be easy to complete the first three trials if what I know is accurate."

Wei Yuanfen's eyes widened. "You guys are not a mother and son?"

Han Li smiled. "Of course, she is my mother. But who said we can't be a loving couple?"

Wei Yuanfen's footsteps slowed down. She hesitated for a second while thinking, 'Do people from big cities have a different culture? Eh… how peculiar. So does he have actual… Se.. Uh… That with his mother?'

She couldn't bring herself to ask it, and they walked forward in a contemplative silence. They saw many people, with interesting looks and attitudes, and many men looked at Yue Li as they passed by; she was a really beautiful woman, so it was natural. Just like that, Han Li also received some intriguing glances from women as he passed by them.

They were all young Sacred Talent Cultivators. Now that they were all safe and back in their comfort zone, their focus started to shift from benefit to romance, and seeing people like Han Li made them feel good about themselves a lot. It was natural.

They were part of such an incredible group of men and women, and compared to them, the people still stuck in Sacred Lust Continent were all just mobs. They were different. They had immense pride in themselves.

Passing by some of them, Han Li would briefly glance at them. There were many whom he knew, many of them who would be incredible cultivators years from now. If he wanted, he could try making a sect or something similar with just his knowledge of the future's extraordinary people who were just beggars now. Yet, Han Li didn't feel like it.

That was quite the waste of time since he himself could teach his woman to be more extraordinary than them. He instead thought about Trials.

The Trials were set by the Sacred Demon of Lust, after all, and as far as Han Li knew, that man was mesmerized by the emotion of lust, and in everything he did, there was a tinge of lustful touch to it. Even though, in the end, there would always be a grander goal hidden behind such intentions.

Of course, he was not too familiar with that person. He just had some hunches about him.

In his past life, the Second Trial was something he obviously had almost zero percentage chance of completing at that point in his life, so he wanted to do better than that.

In the past, his Trial One was for him to resist the seduction of 1000 illusory beauties, something impossible for youthful males, but as he was too depressed about his mom's death, that was easy; his thing wouldn't even rise even if he wanted to at that point.

Trial Two was the harder one for him. It required him to fall into lust entirely and completely lose his rationale, and then return with an intact mind. But of course, he couldn't fall into lust at all, and with cold eyes, he returned to the real world.

But he later came to know that people could enter as teams and that the more the teammates loved each other, the easier the first 3 Trials would be. So, his mother and him entering the trials should make it a piece of cake ready for them to take a bite.

He was a hundred percent sure he stood no chance against the Trials after the third one in his current state, but he didn't mind. His good brother Yang Wei was also working for him to get extra rewards. That was enough.

They walked through another part of the forest, and Han Li came to a pause.

In front of them was a city wall, at least ten times as big as Southern Lust City's wall. It was a dark monument that seemed to have stood the ages. In the bright white mud and surroundings, it seemed eerily special.

Han Li's eyes sparkled. Many people were entering the city from all around, and around three hundred meters surrounding the whole city, the forest parts and becomes a clearing. From all parts of it, like little ants, hundreds of thousands of people calmly walked into the great city.

Unrestrained laughter echoed out from the side as a young man walked by Han Li and said calmly, "As I expected, you didn't fight. You really have such a strong weakness, don't you?"

Han Li calmly looked at him. His gaze was deep.

"Yang Wei…" Han Li said calmly.

Yang Wei chuckled eerily, his pale eyes looking at the thousands of bright faces, all of great talent and upbringing, before he focused intently on Yue Li. Who looked back at him with discomfort.

"Your weakness, Han Li, such an obvious one!" With unrestrained laughter, Yang Wei didn't stop walking as he moved forward, soon mixing into the crowd as he vanished into the gates of the city.

Han Li calmly breathed out while both Yue Li and Wei Yuanfei sweated behind him, their hearts pounding inside their chest. They felt as if a blade of bone had gone through their necks as the man passed by.

Even Han Li himself had a solemn gaze.

"Heart Qi…" he thought to himself. And even though Yang Wei was much weaker than himself, he knew that if they were to fight a battle of Heart Qi somehow, he himself would lose horribly.

Han Li had a lot more powerful Heart Qi in quantity and quality, but Yang Wei was like a perfected swordsman, whereas he was just a young boy with a big sword that he couldn't even heft easily.

'The Spirit…' Han Li's cold eyes shimmered. 'I really want to see your memories.'

He had a feeling that The Spirit was important.

Then, he shook his head. We are in a battle of who grows stronger, not who grows faster. Yang Wei was already defeated when I read his memories; no need to be so agitated by his taunts.

"Come, let's enter... the City of the Sacred Demon," he said, taking his mother's hand while patting his disciple's back, breaking them out of their daze.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 158 - 158: Crouching Tigers, Hidden Dragons
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and his gang walked into the city, just one among the thousands of faces. Even though their gang appeared rather extraordinary, even among the rest of them, at one glance, they appeared to be the weakest gang to take advantage of.

But sadly for the fine big brothers who were looking around for great sisters to lust after, everyone had a bubble protecting them. They could do nothing but watch as they went by them, and for unknown reasons, they couldn't even feel lust looking at the gang.

Every single person currently entering the city through the gateways spread across the Great Wall was an extraordinary talent, and the city was awash in a feeling of elitism and exclusivity.

All of them looked at each other with a sort of pride brimming in themselves. They were in the air!

And by now, many of them had learned where they were—inside the Empty Space of the legendary Sacred Demon!

Other than the few Power Born who were extremely experienced old monsters, the others couldn't help but have their noses go a bit higher. They would certainly look at people in the real world in a different way from now on, and many of them would find out that it was their downfall. As for Han Li himself?

He walked in through the gateway, his red pupils moving around as his Life Vision scanned the people he knew or didn't. Some of them interested him, while some made him curious. On the other hand, some even made him pause with a rather thoughtful frown.

As they finally entered the city through the crowd, they finally took deep breaths. The city was huge, and the buildings around were far greater in architecture than the seven continents. Huge temples stood here and there, their roofs all facing the heavens like spears, while ordinary apartment buildings made of mudstone stood tall all around.

Just at a glance, it was clear that they were all old and unused, but the newly entered people started to storm into the buildings in search of treasures. Han Li didn't bother with that since he knew there was nothing here that was magical in essence, just old furniture and such things.

Yue Li and Wei Yuanfen were still rather frightened after feeling Yang Wei's cold and calloused aura slicing through them, so they were still a bit pale, but Yue Li was relatively better compared to the latter.

She had a Heart of Cultivation and a rather solid outlook on life, after all, no matter how naive that might be. She could even be said to have seen deaths with her own eyes now, and she even had Han Li just beside her, so she wasn't too shaken.

Han Li's disciple, however, was extremely shaken, and her face was utterly pale. But nothing too deep as the bubble even protected her Heart from damage, or else, she would have had to go through the same thing Yue Li had in the past.

"Xiao Li," Yue Li called out, her voice soft but audible to her son. "We also saw that kid in the forest before we found Little Ruo, right? You smiled at him then; what's up with him?"

Han Li looked at his mom. With one hand, she was holding on to little Ruo, while with the other hand, she was holding on to his hand. The girl was small enough to allow that, and Yue Li herself was quite strong physically.

"His name is Yang Wei. If you had to say Monk Hen Shin is a good person, Yang Wei is the exact opposite. He wouldn't blink when killing infants, and he certainly wouldn't hesitate to break the moral code. He is a Demon through and through."

Yue Li took a deep breath. "Then why didn't you two fight if you were enemies?"

They did meet in the forest, where fighting and deciding their final winner was such a simple matter that they could decide then and there. Then why did he and Yang Wei just laugh at each other and run away in different directions?

Han Li laughed softly.

"Of course, he and I both knew we would fight soon enough, and the winner would be decided. But there are hundreds of other geniuses here: he wanted me to kill the others since he believed I was superior to most here; he wanted me to get as many benefits as I could.

"As for me? I wanted him to do the same so that when we fight, I can have everything that is his. That is why I told you you have to love such enemies; they are very predictable compared to the erratic revenge seekers and such."

A bit rattled, Yue Li walked behind him, biting her lip gently.

They were literally seeing each other as pigs for slaughter, making them as fat as possible before doing the deed. Her hold around her son's hand tightened.

Earlier, seeing all those extraordinary Evolved men and women, some who even tried to flirt with her, she always felt a bit contemptuous when comparing them to her own son. Her son was the best of them all, and he could defeat each of them in a battle if it came to it. Entering this world, he had only grown stronger.

She had sensed the Aura he released after he had his Heart Qi becoming stronger. It felt extremely overpowering. But now that she felt the icy killing intent in Yang Wei, she finally realized how strong his enemies were, too. She felt scared thinking about how her son would have to fight such people.

'I have to get something to trade so I can be helpful… so dangerous…'

She bit her lip gently.

Meanwhile, Wei Yuanfen grew calmer and listened from the side in awe and admiration. 'So this is how great figures of the cities are, how great. I also want to make enemies who acknowledge my strength and then laugh at them…'

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 159 - 159: Han Li's hate?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Suddenly, Han Li paused once more and entered an alleyway where there was no one, and when they came out of it, there was a person missing from their group. It was Little Ruo. Yue Li had eaten the Life Type Fruit—Kangaroo Space Fruit and put Little Ruo inside, and when they came out, she could easily notice her son's gaze had changed.

It was now colder. She even felt as if she saw an uncontrollable sense of hate briefly flicker beneath his gaze before he calmly walked forward. She looked at what he was looking at with that hateful gaze, and, just as she wanted to look clearly, she felt her hand being pulled by him and vanished into the crowd.

Yet, she still saw what he had seen, unclear as it was: A destitute man in tattered beggar robes, with a rough grey beard and dirty long hair tousled and falling to his sides. That man was lying against a wall, a bottle of alcohol in his hand, and he appeared extremely dirty.

Just looking at him would induce a sort of disgust, and he was lying on the ground without doing anything, just like a beggar. Among all the extraordinary men and women, he was a stark contrast. Only a rare few had such quirky appearance with so much strength in them.

Soon, their forms mixed into the crowd of people, and Han Li breathed out.

"Who was that, Xiao Li?" Yue Li asked.

Han Li looked at her for a second before he sighed.

"An enemy. Little Ruo's family's dog. Someone who was a major reason why the girl had to go through such suffering…" His mind was filled with an image now. The image of a lifeless Ruo hanging on a tree.

He could never erase that thought, and below her was a letter addressed to him—"Betrayed by the person I love, there is no longer a reason to live."

His heart would have struggled to calm down had he not had a heart as strong as his.

After his mother died in his past life, he was a man who had lost everything. He had no ambition, and he was far from being capable of acting cheerfully like Wei Yuanfen. He was depressed, heavily, heavily depressed.

But even at that point, he had Ruo, who was an extremely precious part of his life. She was his first wife. She was his everything at that point. His beacon of hope.

Yet, the whole Fang Family schemed against Little Ruo and him. They wanted her dead. She was a stain that if it came out, everything of their reputation made with decades of work would go down the drain.

If Han Li was not the slave of the Wisdom Seeking Villa with impressive war achievements, he could have been killed by them outright, but in his quest to climb the ranks in the sect to get a lifespan-improving resource for his mom, he had gained some significant backing.

But along with that, he had gained a great number of enemies.

That beggar-like man was one of these enemies.

He was not yet a person of the Fang Family, just a distant relative who had yet to have an impact on Ruo's life, but he would get a mission in the future: Kill the white-haired girl beside Han Li of the Wisdom Seeking Villa in a way that leaves behind no clues of your existence.

And he would do exactly that, stealing Han Li's Ruo from him in the most ruthless fashion.

He used an illusion of magic power on Ruo and brought her out of the sect using a person who had Han Li's appearance and then coldly told her to die before he came back. He coldly showed Ruo the one thing that she feared the most.

The opposite part of the Gu in her heart.

If the Fang Family crushed it, she could be easily killed. Yet, they didn't want to kill her like that since it would leave tracks for Han Li. So, the person in Han Li's form just threw the worm at Ruo and calmly told her to disappear and no longer be a burden to him.

That person used his means to thoroughly break the bond Han Li and Ruo had and Ruo, devastated, took the only path she knew forward—death.

Betrayed by her only reason for living, she had no reason to live. She was also heavily depressed after Yue Li's death, so she could only look on as a rope on her neck tightened. Even her normal thinking had been affected by magic powers.

The Fang Family really didn't want to make Han Li their enemies, but they didn't know he had the Life Sword; with a bit of Ruo's blood, he came to know everything that he wanted. That beggar was the person who killed Ruo.

The main reason he had chosen to put Ruo into the Empty Space now was because of familiar faces; from now on, they might see people from Ruo's homeland, and if someone recognized her, that would be troublesome.

But even if someone had seen her earlier, it was fine; no one would think Han Li had a Kangaroo Space Fruit in his hand, nor that he was planning to rescue her. Everyone would just ignore her as she was already safe in this place.

But people from her clan, like that beggar, who might or might not recognize Ruo, might ask him to hand her over. He had no fear of that person, but it was better not to let that guy see Ruo since that would immediately put him on the Fang Family's kill list.

Even though they were far from each other, that clan had a Power Born as their head, and Han Li, as he was now, didn't have any backing like the Han Li of the future. He would be killed within days, and they wouldn't hesitate to use the other part of Ruo's Heart Gu to kill her.

So, the fact that he had saved her for now was just ephemeral. To completely save her, he would have to fix her physical heart into a normal one, one that had no binding to the far-away Fang Clan. He knew exactly how to do that and Monk Hen Shin's appearance made it much easier.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 160 - 160: Han Li's Enlightenment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The City of the Sacred Demon. What was so special about this place?

Han Li stood in the central part of the city, above one of the numerous buildings scattered across the city's kilometer-long existence. Looking towards the actual heart of it all, Han Li couldn't help but feel a slight sensation of awe.

There was a great Statue there, its size so vast he couldn't see its full form from where he was standing. Under the statue's legs, he knew the Sacred Demon himself wrote some words.

He had read that in his past life, it read: "A mere attempt at mimicking the greatness of the Sacreds, yet the one who sculpted this one was a great one, the most striking of all statues I had seen, yet only half my size."

A sudden breeze lifted his lustrous black hair, and his gaze moved up. The statue's head would have surely pierced the clouds had it been in the real world. Han Li's eyes shimmered. He couldn't even make out the face from where he was. It was way too vast.

"So, that was how the Sacred Demon of Lust looked?" His disciple said from the side, full of awe and wonder. "He would have destroyed cities by just walking past. Is that even stone?"

Han Li didn't reply. He was contemplative now. Such a great being, born at the primordial times of the whole Seven Continents, the embodiment of the Life Great Dao. Yet today?

He was nothing. Sacred Demon had long died. And what about his death?

It was no different from the death of an ant. Today, he might not be forgotten, but a time would arrive when even he would be forgotten. Then, his existence would have nothing differing from an ant that had died; both would just be ash.

Living for one day, for ten days, for one thousand days, for ten thousand days, or even for one hundred thousand days was the same. As long as they died, they would soon be the same as any. In due time, their marks in history would vanish.

Only eternal life, complete control of life and death, and only by breaking out of the grasp of death entirely would you be any different. Han Li suddenly said particularly to no one, "I don't want to be like the Sacred Demon… he is too weak."

Yue Li and his disciple looked at him with wide eyes from both of his sides.

"The Sacred Demon is weak…" Wei Yuanfen frowned, feeling that her chosen master really was a bit too arrogant. Yet, in the end, she also looked at the statue in the distance with her chin raised and chest straight.

Yue Li, on the other hand, couldn't help but feel his conviction. His goals were not one bit unserious; he was rock-willed at achieving them. Suddenly, from atop the skies, from atop the distant head of the statue, a slow ringing sound started to echo.

It was rhythmic, like the sound of an ancient bell.

Han Li said, "Mom, from now on, everyone connected in one bubble without any evil intentions against each other will be forced to enter together as a team to where the Trials will be held, that is, inside the Statue, which is in fact, a tower of sort in the shape of a statue."

Yue Li got close to him and held his hand while his disciple looked at him without knowing what to do. Han Li smiled at her, "My mother and I live in Southern Lust City; when you reach back to the continent, come there. Take the trials on your own and try to win as many good things as possible."

She scratched the side of her head. "Can't I just come with you guys? Seems complicated. And I haven't even heard of Southern Lust City before… It should be very far."

Her teacher smiled. "If you have no issue with having intercourse with me, you may follow us, but I don't think that would be a great choice for you."

Wei Yuanfen smiled awkwardly. "Yeah, I don't want to give my virginity to someone I don't love…"

Han Li shrugged. "Or you could lose the opportunity inside the trials and enter my Mother's Empty Space along with Ruo, but it would certainly be a waste of a great life-changing opportunity."

"…Would I have to fight in the trials?"

"Probably, but not sure; it's personalized, as I told you earlier."

Yue Li asked, a slightly teasing smile on her face, "Girl, why don't you just enter with us? Xiao Li is really gentle, you know? It even feels great. He has many tricks even I can't completely handle yet~"

Wei Yunfen was stunned.

Can such a beautiful woman's mouth speak such crass words?

Her face reddened, and she mumbled with a bit of aversion: "Shameless! City people are so shameless! How can you be… He is your son!"

Yue Li laughed, hugging her son's waist as he looked at her curiously and wrapped his hand around her waist. Han Li patted her cheeks while she looked at him intriguingly and purred, "It's exciting to do something that is not dangerous for once. What do I get for being a good mom?"

"What good mom? You ruined half my benefits on this trip; such a pity."

Yue Li looked at him, bemused. She muttered in his ear, "Only because I was with you could you trade for the things you wanted, so I deserve some appreciation. You can't really call me a bad mom after that, can you?"

Her son only planted a kiss on top of her head. His expression dismissive. Instead, he looked at Wei Yuanfen. "What did you decide?"

Wei Yuanfen made a sour face from the side but then decided to sigh.

"I really don't know what I want… I am just… uh, master, you should guide me. If you were me, what would you have done?"

Looking at the confused girl, Han Li pondered for a second and spoke about something else.

"On the way here, I paused when I saw many faces among the thousands in the crowd. Do you know why?"

"… I do not."

Because, of many of them, some had Sparks or Dao Souls that made him have second thoughts about many things.

In the Awakening Stage, people had a Spark; in the Evolution Stage, that Spark became a Minor Dao Soul, and in the Power Birth Stage, it becomes an Intermediate Dao Soul. And the path was predetermined at their birth, what Dao they followed: someone born with a Shit Spark was destined to walk the Dao of Shit.

And yes, Han Li had indeed known many such quirky sparks existed in the world long ago, but on his way, he saw many such sparks that made him think deeply. He had, in fact, even seen a woman on the way here who actually had a Lust Spark. If she was not an actual slut, he might have even been interested in her.

Even his own Lust Spark, which had grown to become a Minor Dao Soul of Lust, was nothing but one of the infinite Daos that others could be born with. The fact that the Sacred Demon had given it to him didn't mean it was solitarily his own, but this fact gave Han Li enlightenment that he had been missing.

[Talents]

[Low-Level Lack of Lust]

[Low-Level Fantasy Inducement]

[Low-Level Sensational Barrier]

[Low-Level Seduction]

These were all his talents now, and even if people chanted its words, they couldn't use them, even if they had a Lust Spark. So, what if he entirely absorbed all the Magic Powers in the Lust Dao and made the entire Lust Dao his own?

Then wouldn't those Sparks and Dao Souls be following 'his' Lust Dao?

So if they use Magic Powers, would they pay Dao Crystals to him instead of paying the heavenly consciousness to whom the Lust Dao previously belonged?

Can he even let them use it?

Just like the Heavens? The consciousness who replies to chants?

Wouldn't he literally be another Heaven, then?

After all, it was hearing the chants and taking its fee to lend its powers to normal humans. So, will he become the lender?

"On the way here, my disciple," Han Li said, "I paused, looking at them because I became curious. What if everyone here reached their full potential, their maximum potential, and then died? Was their lived then worthy of having been lived?"

"…Was it?" his disciple asked, her face frowning.

Han Li shook his head. "No, it wasn't worth it as their efforts would end up being vain anyway, but was it not worth it to them? That's up to them. Then I realized it is not that everything impermanent lacks meaning; it is just that it lacks meaning compared to the vastness of reality in itself.

"Even that giant, to it, we are ants, but who says there are no bigger giants that look at it like an ant?"

"Eh… That means?" Yue Li asked, seriously thinking. To no end.

"So, returning to the question, what would I have done in your shoes, little disciple," Han Li laughed. "I would enter the tower if I had a beautiful master of opposing sex who invited me. The world is so vast; who cares about your virginity? You might as well give it to me."

Wei Yuanfen was speechless.

Even Yue Li was speechless as she looked at her son.

A long while passed as the rings from high up reverberated louder and louder.

Biting her lips, Wei Yuanfen shook her head. "I don't want to lose it to someone I don't love, sorry, master. I just don't want to. Even though I do have interest in you, I do not feel love."

Han Li smiled. "So be it. The best you could do in the Trials is pass the first one in your condition, and if I were you, I wouldn't lose a teacher with experience over a random treasure that could be good or bad. If I were you, I'd enter my mother's Empty Space."

She hesitated a bit.

"Are you confident of your teachings?" she asked.

Han Li frowned. "That's up to you to decide, but the one disciple I am proud of, who is also my wife, could totally wipe the floor with you if that's enough, and she is in the same stage as you, yet less talented."

Wei Yuanfen bit her thumbs. She really felt interested in the trials…

Han Li then looked up, his mother's waist tightly held under his grasp as her filled boobs pressed against his chest. He suddenly carried her a bit into the air, and out of instinct, his mom wrapped her perky legs around his waist while wrapping her hand around his neck. She looked at him with stars in her eyes.

With a deep sigh, Wei Yuanfen asked, "Would I be forced to do anything I don't want once I am out of this bubble's protection and we arrive in the real world?"

Yue Li laughed. "You are underestimating my son's charms. And also, no, he won't force you."

"Can't he say that himself… for guarantee?" she asked.

"You look like a man. I barely feel interested in you..." Han Li said in irritation, "And you know what? My mom saying something is the same as me saying it."

His half-hearted disciple finally walked up to them, and Yue Li pointed at her. Usually, she didn't have the ability to bring anything into her Empty Space; only Evolved could do that, but as her red spark came out and her hair became red, a soul sense wrapped around the girl, and a second later, she vanished.

Yue Li looked at Han Li. He smiled at her. Some time passed, and the Trials would begin any second now.

"How is your Empty Space like right now?" he asked.

"The whole 5-meter area of the space inside is filled with a red mist, and in the middle, two people are lying beside my Spark, both unconscious after entering it."

Empty Space it was a very intriguing thing. It was a space filled with nothing like the universe was said to be before anything came into existence. Honestly speaking, even Han Li wasn't entirely aware of what it was; all he knew was that it was a space inside all humans from the day of their birth.

Yet, even if someone dissects a human body, they won't find anything inside, as if the Empty Space itself were in a different dimension altogether from the physical body. It was an enigma. He had to learn from Ruo and his disciple what it was like to be inside such a foreign place. He had never used the Kangaroo Space fruit in his past life.

He had to learn more of it. But not now.

For one last time, the ringing sound rang out again, and from his high vantage point, Han Li looked down at the roaming masses all across the city. Everyone was looking up at the sky, he looked up too. The Trials, it begins.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 161 - 161: First Trial of the Seven Trials and its Reward
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Sacred Lust Continent, Southern Lust City.

As the sun dipped under the horizon, the chicken in the backyard clucked around the feed that someone had thrown around. Beside it, Liyue stood, a long stick in her hand as she impassively trained in spear art. She was already a High Proficiency practitioner of the art.

Her movements were like a gentle bird, her hands an elegant display of fluidity. Sweat matted her forehead, her black hair floating behind her as she practiced different forms and ways of offense. Preciseness, quickness, reach. Strength and unpredictability.

Spear was all those things. For an Awakened, her Spear was nearly perfect. For an Awakened, she was far beyond the average; she was highly nurtured in the path of the spear. There was nothing in the world that any normal mortal could do that she couldn't do; she had ingrained all sorts of spear moves into her body.

Yet, she was not a Master. Only if she became a Master could her training as an Awakened be completed, only that was the remaining part of the Spear. Her movements slowly calmed down, and she slowly breathed out. That seemed far from her grasp; so far, it didn't seem achievable at all.

She could already do anything with the spear; what made her different from the Masters was that she didn't have Han Li to give her an answer.

Li Yao looked at her from the doorway to the kitchen while two other women stood to the side with awed expressions. One of them sighed. She couldn't believe it.

"Even extremely skilled cultivators would struggle to teach someone as young as you this well. You are such a talented girl," The woman said, her face full of suspicion. "It is not just High Proficiency. Your foundation is so utterly stable it is terrifyingly well-learned. To the point that even old people with years of experience would be horrified."

Composed, Liyue looked at the stick that was acting as her spear. Her mind was contemplative, her heart filled with questions.

It had been four days since her Brother Han left in the middle of their marriage; he had told her he would return in three days.

The city's turmoil had long since calmed down after that weird red mist enveloped everything, and she, her mother, and Hua Feilan had come out of hiding right after the red mist vanished. Everything returned to normal even though Han Li told them to stay in hiding till his return, which he had promised to be three days.

There was a simple reason for them coming out: it was Hua Feilan's friend.
