Growling, his thick and disgusting cat tail smashing against the wall in humiliation, Nu Kai jumped towards Han Li. He was indeed fast.

Before Han Li could react, a furry fist landed on his chest, and he was blasted onto a stone wall. His organs and skin were fine, but his shoulder bone cracked slightly, and he hissed in pain as he looked at the man.

Yue Li yelped in surprise and began running for him until Han Li showed her a calm hand, saying 'stop,' and stood up. Had it been any other stone wall, he would have been covered in rubbles, but this place had almost indestructible buildings, atleast for his level of people.

"You are fast. You should have pushed the attack," said Han Li.

"Your sword is too sharp. I don't want to be hurt," The man replied calmly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 170 - 170: Lot of Magic and a bit of Muscle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You are fast, you should have pushed the attack," said Han Li.

"Your sword is too sharp, I don't want to be hurt," The bald Nu Kai replied calmly.

Han Li chuckled and stood up, his legs wobbling. Inside, his shoulder bones were broken and his flesh mangled. Yet, unlike the other party, he couldn't regenerate.

Two cold, scarlet eyes looked at Nu Kai. Han Li walked towards him, his overpowering casualness that was always hidden behind his actions was now gone, a cold, callous aura of a snake looking at its prey traveled like a slithering sensation across the enemy's scalp.

Nu Kai calmly but hesitantly looked at Han Li, his face dark. His confidence in fights were not any good, he was in fact, not a person who liked fighting opponents of his own level. He felt a bit afraid of his opposition and he wanted to end this already.

Like a blur, the bald monk who appeared to be in dark fur zipped through the air and once more appeared near Han Li, his claw was poised to strike his head in a vicious punch. Yet, suddenly, he felt as if an overpowering aura of danger devoured every part of his being, a sort of fear of looking at an absurdly higher existence when he was just a small ant.

Heart Qi, overpowering aura. Han Li didn't know much mechanics of this power, yet, he knew how to frighten people with it. A black sword slashed akin to the moonlight, heading straight for Nu Kai's neck.

Blood would splatter, scarlet would inundate. Nu Kai would die. Yet, nothing of those sorts happened as the sword paused at the man turned beast's neck. A unique pair of lizard-like scales had appeared there. Completely blocking the sword.

"A Protective Magic Treasure!" Han Li exclaimed and kicked the monk away in a hurry, he knew he won't be able to swing the sword again before Nu Kai came to his senses and launched a counter. His Heart Qi was not all powerful.

Nu Kai smashed on to the stone wall this time and he coughed up blood. "You can use your Magic Power in this place," he hissed in pain.

Han Li coldly looked at him. He couldn't find a way to defeat him.

His opponent still thought of him as a Transformation Type cultivator. All Transformation Type Cultivators would practice one Magic Power in the early stages of their cultivation and that would be their only Magic Power throughout their life unless they become Transcendent.

That would be a Magic Power that adds on to the said cultivator's strength or one that removes a weakness. Even Yang Wei must have a Magic Power like that but Han Li had no idea what that Power was. That was the one thing that was unknown to him about the man named Yang Wei.

The black sword was flourished once more and Han Li coldly said, "So you have a defensive treasure protecting you. Did you get it in the trials?"

That thing was only on the neck, he could easily tell with his discerning eyes, but around the neck, even Power Borns would find it hard to hurt Nu Kai.

But the monk on the other hand shivered slightly. "This place blocks all sorts of Magic Powers, I can't use mine and there is no way you can use yours. You also have a treasure that makes people fear you, right?"

"Is that so? I wonder." Han Li once again pushed on his Seduction Talent. This talent, he had never used it to its full potential. The monk's eyes turned red but he wasn't completely on track yet. Nobody could be so easily seduced in such an intense situation.

At the same time, Fantasy Inducement constantly made Nu Kai's head spin with incoherence, and his right ear in particular, seemed bad at times, often losing its hearing, yet, he didn't think Han Li was the reason for that. In his mind, it was his own inexperience in fights that was the cause.

Enraged and afraid of what would have been if his neck had been slashed, Nu Kai became even more cautious, and he walked around Han Li with the intention of another quick move and attack.

He didn't know why Han Li hadn't yet transformed into his beast form, but he could tell that Han Li had a really thick skin for some reason. No attack of his had even left a scratch on the man's skin, that terrified him to no end. Han Li had obviously hurt him many times.

What he really didn't know, however, was that one of Han Li's shoulders was already incapacitated and broken, but it was just that Han Li could endure and swing it around without much of a moan. Even though cold sweat still matted his back.

Han Li was obviously at a disadvantage.

Time passed, and they just continued their pounce and react attack form. Many times, Han Li was blasted on the wall by the enemies' quick moves, but only one more of his bone was broken, one of his left ribs, and that didn't impede his fight. For two hours straight, they continued doing the same thing, finding no holes in the opponent.

Many times, Han Li cut a lot of the man's skin and flesh, but soon, it would regrow, and the fight would continue. The only thing he had accomplished of note was cutting off the pinky finger of Nu Kai, which couldn't regrow.

He could only regrow flesh but not bones!

Thus, their fight entered the next round. Nu Kai felt his head spin slightly and he couldn't help but find Han Li more and more arousing. He couldn't help but think Han Li's sword was beautiful. Another cut arrived on his chest, and breathing heavily, he jumped back and looked at the wound on his chest before growling.

Soon, it healed, but Han Li smirked. He was obviously ten steps slower than he was at the start. All those Talents were getting used, and in such an intense situation, Nu Kai was also taut mentally and physically, so his stamina was also in a very precarious situation.

Smiling to himself, Han Li continued, and soon enough, Nu Kai had his eyes turn blank, and he watched with a gawking gaze as the dark sword sunk into the side of his waist and then his bones before suddenly coming to a halt as a bubble shaped thing surrounded him and he unwillingly cried out as he was devoured by red mist and vanished.

Han Li sighed in relief and excitement, his heart beating calmly as he looked at his Mom who was in the distance, looking at him while seated in a corner, popping the earlier cherry-like fruits into her mouth, her face filled with boredom.

These physical brawls were interesting, but after two hours and a bit more, she had lost interest. Han Li wasn't in danger anyway. She decided she might as well consider it a movie to watch. Of course, she did sweat a lot in the middle of the fights when Han Li's hand limped here and there. She had a nagging feeling that he wasn't as healthy as he appeared.

"A tree that would bore us a Life Fruit every six months, what else can we wish for, Mom?" He sighed as he walked towards her and reached half a hand away from her. Then, his expression suddenly changed, and he looked around the room, and his heart fell to the bottom of his stomach.

"No…" He muttered.

Yue Li blinked. She looked around the whole stone room, but she didn't find anything worthy of being worried about. The door to the next trial hadn't even appeared yet. What was he so worried about?

Then, her eyes widened. She was not so foolish as to not see why his face had become so pallid so quickly. The door not appearing meant that they hadn't completed this 4th Trial's test. A drop of sweat fell from Han Li's temples, a foreboding sensation around him.

Then, he chuckled wryly. "I have been so deluded by wealth and greed again, my eyes couldn't see the truth right in front of us."

In his past life, only one person had made it to the 5th round.

That was Monk Hen Shin, someone who accomplished significant feats, particularly being the only individual to have reached the 5th Trial and referred to as the strongest Power Born of the Era at his peak. There was no way it was that easy to enter the 5th round.

Even if he had failed, Nu Kai would have entered the next room. And he knew from the news that Monk Hen Shin was the only one in the fifth round, and it was said that even he had failed the task. So, the reason was obvious.

Fourth Trial, more than any of its predecessors, was far from easy. This was where everyone who was ordinary in the Sacred Talents of the Sacred Lust continent would be sent back. Most probably, Han Li knew that would also include himself.

Why?

This trial was obviously supposed to be fought with Nu Kai and himself as a team and they had straight up considered each other as enemies. He had been deceiving people here and there, he was deceived by himself. He wouldn't have made such an amateur mistake usually, Han Li composed himself.

Someone was trying to play with his own head.

Eyes calm, Han Li looked around with his life vision and in one particular area of the stone wall, noticed something he hadn't earlier. It appeared the same as anywhere, but hidden in an invisible form, there was…

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 171 - 171: The world was unforgiving.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Eyes calm, Han Li looked around with his life vision and in one particular area of the stone wall, noticed something he hadn't earlier. It appeared the same as anywhere but hidden in an invisible form. There was something that made his pupils shrink.

Even Yue Li felt a foreboding sensation around her nape as a stone statue-like thing walked out of the wall. It was humanoid, and soon, it opened its eyes. A very human-like pair of calm eyes, a unique dark pair of eyes that shimmered with cunning and curiosity, and a smirk on its stone-like face that was full of ridicule.

Han Li trembled slightly, his breathing growing heavy and his sword growing utterly defensive. His heart also beat faster than ever after regressing back with future memories. He snarled, "We meet again, Unawakened."

The rock-made man on the wall tilted his head. Slowly, as if water was flowing out of his skin, the rock faded away, and his true appearance was revealed, making Yue Li's eyes widen while Han Li grew apprehensive. The face was far too familiar.

This face had been in Han Li's dreams and his thoughts all the time for the past few weeks. He had always been thinking of this man. He had been mulling over so much, and now that the culprit was in front of him, Han Li couldn't help but feel a sensation of foreboding.

Starlight Clan Head's direct son, Li Shuanwei. The man who had been the center of all chaos in Southern Lust City. The man who had made Han Li move out of his comfortable cultivation area. A cold sensation of unpredictability hung in Han Li's mind. This one person, Han Li, had no idea what he even was.

The young man's appearance now resembled the signature appearance of a Starlight Clan member—brownish black eyes that seemed to reflect the shining stars of the night sky and hair a deep black, which he had tied behind him in a small bun.

"Didn't think I would meet a clan mate in this place. How intriguing," The man sounded lazy, almost too relaxed. That was scary. Especially scary after seeing the whole battle Han Li had just fought with Nu Kai right in front of him. Han Li's mind rang with alarm bells.

Regardless, his words were calm. "Li Shuanwei, a filthy rich young master of our areas. A close son of the Starlight Clan Head, a silk pants who had often been said to be idiotic or stupid. He was by today more than 30 years old, but the man hadn't even Awakened for once yet. An unawakened adult.

"Not because he had some kind of issue regarding his cultivation—the man just hated cultivation, supposedly. Someone who intentionally avoids all kinds of conflict no matter how they come. A man who likes to sleep at home rather than wake up to eat. A person who is said to be the epitome of laziness."

Blinking, Li Shuanwei rubbed off some remnant mud on his pristine white clan robes. "You seem to really know me. I am proud that a genius with Sacred Talent who had Evolved at the tender age of eighteen remembers me."

Han Li's hair stood on end as the man's gaze focused on him for a second before moving to his mother. He couldn't understand why. A profound sensation of crisis hit him with just a glance. His mother must have felt the same as she paled where she stood.

"Technically speaking, she and I are friends from the same generation, you know?" Li Shuanwei asked. His gait was still as languid as ever, his gaze lazy. "She is just five or so years older than me. I still remember the days every one of my generation was on her back, trying to woo her. How nostalgic."

Yue Li stood behind Han Li, hiding herself. She did not have much of a recollection about this person even though she knew him vaguely as she had seen him.

Seeing that, Li Shuanwei sighed with a saddened expression. "When everyone bullied me for being Unawakened at my age when I was thirteen, you once consoled me. It felt good. I just remembered it. You must have been just a year or two older than me then."

Yue Li hid behind Han Li and said softly, "Sorry, but I don't really remember you. I know your appearance but nothing much."

Han Li calmly said, "You don't have to respond to him. He is making a fool of you."

His own gaze never left the person nor even blinked. His Life Vision was still the same, no difference whatsoever from the past Li Shuanwei he had used Life Vision on. (Chapter 108)

[Li Shuanwei] [Age: 30]

[Current Cultivation: Unawakened]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Unawakened]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Unawakened]

[Death: (in the last premonition) At age 50, a renowned poet who grew enchanted by a woman's beauty, only to get dumped and commit suicide.]

He was obviously a weakling, yet obviously, he was not. Han Li had never met someone like that. And didn't he have a Starlight Spark? What was he doing using something like the power of the Rock clan to hide among the rocks?

And the Trials 'Artificial Intelligence' as Yue Li had coined thought only himself and Nu Kai combined stood a chance against this person.

"Your head is spinning with thoughts…" Li Shuanwei squinted in curiosity. "I almost want to kill you to nip all your secrets out already. That sword is nice. Your body isn't half bad, but you are supposed to have the Starlight Spark. No, you are supposed to have no sparks at all. My head is spinning now.

What curious subject I have had in my own backyard without knowing at all."

Han Li calmly laughed.

"A cultivation base I can't sense no matter what I try, powers that are not of the Starlight Spark. Is it a Magical Resource that hides you in a rock? Yet why do I feel it is not? And also tell me, why were you the cause of all that bloodshed that happened in the surroundings of our city? Do you know how many innocents died due to you?!"

Li Shuanwei sniffed. A languid expression on his face, he said: "Another dog that I should have killed long ago. You see, I had everything down there: money, power, and everything. Yet, I didn't have women. Killing you should get me some, right?"

Han Li's gaze condensed. With a cold gaze, he readied for battle, but Li Shuanwei just shook his head and chopped the air before him with a slash. Han Li expected a lethal attack, but nothing came; his heart dropped, and he looked back.

His mother's painful scream echoed all the way in the room as one of her arms was slashed. Crimson exploded from her shoulder, and she coughed up blood.

Han Li's heart thudded, and he shouted. "STOP!"

Li Shuanwei stopped midway in his second chop. He wouldn't be able to kill the woman right away due to the protection of the bubble, but taking another hand wouldn't be trouble. Yet, curious, he stopped. What will Han Li do to stop him?

He had expected Han Li to do something ridiculous, but seeing what Han Li did next, his mood was ruined.

He moved towards his mother without any care for the enemy and showed the same enemy his back. Li Shuanwei frowned in disgust and was about to attack when Han Li coldly looked back and took the knife he had gotten as a reward in the past trial away from his Mom's hand. She was bubbling in pain and breathing heavily.

His mother's left hand was gone, and her eyes were wide in shock.

Han Li's heart was as cold as an abyss. Yang Wei had made him frustrated but never murderous. Li Shuanwei, Han Li's red eyes, seethed murder. He hadn't even been able to see the attack, and he still didn't know what had happened.

It was far too quick. And it was neither a Magic Power or Heart Qi. It was a sort of different power. It was not any Talent either. It was something entirely different from the usual Cultivation.

He doubted it could hurt his thick skin, but his mother was in far too much of a danger. She was already losing too much blood. She wouldn't be able to survive.

"Li Shuanwei," He suddenly said and caught the man's curiosity once more, making him stop midway through his second chop. "There is a saying I had gotten used to hearing a lot." He stood up and flourished his black sword, placing it near his mom.

He held the knife with his other hand, placing it exactly around his belly.

"I don't know what power you just used. Neither do I know what kind of freak you are."

Many had told Han Li that in his previous life.

Li Shuanwei's eyes burst with curiosity.

A chuckle almost escaped him as hair began to stand behind his back.

Exhilarating!

Did it feel this good to make others so desperate?

This was a first.

Yet, the next second, his jaw dropped.

Han Li's knife sunk into his own stomach, which he himself found a bit irritating to pierce through, and his sword gently pierced his mom's belly. Just enough to bring the bubble out.

Two bubbles appeared and wrapped up around themselves. Han Li coldly said, "I shall wait for you below. And I will kill you there."

Dark crimson smoke devoured them, and just before they vanished, Han Li took hold of his mother's severed arm, and then... they vanished.

Li Shuanwei felt his nerves bulge.

A second later, he spat, "Trash." And looked towards the side of the stone room. Another door had opened. The door to the fifth trial. With a frown, he calmly headed there. With his father's help, it won't be too hard to kill such trash when he was back home.

Yet, the things that must be happening down there also gave him a slight headache. The Power Born Demonic Ice Fairy must be still in wait for him to head down. But he was still happy; this trial was the first one to give him such a rare treasure.

A tree that can bear a Life Type fruit every six months.

Meanwhile, unlike the Heaven Rising, which was full of grand shows, heading down was simple and straightforward. His stomach was bleeding, and a knife sunken into it. With his mother in tow, Han Li appeared back inside the forest surrounding Southern Lust City.

Bathed in a three-meter-long wave of red smoke, he quickly ran out of the place and entered the nearby cave where he and his mother were before they rose to the heavens. Laying her on a mushroom-like bed, he asked, "Mom, can you listen? Is it too painful?"

She held back her sobs and looked at him with a blurry gaze. "Can.. still feel the pain."

Han Li gritted his teeth at the sight. Her stomach was also bleeding, and she was weak, and her blood was losing.

"Mom… It's nothing, don't worry. I will get you standing right away. We have a Life Type resource at hand, after all. Nothing to worry about. Keep sane and trade it using your system, we are back home too. Come, there is no issues."

He really hadn't expected a power to bypass him and appear behind him. The fact that Li Shuanwei was so demonic was also news to him. Yet, his eyes burned with danger. He had never been so infuriated.

His mother was on his back and he still failed to keep her safe.

For the first time, Han Li felt the pain of being a True Demon. To fight their own betters while having their inferiors to protect on their back. This was hell for the ambitious.

Yet, as his mother numbly traded for the apt resource he told her to, he couldn't help but compose himself. He had to heal her first and foremost.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 172 - 172: Calm before the storm?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After making his mother trade the Life-type resource in their hand for something that could heal and mend broken limbs, Han Li waited for the resource to arrive with bated breath. The Life Fruit in Han Li's hand had already vanished.

Han Li took a deep breath.

He was a cold man, a man as cold as ice. Time had made his soul one made of obsidian. He could endure pain himself to a point even the other Transcendents would be stunned if they knew it. He was cruel to others. He could kill anyone other than a few without even blinking. Yet, he was just as cruel to himself.

He could, without batting an eye, hurt himself to achieve what he wanted.

Yet, seeing his mother squirming in pain as blood dripped through her clothing, he couldn't help but grit his teeth. Yet, he consoled himself: 'This is just temporary. Everything temporary would be fine when the time comes. Impermanent things are worth as much as an ant; do not linger on pain. Search for the solution.'

Crrrrrr!

The space in front of Han Li, the literal atmosphere, trembled slightly. A drop of golden liquid fell from the sky out of nowhere and transformed into a hot red fruit. Han Li was stunned and didn't know what was happening at first before he realized it. Yet, he didn't take the time to contemplate and quickly took the fruit and moved to his mother.

Feeding her the fruit, he put her severed arm close to the bleeding stump, and she cried out in pain. He didn't have to wait for long before a searing red flame started to burn his mother's arm.

It was hot and painful, and Yue Li lost consciousness in the middle of it, but her hand was soon fixed and healed, down to her very bone fibers, and she was sleeping with ragged breath that turned calmer as time passed.

Han Li sighed calmly and his forehead sweaty, sighed.

They were in a cave now, and it was nearing night. The chill in the air was a bit discomforting, and the room was laden with shadows. In the middle, the mushroom bed was now covered in his mother's blood, yet she herself was now healthy. Her blood would be replaced by the healing energies in the fruit she had eaten.

His head slightly pounding with relentless questions, Han Li focused his gaze on thin air. The fruit, this time, had appeared out of thin air. Usually, something happens after the Luck Chat System is used, and as if reality and fate in itself were being tampered with, what he wants appears in his hand through coincidences.

What was the difference this time?

He knew it.

Magical Resources were said to be normal fruits and normal resources that had been blessed by heavenly energies. And then blessed by the Great Dao. It was a myth as old as the Sacred Demons, and there was no way to prove it, but after seeing what he had today, he had a theory.

The Heavens, as he had coined the consciousness that lets people perform Magic Powers, bless the fruit or other resources with a power of its own. As in, if it blesses a normal fruit with the power of Life Great Dao, it becomes a Life Type Resource. And this should be the case for every individual Dao.

Including all the 7 Great Daos—Water, Air, Earth, Fire, Darkness, Light, and Life—And the countless normal Daos ranging from Lust to anything else. All these Dao, in the end, belonged to the Heavens, and it was that entity that should also be blessing normal fruits and such to be so valuable.

So, the event of the immediate birth of a Life-type Fruit as a trade using his mom's System meant that the System in itself might be something that was even beyond the Heavens itself. Thinking of these, his thoughts returned to Li Shuanwei.

What was he?

He didn't use Magic Powers. He didn't use Heart Qi either.

Was it a Talent like Han Li himself?

The power stolen from the Heavens to make it his own?

Han Li didn't know, but he had a feeling none of these were the case. He was missing something. Something crucial. Just like how he still didn't know why the Heaven Rising had happened so soon. That crucial piece would tell him everything.

He almost felt like a beast that had been trapped inside a dark room for all his life; such a creature wouldn't know what light was. But Han Li already had a plan, and he knew he had to be quick. If Li Shuanwei were to fail in the Trial, he would be back in the real world in two or three more hours at the most. Yet, before doing anything like that, he also knew what he was missing.

His own shoulder bones were broken, and the flesh inside was mangled. Red eyes bright, Han Li carried his mother on his back and walked out of the cave. Then, he moved to a nearby pond and washed her off of blood. She briefly woke up, her head feeling like mush.

Han Li made her put both his sword and her knife into her Empty Space, and she once again fell unconscious.

Clean of blood, other than the still reddened clothes, hair and body wet, Han Li then moved towards the Southern Lust City. His eyes were calculating, and his mind was in a slight overdrive. He knew he was in an extremely disadvantageous situation with Li Shuanwei as his obvious enemy—Li Xingguang was his father, after all.

A Power Born could find and kill Han Li, as he was now, even if he went into hiding.

And the mere fact that Li Shuanwei knew his secrets alone made it a paramount need that he was dead. No matter what, as long as he lived, Han Li couldn't sleep calmly.

As the shadows of the forestry passed by, just before the dawn sun vanished into the horizon, Han Li reached the city gates and entered it without a second's waste. With eyes a calm pond, he passed by the Evolved guards and soon entered the crowded streets.

Heart just as calm as his gaze, he entered Starlight Zone and, passing the Evolved guardian of the wall who looked at him with a surprised, borderline stunned gaze, he went directly to his home. The door was locked, but Han Li pulled out an iron key from his own Empty Space and opened it.

Entering inside, he didn't see Liyue or Li Yao and was a bit worried at first, but he spread his Soul Sense towards the kitchen; the utensils were wet, and they seemed to have just eaten something. Then, he briefly walked near and found four women in the backyard—Liyue, Li Yao, Hua Feilan, and an unknown woman.

He would have used Life Vision on her, but he now had no time and didn't go to greet them. No one could sense his Heart Qi-infused Soul Sense, either. So, without waiting, he went into his room and found it neat and tidy. Han Li smiled.

Then, he gently laid his sleeping mother on the bed and took out the Demonic Lust Sutra book from under the bed. With a slightly pained frown, he touched his shoulder and sat to a side. Inside his Empty Space, he now had [3000 Dao Crystals] that could be used by any cultivator of any Dao and [121 Minor Dao Crystals of the Lust Dao], a total [3121 Cystals].

He only needed 1000 for him to achieve one more Transformation. Even though that would be his limit at the Evolution Stage, one more Transformation would atleast double his current strength.

He had planned to use these crystals to help Liyue and his mother achieve a better physique before breaking through to the Evolution Stage, but he no longer had such a choice. He would have to do that some other way.

He had set a time limit for himself already: in 2 hours, if what he was thinking of was wrong, he would have to run with his family. He knew a bunch of escape ways.

He calmly flipped to a page in the corpus left behind by the Lust Demon. The ultimate guide in the path of cultivation he had yet seen.

Skin Building, Tendon Stretching, Bone Forging, Blood Condensation, Flesh Transformation, Organ Strengthening, and Physique Perfection.

Of them, he had already completed Skin and Organ, while Physique Perfection was only possible to be trained as the last Transformation. Now, his choices were Bone, Blood, Tendons, or Flesh.

The bone would give him improved strength and defense, making him almost unkillable by Evolved. They can't harm his bones, his skin, and organs. How can they kill him?

He would be invincible under Power Birth.

Atleast by conventional senses. Unless they had a weapon as sharp as his mom's knife or had Mastery of a weapon that would allow them to cut through his skin like he himself could cut through.

If he chose Blood Condensation, he would gain regenerative abilities and could heal even better than Nu Kai from the fourth trial. He could heal every part of him that is already Transformed. He would be able to heal his skin and organs very quickly. Yet, it wouldn't be able to heal his bones, flesh, or tendons. So, that was out.

Then Tendons. If he transformed it, he would be far faster than Nu Kai. He would be like a blur to almost any Evolved in the entire continent unless they have a Light Type Beast Transformation of their own. Yet even then, he would be nearly on par even if they were Sacred Talents.

Then there was flesh. Flesh would give him regenerative power, which wasn't anywhere nearly as good as blood, and a bit of defensive prowess, too. Yet, he would still be vulnerable to bone-breaking attacks.

So what should he choose as his last body Transformation in the Evolved Stage?

His head spun. And soon, he knew what to do. Indecision was vital, after all.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 173 - 173: Choice of Transformation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li took a deep breath. His choices were laid bare. He could either choose the Tendons for achieving an extreme level of speed or he could choose the Bones to achieve an extreme level of defense and offense. Almost no other Evolved in the entire continent would stand a chance against him in a fight if he chose either of them.

Flesh was too weak defensively and too lacking in regenerative powers. Even the strength addition by that Transformation was subpar at best. But Han Li did have some curiosity towards it since in his past life, he had cultivated 5 Transformations and knew all of the 5 Special Abilities that he would get after Transforming them.

The only two Special Abilities he didn't know of—which should awaken after each completed Transformation—were Flesh Transformation and Physique Perfection. All of the rest, ranging from Skin, Organs, Bone, Blood, and Tendons, he knew what Special Ability awaited him in the end.

Just like how he knew Transforming his Skin would give him Vibration and Organs would give him the ability to absorb Stamina. He quickly came to a decision.

Bones would be a really good addition. He had enough Dao Crystals to immediately finish his Transformation but at the same time, one thing made him hesitate. He just couldn't go on to choose the option due to one significant thing.

Should he do it?

There was no room for hesitation in this world, but he hesitated.

In totality, it was believed and known that there were "300" Magic Powers in each normal Dao and "3000" Normal Daos under all Seven Great Daos.

So, if he could cultivate and use enough crystals to use every Magic Power among the 300 under the Lust Dao, ten times each, and turn all of them into his Talents, would he then come to own the entire Dao?

Which would better help him through this current predicament—either escaping or going through other ways—was it a better body or the complete ownership over the Dao of Lust?

Would he become stronger than if he used the [3121 Crystals] in his hand for something else? He just needed a thousand for the current Transformation, but he wouldn't be able to gain all three hundred Magic Powers as his Talents. Sweat beaded on his forehead.

The choice was vital. What would give him more power quickly?

One was unknown, and one was known. A long minute passed. The room was silent, his mother's breaths were calm. Night had fallen now, so the room was soon in a dead silence. Hesitation. No matter how experienced one got, who can say proudly that they wouldn't ever hesitate?

With one deep sigh, Han Li closed his eyes. There was no need to hesitate. His eyes snapped open, and he skipped to a particular page.

"Attain bones similar to mine by cultivating this method to perfection—Bone forging."

When in need, relying on luck had never been his style. What if gaining all 300 Abilities was not good but instead something bad? What if that would cause him to—atleast temporarily—lose all his powers?

Even thinking of it had been a wrong move. His palm moved, and he tore the silk-like paper containing the Bone Forging methods. He scrunched the paper up into a ball and to the size of his palm. The paper vanished from his hand.

His consciousness flowed into the Empty Space. His Minor Dao Soul of Lust was, as usual, bright and semi-transparent. In the dark world, it stood calmly, faintly glowing organs visible under its semi-transparent Skin. It was growing more stable by the day, almost like a red version of himself.

All around, there were specks of bright crystals present, some red in color while some transparent.

He chanted an ancient verse in the Bone Forging paper. It was a long and complicated verse. A spell of sorts.

The crumpled paper moved into the Minor Dao Soul and soon began to morph amidst the organs, slowly moving itself into the position of the bones. The spine, the ribs, the every other bone. The bone marrow running inside the bone to the outer layers of the bone was like iron bars tightly wrapping around his insides.

It connected to the organs, to the skin, to everything. It was like a boneless, essence-less thing was suddenly puffed by air. His Dao Soul became substantially more solid and almost moved out of its semi-transparent mode. Almost.

Then, with a startle, he opened his eyes to the real world and bent over on the bed. His nerves grew taut. They bulged. His face grew ashen. First Transformations were always painful, but bones, especially, were a hurdle unlike any other. Time passed, and pain filled him to his core.

In prior Transformations, tearing the skin away for a new one wasn't that hard. Organs changing, he had ended up unconscious.

Yet, the Spine changing and skeleton changing was the worse, and his head spun, and his gaze darkened right after the pain passed. With a blurry gaze, he fell on the bed.

…

It only took half an hour for Han Li to return to himself. He took weak breaths and sat up from the bed, sweaty. He was stinking, too—adventures in the wild for more than three days wouldn't be easy to wash off with five minutes in a pond. He was stinking badly. Yet, who cared for smell?

There had been a time when he hadn't washed for 17 years straight. This was nothing in comparison. He took deep breaths, focused on his hand, and used Life Vision. His eyes spun slightly from the lingering nausea.

_

[Han Li] [Age: 18]

[Lifespan: 420 Years]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 121]

[Neutral Dao Crystals: 3000]

[Bone Forging: 12% Completion]

With calmness, he used 879 of the Neutral Dao Crystals, and the process was complete.

[Bone Forging: 99%]

[Bone Forging: 100%]

His skin reddened slightly as an influx of energy coursed through his bones. His whole body felt a sort of warmth and relaxation. His broken shoulder bone and rib had already been fixed, but the flesh in his shoulder was still mangled, and it was a hindering thing. Yet, he calmly closed his eyes. He seemed to shine in an ethereal red light for a second. His everything slowly ascended.

From cell to bone to organs to other parts, as a virtuous cycle, he became stronger. With a deep growl that echoed across the room, mist seemed to escape his mouth as he clenched his hand. Air blasted out of his palm in a sort of explosion.

For the first time since he had regressed, he felt slightly, ever so slightly, strong. A chuckle escaped him as he once again used his Life Vision.

[Han Li] [Age: 18]

[Lifespan: 420 Years]

[Minor Lust Dao Crystals: 0]

[Neutral Dao Crystals: 2121]

[Talent: Sacred]

[Body: High-Tier Evolved Physique]

[Bone Forging Completed—Forged Bones—Fused Blade]

His Body had become High-Tier from Above Average. That was his base stats.

His bones had been tempered to a limit. His bones were now similar to those of the Sacred Demon. The mighty and perfect creatures born in the Seven Continents.

One had to know that even Sacred Talent Transformation Type Cultivators would only have [High Tier Evolved Physiques] without Transforming into their Beast Form. When they Transform, they would become Top Tier in all categories of the body. Surpassing the Top Tier in some of them, like Nu Kai, surpassed the Top Tier in the Speed department.

Han Li surpassed Top Tier in Endurance. His Stamina and Defense were on a level of their own. His Strength was also faintly above the Top Tier now. His Speed was still barely touching the mark for High, but if he used his Magic Power—Elusive Movement—that would become Top Tier. The only thing he lacked was rejuvenation.

In simpler words, he was already Top-tier in everything except Speed and Rejuvenation for an Evolution Stage Cultivator. He even surpassed the top tier in endurance, strength, and stamina. Yet, in the regeneration department, he was just High-Tier.

Yes, his body did have above-decent regenerative powers now, but that wasn't something that would help him in a fight. He could heal big wounds in hours or something, but that was useless.

The Sacred Talented Transformation Cultivators were all like that. Only when they transformed into their Beast forms would they be able to have top-tier instant healing prowess. He lamented slightly at the sole remaining weakness of himself that was so glaring.

Even though Lust Dao was a Dao under the Great Dao of Life, it lacked a decent regeneration Magic Power when he was not in a bed having sex. So it was natural he lacked an option there. He sighed.

Atleast, he was far stronger now.

He looked out through the windows. There should still be about two hours remaining, at the very least, before Li Shuanwei returns. And he was also not supposed to be in the city when the Heaven Rising began. So he will have to continue with his plan—A plan which, if successful, would let him stay in the city and maybe even get rid of the threat from the bud—or, if it didn't work within an hour, run.

Chapter 174 - 174: Green
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

His eyes calmly roamed the dark room, and he stood up from the bed. He stretched a bit, making popping sounds echo out of his body, like numerous gears switching into place. With a calm gait, he looked into the mirror in the room.

His appearance hadn't changed much, but it had changed. He had become leaner. Slimmer. His hair was still pure black chaos above his head; it had become healthier at a glance, and normal people would struggle to even cut his hair; it fell to his shoulder, still having a bit of blood sticking to it on the edges. His skin still looked sun-tanned. His eyes were like blood, scarlet.

On the middle of his forehead stood three rather eye-catching tiny little red mole-like marks.

Each mark was a mark of completion of one of the seven Transformations—Skin, Organ, Bone. His body in itself felt as if it had more substance overall. An unsheathed blade seemed an apt word to describe him.

A smile curved his lips. Strength indeed came with beauty. He wasn't one to say no to that. Even though he wouldn't have minded the opposite.

He then looked at his hand, at his [Talents] list using Life Vision.

_

[Talents]

[Low-Level Lack of Lust]

[Low-Level Fantasy Inducement]

[Low-Level Sensational Barrier]

[Low-Level Seduction]

_

Lack of Lust allowed him to manipulate his own or other people's lust and make it void. To a state of lack. Fantasy Inducement allowed him to make people have brief moments of thought-lapse, making them forget something. Sensational Barrier allowed him to bar people's senses for a short time. Seduction allowed him to seduce others.

The most overpowered and signature Power of the Lust Spark was honestly Seduction once used well.

He hadn't needed to use it like that, but it was true. If it was used to its limit, slowly, people could be completely changed to seduced slaves by their own emotion of lust and, in turn, to his slave.

It was much more potent than drugs.

Yet, it was useless for Han Li at the same time.

Low-level powers only worked on Evolved and lower-stage creatures. He would need Medium Level Powers for him to use on Power Born and High-Level Powers for it to work on Transcendent.

He contemplated for a while.

His Talent list was already quite good, but it was still lacking. For offense, he mainly used his sword since no Magical Power of the Lust Dao could come nearly close to his prowess with a sword. But Fantasy Inducement always helped him to confuse his enemies.

For Defense, his skin was better than anything else; now, he also had his Bone supporting that. What he lacked now was a Movement assisting Magic Power, and he also had something that he wished to test. With a thought, he walked beside his mother and checked her forehead. It was a bit hot due to the injury earlier, but the fruit had done its magic on her; she was completely healed already.

Sitting there beside her, he began chanting the verse for the Magic Power Elusive Movement. He pained at the wastage of the Neutral Dao Crystals, but it was best to be as strong as he could be now.

What the Magic Power Elusive Movement did was that it manipulated the lust of the people around the user of the Power and made their senses hallucinate that the user of the Power was moving elusively like a blur. And even though it didn't directly improve his speed, it was rather useful still. He wasn't faster, but it would appear so.

A total of six Crystals were used before the chant stopped working, and the Magic Power vanished from all of the Seven Continents. Even if some other person with a Minor Dao Soul of Lust tried to cast the Power, they would only fail.

At the same time, another Talent was added to Han Li's list. It was akin to an instinct of his own. With a thought, without any Energy loss, he would be able to easily use this Power from now on.

[Talents]

[Low-Level Lack of Lust]

[Low-Level Fantasy Inducement]

[Low-Level Sensational Barrier]

[Low-Level Seduction]

[Low-level Elusive Movement]

He was almost ready for another battle now, but at the same time, not yet.

His shoulder area had completely ravaged flesh, and he was also bleeding internally. It would be hell to treat him traditionally. His skin would be too tough to operate on for normal people in the first place, and he didn't want to go to the healing area inside the Starlight Zone.

But he did have other ways to quickly heal himself.

He opened his mother's jaw and put two of his fingers into her mouth. A slick sensation surrounded his hand as she silently bit him on instinct. He smiled as a humongous portion of his stamina coursed through his hand into her. She must be feeling as if her whole body was in heat. Just about a minute later, she opened her eyes.

The first thing she saw was his hand in her mouth; she felt him play with her tongue using his fingers and, for another minute, stayed silent. All over her body, she felt as if he was hugging her. As if Han Li's aura of life itself was coursing through her. She groaned slightly.

Opening her mouth, he took his fingers out.

Groggy, she sat up with his help and muttered, "Another dose of stamina down my throat… my head is aching so bad I feel as if it would split. Xiao Li, where are we?"

"Maybe I injected a bit too much stamina. Should I take some back?"

She shook her head, trying to gouge where they were but failing to think anything comprehensive. "I feel like I'd fall right onto the bed if I don't have this much stamina. What do you want?"

"Give me my sword and all the important things that are in your Empty Space. Keep Little Ruo and Yuanfen inside for the moment. And also help me trade something. You can go back to sleep after that."

She blinked slightly with blurry vision and waved her hand and, one by one, took out what he wanted. He also took out the things in his own space. A long minute passed, and leaning closer to him, she kissed his cheeks with heavy breaths. She was feeling feverish.

[Luck Trade] A golden screen appeared in front of her.

He handed her one of the three Water-type resources that he had stolen in the Demon World from a rag-tag team by killing them using the borrowed knife that was the Power Born high up in the Sky—Saintess Moon Fairy. He had two more, but he kept it secure to the side.

One of them vanished, the trade having been made. She spoke softly in his ear just before falling into her bed slowly: "Don't get in danger, Xiao Li. Just sleep next to me. I feel a bit weak."

Han Li adjusted her form and caressed her cheek. Gently pinching it. "I'll try not to get in danger."

He knew the Life-Type fruit was still working inside her to heal her. Each and every part of her was healing. It was a good thing in a sense; even her hidden ailments would vanish when she woke up. But till then, she would be groggy and lethargic.

He, on the other hand, waited calmly.

Suddenly, the door to his room was pushed open, and a woman entered the room. She had a rather calm expression, pale face, green hair, and green eyes, in a green robe. There was a sensation of calm throughout her person. Just by standing there, she emitted an innately confident aura full of calm superiority to all other lifeforms surrounding her. She appeared cold and distant by nature.

Like a leaf billowing in the wind, her hair floated as she moved.

Han Li frowned slightly but looked at her calmly. Until his calm broke.

She was the woman that he had seen in the backyard alongside Hua Feilan, Li Yao, and Liyue. He hadn't bothered to even use Life Vision on her, but now, looking at her with it, his heart skipped a beat. No, it skipped more than two beats.

He took a deep breath and calmed down.

[Hua Yu] [Age: 299]

[Current Cultivation: Power Birth]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Power Birth, Intermediate Dao Soul of Moss]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Power Birth, Intermediate Dao Soul of Moss]

[Death: (in the last Premonition) At age 390, she was pulled into the depths of the ocean by a Beast King Octopus and then drowned to Death in the middle of her escape attempt. Suffocating.]

She was a Power Born he had heard about in his past life. He had never seen her, but he knew the name. He knew her famous Death. She had been eaten by the Octopus Beast King, but she was not one to die without leaving behind a surprise.

The whole body of that beast was completely filled with moss, and two days later, it floated above the sea like a dead husk devoured by moss. This woman was no simple person. Han Li exhaled.

Why was such a woman here?

More than that, however, was the hair on his nape standing up.

He had been too uncautious since this was his own home, but what was now spread in front of him was a literal haul of treasures.

Two Water Dao Resources were not that big of a deal; the knife was obviously extraordinary, and there were two beast cores. One from the snake he had killed in the Demon World and another from the vine he had killed when underwater before entering the Demon World. There was also a bunch of red fruits. Then there was his inconspicuous Life Sword. On the side was his Demonic Lust Sutra Book.

Had he known she was a Power Born, he would have known that she could sense him when he entered the home. Had he known.

Luckily, he hadn't pulled out the Neutral Dao Crystals.

Yet, his throat grew parched.

The woman looked at him, her green eyes full of serenity. Then, her gaze moved to Yue Li who was in servant robes full of blood smattering its surface. She couldn't help but frown her beautiful beautiful eyebrows.

Chapter 175 - 175: The nosy old crone, Hua Yu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The woman looked at him, her green eyes full of serenity. Then, her gaze moved to Yue Li. Wearing servant robes, full of blood smattering its surface. A long silence stretched between them.

"Han Li?" Hua Yu asked.

"I am," Han Li replied.

Her gaze lingered on the blood spots and very visible hickeys on Yue Li's body. There were a lot of details on her body that showed strain, a sexual experience that happened recently, and tiredness.

She saw even more details with a Soul Sense as powerful as hers: Yue Li was feverish, tired, and completely spent.

"You… forced yourself on her?" She asked, her face becoming a picture of disgust. They looked similar. So this must be his mother.

Han Li's heart skipped a beat. "Of course not. She is my Mother. We have a very unique relationship."

She didn't have much of a reaction to that other than a loosening of her brows. That was fine, then.

Her eyes moved to Han Li. He had blood spots too, but now that she looked closely, both of them seemed to have made a half-assed attempt at cleaning themselves, but they were obviously still unclean and filthy. Just that they had wet hair.

She had indeed been hinted before that Han Li had a special relationship with his Mother by Hua Feilan. Liyue had told it to her.

"Where were you till now?" she asked. She was curious.

Han Li's gaze turned cold as if he didn't know the person before him was a Power Born who could kill him without much trouble. He didn't want to be scraped inside out for his secrets.

Yet. No matter whether Hua Yu was an Ethereal or Transformation Type Power Born, he would be easy prey. He wouldn't be able to react to Medium Tier Magic Spells or even an Average Power Born Physique. He would die before he blinked.

In other words, mind your own business.

He knew she wouldn't fight another Power Born for a stranger like him anyway. Not that he couldn't use her presence in other ways. Maybe no, she might even fight for him. His thoughts spun.

"Hua Yu. Friend of your hired mercenary, Hua Feilan. She had far outworked her pay because of your disappearance. Thus you owe us an explanation."

Should she awe him with her power?

She had just become a bit worried when she noticed that Yue Li had woken up and quickly fallen asleep. Or she would have watched from outside a bit longer to see if something interesting was happening inside.

Han Li's heart skipped another beat.

Bone Forging completing had made a faint red aura appear around the area. She must have seen it. That was not that big of an issue.

Did she see his Mother's System working? Did she see how one of the Water Resources had vanished?

That could be an issue. He hoped she thought it was just put into the Empty Space. He thankfully had an explanation for that since his mother's Empty Space could hold it now.

He said calmly, "I do not have to knock since this is my home. Ask either Liyue or Li Yao, and they won't say this isn't a norm. I am not on to knock, and they do not mind it."

While speaking, he calmly covered his mom's body under a blanket. He had to bathe her, but she had to sleep for now.

"I have been hearing a lot about you and was curious what kind of man you were. Liyue's boasts and Li Yao's silent approval seemed to have been ill-placed. An arrogant young man, nothing more."

Arrogant?

Bitch, you entered my house. My room. Checked on me with your soul sense. Yet I am arrogant for telling you to mind your business?

But he knew, if he was more powerful, he wouldn't mind such things either. Manners and societal rules were just flimsy barriers for the strong.

The pressure vanished, and Han Li stood up. A wry smile on his face.

He was rather lucky.

She had noticed the knife's uniqueness and how sharp it was, but she didn't care for it, or at least it seemed so for now. Had she wanted it, he was ready to give it away.

Adding to the luck, she couldn't read the Demonic Lust Sutra without one of the seven Sacred Eyes. And she wouldn't have realized the book's specialty by peeping. His Life Sword seemed like any other normal sword. She would deign to lower herself to steal his Water-type resources. He had been unnecessarily cautious.

This was no longer the Demon World, where the world had suddenly split into two parts: kindness and greed. Here, people were tamer and more held back.

Even if she was a greedy woman—which he doubted she was—she wouldn't so blatantly snatch things from him. Unless, of course, she planned to kill him. Or she had a quirky personality. Which was often the case with these old women.

"Junior?" Han Li was 'stunned.'

Why would a young-looking woman like the person before him call him a junior?

She looked younger than even his mother.

A silence passed.

Then, he grew contemplative and sighed. But he didn't grovel. Powerful people didn't hate arrogant people, but they did have a dislike for those who groveled. As far as he could judge, this woman was like that. Judging could be wrong for sure, but he could take his chances.

"Apologies, Senior." He bowed slightly. "I don't know who you were. I am just too tired after what I have gone through, especially after seeing my mom so hurt... As you can see, if you use Soul Sense, she is healing; her hand had been chopped off by someone of my own clan.

"He could also arrive here at any time to remove grass from its roots. I am very worked up even now. So, you can't blame me. Please show mercy."

He already saw her as a tool that could help him get through his current predicament more easily than his first plan—She didn't really have to help him. He could use her in many ways.

Hua Yu squinted. Her Soul Sense traveled around Yue Li for a second, searching her hand for such wounds, and her eyes widened. "The aura of the Life Dao! She ate a Life Dao Resource!"

A brief flicker appeared in Han Li's eyes. There was no greed in this woman's eyes. Not a bit. What was she? A log of wood? Or was she that good at hiding her emotions?

Greedy would have been easier to manipulate. Yet, he sighed with a tired expression.

"Heaven Rising," he groaned in pain. His own shoulder was a work of wreck. Her quick soul-sense sweep also told her that this time. "We were risen to the heavens and then forced into the Demon World. Supposedly, the Sacred Demon's Empty Space. Then forced into ridiculous trials of murder and bloodshed.

That was where I was till now, with my mother. I won the resource as one of the trials."

Hua Yu came closer and checked Yue Li's forehead for heat.

She also began muttering strange verses, and a green light spread from her hand, slowly worming through Yue Li's body. Han Li didn't stop her.

[Medium Level Magic Power of the Moss Dao—Parasite Healing.]

Han Li was not familiar with the power, but he knew what kind it was by her verses. He could have been better at the language of those verses, but he was quite proficient in understanding the meaning. He knew it would heal and not harm.

Not that he would have been able to fight against her if she wished to harm.

"Very many gratitudes, Senior." He bowed down even more. "This junior has sinned by not giving proper respect."

"I wouldn't have helped after that, honestly. I would have just left. But Liyue's 'teacher' had left a big impression on me. You taught her the spear?"

Han Li took a deep breath, his cogs spinning.

"I did."

"What Dao do you cultivate? I can sense that you are an Evolved…" She wondered if he was 18 for truth or whether that was a lie, "but people around seemed to have considered you a Spark-Less."

Han Li himself had chosen to show her his cultivation by not hiding it using his Heart Qi, or she wouldn't have sensed it.

That would be way too obvious once Li Shuanwei comes back. Chaos was sure on the way; he might as well reduce the impact by hiding as much of his powers as possible. He might as well try to use this woman to his advantage somehow.

She really was an invasive person.

What is this woman?

Since he didn't want to tell this woman he had a body as good as his while being an Ethereal, that was the best choice.

Even as he healed, he couldn't help but scheme.

He knew if he tried to use petty schemes like the one he used on Saintess Moon Fairy, he would end up mossed to death. There was no doubt.

But he could tell she wasn't a completely insensible Power Born by his rather probing attitude of arrogance earlier. Maybe she might help him through this predicament by just asking honestly?

A meeting of geniuses arranged in heaven by the Sacred Demon?

How novel.

"Oh?" Her curiosity was picked.

She was a Power Born. She didn't have any fear of Li Xingguang, just genuine caution. If she could hoodwink him without any disadvantages, she was all in. Cultivation was an all-in game, after all. Of course, she wouldn't do it if she didn't feel confident.

"So be it. Tell me the plan. I have a liking for Liyue and I am curious. Yet be warned, a simple liking I gained for a girl in just one week won't make me put my life on the line. I wouldn't be bothered to listen if I didn't have my own benefits in this. Benefits that are solid enough to make me move."

Can he even give something like that to her? She was curious.

Han Li straightened.

A smile on his face, and a normal red cherry appeared in his hand. "This…"

She wanted benefits that could make her move?

Okay, I'll give it to you.

***

Night had fallen. Han Li's breath frosted as he walked through the Starlight Zone. His eyes were calm. He was going to go through with his first plan, which he had cooked up right after returning to the real world. He didn't know how well it would go, but it shouldn't turn disastrous.

Soon enough, he reached the central part of the Starlight Zone and looked up at the flagpole. This was the area where the Awakening Ceremonies used to happen. Yet, he didn't stop and walked around the area till he reached a manor. An empty but rather huge manor.

In front of the manor, a young man, someone Han Li was very familiar with, was standing. He had a bored expression and was cultivating in a lotus position. Hearing the noise of Han Li's footsteps, he opened his eyes.

His eyes immediately brightened. "Han Li!" he exclaimed, but then immediately noticed the woman beside Han Li who was walking hand in hand with him, like a couple. His eyes widened like two saucers, and he pointed at the smirking Han Li.

"You..you pull… pulled a…" Yet he immediately stopped and bowed down, beads of sweat forming on his back. "Exalted Power Born, My Clan Head would have been joyous to host a banquet for you if you announced your arrival."

Han Li smiled. Then feeling a tug at his hand by Hua Yu, he left her hand. That meant Li Xingguang had spread his Soul Sense here, and even though Han Li noticed it, it was a second later than Hua Yu herself. His Heart Qi didn't make his senses sharper. It only made his own Soul Sense harder to sense.

But being holding hands when his Soul Sense was spread and Li Xingguang having seen it, the seed was already in the soil. He just had to add some magical water once he met the man.

"Li Ming," Han Li spoke, chuckling in his tone, a perfect image of a contended man. "I heard your harem of men are becoming bigger by the day? You are faster than me in making your own gang."

Li Ming's eyes lit up. "You are also making a harem of men?"

Han Li's face darkened. "What?"

Hua Yu smiled slightly, as if Han Li being made a fool of like that made her happy. But then, a calm flow of fireflies suddenly jumped around them, like little stars, and formed a word: Enter. Li Ming immediately bowed even deeper.

Hua Yu walked past Li Ming with a curious glint in her eyes before Han Li walked past him, but he stopped and kicked the man on the way. "I only love women. Never repeat it." That seemed like a simple kick, but Li Ming was pushed quite far away, and with Li Xingguang's perceptive eye, he should have noticed Han Li was far stronger than a simple Spark-less.

Li Ming growled in grievance. He should go and vent his sadness on his new harem member.

Calmly, Han Li entered the manor of his soon-to-be enemy, a smile like sunflowers on his face as he quickly reached beside Hua Yu and walked into a room together with her. The hallway was wide and minimalistic, but the room itself was the most minimalistic of all. Just a simple room with nothing inside but a man seated in the lotus position.

[Li Xingguang] [Age: 230]

[Current Cultivation: Power Birth Stage]

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Power Birth, Starlight Dao]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Power Birth, Starlight Dao]

[Death: (in the last Premonition) at age 235, Killed by Yang Wei of the Bone Clan after inciting a war between the three clans of Raven, Rock, and Starlight.]

A man in white robes, his face radiating the energy of youth and his long black hair cascading toward the ground. He seemed like the picture of a mature man that women would dream of in their poetic dreams. His casual white robes flew in the wind.

Even though he had gotten interesting news, Han Li appeared as calm as a rock as he moved before the man. He didn't have to think of anything; for now, Li Xingguang was his target.

"Clan Head, this descendant greets you." He cupped his fists and, seeing the cue, sat in front of his ancestor in the lotus position.

Hua Yu walked beside them and took out a chair from her Empty Space before sitting on it. She looked down at both of them, a curious glint hidden behind her serene green eyes.

Both Han Li and Li Xingguang had their eyebrows twitched. Yet, they didn't address it.

"What do you visit for, Lady Hua Yu?" Obviously, Li XIngguang didn't have any reason to show him any face, and he didn't. To him, Han Li was just a junior.

Hua Yu shook her hand. "Not me, it's your descendant. Ask him."

Li Xingguang wondered inside with a lot of hesitation: 'Did this Han Li really make this old crone Hua Yu fall for him? She was already famous when I was born…' He didn't think so, but they had no reason to act. Why would she act like this? Unless, of course, she had brought other Power Born here to ambush him, which didn't seem likely.

After all, even if he was attacked 3 on one by Power Borns, he had the confidence to last till the other two Clan heads, Raven and Rock, came for rescue. Yet, he still thought of a worst-case scenario just in case and increased his wariness. No way this old crone fell for this young man. Even though he was his own descendant by a long distance.

Was he here to speak some kind of benefit? Why was Hua Yu protecting him? It was subtle but he noticed she had some kind of relationship with the boy. Is it because of Shan Liyue or Li Yao?

Hua Yu had been staying there, so of course, he had researched them all. Han Li included. Even Pang Li, the fat merchant and father of Liyue, who was in a distant city, was researched by him.

"Let me speak, Clan Head." Han Li flourished his sleeves. Elegantly presenting himself before speaking, "You should know that I healed my Mother-in-law, Li Yao, right?"

Li Xingguang raised his eyebrows. "Yes, I do remember that."

Han Li calmly waved his hand, and a Dao Crystal appeared in his palm. "Dao Crystals of Lust. I am an Evolved cultivator with the Dao of Lust."

Li Xingguang's eyes widened, and Hua Yu smiled, waving her hand as if she were removing some kind of magic cast on Han Li. Han Li removed the Heart Qi hiding his cultivation.

Now his ancestor could clearly sense Han Li's cultivation. An Evolved!

Li XIngguang's ascetic calm changed into the pressure of a star's light. "Many things seem to be happening in my own clan when I hadn't noticed. Why don't you elaborate now?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 177 - 177: Was this carelessness?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li calmly waved his hand and a Dao Crystal appeared in his palm. "Dao Crystals of Lust. I am an Evolved cultivator with the Dao of Lust."

Li Xingguang's ascetic calm changed into the pressure of a star's light. Like an oppressive heat pressing down on them, like gravity itself pushing them down. Han Li's face changed slightly but Hua Yu was calm.

"Many things seem to be happening in my own clan when I hadn't noticed." The Clan Head's expression was not so cordial. "Why don't you elaborate now?"

Han Li cupped his hand, talking while feeling the pressure mounting on him. He knew what this pressure was. The Soul Sense. Power Borns have Soul Sense that literally worked as psychokinesis, moving reality with thought. That was why they could fly in the first place. They used their Soul Sense on themselves.

"My father had the Lust Spark and instead of inheriting my mother's Starlight Spark, I was born with a Lust Spark."

The weight over him vanished. Smilingly, Hua Yu had placed a hand on his shoulder and her eyes had lost their friendliness. Li Xingguang's heart tightened.

"Continue," He said, looking at Han Li with deep eyes.

Han Li said humbly. "I wanted to train my Heart of Cultivation being in the middle of a place where everyone considered me a waste of space. I strengthened my Heart throughout the past few years."

"Do you see me as a fool, Hua Yu?" Li Xingguang's tone wasn't too friendly either. He usually disliked violence, but he wasn't so meek. "You literally used some kind of magic on my descendant and let him roam in my backyard. Do you think I appreciate that?"

Hua Yu sighed. "Listen to your descendant first."

The man's deep, night-like eyes moved to Han Li. Han Li continued: "I am terribly sorry that I had kept this a secret from you, Clan Head. I didn't wish to, but I had no choice since I had given my father a word that I wouldn't reveal my Powers till I turned eighteen. I began using it since then. I used my Lust Spark to eat up the Chaotic and Untamed Energy inside my mother and my mother-in-law."

Hua Yu raised an eyebrow inside.

So this guy had sex with them to heal them?

She remained outwardly stoic. Sons who had intimate relationships with their mothers were indeed rare but she had lived far too long to have her eyes widen.

Li Xingguang calmly looked at them. "Think as if I believed it, but then what? Why do you visit now? Are you leaving the clan with Hua Yu? Here to bid farewell?"

In his mind, he had already put Han Li's father on an equal level to himself. His father couldn't be one of the Transcendents since Li Xingguang himself had knowledge of each of the Transcendents' powers, and none of them had a Lust Spark.

There was no way his father was a Demi-Sacred since it had been millenniums since a Demi-Sacred was last seen in the world. An Evolved couldn't make Hua Yu protect a son who was in a distant land. The puzzle was done.

Han Li was the child of a Lust Dao Power Born and Yue Li, and that Power Born had made Hua Yu look after him in secret. Now that he was of age, he was being taken back after enough training. That wasn't too far-fetched.

Smiling inside, Han Li shook his head. "I am here to propose a slight deal, Clan Head. I can heal the entire Starlight Clan's people. Everyone who had the deficiency would be healed. I know mortals don't count for much, but they are indeed not so useless when in huge numbers. And in the end, this is my clan.

I had grown up here. I can't bear to part."

He had no attachment to this place. If he was strong enough, he would just kill Li Xingguang without batting an eye. Of course, he couldn't tell him that.

The Clan Head rubbed his jaw. "Indeed, that is a good deal." Especially since people would have more confidence in attempting to Evolve. Even if they failed, they would just lose their chance to cultivate and not have a normal life. "It is a good deal, but what do you want in return?"

Han Li bowed slightly. "How can I want something? I just want to live here for as long as I can, but my guardi— Lady Hua Yu, cannot stay protecting me here forever. I just wish that you would protect me while I am in the city."

Li Xingguang calmly looked at the duo. Who was the brat trying to intimidate by calling Hua Yu his guardian and stopping halfway? He was too wet behind his ears.

"Protecting you to my maximum capability when you are in the city is my responsibility." The Clan Head closed his eyes. "If that is done, you may leave. You can heal the clan members if you want to. If not, just leave it be."

Han Li sighed. "I know Clan Head would protect me, but I made some enemies in the Demon World. Just Evolution Stage, but… they just, atleast I offended twenty of them."

Li Xingguang's eyes opened widely. Shuanwei was also there. He wondered what he had gotten from there. Then, he also wondered what Han Li had gotten from there. Of course, he had no idea the duo had met in a Trial. He smiled for the first time.

"I hadn't wanted to probe you, but you really are a Sacred Talent. Unfortunately, you are not a Starlight Dao cultivator or I would have taken you in as my disciple. You also seem wise for your age."

Han Li 'ashamedly' looked down.

He knew Li Xingguan didn't really have much care for the clan. It was just an obligation for the man. If it was going to ruin, might as well move on. But of course, there were benefits to having a clan, like getting Magical Resources from the city all the time in a regular cycle so it was better that they survived.

"I am cursed to walk the immoral Dao of Lust. Clan Head," Han Li said, "I just wish that you would promise me that you will protect me no matter what unless a Power Born level or higher Stage cultivator who are your equals attacks me. And I also wish you would give Miss Hua some face and promise me that you won't leak any of my information out. As my Lust Clan has many enemies in the far west."

Hua Yu stood up and took a deep breath before bowing. "I shall be indebted to you if you help him. I would leave soon, but I will help you fight the Power Born after your son for once before I leave so you can intimidate her."

The Hua Yu an hour ago, would have never done this, but their earlier conversation had entirely changed the importance of this event for her. This was no longer so simple, if it went well, it could change her life for the better.

Li Xingguan was a bit startled. For him, Hua Yu was a legendary Power Born when he was just a baby. To see such a person kneeling, this Lust Dao Power Born must be really something. Atleast he must have authority.

What was this Lust Clan in the first place?

Why hadn't he ever heard of it?

"If a promise it is you seek, a promise I shall give. A promise on my name: I will not let any cultivator below my own Stage of Cultivation harm him. And I shall also keep him protected while he stays in my zone."

She added, "Please also add that you yourself won't harm him in any way, shape, or form unless he attacks you or something. Sorry for the distrust. I just like being cautious with such things."

He looked at her coldly. "You think I will harm a junior?"

Han Li nodded in his mind. 'I think so.'

She shook her head. "Sorry, this is what I wish for. If you do it, I will fight for you once or me and Han Li can only leave now and return him to the Lust Clan." That was true. If he rejected, they would immediately leave before Li Shuanwei reached here, and the Starlight Clan Head officially became their enemy.

Li Xingguang thought for a second before sighing. "It's just words. I won't harm him on my own or try to unless he tries to harm me, and I would also protect him from people with lower cultivation than myself."

Han Li sighed and shook his head. Then, he took out a red fruit. A normal, nonmagic resource, simple fruit. "This is a Magic Resource I got from the Demon World," show me the greed of a Power Born for Heart Qi, oh ancestor of mine, "and this fruit can let me use Heart Qi for once to make a promise with you. I have three such fruits. I will also give you one of them if the promise works."

Li Xingguang's eyes brightened immensely.

That was Heart Qi?!

A power only Transcendents had.

The Demon World did give great things!

He couldn't have Han Li move now, either. And he knew what Heart Qi was like since when he had gone into the Demon Restraining Temple and sworn to look after one of the cities, the city at the edge of Sacred Lust for the temple, a Transcendent had used Heart Qi on him. He knew what it was like.

He couldn't be fooled.

A cold gleam appeared in his eyes. If it were a mental attack trap or something similar, the traps littered around this room would immediately activate. He had his own ways of doing things. "Go on, I will promise you with Heart Qi."

Han Li ate the fruit, and his Heart Qi was calmly released covertly, and the Clan Head made the vow. He didn't mind people below Evolution, and he had no reason to hurt Han Li himself. In fact, he felt a bit fond of the youth. The more cautious one was that good they were on his list.

Sighing, Han Li relaxed. A significant weight was lifted from his body. Now, Li Xingguang could no longer harm him. He took a deep breath. The man would even have to protect him from lower-stage cultivators when he was in the city.

Of course, there were loops to this rule.

Was Li Shuanwei even a lower-stage cultivator? Was he even a cultivator at all?

Han Li sighed as he took out one of the cherry-like normal fruits. Reluctantly, he gave it to Li Xingguan. "It is very normal-looking and simple. Yet, this fruit is just as ephemeral as Heart Qi. They taste heavenly. In my whole life, it was the tastiest thing I had eaten.

With it, you can briefly sense Heart Qi just like I did, and if you are capable enough, use it."

Li Xingguan scrutinized the fruit. "You have one more, right? Sell it to me."

"It is too precious." Han Li shook his head, adamant.

Li Xingguan calmly scoffed. "I will give you a reasonable amount of Magical Resources that is better for you. You don't have use for Heart Qi anyway."

Li Shuanwei might find it useful, but The CLan Head himself also wanted to try one. Having two was a must for him. Han Li smiled wryly and bowed, "Indeed, Magical Resources are better for me, but I have some conditions." Then he made a long condition of what resource he wanted to make it more realistic.

Li Xingguan waved his hand. They were rare Wind Type or Light Type, but almost all of them were in his collection. He didn't mind them.

Han Li handed the last fruit with a reluctant sigh and then left with Hua Yu.

Li Xingguan took a deep breath. He would have waited for a chance to establish a promise with someone, but what if this was a trap?

He put one into his mouth, and his expression immediately slackened. His whole body relaxed as a heavenly taste made him forget anything about Heart Qi. When he remembered, he had already digested it. He couldn't help but feel his heart sink.

What a precious thing he had wasted. The taste was too heavenly. No one can focus on their Heart if they eat it. Was my heart too weak? He wondered.

Han Li had eaten it so casually.

He would have died if he knew Han Li had a humongous collection of this fruit. It had nothing to it except that it tasted so disbelievingly good. And even that taste would lose much of its charm after about five of them down the tongue.

Of course, Li Xingguand would lose both chances.

Han Li, on the other hand, smiled and entered his home, slumping onto a chair in his room. Now, it was going to be Li Shuanwei's return. He looked at Hua Yu. He still didn't know if that man was stronger than himself or not.

What if he could bypass the trap Han Li had set on the Clan Head?

Hua Yu looked at him curiously. A calm but cold smile on her face. She hadn't worked for free. Han Li sighed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 178 - 178: How curious.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Some time ago, in Han Li's house.

Han Li straightened.

He smiled, and a normal red cherry appeared in his hand.

"This…" His voice dragged.

She wanted benefits that could make her move?

Okay, I'll give it to you.

"Try eating this." He gave it to Hua Yu.

She curiously scanned it with her soul sense. "A normal fruit?" she questioned.

"It is the tastiest thing I have eaten. Ever." Han Li added.

Even in his past life, he hadn't tasted something better. This fruit didn't have a particular taste of its own. It was more like it suited each person's taste buds differently. To some, it might be the spiciest thing ever, but to others, it might be the sweet kind.

She looked at it a bit more closely but then popped it into her mouth. She wasn't too worried about poison since it wasn't even a Magical Resource, and she kind of had a good deal of understanding of poison herself.

The fruit slowly sank into her mouth, and her head spun in clouds as if she had eaten something spicy and sour at the same time. As if a ball of fire had gone into her stomach.

A second later, she opened her eyes. "Are there more of this?"

Han Li nodded. "I have many more."

"Give some of them to me."

"Will you work against Li Xinnguand for them?"

"No, but still give it to me. It's just a normal fruit. I can kill an Evolved for you if you want, as long as they don't have any background or dangerous undertones to their stories."

Fighting a Power Born or even scheming against one was far too deadly for some tasty things. She wasn't tired of living.

Han Li would have liked her help in handling any of his current two main enemies: Yang Wei or Li Shuanwei. Yet both of them were out of the choice.

Yang Wei and Han Li had a Heart Qi binding, which made sure that one of them would end up being the victor in one final battle, which no outsider could partake in, and Li Shuanwei did have a backing.

Suddenly, Han Li chuckled, unlike his submissive self till now. He was about to take a little calculated risk.

"You want benefits. Tell me one thing: are you a Transformation type Cultivator or an Ethereal Type?"

The question made Hua Yu furrow her brows. She didn't like it when someone pried into her own details. Yet, she was curious. Many in the world knew it anyway.

"I am an Ethereal Type cultivator, and my Spark was the Moss Spark, which had now transformed into an Intermediate Dao Soul of Moss."

She had [Average Talent] and was only a lot of distance away from being a [High Talent], but she knew her own worth. The amount of Moss Dao Magic Powers she knew was not many, barely around thirty, and her own chant speed and strength in itself was very average.
