Not everyone was like Han Li, who knew each of the three hundred Magic Powers and their chants under the Lust Dao from the get-go—that was only because he had the Sutra left behind by the Sacred Demon.

Most only knew some of the chants for the Magic Powers in their Dao.

The most important facet of a clan or sect was having a huge bible of notes regarding their known Magic Powers in the first place, like how the Starlight Clan knew more than 30 Starlight Magic Powers.

Yet, Hua Yu was not any simple powerless girl. Having Magical Powers like Parasite Healing, Hua Yu could survive for a long time. Her whole setup was focused on survivability when it came to combat.

Han Li could guess most of these things by the way she carried herself.

Afraid of the strong and suppressing of the weak.

Even though she seemed to lack ulterior motives and just wanted to have fun, it was clear she was stifling her true nature to be more cautious.

Curiosity killed the cat, but satisfaction brought it back.

That perfectly summed up her mentality.

This woman was curious about everything. No wonder she died in some forsaken ocean eaten by a Beast King octopus. She wanted to satiate her curiosity but was hiding her own neck when it came to things stronger than herself.

Who in their right mind would go into the oceans of the Seven Continents without being prepared and having a strong crew? Only the curious minds.

She must have wanted to find something to go beyond her limits.

The freest of minds were also like this. He could stretch her bottom line quite far.

The fact that she said earlier that she was not as strong as the Starlight Head was also proof enough that she was conscious of her own weakness.

Han Li sighed.

"As I expected, Miss Hua is just average. A very run-of-the-mill Power Born. Unlike my own Clan's Head. He has High Talent, good looks, and the Starlight Spark; he also has many Magical Powers that are very useful. Especially in the speed department. And he also has a Body as strong as [Below Average Power Birth] Transformation Physiques."

Hua Yu frowned.

"Yes. I am not as good as him." She nodded.

"What if I make you better than what you are? What if I help you reach another level of strength? A level matching him?"

Normal techniques cannot allow an Ethereal Type Cultivator to stand toe to toe in a body slam against Transformation Type cultivators; even the most [Average] talented body would be stronger than the bodies trained using techniques.

In Yue Li's words, Transformation Type Cultivators were Knights with strong bodies and unique Transformations. Ethereal Type Cultivators were Mages with many magical powers like explosive fire or similar.

Unlike Han Li and other possible Inheritors of the other Sacred Demons, for all others, as long as they were Ethereal Type cultivators, they wouldn't be able to train a body equal to even the least talented Transformation Type Cultivator. That was a limit.

[Average-High-Sacred] Those were the foundation of the bodies a Transformation Cultivator had and their Talent.

The [Below Average Transformation] level of the body was the limit of their physiques as long as they were Ethereal Type, and that was only for people with the best techniques around. That was a limit for Ethereals. They cannot stand equally with the Transformation types.

Only once a cultivator reaches Transcendence after breaking through from the Power Birth Stage would the barriers between Ethereal and Transformation types vanish for them; only then would they be able to have bodies that are absurdly strong and equal to any other under the sun.

For Hua Yu, even if she had the best of techniques available, she could only reach—Below Average Power Birth Body. And she didn't have that either. Her body in itself was only as strong as Han Li's and other top-notch Evolved Bodies. She even lacked in some aspects.

If she was taken by surprise.

"Have this." Han Li threw her another cherry-like fruit.

She had already eaten one. Her caution was low against a weaker person, too. With a quick soul sense scan, she popped it into her mouth, and she was briefly lost in its taste.

When she opened her eyes, a man's hand was firmly wrapped around her waist. His face was dangerously close.

Her instincts kicked in like crazy, and she would have immediately crushed him using her soul sense, which would have easily killed him.

Unlike Han Li, her soul sense had weight; it was psychokinesis. She could easily crush Evolved Level physiques with it as long as there were no equal levels of Soul Sense protecting it.

Yet, before she did it, strong hands tightened around her. A sensation of deadly crisis washed over her as a feeling of fear came from the depths of her bones. From her soul.

Heart Qi—Aura of Fear.

"Calm down, Miss Hua. My hands are too strong for that tender waist. One powerful hug before you get to crush me, and you will be crushed alongside."

Hua Yu was frightened a bit more, but she immediately calmed down and squirmed in his hand before looking at him coldly. "Do you have what it takes to die with me? I'd kill you too."

Han Li had a body at the level of Evolved Sacred Talent Transformation Type Cultivators. Her own body was just as strong; she might have been able to resist if Han Li hadn't transformed his bones long enough to crush him with her soul sense, but now, if Han Li moved fast enough, he could kill her before dying himself.

Both of them knew there was no reason to die together. Cold rationality took each of them hold; age had given them a wisdom that surpassed most circumstances that life throws.

"If I left you now, would you kill me?" Han Li asked with a smile, his breath against her face. She could feel her body tightly pushed against a chest stronger than hers.

She couldn't cast Magical Powers either since that required chanting.

With a smile on her face, she wrapped her hand around his face, tousled the hair on his back, and brought his face close to her face. They were practically an inch away from each other.

"I might kill you just out of the frustration of being outwitted by a junior. I usually refrain from killing, but I am not against it. In fact, I even enjoy it a bit."

Han Li clicked his tongue. "So I can't let you leave my embrace, Miss Hua. You must understand."

He wouldn't have had to do this, but it was the most efficient way. She was more of an ally rather than an enemy; he had no reason to sow seeds of enmity. But for his current dilemma, a Power Born helping out with all her powers would do wonders, especially in convincing Starlight Head to hear him out.

If there was no equal power to hold the man at bay, he might as well just tie Han Li up and squeeze him dry of his secrets before squeezing him dead with his Soul Sense. That was not too far-fetched. Especially since the man had a Below Average Power Born Physique, even this little idea was of no use.

His body wouldn't even be scratched by Han Li.

Hua Yu chuckled. Rather curious now.

What did this youth want?

Now, for the first time, she was genuinely interested.

Her hands drew his head closer to hers, pressing his forehead tightly against hers.

"Won't let me leave your embrace? What are you about to do?" her breath tingled him.

She tried pressing harder, but even among Evolved, Han Li's skin was far too thick, his bones far too rigid. He hadn't done this out of audacity. She couldn't burst his head so easily.

"Why don't you become mine?" he calmly asked.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 179 - 179: A Power Born
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Why don't you become mine?" he asked, "I can make you stronger. Way stronger than you currently are. And in fact, I can prove it to you today itself. And in the next Transcendence War, I will make you Transcend."

Hua Yu giggled in derision. Her green eyes glowering at Han Li.

"Even I myself won't dare to say something so ridiculous. Do you take me for a clown, Junior?"

Han Li used his Talents to attempt making her more irrational but it had no effect on her. It was way too weak. He didn't count on them anyway.

He caressed her cheek, his face leaned down and his nose met hers. She smelled like wild grass.

"I just had this idea. Either I die now with you, or I stand to gain a bunch of things. Or you die with me and you stand to gain a bunch of things."

Suddenly, a force of power wrapped around Hua Yu. It was Han Li's measly Soul Sense.

But then it vanished and that power of fear began to attack her once more as Han Li looked into her eyes. Red eyes met green eyes. Both hardened by time. Han Li was colder, more rational. More practical. More fearsome than anything and anyone she had seen.

This was the power of the Heart Han Li had.

Heart Qi was ephemeral yet supreme against all below Transcendent. Fear was palpable.

Hua Yu looked at him for a second before blurting out in fear, "Heart Qi." She knew what it was like most other Power Born. A power exclusive to Transcendent.

He was no ordinary Evolved. She could now guess his plan by trapping her in his grasp if he had access to Heart Qi.

He wanted to make a binding with her to leave this situation!

"This is my offer. I promise you. I will get one of the Transcendence Orb for you, I will make you Transcend. Become mine. I won't ask you for a lifelong thing, protect me for the next twenty years. Become my servant for just that time.

You know what to do, a Heart Qi binding is all it takes."

Hua Yu calmed down. Her heart thudded in her chest. Anyone would know the worth of Heart Qi. Only Transcendents had it. Most Power Borns would accept to work for him in this situation she was in.

"Can I make my Talent stronger than now? Can I become a Sacred Talent like you if I work for you?" She had always envied them. Did he have some secret that could help?

True Transcendent cultivators were said to have it. Maybe it was a myth?

She herself wasn't completely vulnerable now as she could still take out her weapon from her Empty Space. Yet, if Han Li was stronger, it would still leave at least dangerous injuries on her as his last attack. Maybe even death since drawing a weapon was no faster than Soul Sense.

But now she didn't really want to fight him. She wanted to know. A curiosity burned within her. Heart Qi was the signature power of Transcendence. How did he have it?

"No. I cannot improve the Talent of a cultivator past Awakening Stage. But let me teach you something, a Transcendent Orb fundamentally changes you. Once you Transcend, you won't have to worry about Talent since that is a fundamentally different level of its own."

That meant he promised her to get the orb for her.

She cupped his face. A servant? Of an Evolved?

How outrageous. Yet, she felt somehow dumbed by how natural it felt to accept.

"What an outrageously curious youth." She shook her head. "My life has been boring for a long time. Seems like it will be interesting for the next part. As long as you yourself believe completely that you can do what you promised without putting my own interests in line, Why not?"

As long as he didn't believe it, his binding shouldn't work with her condition now.

Now that she thought of it, she hadn't ever been someones.

That was also a curious thing.

Why not?

As long as it doesn't stray her from her path of cultivating to higher levels, it was all good.

Han Li on the other hand, smiled. Soon enough, a Heart Qi binding of equal interests was bound. He couldn't make her do something she didn't want to but she would protect him to a decent degree until the last moment he could keep making her a bit stronger every month.

Contradicting his thoughts, Hua Yu smiled in his embrace.

She doubted someone can constantly make a Power Born stronger every month for long anyway. No matter how much of a freak they were. Might as well see the fun in being submissive to a youth not even half her age for a while before moving on to the next interesting thing.

Han Li, on the other hand, felt his shoulders loosen a bit.

A Power Born Guard...

It might have made others cocky but he knew this was just a completely practical partnership. The moment she felt she had the smaller end of the stick, she could leave. Their binding was set up like that. Of course, she had to warn him a month before she actually left.

And their binding also made sure she couldn't harm Han Li's interests just like he couldn't harm hers from this very day unless situation proved to be against that notion entirely. Yet, in the end of all, it was an equal to equal contract.

Otherwise, she was a Power Born, she could totally blow herself up into moss.

Equal benefits had made her bend the knee.

Of course, him being allowed to touch her was not a part of it at all. Nor was he offered her body. It was a partnership of benefits. Nothing more, nothing less. She was just his guardian.

And constantly, he had to show her that he could make her stronger. Every month. And prove his capability by the end of today itself.

Or it would break the partnership the day it started. He wondered how long it would last.

Caressing Hua Yu's face, Han Li leaned down and she wrapped her hand around his neck. Their lips met and she was briefly lost in a world of sensations.

They were partners anyway, why would she say not to skin-ship?

Han Li was a very, very handsome young man, after all.

Even if all this didn't happen, if he tried a different way, he might have seduced her. He might have even made her fall in love now that she thought of it practically. He was already interested in his spear teaching prowess, after all.

Yet.

She hadn't expected this at all.

How uniquely curious.

Who would have known she would be kissing and making out with a junior, when she was just here to be with Hua Feilan for a while before returning to traveling the world. The world was indeed a curious place.

She felt her tongue be dominated by simple moves and couldn't help but grow defensive and a bit shocked. How could he be more skillful than her in kissing when she had more understanding of the human body in general?

This youth really was an enigma.

After passing 200+ years of age, nary any person cared for their virginity or their bed habits. Even though Hua Yu wasn't one to sleep around—in fact, she had never actually felt interest in a man and, nor had she slept with someone. After all, she was not curious about such things.

But she wasn't one to care too much about such things either if it came to be related to her path as a cultivator. And now it was.

Han Li seemed to have the Powers of a Transcendent and her life was in his hand. He also promised her enough benefits—improbable as they may—which she couldn't refuse.

If Han Li proved truly capable of improving her prospects, deep down, she didn't mind settling down for a while.

It was her womanly instinct after so long of traveling around. Of course, Han Li didn't know her thoughts.

Han Li broke the kiss and looked at the woman's red face. Her green eyes were calm yet filled with charm. He knew this was the most dangerous woman he had ever touched. He had no doubt about it.

She was not like others.

There were no underlying emotions that bound them. Just pure benefits and fleeting interests. Such things were flimsy for them old monsters unless they wanted it not to be.

Both of them could easily betray each other if it came to it, she was just here for the benefits just like himself, but as long as their interests were the same, they wouldn't stray away from each other. Their relationship was just simply transactional.

As of now atleast. If he had to, he could easily kill her without second thoughts.

Both of them knew that.

They were enemies in a gang for benefits.

He brushed aside her green hair, one hand behind, and pushed her waist closer to himself.

But then, he stopped and let her go.

"Let's go make that ancestor of mine no longer a threat. Then we can have our time together and maybe even remove misunderstandings."

Her lips curved into a smile.

Holding his hand, she asked, "Let's go but now what? I have to act like a girl in love with you to pressure Li Xingguang into signing a Heart Qi bond with you?"

Han Li shook his head. "Not pressure, compel. He would do it since he doesn't know I am his son's enemy. I am just an ant in you exalted Power Borns' eyes after all. You guys are all too careless against me."

She chuckled. "Curious. I was indeed a bit careless."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 180 - 180: Primal Tremble of blood in his veins?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Convincing Li Xingguang was neither hard nor easy.

Yet, Hua Yu promised Li Xingguang that she would fight against the enemies behind Li Xingguang's son and Han Li's enemy, Li Shuanwei, for once, in exact words: "I shall be indebted to you if you help Han Li. I would leave soon, but I will help you fight the Power Born, who is after your son once before I leave so you can intimidate her."

This promise had done the trick for Li Xingguang. The fact that Hua Yu would help him fight his enemies—even though only once—made protecting an Evolution Stage cultivator a meager task. Profits definitely outweighed the cost. Han Li even promised to heal everyone who failed in Evolution.

The man was, of course, easily convinced and even sold many Magical Resources for a normal red cherry, believing it to be some kind of Heart Qi resource of the myths that Han Li got rewarded from the Demon World. He had also given an oath not to reveal any details of Han Li, like how he was an Evolved at the age of eighteen.

He would also protect Han Li from people below the Power Birth Stage.

Had his son returned, it wouldn't have gone like this.

This was how you use your enemies to your advantage. Of course, this could still go in many unexpected ways since nothing was absolute. Especially since Hua Yu, his protector for time to come, would have to fight once on his enemy's behalf. But that was also a calculated risk.

Han Li and Hua Yu smiled and entered his home, slumping onto a chair in his room. Still unnoticed by Liyue, Li Yao, and Hua Feilan, too.

Now, it was going to be Li Shuanwei's return. He looked at Hua Yu. He still didn't know if that man was stronger than himself or not. Maybe he might even stand a chance against a Power Born?

What was that freak exactly?

What if he could bypass the trap Han Li had set on the Clan Head and Hua Yu to kill him or his family?

Hua Yu looked at him curiously. A calm but cold smile on her face. She hadn't worked for free. "Make me stronger, or I can leave you here right now."

Han Li calmly took a note from the side, a brush, and an ink pot. "What Great Dao does the Dao of Moss come under?" he asked. He had a vague idea of what it belonged to.

Her being strong was for his own good, too.

"I do not know, honestly." She said with a sigh. "I think the Dao of Moss comes under the Life Dao, but I had tried all sorts of methods to increase my Cultivation Talent like all the other Life Dao Sparks, and none worked. For example, you should have increased your cultivation talent with Lust at the start to make your Spark Sacred Talent level, but none of those things worked for me."

Han Li sighed. "As expected, a Life Great Dao spark which had gone to uselessness. After the Awakening Stage, you cannot improve your talent. Moss Dao should have required you to move deep into water bodies and meditate there to increase your talent but no use saying that now, you already lost the chance."

That was why he wanted his mom to improve her talent to Sacred before she became an Evolved. He had used many of his resources for that. He even used the one thing he didn't want to—Death's Return Diagram. He had used that to get a Spark for Yue Li.

Taking a deep breath, he looked into Hua Yu's eyes like he was looking at a disappointment. She could have become a Sacred Talent cultivator with the Dao of Moss. She could have. How wasted an opportunity. What a disappointment.

She looked at his gaze and couldn't help but blurt out, "I had to cultivate under deep waters to make my Cultivation Talent rise?" she couldn't help but feel her stomach opening a pit in it.

She could have lived a life far more luxurious than this. She could have sated her curiosities easily. She would have been able to disregard people like Li Xingguang.

"Trash." Han Li added.

She felt it right in her heart and couldn't even deny it. He harrumphed slightly and began writing on the book he had taken with a brush and ink. It only took him ten minutes to complete his writing, and he nodded in satisfaction before giving it to her calmly.

"Ancient Art of the True Life Body." She read, and her eyes quickly skimmed through it with her heart thudding inside her chest. Any Power Born could cultivate their body, even if they are Ethereal Type, to the level of Below Average Power Birth Physiques, but they needed a technique to do that, just like the chants of Magic Powers.

This was exactly such a technique, and any cultivator belonging to any Dao under the Life Great Dao could practice it. She couldn't help but have her heart shiver as she read it.

To complete the process of turning her body into that of a Below Average Power Born, she would need to use hundreds of Magical Resources, each in a uniquely disbelieving sequence.

There were Water Dao Resources, and there were all types of resources in the list, and when she ate it all, her own body would refine it into a pseudo-transcendent Resource—a Resource made by cooking multiple resources—and her body would evolve.

Power Borns can't use Transcendent Resources. Only Transcendence Stage cultivators can, but this was a unique process. This would work. And surprisingly enough, the resources in it were extremely common but just in huge numbers. She even had most of the mentioned resources in her own hand. She was thrilled.

She could also use a Life Dao Resource to immediately achieve this body, but getting all those other resources was much easier. She took a deep, deep breath. "Where did you get this from?"

Han Li calmly said, "None of your business, but we are partners, I'll tell you. I won it in the Demon World."

She laughed in derision. "You think I'd believe your bullshit? Obviously, that's fake. What if I had a Great Dao of Darkness-related Dao? Would you say you got that, too?"

He shrugged. "Immediately go and collect the Magical Resources you want and the Magical Resources Li Xingguang promised for me from the Library in the clan. There is everything you need there. Do it before Li Shuanwei comes back. Make your physique stronger."

She would not have acted as his servant like this, but she was too stirred up now. Who would say no to leveling up? She floated from her seat and immediately flew away in the cross-legged form. Han Li looked at her, receding back with a deep gaze.

"Troublesome." He muttered before looking at the woman sleeping on the bed beside him. It was his mother, still too exhausted after what had happened. He couldn't help but smile. "I feel trouble coming. That Li Shuanwei is too weird."

He held her hands and kissed it. "But worry not. He shouldn't be stronger than a Power Born since the Trials believed I, you, and Nu Kai could handle him. The fact that we matched up against him means he was not stronger than us. And the fact that he hid against the rocks until Nu Kai and I ended our fighting is another thing. He shouldn't be stronger."

But he didn't like this. Know yourself and know your enemies, and you shall fight a hundred battles victoriously.

He made his dark sword fly towards him. He petted it. The rusty surface of the blade reminded him of the Sacred Demon. That man, that creature, had had it easy from the start. It ate a single fruit at the beginning of the world and was at the top of all lifeforms.

Han Li had seen far too many storms in his past life. He had seen hardship and luxury. He had seen love and hatred. He had been washed in blood; his heart was as cold as ice.

But why was it that primal urges were now making his hand tremble?

He held the sword up. It was trembling. His hand was trembling.

He didn't want to scheme against Li Shuanwei.

He wanted to cut that man.

Primal urge of protection?

How dare that man hurt those he loved?

"How shameful of me not to see the bigger picture." He hefted the blade in a fighting position. "How shameful of me to want vengeance. But I guess it comes along with being a True Demon with a will to defy the odds."

He looked at his hand. His crimson eyes bursting with a sort of conceit.

"How shameful of me to be so conceited in my own strength."

[Skin Building Completed—Tempered Skin—Vibrating Skin]

[Organ Strengthening Completed—Healthy Organs—Vitality Channeling]

[Bone Forging Completed—Forged Bones—Fused Blade]

He didn't feel like there was a chance of him losing against Li Shuanwei in any ways he looked. He wasn't the inheritor of a Sacred Demon for nothing.

Even though Han Li was still far from having completely juiced the inheritance's potential in the Evolution Stage, he didn't think Li Shuanwei, who, according to the trial, was someone whom the Han Li before completing Bone Forging, working with Nu Kai, would have stood a chance against could stand on equal terms to him now.

Even if Han Li of yesterday and Nu Kai teamed against Han Li of this moment, he would slaughter them in mere minutes.

A single Transformation had only made his body reach the level of Sacred Talent Transformation Cultivators, but everything was not always as it seemed. Vibrating Skin ability was useful in making his cuts deeper. Vitality Channeling made his stamina nigh-endless in battles, and Fused Blade?

Since the Trial believed Han Li and Nu Kai, the man whom Han Li had fought and defeated in the 4th Trial, stood a chance against a fight with Li Shuanwei, it meant he was only that strong. Han Li was stronger than that.

He couldn't help but be ashamed of how he was being so reckless. He didn't know his enemy well after all. But sometimes, it is like that.

He smiled.

He himself had stifled his own self for so long. Recklessness didn't suit him, though.

Han Li watched as the curious Power Born woman returned to the room, a packet full of many Magical Resources in her hand.

"Time to start cooking, just in case."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 181 - 181: Burning of the Dao Tree
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As Han Li got busy helping Hua Yu achieve a Power Born level physique awaiting Li Shuanwei's return, in another place in Sacred Lust, something else was happening.

It was atop a small hill. Surroundings were a sight of their own, with dead mortals of all ages littering the area. Women, men, kids, and the elderly of all skin colors. Their gory bodies, which could have once been beautiful things, were now just bones. Nothing more, nothing less.

In contrast, the hill in the middle was a pristine grassy place. No blood, nothing. On top of it was a young man with pale grey eyes, violet nails, white and old material robes, and a silent and calm aura. He tsked through his teeth and spat out some bloody flesh.

"Evil is such an easy thing to be addicted to. But such afflictions are for weak men. Murder, rape, plunder, all of those should be ways to move forward in the path of cultivation, not to be a thing that holds you back." The spirit inside his spoke, her usual flowery voice filled with the tone of a sage who had seen it all.

Yang Wei coldly shook his head. "Killing is the one way to achieve whatever I want. If there were no others in this world, if I could kill anyone at my wish, cultivation would have been so easy. All resources would be mine. If I want a Transcendent Orb, I could have had it so easily. Killing is the way."

The woman snorted in disdain.

She knew what was going to happen now, but she had no way of stopping it; her words would only be heard by deaf ears. She turned silent as Yang Wei took out a flower with a pristine white color from his pocket. It looked like a palm-sized lotus, with dew still evidently trickling from it.

Yang Wei had won three of the Trials and had lost in the fourth Trial; he had been thrown into a verbal debate against a Power Born Monk; had he won, he could have moved to the fifth Trial and gained a truly disbelieving treasure, but he had failed. Even though she herself had helped Yang Wei debate against the words of the Monk, who was called Hen Shin, that man was even wiser than herself.

No wonder Yang Wei had grown even more derisive of her. And more than that, on one of his three Trials, he had won something heinous. The flower in his hand.

It was a Magical Resource that could help the user ask any questions to anyone under the Transcendence Stage of cultivation, and they would have to answer with 100% truth. Any personal question could be asked, and they would end up answering as if it were normal. It was a Life Dao Magical Resource.

Of course, it would only last for an hour.

If she was in her prime, it wouldn't have worked on her, but now, it would easily work on her.

Even though it was not the best of his three rewards, it was the bane of her existence. Even though she and Yang Wei were a team, they had boundaries. He didn't know everything about her. Nobody in this whole world did. Her tone was icy, "If you used it, our cooperation would surely be affected. Why don't you wait till I reveal it to you on my own?

Why don't you try to crack me little by little instead? Wouldn't that be more fun for you? Conquering me, that is."

"Heh." With a derisive chuckle, Yang Wei popped the flower into his mouth.

A profound sigh of coldness was released from the Spirit.

"Ask away, what do you wish to know?" She asked, quickly changing her mask to that of indifference.

Yang Wei calmly questioned, his eyes sharp and hollow. "What are you?"

She tried not to speak, but she ended up speaking regardless, "I am a Defier of the Heavens." She immediately knew that all her future plans of slowly retaking her influences were all down the drain. Gaining back control over Yang Wei was also out of the question.

"Defier of the Heavens?" Yang Wei corked his eyebrows. "What is that?"

"The ones who walk the true path of cultivation instead of the Path of the Dao Tree."

"Path of the Dao Tree?"

The Spirit's cold but flowery voice spoke, "In the primordial times when the world was not yet born, a seed was thrown into a dark and empty void. That seed became a majestic Tree. The Dao Tree. The source of the world and everything you know. That tree had a consciousness on its own, a will that is the Heavens of this world of the Seven Continents."

Yang Wei growled. "Explain in a way I can understand."

"The Dao Tree was the origin of all things. It was the embodiment of perfection. It also represented a Dao called the Dao of Origin. It had a purpose straight at its birth—it wanted to create a world that would last for Eternity, but the Tree in itself had a fault.

Before it could complete the world, it started to decay, and thus, it knew that it couldn't complete the creation of a True Everlasting World."

"What do you mean?" Yang Wei's words were a hiss.

"There are countless worlds in this universe, oh Yang Wei. Each was born from a Dao Tree. But rarely did a world get the chance to complete the process and transform into a True Everlasting World, to the point there are only two in existence among billions of worlds like the Seven Continents."

Yang Wei stayed silent.

"Do you know what these Dao Trees do when they fail to create an Everlasting World where they would exist for Eternity?"

"What?" Yang Wei's hollow heart was thumping.

The Spirit coldly smiled. "When the Dao Tree, which some call the World Tree, is sure to fail, its Dao of Origin starts to deteriorate. Usually, it just deteriorates and leaves behind a world like this one. There are many such worlds, after all. But the Dao Tree of this world was especially cunning.

It split the Dao of Origin into seven different Great Daos—Life, Light, Darkness, Wind, Water, Earth, and Fire and turned it into fruits which it fed to seven of its inhabitants."

Yang Wei had his eyes turn bright. "The seven Sacred Demons."

"Yes. Each of the Sacred Demons was one of the Seven Daos it had left behind. Then, the Tree should have vanished entirely, but what it did was it still stood tall for a long time. Until the Era of the Seven Demons came to an end, that is."

"Then?"

"Then what?" The woman's laughter was full of madness. "Then the Seven Demons sacrificed themselves and used the combined power of all seven Great Daos to create a Fire. It was a Fire of Origin. The Fire burned themselves and changed the whole world."

"How?"

"Heh." She derisively chuckled, but her voice was full of awe. "The tree died, burned into cinders with its own powers. The Sacred Demons died, and everything was back to zero. But then, they were not idiots. They had a plan.

"The Two Everlasting Worlds. The Everlasting Human World and the Everlasting Beast World. They created a gateway to those places and made some of the people of those worlds enter here forcibly. The last remaining power of the combined seven Great Daos—Fire of Origin made that a possibility.

"The empty world after the catastrophe of the Burning of the Dao Tree had become filled with creatures and wildlife stolen from those two superior places. The world became bustling with activity."

Yang Wei had his eyes widen again and again.

"But the people of the Everlasting Human World were all mortal people with nothing other than their human bodies, whereas the people of the Everlasting Beast World were gigantic and fierce Beasts that had no equals in might. What do you think happened?"

Yang Wei had no answer.

"The Beasts ruled. They were not Beast Kings at the level of Power Borns. Nor were they Beast Emperors equal to Transcendents that ruled. The rulers of that Era were the Beast Daemons, beasts equal to the Demi-Sacred Stage cultivation of this day.

"Beast Kings have no intelligence, but from the Beast Emperor stage, they are no longer any inferior to the Humans. They are strong."

Yang Wei asked, frowning, "If they were so strong in the past, why are humans in supremacy now? Why are they so weak to the point there is not a single Beast Emperor in the whole world, whereas we have 4 Transcendent Humans in Sacred Lust Continent alone?"

"Hehehe," Her flowery laughter was calm and cold. "The Dao Tree's embers. The tree was burned and slowly destroyed by burning, but its Dao hadn't vanished. For the first time in history, one of the Dao's, an ephemeral ember, a Spark of the Dao, entered a Human Being."

Yang Wei was alarmed.

"The Spark gave birth to a void inside their bodies. The Spark represented one of the Daos; now, the Humans could cultivate on their own. They had weak bodies, to the point they were just extremely pathetic in that department compared to the beasts.

"But they had Magical Powers of creation. Creating fire from thin air, water from thin air, and so on and so forth. Slowly, in hiding, they grew stronger. The Heavens itself seemed to be helping them; on a day, a Demi-Sacred was born amidst them."

Yang Wei took a deep breath.

"Beasts had their bodies and Beast Cores to make them stronger. Humans had their Dao Souls and Magic Powers. A war began. It was a huge war of supremacy that ended in devastation. But it was then that the goals of the Sacred Demons were revealed.

"When the Beast Daemons and Demi Sacred cultivator ended up falling in love instead of ending the world in their wrath. When they had their first child—The ancestor of today's humankind was born.

"Today's humans. A creature who has Transformation Sparks, the power of the Beasts, and Magic Powers, the power of the Heavens. It was the goal of the Dao Tree and the Seven Demons."

"Why?"

"Of course, to create a creature who wields all the 7 Great Daos. Only a creature from the Higher Worlds can do that since their birth was done with the help of the Everlasting Dao Trees in the Everlasting Worlds, and today, in this whole world of the Seven Continents, only creatures from the higher world exist. The Seven Demons were all of this world. They couldn't do it.

But the chosen one can do it."

"To wield the power of the Dao of Origin." Yang Wei's hand trembled.

The Spirit took a deep breath. "The Seven Demons must have left behind ways for the people of this world to slowly regain the power that the Sacreds had had by themselves. There should already be seven inheritors out there, each of them on their way to attaining one of the Seven Great Daos. In this world that hadn't seen a Sacreed in the last few Eras, it should see seven of them this time.

"Then, one of them would inherit all of the Powers of the Dao Tree as a whole after defeating the rest and become a Dao Lord who would make this World ascend into a True Everlasting World. The Third Everlasting World in the whole universe."

"Inheritors?!" Yang Wei felt his heart drop. Then, he calmed down a bit. "Even all that you have mentioned doesn't really tell me the whole picture. You seem to be intentionally hiding something. You haven't told me who you are."

"Me?"

The woman smiled calmly.

"I am…" She smirked. "Sorry, 1 hour has passed, and I no longer have to tell you that."

Yang Wei felt his nerves bulge.

Yet, immediately, he decided to keep some of the words she had spoke of earlier etched in his mind: Defier of the Heavens, Path of the Dao Tree, Fire of Origin, The Dao of Origin and the Everlasting Worlds of Human and Beast. He had to analyze her words.

He also wondered how much of it was true.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 182 - 182: To Yang Wei
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and Hua Yu were on top of the walls of the Southern Lust City, looking into the distance. Both of them had calm expressions, but if someone who was perceptive looked at them, they would notice that Hua Yu was trembling slightly with a bit of excitement.

She had eaten nearly 70 Magical Resources, and she could feel her whole body fussing with warmth. Many of the resources she had eaten were Poisons, but Han Li had assured her that when the combined forces of all the Resources worked on her, the poison would be neutralized. That had indeed happened, and she could feel her body steadily growing stronger.

Like a confluence of warmth, her whole body felt hot. She had already reached the cusp of physiques between Power Born and Evolved levels. Soon enough, she would gain a [Below-Average Power Birth Physique].

Evolved top-tier strength bodies could withstand even normal blades, and they could topple trees with their best attacks. Han Li could also topple trees now. He hadn't tested himself after forging his Bones, but he knew he could easily destroy mountain boulders with a punch.

But regardless, that was nothing compared to even the weakest [Below Average Power Born Physiques].

Suddenly, Hua Yu's eyes constricted to pins, and her nerves bulged like thick snakes coursing through her skin. She trembled, her teeth grinding against each other as she fell on the wall and squirmed.

Han Li calmly looked at her and took a sip of wine from the flagon of thick alcohol in his hand. She looked to be in pain, he noted calmly as his gaze moved to the distance. Far away, a dust cloud was rising, and he could tell why that must have happened.

His eyes darkened.

Exactly ten minutes later, heaving, puffing in pain, Hua Yu stood up with a sweaty body. Her green dress was now soaked from head to toe, giving her an extremely alluring appearance, with her twin mounts an especially sweet sight for those who adored feminine beauty.

With a groan, she snatched the flagon from Han Li's hand and downed several sips before looking at the dust cloud—a serious expression cast over her excited face. "Your conjuncture was indeed the right one," she said.

Han Li nodded.

According to Hua Yu's quick information hunt, The Clan Heads of Rock, Raven, and Starlight Clan were all in their own places, cultivating. But what about the enemy and the place where Li Shuanwei would spawn?

As far as Han Li knew, a Power Born woman had captured Li Shuanwei before he entered the Demon World, and Li Xingguang had chased after that woman in rage, yelling and revealing himself in the middle of Han Li's marriage.

But. But!

He had not been saved, and that woman Power Born, Li Shuanwei's captor, had not been put down. That woman, Demonic Ice Fairy, was still out there, exactly where the cloud of dust was rising from.

A silent sigh left Han Li.

Only People at the Power Born level could raise such a ruckus. Even the ground was slightly shaking.

"Do you feel strong?" he asked, looking into Hua Yu's emerald eyes.

The woman took a deep breath and clenched her hands. "I feel invincible. Three or four punches, and I can destroy this fortress wall of the Southern Lust city. I am strong. Beyond my own expectation."

Han Li flourished his dark sword and struck it at her. She didn't dodge.

It was sharp. It would cut anyone at the level of Evolved like Tofu, but striking her neck, it stopped and just vibrated with a "Shing!" sound. It couldn't move even a centimeter into her skin. She had a body stronger than iron.

She smirked.

He smiled, too.

"Strong indeed. A Power Born is indeed on their own level."

But then, their eyes became slightly downcast. Both Han Li and Hua Yu looked towards the dust cloud with a silent and defeated back. No matter how strong they could be, there were always bigger mountains with deeper foundations.

If no one among the Power Born Clan Heads was fighting the Demonic Ice Fairy, it was obvious why the fight had suddenly begun out of nowhere. It was painfully obvious.

It was around time for Li Shuanwei to return from the Demon World, and he should have returned. Exactly there, the man who had chopped his mother's hand with nary but a single wave of his hand, utterly inconceivable. That guy was fighting a Power Born.

Demonic Ice Fairy, while not being as strong as the Starlight Clan Head in a fight, was still better than Hua Yu. And Li Shuanwei himself could fight her. But Han Li also noticed more things.

Ordinarily, Power Birth Stage cultivators fought their battles in the sky, but they were fighting on land. Since Ice was a power that would have more advantage on land if Li Shuanwei was a Power Born himself, he should have flown up to the sky and fought, but since he didn't, it just meant that Li Shuanwei couldn't fly.

But he could no longer judge the man. That guy was far too much of a mystery.

Suddenly, another man flew up and landed beside them with starlight lingering on his path.

Hua Yu looked at him with a curious gaze and asked, "Your son really is something. Even more monstrous than the little guy beside us. What age was he again? 30?"

Li Xingguang laughed, unlike himself. "He is my pride, my arrogance. I used to worry about him all the time, but I no longer have to." He then snorted. "What Demonic Ice Fairy, my son would defeat her and show her her true worth. But just in case, come, the other two, Raven and Rock, are also pissed at the woman for ruining our peace, and we plan to encircle and kill her for sure this time.

Let's go, fulfill your promise for me."

Hua Yu looked at Han Li.

Han Li looked at Li Xingguang with a veiled gaze full of meaning. If Han Li himself had come back from 500 years in the future and was still just at the level of meager evolution, what was then that made Li Shuanwei so special?

"Clan Head certainly is blessed with a great heritage. Do I know when Brother SHuanwei broke through to the Power Birth Stage?" He asked with a smile. "I would like to congratulate you if so, but wouldn't that bring you trouble? After all, such a young Power Born… I can see my own type of issues but more inflated happening for him."

The Clan Head sighed. Seeing the probing intention.

"Power Birth, Evolved…" He flew towards his son, floating a meter away from Han Li. He said, "I wouldn't have told this to anybody else, but you are from my own lineage. As much as Shuanwei is my blood, you could also be considered a distant blood of mine. Let me give you one advice: if you leverage on it, it's up to you."

Han Li cupped his fists, and his eyes turned sharp inwardly.

"There are many things in this world that are not always as they seem. There are many powers inside even the most normal of cultivators. Heart Qi, Dao Crystals, Magic Powers, Weapon Proficiency… they are all scattered and never one part. The day all of it combines into one perfect thing is when a cultivator reaches his apex."

"Uh?" Han Li's face turned into a confused mess.

All of those were different facets of one cultivator.

What was this old man on about?

Was this a misleading tactic to change their attention from his son's brilliance?

There was nothing like all powers becoming one. Even among the Transcendent Stage, Heart Qi was Heart Qi, Proficiency was Proficiency, Body was Body, and Magic Powers were Magic Powers. There was not even a point in making it all one.

In the first place, how does someone even fuse the powers of thoughts like Proficiency, which lets them cut deeper to something substantial?

"Clan Head must be joking…" Han Li rubbed the back of his head.

Li Xingguang sighed. "Even I think I am joking, but this was advice from my son. Do what you do with it." He glanced at Hua Yu. "Demonic Ice Fairy won't go down without at least taking one of our limbs or even heads; be prepared. I will go, I can't wait for you, but I hope you arrive in ten minutes or so."

With that, he flew away, and Hua Yu looked at Han Li.

Han Li asked, "Do you care for honor and meeting your promises?"

"I don't care for the promise I gave Li Xingguang, at least." SHe wasn't bound with Heart Qi to do that after all, it was just a verbal promise and not going through with it would only make her name a bit bad at the worst.

Han Li stretched, and his bones started breaking out of their languidness. He took out a small blue vial from his Empty Space and downed the liquid inside it. A second later, he vomited a drop of water, which kept floating in the air.

"Come, let's bail." He said, and Hua Yu carried him down the wall back to his home.

This time, Liyue immediately noticed him upon entry, and the wooden spear in her hand fell out. She walked towards Han Li and gently placed her hand on his chest. She took a deep breath and asked, "Where is Mother Yue?"

Han Li patted her head, "Not the time for this. We'll talk."

She was reluctant but silent; she didn't say anything and just nodded. Her eyes kept breaking out tears, but she sniffed and, keeping his hand tightly in hers, followed him. She really, really had thought of many things that could have gone wrong in his nearly week-long trip. The relief was like a stuck blade taken out of her heart.

They then also met Li Yao, who looked at Han Li with a sigh of relief and gave him a long embrace. Hua Feilan asked Hua Yu something from the side.

Li Yao said, "I saw your mother in the room a second ago, but I didn't tell Liyue. Yue Li looks roughed up. You guys seemed to have gone through some serious stuff…"

Han Li sighed and, with all of them, and a quickly caught chicken with him, returned to the room where his mother was sleeping.

Then, he woke her up once more; still rather fuzzy in the head, she put all of them—Liyue, Li Yao, Hua Feilan, and the chicken—into her Empty Space before once again falling asleep a bit later onto Han Li's chest. Han Li had also made her put the important stuff, like extra Magical Resources in their hands, into her Empty Space.

The room was once again deadly quiet. Only him, Hua Yu, and his mother were here now.

Han Li looked at Hua Yu. "That was really a humbling experience… I had overestimated myself and also The Trials. There was no chance that Nu Kai and I stood a chance against Li Shuanwei. He is someone we can't judge."

But there was no reason to fight him now.

Even now that he had forged his bones, Li Shuanwei was beyond him.

"Nu Kai?" Hua Yu frowned.

"Someone I know. Anyway, let's go." He took his mother in a princess carry and looked at Hua Yu with a deep breath.

She could carry them and fly. That would be their way of travel.

"Any plans?" She asked. She had many ways of her own to avoid a chase.

Han Li smiled. "There is a friend of mine, Yang Wei. Let's move towards him; you can't interfere in this, and nor can we move too close, but if I invite him, he should come to me on his own. I have a bit of frustration to release, and he seems like an opportunity that is ready for the picking."

Hua Yu corked her eyebrows.

He smiled, and they both walked out of the house. Floating at the level of the rooftops of the homes, Han Li looked down at the city from his vantage point. Everything was shaking slightly due to the fight in the distance.

"I have a route in mind. One where no one can chase us…" Han Li said. He had not much of a reaction to being afloat, and Hua Yu was not physically carrying them; her soul sense was at the level of psychokinesis, and it was hefting them.

"To which route?" She asked.

She preferred moving in her own hidden route, but maybe he had more ideas of better routes.

"There is an underwater cavern, and through it, we can reach another part of the continent. No one would chase."

They moved quickly and were soon far away from the city. Moving towards Yang Wei with a letter of challenge.

After 3 hours, the battle in the distance ended, and four figures flew and landed on the wall where Li Xingguang had parted with Han Li. Their faces were filled with frustration and even pity.

"If that Hua Yu actually helped, we would have killed the Ice Fairy!" The Raven Head spat.

As all of them began to bicker, though, Li Shuanwei, who was silently standing beside his father with a calm gaze, paused and looked at a small floating glob of water in front of them and frowned.

"…What is this?" he asked and frowned.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 183 - 183: Blue Blob, Blue... Blob!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As all of them began to bicker, though, Li Shuanwei, who was silently standing beside his father with a calm gaze, paused and looked at a small floating glob of water in front of them and frowned.

"…What is this?" he asked and frowned. Everyone's attention shifted toward the baby-fist-sized orb of blue liquid floating in the air. Immediately, powerful Soul Senses moved and surrounded it before all of them encircled and watched it with caution.

Li Shuanwei himself was in the middle, his gaze filled with doubts. He couldn't sense what was making this thing float. In the end, his Soul Sense was not as good as the Power Birth Stage Cultivators…

He looked at his father, Li Xingguang, and saw how pale the man's face had become. Soon, Li Shuanwei's gaze moved to the rest of the two Clan Heads, Raven Clan Head and Rock Clan Head—their faces were pale too, but not as much as his own father's. A bad premonition came over him.

What was going on?

Did they sense something he couldn't?

Suddenly, all three Clan Heads bowed to their knees and said respectfully, "We greet the Transcendent."

Li Shuanwei felt his heart tremble as his father's Soul Sense pushed his own body into his knee. "What are you doing, Shuanwei? Greet the revered senior!"

Coming up with conclusions with a frown, Li Shuanwei bowed.

"This junior greets the senior."

At the same time, the globe of water started to morph and spasm for a second before it took the form of a miniature humanoid creature. With that alone, a sensation of fear started to bud in their hearts; even Li Shuanwei felt his heart tremble as fear began to rise within him.

Yet, deep within, he concurred, 'Heart Qi, only that can make me so fearful… a true Transcendence Stage Cultivator.' He silently gritted his teeth. Then, this must be the famed method of communication that only Transcendence Stage cultivators are said to have—using 60 different Magical Resources together to cook a Transcendent Magical Resource.

He had heard about it but had never seen it face to face. This method was said to be used by all Transcendent Cultivators to communicate with the outside world since they themselves are in secluded cultivation most of the time. It could carry their wills to have conversations hundreds of kilometers away, thousands, even.

But even though it was so useful, only Transcendence Stage cultivators can use it since it is a Transcendent Resource. If any below used it, they would just blow up their organs and die, unable to digest it.

Why was a Transcendent here? Which among the four in the Sacred Lust Continent was this?

The little creature moved sluggishly, standing up only to fall a second later, then waving its cute blue legs to stand in a better form. Then, it sized up the people around with its featureless face.

"Gaaa…" It said and then smilingly touched its mouth area. "No matter how much I have done it, I just can't get used to it." A cute voice said, sounding like a doll, but none of them felt amused, just scared and restless.

Of course, the Clan Heads of Raven and Rocks exchanged a slight glance. They hoped the new person would suppress Li Xingguang. As it was now, it was headed in a way that Li Xingguang would have a superior stand to them in all matters in the entire city from now on.

The Magical Resources would play a huge part in him, and they would also lose more advantages. Li Shuanwei was a monster. Regardless, they bowed respectfully without any movements to the 'Transcendent'.

Clearing his throat, Han Li chuckled.

"Where am I right now? He asked.

Of course, he was the one using this thing. It was a loss of a huge number of resources he had gotten, but indeed, he could pull a rug under them pretty easily with this. Who would believe a person, not Transcendent, not even a Power Born, just an Evolved, to use methods that only Transcendent used?

But he could do it. He had organs like the Sacred Demons.

What couldn't it digest?

He could digest anything below Sacred tier danger level things, if they existed, that is.

It was out of their entire belief system. He was unthinkable.

He was a monster.

"You are in the Southern Lust City, Revered Transcendent." The Raven Head said enthusiastically, and the featureless head of the little thing nodded.

For a long second, Han Li turned silent. He couldn't see much without eyes, but along with his will, a bit of soul sense seemed to have entered this; it could last for a while longer, but within around 2 hours, it should vanish. Such a temporary thing cost more than 60 Magical Resources.

Almost all of the resources he had had been used up; helping Hua Yu and making this Walkie Talkie Transcendance Resource had made him poor. But he now had some advantages to make out of the cost put in.

He showed his little palm to Li Shuanwei.

"I watched your fight, Junior," he said, his voice praising.

Li Shuanwei's heart skipped a beat.

"You are the best at your age I have ever seen. What Dao do you cultivate? I can't really sense whether you are an Evolved or not in this form…" He scratched his blue head and wondered if the man in front of him was 30 for truth or whether that was a lie, "but people around seemed to have considered you Unawakened when I asked earlier."

Hua Yu would have been proud had she heard him speak like this.

Li Shuanwei's face changed. He couldn't help but curse inside.

This fucking old man, I'd squeeze you to death. Of course, he didn't dare to. If he did, a Transcendent could reach him quite easily, and he would be dead in a day or two. No questions asked.

Li Shuanwei bowed down slightly, sweat covering his forehead. He sighed, "This junior very much dislikes violence. I am a very ascetic person. I usually stay in my home without disturbing anyone—"

Han Li's featureless face twitched slightly. That answer was eerily familiar, too; he had said the exact thing to Hua Yu when she was invasively asking him questions, but this time, it seemed to be the truth since that was what he had figured seeing the man himself.

Han Li waved his little hand in irritation. "I asked what Dao your Dao Soul has, not all that. Quickly say it, or this old man will bring his true body here to scan you myself."

Face severe, Li Shuanwei asked, "Senior, do you have to do this?"

Han Li suddenly seemed to have realized something and looked at the three Power Birth Stage Cultivators. "What are you bunch doing here? Scram for this Old Man!"

Immediately, they bowed and flew away. Even Li Xingguang was the same. When a Transcendent spoke, you listen, not question. The blue being looked at Li Shuanwei, curiosity etched across its expressionless face.

"I have no reason to do you harm, kid; you are too conceited. To me, you are just an ant. Now that's out there, tell me, what is your Dao Soul? Why are you so strong for your age? I also want to train my descendants to be like you."

Li Shuanwei grimaced. "Senior, isn't this breaking the unspoken rules of the cultivation world? I am just a weakling. Why are you bullying me like this? Aren't all these opportunities destined by heaven?"

"Snort!" Han Li cursed, "Such hypocrisy at such a young age. You might as well have walked the Demonic Path if you are to keep everything for yourself. Don't worry. I will give you something of equal value if you tell me what is happening. I might even take you as my disciple."

Li Shuanwei became silent. When his secret of being strong was even spread slightly, and when it attracted the attention of a foreign Power Born, he knew he was in for a lot of trouble. He had even expected a Transcendent coming for him, but he had a bunch of plans for that.

When they arrived, they would obviously arrive all four together since he would be quite famous by then; he could have pitted them against each other in a disciple-hiring battle and then become a disciple with all of the world looking at him. They all cared for reputation; he would be safe then, and he would also have a straight path to Transcendence… until this wild blue weed showed itself.

Even he himself didn't know who the person he was speaking to was.

"Brat," Han Li sighed seriously. "I won't make it difficult for you. I will come here myself in a bit."

"Senior," Li Shuanwei suddenly became calm and cold. "Why don't you use your Heart Qi to bind us in one path? I am sure what I can offer is quite a lot to you, but I don't want to let my secrets be out for no reason."

A blue indent of a smile appeared on the smooth face of the walkie-talkie.

"No, it is not about what you want. It is about what you get. If you say no, I can just bring myself here and slaughter you, and I can check everything myself."

Li Shuanwei coldly looked at him. "A Demonic Cultivator with no morals? So much for being a hypocrite." He smiled. "I can self-implode here before you arrive if that makes you reconsider. Even for a Transcendent, it should take a day or two to arrive here from one of the Four Sects. All my secrets, I'll take it with me."

Han Li's smile vanished.

"Self Implode then."

A painful silence stretched between them for exactly an hour until Li Shuanwei's gaze moved to the city beside him and the distant image of his father. His shoulder dropped.

He sighed.

"I do not practice Dao cultivation. It is the truth. I am just an Unawakened..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 184 - 184: Heaven, Beast, and Human
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I do not practice Dao cultivation. It is the truth. I am just an Unawakened..."

The words stunned Han Li. But at the same time, it made him doubt. His Life Vision had always shown Li Shuanwei to be an Unawakened indeed. What was this?

A long silence stretched between them, and the walkie-talkie walked around. He was thinking to himself, what could have made Li Shuanwei so scarily strong if he was an Unawakened?

What was the reason?

"Elaborate," he said.

Li Shuanwei shook his head. "I am put between a rock and a hard place if you say it like that, Senior Transcendent. Now that I have your interest, why don't you bind that Heart Qi promise? I don't want many benefits, just two things. I don't want to give my method to everyone in the whole world; I want it to remain a secret between the senior and me.

"Even if you train your descendants, just bind them so that only one or two people can do it. This is just a simple request, isn't it? And also another request: you would not harm me or my interests unless I harm you after I give it to you. If you can, please also help me avoid other Transcendents interrupting my cultivation."

Hmm… If Han Li himself were a Transcendent, he indeed would have agreed to all. A measured level of request. After all, nobody would want a genius to be stolen from them by their opposition or rivals.

Han Li thought for a second and didn't act too stubborn. It was not that ridiculous of a request, anyway. In the first place, had they known his real identity, nothing would have been possible. Greed wasn't going to do it.

He knew that Li Shuanwei saying he would implode if he didn't get a Heart Qi promise was a very intelligent move on the man's part. Han Li would have done the same because, at that moment, Li Shuanwei himself was the most precious benefit here.

His death would hurt the interests of the opposition, but the man didn't seem to have the staunch heart to follow through with the idea and chose not to go through with that heated bargain with his own life as a price.

Li Shuanwei was not really afraid of death. Han Li could tell as much. It was just that the man could guess his own fate.

Now that he had been noticed by a 'Transcendent,' a greedy one at that, it was time to bow.

Han Li felt no scorn, though; there was no dishonor in bowing to your betters. Han Li himself could agree with that; he had killed many higher-status men with a knife wrapped in silk clothes.

He could also see the small path Li Shuanwei had left for himself.

Li Shuanwei's promise was an open-ended one; Han Li himself wouldn't be able to harm him as long as he didn't harm Han Li or his interests knowingly… the youth was extremely prideful of himself deep down and knew that if he had time, he could defeat Han Li.

There was also another thing: Li Shuanwei didn't really give so much importance to the method he used to fight higher levels. To him, it was not what made him special; it was being himself. In his mind, it was not the power itself but the man.

Han Li could judge many of these details now that he had seen the youth straight up. He felt a bit amused. A genius… huh… what actually made you powerful? Now, I am really curious.

The other party was just a 30-year-old youth, after all; how much could he hide from someone as old as 500 years? To Han Li, he was just a child, but a venomous one.

Calmly, Han Li and Li Shuanwei bound the Heart Qi binding.

Li Shuanwei would teach Han Li the truth about his own strength, and Han Li wouldn't harm him or spread that truth around with abandon. They were bound.

A way to avoid Transcendents? Might as well give it to you. Han Li smiled slightly. He could use this man. But then, his face grew thoughtful.

"Tell me, what makes you so strong?"

Filled with relief, Li Shuanwei sat cross-legged instead of bowing. "It is going to be a bit long. I can speak like this, right?"

"I am not one for ceremonies. Speak."

…

"In ancient times, the creatures of this world didn't have any powers. But then, the Dao Tree and the Sacred Demons brought people from both the Everlasting Human World and Everlasting Demon World here, and people gained powers.

"The people from the Beast World are the Beasts who can evolve to more perfect forms by upgrading their Beast Cores. Humans? Humans didn't really have any powers at the start."

Han Li listened carefully.

He could see Li Shuanwei's indifference to the story. But he himself was reeling slightly inside. He knew of this story since it was part of the Sacred Demon inheritance. Inside the Rust of his sword, all of these things were recorded. But how did the youth know it all so clearly if he had no inheritance?

Was he… another Inheritor?

"Humans got the power by cultivating to become closer to the 'dead' Dao Tree. The Dao Tree had the greatest Dao, the Origin Dao. The Origin Dao was split into seven Great Daos: Life, Light, Darkness, Water, Wind, Earth, and Fire. The seven Great Daos were then split into a multitude of normal Daos like Starlight, Rock, and similar."

Han Li, in walkie-talkie form, nodded. But he also couldn't tell what made Li Shuanwei different.

"In Cultivation, people are blessed with a small part, an entry ticket to a Dao in the form of the Spark. Starlight Spark, for example. And people awaken to it, becoming Awakening Stage Cultivators.

"In the next stage of Evolution, they truly step into the next level, where they have Minor levels of control over their Dao. For example, Starlight Clan members gain a minor level of control over Starlight Dao. That also makes their Spark a Minor Dao Soul of the said Dao.

"In the Power Birth Stage, they gain even more control over the said Dao. They gain Intermediate Dao Souls and Intermediate control over it."

Han Li nodded. He could tell where the conversation was headed.

"But in the Transcendence Stage? We should have gained full control over the said Dao, but we haven't. As senior himself doesn't. Instead, we gain Minor Control over a Great Dao. An Intermediate Starlight Dao Soul would become a Minor Great Dao of Light Soul."

Han Li nodded. "That's obvious. If a single person gained control over a Dao, what would other people on that Dao become?"

He himself had been stunned when he learned he could slowly make Lust Dao his own. Maybe even the Great Dao of Life someday.

"Yes, senior." Li Shuanwei calmly spoke. "But after seeing such things, I began to question everything. I became hateful of cultivation as a whole. I understood at that point something that I had been missing.

"The Dao Tree was not dead; its consciousness was the Heavens; when we accumulate the Energy around and make it into Dao Crystals of a said path, we can use it to trade for Magical Powers. It is using us as the root of the tree now."

Han Li didn't need more explanation than that. Indeed, when the Dao Tree was complete, it had its roots in the emptiness around it, and it absorbed the power from the void to create this world. Now, where did it get the power to sustain itself in a new form, as the Heavens?

Of course, from the 'trades' it did with Cultivators. It was all along right before him.

Han Li took a second to nod, but he still nodded. He already knew it, but that was in this life, his second life. He felt ashamed.

"We are the root and also the hope for the Dao Tree. It wants to create Sacred Demons who can join together to make it be reborn again. Seven Sacreds joining together would have equal powers as the Heavens, equal powers to the Dao Tree.

"It wants to create Seven Sacred Cultivators and make them help it rebuild itself by combining their forces and absorbing energy from the Void. Only the combined force of the seven can do it."

"What is this all related to you?" Han Li asked calmly.

The youth sighed, his laziness evident. "The schemes go deep. These are mostly what I speculated. Everything in this world has already been planned by the Heavens, and we are merely pawns in the way. Seven people should be out somewhere whom it had chosen. Only they matter in the end.

All others are just never going to become Sacreds.

"They seven are the Heaven's children, we all? Just those seven's fuel. No matter how good we are… we are all destined to be slaves for the seven."

Han Li looked at the man calmly.

"When they gain control over the Great Dao, a Sacred Cultivator for each of the seven Daos… they will probably own all our lives. We are nothing but their Crystal collectors. We would collect Dao Crystals, pay them to the owner of the Dao as if in debt, and when the time comes, die.

"When I myself realized that, I was just 13. I compiled all this through my observation of the world… I lost hope. Cultivation was meaningless; it was not meant for me. Not anybody. I never Awakened even though my father asked me to do so many times. I hated cultivation, and I still do.

Thus, I am but a mere Unawakened."

Han Li was genuinely awed. If it was true, the person in front of him was probably the most terrifying mind he had ever seen. No wonder the youth had such a big eye bag. It was the mind of a Nihilist. No wonder he was lazy. He found no meaning in living.

Han Li wasn't so discouraged. At his age, he had seen many types of geniuses, and even though this one stood out, people better than him existed in this world, but in the end, what mattered the most was how one handled such people.

Circumstances were above men, always. Even now, he wasn't contending against this genius using brute strength after all. He was using conventional beliefs. Maybe, the youth himself would realize he was used some time later.

"So what did you come to know in the end? Why are you so strong?"

The youth chuckled. A profound aura began to emanate from him. An aura much weaker than the aura of the Heart Qi that Han Li could use but much more exotic.

The aura was pure. Calm. Lazy, almost. But with just that, he felt as if a blade was put on his neck, not only here but even far in the distance; his real body itself felt a shiver go through it… even while it was unconscious.

Han Li's heart tightened. "What is this Power?"

The aura of power receded, and the youth waved his hand. "It's simple, really. Beasts had the Beast Core that they inherited from the Beasts of the Everlasting Beast World. Humans? We are and have always been using the power of this world's Dao Tree, not any power we inherited from the Everlasting Human World. What power… Do the Humans have that they inherited?"

The walkie-talkie form trembled.

Indeed, what was the original Power of the Humans from the Everlasting Human World?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 185 - 185: The Strongest
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Beasts had Beast Cores they inherited from the Everlasting Beast World…

What about Humans?

What power did they inherit from their interworld ancestors?

Li Shuanwei liked to speak deliberately and slowly, as if he were giving Han Li a picture to figure out the bigger view himself.

Han Li calmly pondered.

Sparks were just powers of the Dao Tree, the Heavens of this world—The World of the Seven Continents. It was a power originally from this world.

But Beast Cores were not. Han Li didn't have much knowledge of it and didn't even really know if it would be possible for Beasts to go beyond Beast King level, which was equivalent to Power Borns. What were the Beast Emperors like?

He had been rather unclear about what even the Heavens were, but now that he had the perspective of Li Shuanwei to think from, he could figure things out.

Indeed…

The Dao Tree being alive in the form of a world-encompassing consciousness made him quite a lot worried about its intentions and also about his Talents.

Would the Dao Tree, the origin of the world itself, actually allow him to steal one of its Daos? Would it? It was basically like stealing its flesh, after all.

Would it obediently give him the ownership oveer the Dao of Lust?

Li Shuanwei didn't interrupt his thoughts.

A minute later, Han Li's voice was severe as he asked, "You found the power of the Humans from the Everlasting Human World? What power do they have? How can we use it ourselves?"

This was not at the level of simple battle strength anymore.

This might as well influence his ambition of attaining immortality and gaining control over life and death.

This was now, actually, solidly important.

Li Shuanwei showed him a calm smile and said, "It is actually rather refreshing to have spoken this out. I also deduced something else. Would you like to know of it?"

Han Li shook his head. "I could guess what it is. I have heard of the multiple worlds in this universe. Each world has a wilted Dao Tree, and among them, people could not even cultivate most of them. But two worlds stand out from all of it, right? The Everlasting Human World and the Everlasting Beast World.

"Those two worlds are the only two worlds with a mature Cultivation System and even complete echo systems. All of the rest of the multiple worlds, including our Seven Continents, are incomplete. Even though I don't know how or why that is since I haven't seen what a complete world like the Everlasting World looks like.

"But our world is special in that it has the powers of both worlds here. Your deduction must be about how this world can create the strongest cultivator in all three worlds. The strongest—Someone who walks all three paths of Beast, Human, and… Heaven/Dao Tree."

In fact, Han Li himself had a wild thought from long ago… his mother had come from a different world. Where was that from?

He had had some thoughts, but he hadn't yet gotten the chance to read her memories and make sure of them.

Li Shuanwei couldn't help but slightly pause.

"Exactly, senior…" His voice was a bit surprised but not too much. He himself had compiled the truth of places like different worlds and such from the hundreds of thousands of myths and legends regarding the Sacred Demons; only then had he been able to compile the most possible truth.

But all Transcendents must already know of these things… or so he guessed.

"So I began my research and…" Li Shuanwei sighed this time before opening his palm. A small transparent orb was there. "You asked me my Dao Soul, I don't have one since I am an Unawakened. This is my Beast Core, or as I like to call it, my Human Core. It is of no Dao or Attribute. It just is."

Han Li's heart trembled slightly.

"What does it do?"

"It makes my body stronger like Transformation Path Cultivators. I could totally stand toe to toe with Sacred Talent Evolved even if they transform. My body is as strong as any in the Evolution Stage and I have more potential than them since I am like a Beast but with intelligence. I am far inferior to Below Average Power Birth Physiques."

'About as strong as myself physically…' Han Li noted.

"So, how did you gain this Beast Core?"

"Simple…" Li Shuanwei dragged there. No matter how deeply he knew it was all meaningless due to him not being one of the seven inheritors, it was still painful to give away his own fortune.

Yet, part of being a nihilist came with calmness. He continued, "I gained the Beast Core in a very lucky encounter. I don't know if senior himself would struggle to do it." A target!

A fat target was on his head now. All over the world, no matter which city he visited, he would never be able to gain legal entry unless he sorted this out.

Han Li looked at the monks flying around him, all carried in the sky by Monk Hen Shin, as they moved towards the nearby Temple.

The boy he had wounded earlier was floating beside him, telling him what he had meant by the word 'Fame.' Han Li had indeed become infamous. And just as his mother wanted, she had also become infamous along with him—as his mother. That was quick.

His face became even more unsightly when all of his women looked at him with expressions of unease. They didn't know what would happen from now on, but Han Li had a faint idea. If it was not for Hen Shin, he alone could slaughter all these tender monks like sheep, but with Hen Shin, it was the opposite.

The old man's smile as he floated in the air was immensely languid.

'He must have been able to see the whole world when he got the chance to Teleport,' Han Li guessed, 'and then should have ordered these monks to come surround me where I am.' That was the only guess he could come up with.

Or else, the monk had somehow put a tracking method on him or his mother when they were in the Demon World when they met. That was plausible. Regardless, the man's smile was inscrutable even to Han Li.

If the monk wanted to kill, though, Han Li knew they'd be dead.

He looked at the little monk whom he had hurt and asked, knowing that Hen Shin would hear this, "Did Monk Hen SHin order you people to surround me?"

The boy nodded. "The Monk did. The monk said to track you down and send us a location where you might be. We found you and he teleported there."

Han Li's brows shot up. So, can Hen Shin speak with people all over the world when not using his teleportation power? Information was power; if someone had such a world-encompassing way of communication, he would rule the era.

Han Li prodded Hua Yu, who was silently following beside him, to make him move forward to Hen Shin's side. Hua Yu complied, and Han Li floated to stand in the air beside Hen Shin.

"Monk, does your Teleportation power come with any other authorities?" Han Li knew he was being awfully nosy, but monks shouldn't mind. Or so he hoped. This power could really put a stain on all of his plans for the future in one way or the other.

He might have to think of a way to kill this monk when he could. As friendly as the person was, and as much gratitude as he felt for him because he had looked after Ruo, Han Li couldn't risk his future ambitions for that. Not that the man seemed killable.

Yet, the biggest issue of all was another thing. Monk Hen Shin had a Lie Detection Magic Power if he remembered correctly. So, it would all get slightly tricky if he were to antagonize the man.

"Other authorities?" The monk rubbed his white beard. Monks were usually not people with a beards, but Hen Shin was quite different from a run-of-the-mill monk. "I do have other authorities. For example, I have a perpetual connection with all of the continent, as in, I can contact any person I wish to with a thought."

Han Li felt a chill in his heart. Calling a person from one end of the continent to the other with a thought? That would make Hen Shin one of the most influential people of this era when added with the power to teleport once per year. It was almost assured that the monk would get one of the Transcendence orbs in the next Transcendence War.

"…That is convenient," he chuckled wryly.

The monk also laughed, merry. "Indeed, convenient. But I need something they use, like a piece of clothing or a lock of hair or something. I contacted these monks earlier and made them find you. Then, I had the location and teleported right beside you."

Han Li grimaced slightly. "So if you had a piece of my clothing, you could appear beside me any time?"

No matter where he hid...

The monk smiled. "Indeed. This monk has become quite something."

All the monks around laughed and clapped their hands, to which Hen Shin also laughed and clapped his hands.

Han Li sighed.

"This is your era, Monk Hen Shin," he said. Then, Han Li looked at little Ruo, who was cradled in his mother's hands; his women had all already been quite familiar with flying due to Hua Yu helping them up and down the waterfall cavern, but they were still amazed by this long-distance flight.

Hen Shin shook his head and also looked at little Ruo.
