Chapter 205 - 205: Liyue's tribulation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Liyue's Existence was in an utterly dark space, her mind full of panic.

What should she do? What should she do?

Inside her own body, her senses were extremely powerful, and even from where she was, she could sense the invader's Existence. It was a woman and a particularly striking one with a devilish countenance. Even though Existence in itself was transparent, the personality, ideals, and everything just seemed right there for sight.

She could see her.

The Invader waved her hand, and mirrors manifested around her; Liyue's gaze trembled at the sight of her family—Li Yao, Yue Li, Ruo, and Hua Feilan. They were all playing ball; she loved to play that too, and they were talking animatedly about… her. They were conversing about the surprises they could give her for her breakthrough.

Liyue's eyes filled with tears. Yet, she tried to remain calm—keeping calm had been ingrained into her by many sorts of training her Big Brother had given her, but it seemed impossible to keep calm.

But just as the intruder looked into the mirror, Yue Li frowned and looked at the gaze as if she could feel it, and Liyue's gaze also grew clear.

A golden shadow loomed over her Mother Yue's Existence; it was heart-rending, peaceful, yet corroding—it almost felt as if she was standing before something beyond mortality when she looked at her existence.

At the same time, she could see more—The golden shadow was not only on Yue Li. The shadow split out from her and covered Ruo, Li Yao, and Hua Feilan with different degrees of intensity.

Ruo had the highest amount surrounding her!

She then quickly turned to look at herself. Now, she could also see that golden light on herself. It was abundant, no lesser than Ruo in intensity. She didn't know what the Golden Shadow was, but she felt that it was something good rather than bad.

"Oh, young girl, wherever you hide, it is no use!" The invader said eerily, removing the mirror, her voice echoing with confidence. "Just think of it, Liyue. I might not be able to kill Han Li, but I'll gently move toward your mother, Li Yao, and then a knife in the heart. Ahh… just think of it: how betrayed would she feel as life left her?

"Her own daughter had killed her!

"Then I will move to Ruo and kill her, too. The tender thing would surely scream, and believe me or not, your Big Brother Han would be nothing more than a lifeless living after that—he cares far more about her than the likes of you!"

"I will then move and kill your dear Mother Yue too…" The voice giggled. "Or I will concoct a poison and put it into all of your loved ones' food. Nobody would be wary of you, the loving, gentle Liyue, after all."

Nobody answered her. Her smirk grew.

"Come out, Little Liyue! Come! Wherever you are hiding, come out! I promise you I will not hurt anyone if you are obedient."

Liyue then looked at herself once again.

Where was she hiding? She wondered.

There seemed to be at least six more places like this where she could hide inside her body. One of those six places was where… the enemy was standing.

That was her Empty Space.

Then where else was she?

Another… Empty Space… in her own body?

There were six more in her?

A total of 7?

What…

An old memory came back to her, the memory of her first marriage attempt with Han Li three years ago.

"Basically, many cruel cultivators are attacking the city, and all three clans are in a startled state. Some of even the Starlight Clan kids are in their hands, supposedly—kidnapped. And we can't enter the city now; most probably, some of the Demons are inside the city, too."

"Why are they attacking?" Liyue asked, grasping her big brother's robe. Nervous.

It was her marriage, and exactly now, the world was going mad.

Han Li thought about this question deeply for a second and then spoke out his thoughts, "Detached Empty Spaces. Have you heard of it, any three of you?"

They shook their heads.

"The Empty Space inside us is a mysterious space that holds our Sparks, Dao Souls, and everything of that sort. And when someone at the stage of Power Birth or higher dies, their Empty Spaces disintegrate into the atmosphere, while their Sparks or Sao Souls rise to the sky—in myths, they are said to one day transform into stars."

They nodded once more.

They had heard of it before.

"But there is a rare chance of an Empty Space not disintegrating after a cultivator dies, and then it transforms into a treasure. A physical orb that could actually be touched physically and used.

The Dao Soul inside it would still phase through its physical bounds and fly off into the sky once the cultivator dies, but not the Empty Space in itself—that is, if the rare instance occurred in the first place."

"What can it be used for?" Liyue asked softly, feeling him stroke her lips through her veil with his own lips. She could also feel the warmth of his breath.

Han Li smiled and spoke, "It can be absorbed to gain a hell of a lot of energy, and I can absorb it slowly over some months; even one of them would cause my cultivation speed to skyrocket for a few days. And not only that, it can be used to cook some special recipes in my hand."

Liyue came back to herself.

That wasn't the case in the end, and everything was because of the man named Li Shuanwei, but they didn't know that at that point. Yet, her Big Brother had guessed that it was from the Southern Lust City and something of that sort would happen.

She then thought of the weird dream she had had during the end of her Breakthrough Dream. As a child, she had touched some Dark Orb in the snow. Not only that, but her physique itself somehow felt unique.

The invader was growing impatient. It barked, "Your secrets won't protect you forever. This body is now mine, all your secrets too! If you die obediently, I will live as Liyue from now on without tainting your name. Your big brother won't even have to feel sad for losing the woman he loves. Just think of it!"

"You!" Liyue gritted her teeth in hatred. Whatever her conclusions had been, it was all useless to escape her circumstances. Somehow, she should find a way to tell her Big Brother that it was not herself that he was dealing with.

The faintest of hints, and he would notice.

"Who are you?" she asked.

The Invader smirked.

"I am a past Transcendent. Someone who you have no chance of tricking in mind games—the name is Munyu. Some call me the Spirit."

Liyue thought and thought, but no way came to her mind. Had she known any Magical Powers, she could have tried using those, but she did not know Magic Powers, nor were there any Dao Crystals in her hand.

Munyu was cautious, for all the arrogance she displayed. She hadn't taken any chances with the process and had chosen the best and weakest moment Liyue would be in.

"Why are you doing this?"

"To live, to grow stronger. You could see it many ways." The Spirit shrugged.

Liyue asked, much calmer, "If you let me go, I promise you I will get you another person to possess and gain a body. Just let me out. It's a promise!"

The Spirit chuckled.

"What a nice idea, but no. I would have agreed to it some time ago, but not anymore; your body is too unique. I want it!" Then, she gave a final declaration: "And I don't want people with the knowledge of the Existence to remain alive, you see. You have two choices: either die or see the people you care for die. That's it. There is no room for negotiations."

There was no one in the Seven Continents who knew of what an Existence was, and that had to remain so. Liyue hung there in the darkness, her thoughts tumultuous.

She'd rather die than let any one of her family die.

All of them were too precious!

She would never be able to live with herself if she caused Han Li grief.

Yet she didn't want to die… she hadn't had her first night, nor had she borne Han Li his child. She hadn't yet seen the limits of what the spear could achieve, nor had she had enough of her blissful life. Tears welled up in her soul.

If she entered the Empty Space where the Spirit was in, she would probably die.

Nobody would even know her sacrifice.

She didn't know what to do, and then, her soul suddenly condensed. Being in this different Empty Space for too long seemed to have done something to her, and she felt nauseous.

She wanted to vomit!

This was not supposed to be a natural thing.

Even if she tried to drag this out, she probably couldn't stay here long.

She checked all of her Empty Spaces one by one, searching for that one clue in everything.

There was no absolute. There must be a chance!

She didn't want to give up so easily!

"Promise me that you won't hurt any of them! Promise it by your Existence!" Liyue said.

The Spirit soothingly nodded.

Promising on their own Existence was something just as powerful as a Heart Qi Binding. And anyone who sensed their own Existence would know that. A person who sensed it and a person who didn't was a different breed.

"I wouldn't hurt them in any way unless they are against me and have hurt me of their own accord."

A long minute passed.

Liyue felt the passage between her Seven Empty Spaces and appeared in a blink before the Spirit. They looked at each other with blank faces.

"They would never hurt you when you are in my appearance," Liyue said.

The Spirit harrumphed, but then, Liyue shook her head, a sense of clear reluctance in her soul's eyes.

The Spirit snorted at the woman, but she didn't immediately attack.

Something was weird. She could feel a sense of oppression.

She became more cautious. The last time she tried to possess someone, she got trapped within Yang Wei and almost became one with him. She had learned from that lesson to completely break the opponent's morale before she attempted to devour the Soul.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 206 - 206: The situation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Spirit snorted at the woman, but she didn't immediately attack.

Something was weird. Anxiety began to crack the Spirit's mind.

She could feel the source of the pressure on the tip of her tongue but just couldn't name it.

The last time she tried to possess someone while looking down on them, she got trapped within Yang Wei's body and almost died. She had learned from that to completely break the opponent's morale before she attempted to devour the Soul of a person.

"Tell me what I have to keep in mind living as you. I will do it for you in this lifetime; I will become a good, obedient wife for that man." The Spirit said, hiding her nervousness; her face even seemed a bit sad she had to do this to Liyue.

"They would never hurt you when you are in my appearance," Liyue said, her tone soft, wiping her eyes. "But sadly, you won't have the chance. And today, I probably will murder a sentient life for the first time."

The Spirit's face changed.

She herself had an extremely oppressive feeling the moment Liyue entered the Empty Space.

She finally got it!

It was as if she was not standing before a single Evolved but seven of them!

Liyue looked at her, and her sad bearing changed. It became contemplative, self-deprecating.

"Big Brother taught me so much and is proud of me…" She shook her head. "I am not worth it. When an actual adversity happened, I was crying my eyes out and not reacting calmly enough."

From the moment she had arrived here, Liyue had been sure of something. She had been panicking for no reason. She had been putting the Spirit on… too high a pedestal when she was on the other side.

It was afraid to attack an actual Evolved, and she had 7 of what constituted an Evolved. She was not normal, and not normal meant… good in her man's words.

She had 7 of the Empty Spaces in her body, and that should be the reason for it—her Existence was stronger than the current Spirit's punching weight. It had even been apprehensive of Hua Feilan… not to mention her.

And then calmly, ignoring the squirming Spirit, she looked at herself.

She was covered in golden shadows. Lights of golden brilliance.

When she was here, surrounded by the golden shadow around her, she felt secure. Even if she was weaker than the Spirit, she had a feeling that she wouldn't need to be worried—this light protected her.

Indeed, she was right.

Outside Liyue's body, Han Li's existence was also covered in this golden light, but his golden light was intense. Far, far more intense than anyone else. Even Ruo and Liyue's golden light would hardly compare to a glass when he was beyond a river.

The golden light around him spun in unique ways, and suddenly, he opened his eyes. He was filled with worry. Did Liyue have any issues with her Dao Soul after Evolving?

It was a worry out of nowhere.

He opened his eyes and focused on Liyue's beautiful face with Life Vision. His face immediately changed when he saw the… Unawakened Status he was all too familiar with.

Yet, it was still Liyue. Had he looked a second ago, he might have seen a different name.

The Spirt itself had started to be entwined in this golden halo now, but unlike people on their side, it was being tied down, and everything was slowly becoming more transparent in its body. Liyue calmly looked at it.

Then, she asked, "I do instinctively know how to, but I don't want to devour a living thing; why don't you leave my body?"

The Spirit was no fool. She already knew she was trapped. There was some kind of thing around these people's Existence that she seemed to lack entirely—golden shadows. She could also feel how bad a choice Shan Liyue had been to possess.

This woman was super weird!

No, everyone around here was super weird.

She coldly looked at Liyue. "Don't be too arrogant. Indeed, I had been too arrogant, thinking you would have a weak Existence, but even if yours is stronger, I have more experience. I'll take you with me if you try to kill me."

Liyue looked at her hand. Ignoring the Spirit again.

"So this Existence thing is where our memories, our Proficiency of weapons, and every such is stored?" she asked.

The Spirit grew even more apprehensive. 'I should leave!'

She would die if she stayed long outside a physical body, but her Vital Weapon—the Mirror Mind, would be able to carry her Existence for a while. She would not be greedy this time and would choose a more normal boy to start cultivation.

She had been too greedy this time!

She was too injured, and her Existence was too weakened.

She started to fade away, and Liyue didn't do anything.

Honestly, she didn't know what she could do to kill or stop the Spirit. Just that, before the woman left, she aimed a palm at her, thinking of her Spear in mind.

The woman harrumphed. "My Existence might be weaker, but my Proficiency?" The phantom of a war blade destroyed the slight phantom of a spear before it even manifested.

Yet, the Woman's face changed as six more spears appeared around her before she could react and pierced her. Even Liyue was a bit dumbfounded.

The Spirit's expression was so transparent that she was almost nonexistent as Mirrors manifested around her. She couldn't use her own powers more than that.

Liyue chuckled amusingly.

How the positions had shifted…!

"Why don't you become my pet? A chicken I am looking after has many unhatched eggs. You can enter one of them, and I will not mind."

The Spirit desperately tried to move out of Liyue's Empty Space, but somehow, the passage was not there. She tried again. It was not there.

Liyue smiled. She had just noticed that she had a passage around all seven of her Empty Spaces. She could enter any of the seven using the passage and just as easily come back. She could also close the passage.

The Spirit could only moan in her heart.

Failure!

A great failure!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 207 - 207: Liyue's touch at the Existence
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Liyue opened her eyes groggily. It felt as if she had slept for days on end. In fact, it was only now that she woke up after her Breakthrough Dream.

Gentle evening sunlight filtered through the curtains on the side. A cool breeze lifted them away from the windows.

Feeling comfortable, she looked up and smiled widely. Han Li's red eyes were curiously looking at her; she could see the anticipation almost bursting.

She was the one on the lap now.

Liyue laughed almost unknowingly.

"What are you gawking at? Too amazed by how beautiful the woman before you is?"

Han Li smiled. "Maybe. You have indeed become dangerously beautiful. Alarmingly so."

Her sword-like eyebrows danced. She was in a good mood even though her heartbeat was basically drumming with adrenaline. It was the first time she had met death to the face, and it had ended up quite nice.

She wanted to hold the secret back for a while before saying it, but her mouth just couldn't stop. "Big Brother, you guys already lost in giving me surprises today! Someone gave me something truly wondrous."

Han Li squinted, and she sat up in a quick motion. Then, she sat on his lap, her face facing him from slightly above him, and her legs wrapped around his waist.

He wrapped his own hand around her waist.

"Guess who is in a good mood," he commented.

She hugged his face tightly, and he was a bit disoriented as she continued to throw kisses at him from all sides. "I have so much to say I don't even know where to begin!"

Han Li pulled out his head from her tight embrace and looked at her curiously. He had a guess but he decided not to be a buzzkill.

"Oh, enlighten this humble husband."

"First of all, this Miss Liyue has officially found out the reason for our marriage failures!"

Han Li furrowed his brows. That was completely out of his expectations.

"What do you mean?" he asked, much more solemn.

She was still smiling. "It's our Mother. She is the reason for our marriage failures! All because of Mother Yue."

Han Li's face was a funny sight.

Liyue didn't know whether to laugh or just hug him more tightly. Before that, she asked, "Big Brother, do you know this… Spirit? Munyu?"

That made Han Li's face change. "Where did you hear it?"

"Answer me first; mine's a longer story."

"The Spirit is the expert I told you about once. Remember that Yang Wei I dueled? She was the creature that resided inside him. That was the Spirit. Munyu was her name."

"Expert…" Liyue almost had a condescending smile on her face. "Did you kill her too?"

"Probably. I've told you about this before, but yes, it shouldn't be a threat to us… Where are you headed with this?"

"I've told you something before, right?"

"You've told me many things before."

"I said 'That I would beat you in a fight.' Do you want me to show you something?"

The room grew silent except for the flying curtains and the breeze. They were seated on the bed, their shadows hanging on the walls. Han Li pushed her down and looked at her stunning face lying in the bed.

"Who taught you to be so roundabout? Go straight to where you are headed. So irritating!"

Liyue nodded and gave him a light hit on his back.

He had been completely unprepared, and suddenly, he felt his world spin; it was as if her touch had moved beyond his physique and touched him right at the soul.

When he opened his eyes, Liyue was proudly seated above his stomach with two legs on each of his sides. Her brown monk robes, added to the warm sunlight on her face, almost made him dazed.

Her long and wavy black hair was flying in the breeze.

"That was…" He asked, his face full of disbelief. She had just lightly touched him, and he felt as if he had been rammed by some powerful club—painless as it was.

What was going on?

This… This…

"Tsk, tsk," She leaned down and planted a kiss on his dumbfounded lips. Then, drew back, patting his cheeks. "Only brawns and no brains, such a disappointment. And you hadn't seen half of what I am capable of."

Han Li blurted out, "What are you capable of? How does this work..."

She drew a line from his forehead to his nose with her fingers.

"Do you trust me?"

Han Li was peeved.

"I would normally… but I don't trust you now. Truly speaking… I am kind of scared right now. Did that Spirit come back to life and do something to you?"

Liyue was speechless. She showed her palm, baring her cute claws.

"I'll use my power again if you don't trust me!"

"How dare you threaten me!" He flipped her on the bed, slamming both of her hands against the bed and completely trapping her beneath him. Even her legs were completely immobilized. "Use the power now? Heh, you are still too weak!"

Liyue smirked. "Not too sure about that. I don't need to use my hand to use the power. You are strong. Far stronger than me but in the one aspect I am strong at, you are weak."

Her Existence flared in her eyes, seeing right into Han Li's powerful, gold-tinted Existence. She sent a wave of shock from her Existence to Han Li through the hand by which he was locking her own hands.

Once again, Han Li was disoriented, and when he opened his eyes, Liyue was on his stomach, her gaze condescending and playful.

Then, she grew slightly serious, thinking, 'If that Yang Wei person had this power, Big Brother would have been in danger. Seems like he wasn't allowed or couldn't use the power even though he had connections with the Spirit.'

She was not really that strong. If she was looked at with critical eyes of analysis, she was just a newly Evolved. She was weak! A normal Evolved could defeat her easily!

But her touch was unique. If she touched a person, she could startle their Existence and that is enough to make them faint shortly. Han Li was strong, but his Existence was completely unprotected. It was like an armor that didn't protect the eyes.

She would probably die in a second if she tried to fight him when he knew that her 'touch' was deadly. But if someone didn't know that, her 'touch' would be deadly!

She could startle them right at their core!

Maybe even Power Born could be startled... and killed, with this specific power!

If they didn't know it and they weren't people who had sensed Existence themselves, a touch alone would probably be enough to handle them.

He looked at her with an eye full of doubt. His 500 years of experience had included nothing about an... Existence.

She squinted. "What? You have a problem I defeated you?"

Han Li caught her by the collar and pulled her into his embrace. She was trapped in a hug, and he wrapped his hand around her. "I am proud. So teach your big brother the trick."

"No way. Let me harass you a bit more. Maybe… let me throw you into a cold pond in the break of dawn, then beat you up with a stick in the name of training."

"Vengeful!" he cursed.

She laughed at the name. "But then, I'll kiss you after taking you out of the water like a rag doll. Soothe you, and then make you give me an excellent oral service down there. Fantastic things! Exactly how you've been teaching me. Of course, I'll be gentle at times and such."

Han Li looked at her eyes with skepticism.

The best thing to do not to give oil to fire was not to talk about it.

"What is with this power anyway?"

"I have so much to tell you, I just touched your Existence and you were too unguarded..." She changed her tone, smirking at his dubious face. "I even made your Expert Spirit promise to become our temple's guardian beast!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 208 - 208: I'll teach you in a bit!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and Liyue were still in the room.

Liyue was comfortably lying against his shoulder while speaking, and Han Li was listening to her as he massaged her hand. He was totally mind-blown.

She had indeed destroyed his image of the world and then rebuilt it in one mere hour.

The fucking… Spirit tried to possess her?

She gained the ability to sense her own Existence? And then also found out she had 7 Empty Spaces instead of one?

Color him surprised.

That much was already rather quirky enough for a mere Breakthrough to the Evolution Stage, but there was more. The golden shadowy mist from his Mother was supposedly binding his own Existence and all the others into one and many more.

Everything honestly left him in a really hard position.

He couldn't even believe in all of her words... It was too hard to believe. He couldn't even entirely trust her after The Spirit was a part of it all.

But her actions spoke for her.

The current Liyue was strong. He could probably defeat her from a distance if he used some tactics, but if she touched him—he was done for. She was touching this supposed Existence of his and he had no way to react against it.

Han Li had a sneaking suspicion that she might even be a threat to actual Power Born now.

She had barely Evolved yesterday. This all left him clearly and harshly confounded.

There is always more Secrets...

This actually cleared some things, though.

For example, Liyue's goddess-like body. Liyue's figure and beauty were just too astounding.

If she ever Transcended, her beauty alone would probably have more than half of the male population of the continent red with envy towards Han Li. Even now itself, that was truly the case, just in a smaller proportion.

Maybe this was the reason she turned so beautiful in a matter of years?

Then, another fact was also cleared.

Why were many people shown as Unawakened when they were not?

Obviously, sensing this Existence was important. It was probably a game-changer.

He had his doubts, but so did Liyue.

She was also worried.

7 Empty Spaces? What did that mean?

She had tried to bring out the Spirit and show it to Han Li, but it couldn't leave her Empty Space without being in peril, and it also told her it was impossible to see her for Han Li.

Someone like Han Li couldn't see her, at all. Only people who have sensed their own Existence can see and sense other people's Existence. Only people who sensed their own Existence can fight against others who had sensed it.

It even made sense now why he couldn't read the Spirit's memories for so long.

The only thing he had yet to make sense of was why Liyue said that his mother was the reason for their marriage being a failing streak. Her words were in a positive take, so he was even more in the middle of a puzzle.

In the end… there was only one thing to do. All doubts could be cleared.

One logical thing that would let them know the best course to take from here.

Han Li asked, sincere, "Liyue, can I read your memories?"

That was the most logical thing to do and there was no reason not to do that. Unless she didn't want to. After a second of surprise, she nodded without a hint of reluctance.

Why would she say no?

Han Li smiled. Then shook his head at himself.

There was no reason to be suspicious of her.

He'd be suspicious whether the Spirit did something to her had she been weaker than him, and had she not had a chance to really take him off guard. But she already had some chances to completely take him off guard if that were so; she could have kept silent about this whole fiasco, even.

His Life Vision was also clear in who she was. Even though he knew not to trust on it too much, he had trust in his intuition about things.

He'd be able to read Liyue if she was not Liyue—earlier, he had been unguarded of the Spirit and its weird powers, but he was now aware. That was Heaven and Earth's difference.

If she had malevolent intentions, she could have killed him when she first used her power to stun him with a touch, too. She was Liyue, and Liyue was a woman he had trust in.

"You really are Brother Han; your bag of tricks never ends," she smiled charmingly at him.

Reading memories? That's surprising! But reasonable, reasonable... if it was him.

"Of course, I can do many things."

Han Li looked at her, and their eyes reflected each other.

Smiling, he leaned closer, shifted his position, and nibbled on her neck. A bit surprised, she looked sideways at him in interest.

What was he doing?

She caressed his hair.

His tooth gently sunk down, yet she could feel no pain. Only a surging sense of desire.

Lust Dao Talent—Painless Lust.

It transformed Pain into Lust!

"Such a convenient power," she shifted slightly, letting him more comfortably suck her blood. "So really, Big Brother, are you the successor of the Sacred Demon or something? I don't think you are just a normal Sacred Talent Cultivator—just like me."

Stronger people had more right to know his secrets, after all.

She felt slightly smug inside, but her face was just flushed.

"Something like that," Han Li replied and then immediately felt trickles of her blood dripping into his throat, and used another Magic Power.

Lust Dao Talent—Lustful Healing.

Her lust burned itself, and she started to heal. It was a painfully slow process, but the wound was shallow.

Since the Life Sword was now inside his body, as long as he touched someone's blood, he could read their memories. Yet, he was doubtful whether he'd be able to read Liyue's memories.

After all, she was similar to the Spirit.

She hadd sensed Existence.

He laid down on Liyue's lap, and she looked at him curiously. His lips were slightly bloody.

The red mark on his forehead, barely visible, took her focus next.

With her eyes infused with the power of Existence, she felt as if she could see more of this mysterious man who had stolen her heart so casually, but he was ever mysterious.

She waited for him to wake up from reading her memories but suddenly, a twang of uncomfortable strangeness hit her straight in the Existence that she had grown so fond of. Herself.

She could feel someone try to steal a glance into her soul. It was as if someone was trying to enter inside forcefully, and her focus shifted to the feeling of familiar warmth.

It was Han Li's Existence.

Suppressing the feeling of strangeness, she let him into her mind; if she wanted to, she could totally resist it, but of course, she didn't want to resist. If she wanted to, she could have even made him have a nightmare there and then!

The Spirit had always been doing exactly that when Han Li tried to read her memories. People who sensed their own Existence were indeed on a different level.

Suddenly, Liyue was surprised to find herself inside her mind. In memories—Her memories.

Han Li was before her… blank.

His eyes were… an abyss.

As if he lacked eyes.

He was an eyeless creature. Incapable of seeing.

She almost gasped, frowning slightly at the unsightly sight he had become as she touched him soothingly. "Big Brother, can you see me?"

Her soothing call gained no answers. No matter how much she prodded him, he didn't seem to see. Indeed, he didn't have the ability to see an Existence.

Was it necessary to shock him into seeing it?

Like the Spirit 'shocked' her into it?

She felt the power in her own Existence. Liyue leaned towards him, and their lips met—his form was horrible, and she was like a goddess of beauty.

The power slowly inundated him.

…

All he could see was a dark forest, an Eyeless creature looking at him.

He couldn't pass that, no matter how much he tried.

It seemed as if the creature was devouring him. He knew the memory watching was bound to fail. He wondered about it: what was an Existence?

Did he lack one or something?

His birth wasn't natural, after all. That made him worried.

Soft lips caressed his senses as his gaze slowly came to a snow-studded city. Face filled with wonder, he looked around.

His heart must have been beating heavily, and his face must have been blushing when his gaze finally turned to Liyue. She was smirking.

"How's it?" She said, "It's my Breakthrough day, and it's me giving gifts to you… Tsk."

Han Li had no words.

This was Existence?

He only looked at her in wonder.

Normally, Existence lacked color, but inside memories, an Existence was the most beautiful thing. The most colorful. The only thing that truly Existed.

Han Li was suddenly struck.

…Fuck.

He was in love.

Chapter 209 - 209: Claimed!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Returning back to the real world, Han Li's scarlet gaze had gained yet another layer of mystery. Like a twinkle, it checked the world around it.

Everything was just… the same.

Now, the time had passed even further, and it was near time for night to arrive. Sun had turned orange, and it was now dipping down into the distance and would soon vanish, replacing the world with night.

Yet, the only person around him, Liyue, seemed to have become much brighter than everything. Her existence was striking, just like her true body. He was quite baffled by it but then saw her… pouting.

"What?" He asked, squinting. He was in a really, really good mood.

"You know… every most embarrassing thing about me now. So unfair…" Her face was flushed.

Her childhood, her days of suffering to handle Li Yao's illness before Han Li arrived, her tearful nights—nothing was a secret to him. Han Li smirked at her pout.

She tried to use her power, but it was useless. "And I wanted, I really WANTED to bully you for at least a bit at least..."

"Liyue, that's a bad way of thinking. Now you can bully all of the others, you know? I don't think anyone would be able to beat you even if they catch up with you in cultivation anymore."

Her eyes twinkled. "Really?"

The power to see the Existence was indeed a… cheat.

He nodded.

Then, he sat up, seriousness clouding his visage.

He had seen her memories from start to finish. He had obviously sensed… the Existence, too. It would not be too hard if another person who knew how The Existence worked helped out—just like Liyue was helped out to do exactly that by the Spirit.

But now he had more to be worried about. Seven Empty Spaces.

"When you were young, you happened to see a Detached Empty Space in the snow, and when you touched it, it sank into you."

She nodded, worried.

She had also seen her blurry childhood memories using the chance when he was watching it. From start to finish, she also understood that many weird things were happening even inside her own body that she didn't understand.

He had seen everything embarrassing about her, but it was rather… releasing. He had been laughing when some of her stupidness had passed by in the memories. It was such an… amazing experience. Who would be able to say that they had gone down the memory line of their lives along with the love of their life? She definitely counted her life as a blissful one.

He had seen her from her birth to this very day, including her brawl with The Spirit. He knew her like a part of his life.

"So that black orb I touched as a child was indeed a Detached Empty Space… so do they work like this? Becoming extra Empty Spaces in the toucher's body?"

Han Li shook his head. "Never seen this happen. Your body is weird, Liyue. I don't know; something's just… different about it."

"In a good way?"

"… I am not sure."

She bit her lips. "If I had an Empty Space naturally and gained more from somewhere. Like if the rest of the 5 in the total 7 were Detached Empty Spaces, we should have seen it in the memories…"

"I have two theories. A wild one and a wilder one," Han Li said. "Liyue, you were either born with 6 Empty Spaces in your body or… you can extract Empty Spaces from the dead."

"…What?"

"You have been to exactly five funerals in your life, and if you count those 5 Empty Spaces. Add the one you naturally have and the one you got in the snow; it adds up. That alone is unbelievable, but it even seems to make the taken-out Empty Spaces into your own." He then paused, "You have more Empty Spaces, but having them are just appearances… rather useless."

Han Li suddenly frowned heavily, and then his mind suddenly paused all functions.

… He was such a fool!

He slapped his thighs. Indeed!

Han Li calmed down forcefully.

His Heart Qi started to emanate out from him, and his Existence controlled it like a general watching over his soldiers.

It was the essence of his first life, Heart Qi. As strong as a Transcendent!

The ephemeral and impossible-to-control Heart Qi, until this point, was like an obedient child in front of his Existence!

Indeed, it was not that The Spirit had some unique way to control it but that it had sensed its… Existence! Since he had done that, too, he could also control the Heart Qi at his fingertips. Thousands upon thousands of doors opened in his mind.

Han Li could think of a bajillion ways to use this.

Clenching his palms in excitement, he used the power of the Existence on his eyes.

The Life Vision flared to life.

It was no longer just simply powerful; it was seeing straight to the soul of things.

He planned to first look at Liyue, but the first place his gaze moved to was his surroundings.

In every block of creation, from dust to wood, from the bed to floor, he could see something, a will, an omnipresent thing that was everywhere and in everything—for the first time, he had finally come to see the Heavens.

The consciousness of the… Great Dao Tree.

A solemn expression plastered itself on his face naturally.

It was an amalgamation of a complex thing. It just lay there, silent, powerless. Waiting to revive one day.

There was only a part of it here, while the rest of it was spread around the whole World of the Seven Continents. An immediate picture of the whole thing formed in his mind by just looking at one part of the consciousness.

Oh, Save me, child!

The cry of Heavens was discordant, but Han Li coldly pushed it aside without a thought.

He focused on its full form.

It was the form of a complete circle with an unbelievably complex pattern. Yet, in the huge puzzle piece, many things were missing. He focused on one part.

'The Lust Dao…'

That part was the Lust Dao.

'299' pieces among the '300' were not there. Indeed, looking into himself, those were his Talents now—it was part of his Existence. Then, his gaze moved elsewhere, and his face changed.

He could see many missing Daos.

'The Dao of Explosion is missing entirely,' he saw a gaping hole in the Heavens, 'and some other Daos under the Fire Great Dao are also missing.'

Han Li could also notice some others too. The other Inheritors… some of them had already made some of the Daos their own, and he was sorely lagging behind!

Han Li was the inheritor of the Life Great Dao among the Seven Great Daos, and he was the most backward among the Seven Sacred Demon Inheritors. He had been too cautious.

With an inquisitive gaze, he looked into his own Existence. The Talents he had gained were all inside there, forming its own incomplete circle—incomplete Heaven. One with him.

They had no connection with the Heavens anymore.

In totality, it was believed and known that there were "300" Magic Powers in each normal Dao and "3000" Normal Daos under all Seven Great Daos.

Han Li looked at the last Magic Power in the Lust Dao, which was not his own but the Heaven's. With just a gaze, the chant to call for that power came to his mind.

He just had to use it ten times using Dao Crystals as pay, and it would become his own. He had already used it nine times before; one more time and it would become his.

His gaze shimmered, and he looked for another Dao—The Dao of Starlight.

With just a glance, he could see all 300 Magic Powers available in that particular Dao, and he could also understand their chants. Of course, without their particular Spark or Dao Soul, he couldn't use them.

"Heh…" Han Li breathed out.

There really was…

He shook his head in disbelief.

It all made it seem so easy. It had always been there. He had just been too reluctant to claim it.

He almost wanted to kiss Liyue to breathlessness at the moment.

Eyes bright, he focused his gaze on the Lust Dao again and chanted the words for the last remaining Magic Power once. A Dao Crystal was lost.

The final Magic Power of the Lust Dao in the tapestry of the Heavens loosened and became a part of his own Existence.

Lust Dao vanished from Heaven and Earth.

The Dao of Lust had been claimed.

Chapter 210 - 210: Fiery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Liyue looked at Han Li and felt an instinctive urge to just close her eyes.

She gulped and walked some steps back.

Han Li seemed like an explosion through her Existence-infused senses. There he was, sat in a meditative position silently, eyes closed.

The last glimmer of light in the outside world made him seem as simple as a normal monk, but his stature was like that of a mountain.

His eyes always had a different shine when he looked at her. She could read the room.

He had never refused any of her requests. He had never kicked her into any cold pond like he had done all the others in the room, even though she, too, was practically one of his disciples, too.

Han Li treated her differently. As if she was a delicate doll that could break easily.

She respected him, but that was respect. She was not interested in becoming one of his harem members.

Ruo was a person who was with him at his lowest. She had seen him as her everything. She and his Mother were his worlds—until his mother died of her lifespan shortage, and Ruo was played into suicide.

If she regained her memories, how would she look at him at this moment?

Probably with teasing warmness. She was not that different from the Ruo at this moment, but they were also subtly and profoundly different. It boggled him how memories were what made a person.

Wasn't memories what made a person who they are?

"Rule one: What happens in Paradise stays in Paradise."

The rules were clear.

No one could betray each other out of maliciousness. Yet, other than true betrayal, everyone had their own freedom, and nobody restricted their actions in the true essence.

They were not Han Li's puppets. They had full autonomy of themselves but they could think whatever they wanted to think. But they couldn't act against any of the members of the sect maliciously while knowing they were part of the sect.

With Han Li's experience, creating a set of laws without any loopholes that didn't restrict anyone's true freedom at all was not especially hard. The position of the Sect Master did command more respect than the other members but it was not to the point of slavery.

And thus, The Demon Paradise was born.

Han Li had become a Sect master. If the world knew, Transcendents would probably send killers after him already.

It was not really an ordinary Heart Qi Binding either. He was much better at using Heart Qi after sensing his own Existence and so, he could create a sort of Heart Qi Binding that would warn the bound people without outright hurting them first.

If they were close to breaking a law, they themselves would be warned by a sort of pain—they would be warned (You are close to going against the laws).

As the caster of the binding, he himself would also feel that their binding was being strained. It was indeed similar to how a sect functioned.

Just that the sect would require absolute patriotism.

The Dao was his, his alone—and he had full power over it. He could even change the word for summoning the Magic Power if he wanted to.

"Kiss and cuddles all night?" she asked him.

He just kissed her. "Take out the Spirit first. Let me have a talk with that thing."

Liyue growled. "So you didn't come here because of a craving to sleep with me!"

"I did; that was not a lie."

"Lies!"

Han Li looked at her fair face, puffing up with anger, and placed a kiss on her juicy lips.

She tried to wiggle away but then submitted pretty quickly and began devouring his tongue instead.

They broke away.

"Big Brother, you really did come here just to speak with the Spirit, didn't you?" she lamented exaggeratedly but cutely. "You meanie. If you loved me, you could have asked for the Spirit late—"

Her lips were locked.

"Meani—"

Lips were locked once more.

"Hatefu—"

"Bastar—"

"Annoyin—"

"Dummy!"

She suddenly stopped speaking and looked at Han Li's smiling face.

"Why did you stop? Continue," she complained.

"How dare you call me such words." He bit her juicy lips gently, and she didn't dare to speak for fear of hurting herself with his sharp teeth, "I thought you respected me as your master."

He broke away with a smile. "I won't harm you, you fool. I have good control over myself."

Liyue blushed. "I know, but my lips are one of the things you love the most. How can I harm them?"

"Oh…" Han Li sighed. "Then you can't harm no place in you now."

She paused in her moves. Han Li could literally see her slightly panicking in her lying position. Even her heart had skipped a beat.

Then, she became silent.

"…"

"…"

Her breath all but squeezed out of her chest. "Damn you, Big Brother. My soul almost jumped out of my head."

Han Li smiled. "Is it really the first time I told you something like that?"

"It…"

Her heart was still fluttering, and she just wanted a kiss now. She pulled his head to her lips, and they were both in a land of each other for a while. She didn't let him pull out until she was breathless.

They just looked at each other for a long time after that. She could almost scream.

He had never really said he loved her—he did say it, but that was to the Spirit when she had just woken up after Evolving and not directly to herself. She herself didn't think she would be this startled when he actually said it for once.

She gulped. He just looked at her amusingly.

She was an absolute masterpiece of beauty, her brows like slanted swords perfectly carved, while her eyes were like those of dewy waters. Her face, too, was just a sight to behold as she blushed. One would just want to bite her cheeks and make her say sweet things.

Only Han Li could make her do that, though. Only he could bite her cheeks, too.

"Now take out the Spirit. Let's do the important stuff first before we fall into bed for actual."

Liyue nodded, still feeling thrilled. "The Spirit says the Heavens will devour it if it comes out into the open without a body. I can't take it out. You will have to enter my Empty Space with your Existence—Spirit taught me a way to do that."

When it tried to possess her, she had obviously had the higher hand, and of course, she had used the chance to make the Spirit into her pet.

She had made the Spirit promise on its own Existence that it would not lie or act against them without her permission.

Soon, she planned to let the Spirit possess an unhatched egg. She really planned to make it her pet—her chicken pet. If people knew that she planned to make a past Transcendent into her chicken, they would cry blood.

Han Li himself was secretly amazed inside.

"Let the Spirit come out," he said, "Heaven can't devour her when I am here."

Liyue smiled. If he says so.

'Spirit, you are allowed out of my Empty Space. Come out, and don't you dare disrespect my Big Brother. I'll kill you if you do!'

The Spirit was filled with fear and sadness.

Her lofty goals, her lofty ambitions, her sacrifices… She felt simmering hatred and slowly moved out into the real world. When the Existence moved out of the body, it was the same as someone trying to Evolve.

When someone tried to Evolve, they brought their sparks far out of their body, and that would make them also subconsciously pull out their Existence; vulnerable to the heavens, they could only have a quick fight with it and try to go back into the body.

The Heavens were omnipresent.

Just as the Spirit came out, a so mighty Existence began to move and surround her. She could feel that she would soon begin to get devoured by the Heavens. The Heavens were the 7 Great Daos combined—it was the mightiest of all in the Seven Continents.

But it was weakened. It couldn't even easily eat a person trying to Evolve, not to mention her, so quickly. She could hide inside her Vital Weapon, Mirror Mind, to hide from the Heavens, too, but she didn't have much energy remaining after failing to possess Liyue.

She felt as if she was about to die in a mere few seconds and waited, but a smaller but still unique power covered her in a blanket, and the Heavens could only stare helplessly.

The Dao of Lust had protected her.

She felt a chill up her sleeve as Han Li's red gaze looked at her like food ready to be eaten.

'Life Vision,' Han Li thought calmly. Her information began to appear before him, but it was no longer as it used to be; now he could see deeper. His gaze could well and truly penetrate souls.

His Sacred Eyes gave him every facet of the Spirit, Munyu.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 223 - 223: Of course, you still have it, right?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Spirit felt a chill up her spine as Han Li's red gaze looked at her like food ready to be eaten. She felt naked in front of his gaze.

'Life Vision,' Han Li thought calmly.

Her information began to appear before him, but it was no longer as it used to be; now, he could see deeper. His gaze could well and truly penetrate souls.

The Sacred Eyes gave him every facet of the Spirit, Munyu. There was no way she could hide anything—being able to see Existence had changed Han Li's perceptions itself, so of course, his greatest investigation ability, "Life Vision," improved.

________________________

[Munyu] [Age: 1200]

[Existence Level: Rank 5]

________________________

[Dao Tree Cultivation: None]

[Beast Core: None]

[Vital Weapon: Mirror Mind]

[Physical Body: N/a]

[Heart Qi (level 1)]

[Cannot use without a Physical Body she has full control over]

________________________

[End time cultivation in the first life Premonition: Unawakened]

[End time cultivation in the last Premonition: Unawakened]

[Death: (in the last premonition) never died.]

________________________

The options to see what her cultivation was in the previous life were unfunctional. After all, his previous life memories were premonitions, and he was now reading her own Existence to understand her powers. He was, in a way, forcefully reading her memories about herself without doing that.

Then, he would compare her memories with his own to make a true understanding of this once-enemy of his. How can she know how strong she was in her past life? From her memories, of course, he couldn't gain future knowledge regarding her.

But in the Spirit's case, her future was not important. What was important was her past.

He knew that in the future, Yang Wei would become a Transcendent out of nowhere and become the sect master of one of the four sects. Han Li was the extra then, and the effort of a combined attack from everyone had killed him.

That meant… something.

For a Power Born to become Transcendent, they only needed one thing.

They didn't have to be extremely talented, they didn't have to be the best at what they do, they could be the most unremarkable Power Born, and they could still Transcend. But there was one thing that they needed to do that.

And for that one thing, every time it appears, the Sacred Lust Continent itself goes to war.

Transcend Mortality Orb.

Han Li and Yang Wei had no real connection in his past life, but he knew who the person was even before he regressed. He knew Yang Wei.

Every 400 to 600 years or so, a battle, a war, happens.

This battle would always be extremely important — it was called the Transcendence War.

The next Transcendence War was merely about 12 years away now.

In this war, Power Born individuals—people at the level of clan heads—would all join in an attempt to claim a unique magical resource that was essential in breaking through from Power Birth to the Transcendent Stage.

It was a war for breakthrough!

The resource was indeed… Transcend Mortality Orb. Only if they absorb it can they Transcend. If they had it, they needed nothing more.

Every Continent among the seven great continents only had a set number of this resource, and it was an absolutely important thing for a Power Born.

If they didn't get it in their generation when they were young, the next time the war happened, they would be nearly dying due to age after all. Their lifespans could only last for one generation of the war.

If they got it this time, they could live long. If they didn't, they would have to die.

Sacred Lust Continent only had 4 Orbs every generation.

So, the wars were absolutely savage, with almost all the clans getting involved, as long as the Power Born had even the slightest hint of ambitions.

In his past life, he had been nothing in the war 12 years from now other than one of the thousands upon thousands of Evolved.

Only in the war after that, 463 years later today, had he fought in his own war and won one of the orbs, becoming a Transcendent.

But that was not the intriguing part.

To Han Li, that was just a memory. What mattered was Yang Wei.

He was a legendary person in his past life.

All four of the Orbs were taken by some Power Born as the war concluded—one of them being in Han Li's hand—and the other Power Borns were completely out of steam to fight back.

The war of Transcendence ended, and the next generation's Transcendents were decided.

Han Li was one of the four.

His life was supposed to be calm for a long time from then. Transcendents usually had 600 years of Lifespan at best, after all.

Yang Wei was not one of those four. He was just a failed Power Born in their eyes; he hadn't been able to get the Orb, and he would die pretty soon.

But as the four people started to get ready to settle back and inherit one of the four sects each, as was usual after someone Transcended, something unusual happened.

A fifth Transcendent appeared out of nowhere and inherited one of the four sects' mantle, the Bewitching Beauty Sect—the sect that owned all brothels throughout the continent—before Han Li could.

Each sect could only have one Sect Master in a generation.

The sects seemed to hide some immense secret inside them, and the other three sect masters began to fear Han Li's existence.

In each generation, there could only exist four Transcendants, and at the time of the Transcendence Wars, the alive Transcendants would be at their weakest.

Their lifespans would be nearing the end, and each of them would only have 200 or 300 more years to live.

Once the war ended, they would soon die of age. Treasures like Lifespan Increasing Dew didn't work on them. Only Transcendent Resources worked, and there was no such resource that could increase Lifespan, which they knew.

So, once the four old Transcendents die, the next 500 or so will be peaceful times of rule for the new ones.

There would be no wars, and there would be no need to fight—even demons who killed millions won't be considered like one after they Transcend. They could change their life around and call themselves saints, and no one could say otherwise.

There would only be four of them as equals at a time in the continent. Why fight and destroy peace for no reason?

Naturally, none of them wanted that.

But the 5th Transcendent's entry changed this. He was a threat to the power they held over the 4 Sects. The power of the 4 Sects was more than what met the eye, and no Transcendent wanted to let it go.

There were secrets inside that Han Li still didn't know to this day. That was one of the reasons he wanted to build a sect for himself.

So, since Yang Wei already inherited one of the four, only Han Li was left without a Sect to inherit.

He hadn't bothered caring about it at first.

Thus, he lost the chance to win it for himself.

Thus, in the peace of the other four's peaceful future life for the next hundreds of years, a potential danger appeared—Han Li. The Great Demon of the era who wouldn't mind killing infants to achieve his goals.

What if he one day coveted one of the Sects after knowing its true secrets?

What if he assassinated one of them when they were enjoying life?

Indeed, even bold men who had won wars would become cowards when they had things to lose. They feared such a possibility and teamed up in order to kill him.

And then, Wang Ren—the new Transcendent Sect Master of the Demon Restraining Temple of that generation and a current Power Born—killed Han Li by stealing his Sword.

Han Li's sword was connected to his Existence. If someone stole it and removed the Mark of their own Existence on it, Han Li would die.

He hadn't known how his sword had been stolen before, but he now knew. Some people in this world did have knowledge about the… Existence.

Most probably, that was part of the secrets that the Four Great Sects—Demon Restraining Temple, Wisdom Seeking Villa, Sacred Cult, and Bewitching Beuty Sect—were hiding from the normal people.

And Wang Ren had probably used that knowledge, which Han Li never had the chance to get in his hand, to ambush him by stealing one of his still greatest weaknesses—Han Li's Life Sword.

If someone who knew about Existence could get their hands on it, killing Han Li wouldn't be too hard. When they get the physical sword in hand, they just needed to touch the part of his Existence that was in the Sword.

The Sword and him were practically one, after all.

That was why he never took it out of his body these days, even for training. It was the safest if he kept it inside him forever. He wasn't a fool to jump in hot water twice.

But Han Li himself was a bit proud of it all now that he thought of the whole ordeal from a different angle.

They were Transcendents who even had the knowledge of Existence, whereas he was an ignorant Transcendent—who couldn't get new knowledge from the Four Sects' generational wealth of information as they did after leveling up.

Yet, he was an unkillable cockroach even for them.

They could only ambush him.

Now that he thought of it, he had never been touched by any of them, as he was extremely cautious of them at that point. If he had been touched, he would have probably not lasted as long as he did and would have fainted like when Liyue touched him.

He could still remember that they had indeed tried to touch him. Only now did he notice that little detail. Who would have known they could make him faint with a touch?

Thankfully, he hadn't really fought them with fists like he fights these days or he would have been offed so much quicker.

But at that point… he didn't even have the Lust Dao as his own.

He just had his sword mastery, his powerful physical body, and normal Transcendent powers. And his Sacred Talent Cultivation.

Now, he was even more of a monster. He couldn't even fathom how strong he would be when he Transcend.

He had a lot of things he didn't have in his past life already: complete ownership over Lust Dao, Life Vision, knowledge of Existence, level one Heart Qi that only Transcendent had at Evolution Stage, and way more.

Just Heart Qi alone would raise a level with each Cultivation Breakthrough.

Han Li didn't even know his actual battle strength at the moment, not to mention the future.

He had to fight to find out, but he didn't want to reveal it to Monk Hen Shin; that had been why he was so restrained. If he fought, it might cause a ruckus.

Sighing, Han Li squashed that bit of pride he had in himself away from his mind.

He had died in the end.

The way it happened didn't matter, how he suffered didn't matter, and the ends justified the cause.

He died. He lost. They won.

Pride in losing?

How disgusting. It was only less disgusting than pride in winning.

It was all the same.

If he won or lost, nothing would matter if he died in the end.

Only Eternal Life, control over Life and Death, could make him stand beyond it all and have the final laugh. Only that win was worth having.

He looked at the Spirit with glittering red eyes.

"Oh beauty, tell me, where is the Transcend Mortality Orb in your hand?"

For a Power Born to become Transcendent, they only needed one thing. Transcend Mortality Orb. The sweet, sweet, one thing.

Of course, if Yang Wei was anything special, the reason for the specialness was obvious.

The Spirit.

It must still have that Orb that Yang Wei used in Han Li's past life. Han Li had been so regretful when he thought the Spirit died without him being able to get that. Now, there was no need to worry.

It was better left with him.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 224 - 224: Just had eyes for.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Spirit must still have that Transcend Mortality Orb that Yang Wei used in Han Li's past life. Han Li had been so regretful when he thought the Spirit died without him being able to get that. Now, there was no need to worry.

It was better left with him. Transcendenting without fighting a war sounded all too sweet for him not to use the chance, and he already had plans for the Orb.

Han Li teasingly asked, "Oh beauty, tell me, where is the Transcend Mortality Orb which was in your hand?"

Squirming in front of his gaze for a while, the transparent form of the Spirit awkwardly stood silent.

From the side, Liyue said with a face full of curiosity, "Little Spirit, Big Brother just asked you a question. Answer it!"

Han Li's face grew immensely weird at the way Liyue called the Spirit. Little Spirit?

Little Spirit? Really?

A 21-year-old girl calling a millennium-old monster with that name? He wanted to laugh, but the laugh was stifled in his throat when he thought about how… he and the Spirit were equals in one sense.

Liyue really was a lucky girl. And the Spirit was sure unlucky.

"Little Sp—" Han Li stopped himself short and couldn't help but cough into his hand. "So, Munyu, where is the Transcendence Orb?"

The Spirit ignored the innuendo and looked at Han Li calmly. For now, she completely ignored Liyue.

Then, the Spirit said, out of context: "I have stood at the level of Transcendents at my peak. I was undefeated. And I am 1200 years old. My real name is Munyu."

Liyue was surprised, and she immediately jeered, "That's really something for you to be that big of a deal, and yet, you wanted to shamelessly possess a little girl like me."

The Spirit shrugged in her... Spirit ways.

"Shame is useless," she said calmly.

Liyue didn't get the meaning behind Munyu introducing herself, but Han Li did. It was a question of his own identity. While they were fighting, the Spirit had guessed that he was most probably one of the 7 Sacred Inheritors, and she had many hypotheses of what Han Li was.

It was true. Often, one's enemies knew more of oneself than one's friends or family.

Battles could really make you know the sharp edge of others—especially if it was the deadly sort of battle, just like he himself had figured out that the Spirit could be from another world.

She was his greatest enemy!

He had been her greatest enemy, too.

Han Li knew the Spirit was leading him somewhere by asking for his identity, but he let her do it. Just where else could he lead him?

She was not going to escape. With his eyes that could see through souls, it was clear to him that the Spirit had no other way but to obey Liyue completely—she was more trapped than a trapped animal.

Han Li didn't feel a bit of pity for it. It had been one of his life-and-death enemies in the past. What else was there to say?

It had tried to possess, kill, and replace Liyue!

Death!

He would kill her for no reason at all, but this was much more of a motivation to kill her. But the benefits were right there to see. She was not worth killing.

But he didn't answer her unquestioned question until he comfortably shifted his position and laid on Liyue's lap with his legs crossed. She began to gently brush his hair with her pearly fingers.

Han Li introduced himself.

"Han Li. A humble cultivator, a normal man," he said, "At my peak, I was still Han Li."

Liyue blinked, pausing to brush his hair with her hand. Then laughed charmingly, "Good answer. But now tell the truth, you can tell it in my ears if you want and not tell the Little Spirit—I am really curious about who you really are. Big Brother, what are you really?"

Han Li pinched her cheeks. "I am Han Li. What else do you want to know?"

She pouted, her fair cheeks filled with discontent. It was still very much cute.

Then Liyue pinched his cheeks, making him and the Spirit both speechless. "That's for you not answering me when you know every little detail there is about me, you jerk!"

Han Li speechlessly looked at her.

"Don't pinch me in front of my enemy. It is disgraceful," he said in exasperation.

Liyue coughed gently and stopped.

Han Li looked back at the Spirit, a teasing look on his face.

Munyu took a deep breath. She looked like one of those people who would scam you into a sales pitch.

"I am the oldest person alive in the Sacred Lust Continent," the Spirit said.

Han Li gestured for her to continue.

"I have both the potential and knowledge to rule this world. I am one of the best in this world." She snorted, her derision clear through her Existence's aura: "The other Transcendents are all mere wastrels; there are powers that they have the potential to use but have no way to. I know how to make a person from this world reach their highest potential."

Han Li smiled.

"Like the Dao Tree, Vital Weapons, and Beast Path—all three combining in one body?"

The Spirit grew silent.

"It doesn't matter if you already know of it all. I once became a Transcendent in the Vital Weapon path and a Power Born in the other two, and believe me—I am an expert, and there has been no one like me in existence."

That intrigued Han Li, and he sat up. Eyes as bright as red infernos.

"I know what you are thinking, Han Li." Munyu crackled, and the sound noiselessly echoed in the room, only audible to people who had sensed their Existence.

Han Li's eyes darkened.

Munyu jeered; her sight was so eerie that Liyue had goosebumps. "You must be thinking that since you enslaved my freedom, you could do whatever you want and inspect my memories. Hahahaha! You must think that you could read my mind to gain all this precious information I gathered throughout my insufferable life."

Han Li didn't speak. That was exactly his plan.

The Spirit spat without hesitation, "Can you really read my memories? Do you think I will allow that to happen and just look at it all like a fool? Heh, you are a fellow Demon. You must know our mentality without me even saying."

Liyue was very silent. She felt tiny for some reason but then perked up and asked, "How dare—"

Han Li raised his hand. "So if I tried to see your memories, you would die by breaking the Existential Bond on yourself. I can understand the thought process. But since you accepted to give up your freedom to Liyue, you must have a reason. What do you want in order for you to cooperate?"

"Oh, Han Li," Munyu's tone changed again. "I am one of the most terrifying things in this world. There was a time when Demi-Sacreds roamed the world, and even they used to fear me—for my potential as it may be."

That made Han Li's pupils constrict.

"I had Vital Weapon Transcendence already; once I transcended using the other two paths, too, I would have been deadly. But sadly, that could never have happened—I am not you."

Han Li sighed. "Seven Sacred Demon Inheritors."

"Indeed," Munyu nodded. "If I became a Transcendent or not, it doesn't matter in the Dao Tree Path. Just like you devoured the Lust Dao some days ago, if an inheritor devoured my Dao, all my progress would be for naught."

Nodding, he said, "Only an Inheritor can have the last laugh at holding all three Paths to Power in one body. Or their Daos would just get stolen and they wouldn't be able to use that power."

"And you are an extraordinary man, Han Li. You are not only a Sacred Demon Inheritor, you have already devoured your first Dao. If I am correct."

Han Li nodded.

"You are a monster in growth," the Spirit said with genuine admiration. "When you reach your full potential, you will be a nightmare even for higher-stage cultivators. You already are, probably. But you will surely struggle in that way."

His eyes began to glint.

"If I and you are in one team..." Munyu said, "Who would dare to stand in our path?"

Liyue licked her slightly dry lips. She couldn't help but shake her head in amazement.

"Han Li!" Munyu continued with even more fervor. "Both of us have similar ambitions. I want to see the final and most perfect evolutionary form of life, and you want to control life and death.

"If we are one team, we will be like an unstoppable force joining together with an unbreakable wall."

Then, the Spirit calmed down and gave her final piece, "And I am a virgin who, a man in the sexual sense, had never touched. My soul is untouched, and nobody had ever touched me in that way when I was in a body either—I never cared for such a thing.

"I'll devote myself to you. I'll be your greatest supporter!!

"We are just meant to be, Han Li. I'll help you reach your potential, you will help me see my wish. We, together, can be invincible!"

Han Li looked at her with mixed eyes.

"Or you could kill me now, and you will get only the Transcend Mortality Orb." She was calm. If she died, that thing would end up in Han Li's hand, but she didn't care.

"I don't plan to remain a chicken or your wife's pet for life. Death doesn't scare me. I wanted to say this to you, and that's why I accepted Liyue's demand for me to become her pet. Would you accept me as one of your wives?"

From the side, Liyue said in a low voice: "There is nothing wrong with a chicken. Chickens can ascend to the heavens and become phoenixes, you know?" Han Li glanced at her, and she added: "My Mother Yue told me so."

Munyu just had eyes for Han Li.

For a final part, she added: "I will be your most loyal wife."

That really, really irked Liyue.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 225 - 225: From Munyu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I will be your most loyal wife."

That really, really pissed off Liyue, but she didn't say anything. She just frowned her beautiful eyebrows and silently looked at the Spirit.

Han Li didn't say anything for a while.

He wasn't a short-sighted person; getting only the Transcend Mortality Orb was good, and making her a pet would serve his vanity. It would be a satisfying sight. But that was not nearly worth having a loyal Munyu on his side.

Munyu was a deadly addition to his harem if he actually accepted the proposition. Her knowledge alone would—if his thoughts were in line with reality—make him stronger. His chance of actually achieving his still distant goal would become much higher.

Munyu could be a breakthrough in his destiny.

He could see the allure of it all. Not choosing her was not an option to even think about.

"Munyu," he called.

The Spirit bowed slightly.

"I don't choose wives lightly. I give my heart to even fewer people. And you have once harmed—tried to harm—one of them who are the dearest to me."

"I am well aware."

Both of their gazes, Han Li's and Munyu's, met in midair, and sparks of brilliant ambition began to brighten between them.

Both of them WANTED to join forces.

Eternal Life and Control over Life and Death.

Han Li wanted to achieve that!

He didn't want to see his mother die; he didn't want to be thrown into a place full of regret where there was nothing he could do. He wanted to live without the worry of death hanging above his loved ones.

The greatest biological evolution of a lifeform. The greatest and most supreme form of life.

To see that was why Munyu sacrificed everything. Love, wealth, beauty, peace, and nothing mattered to her.

She had once screamed, looking at the heavens. After all, everything she had ever wanted was lost to her and her physical body was destroyed.

She had cried.

She had possessed other creatures and became strong, died, and done the exact same thing again and again.

Reach a high level before becoming nothing again.

She had broken her bones, been thrown in the fire, and been frozen in icicles. She had been eaten by the wolves before. But she had never once stopped persevering for her goal.

That was her purpose, her life.

For that, she would kill infants, drink their pregnant mother's blood, and still laugh proudly. Emotionless like an ice block, she would walk up any mountain and reach its top with corpses as her stepping stone.

Han Li laughed, and the Spirit laughed alongside.

Both of their laughter was rather restrained and just a slow chuckle at the start, but soon, it turned into laughter full of meaning, and by the end, the Spirit's transparent form was sitting on Han Li's lap.

It was merely the size of a hand or two, but she still sat regally in his lap with all calm.

Even Liyue just looked at her irritantly and didn't say anything. It was her Big Brother Han's choice.

"Munyu," he said, his voice calm, "Begin with a good note and apologize to Liyue."

Munyu nodded readily and looked at Liyue with her transparent form. Her voice sincere, and she said, "I won't apologize to you with my words. They hold no meaning when I have zero regrets for my actions. I will give you a gift on behalf of me and my husband, a gift that should be more than enough to forgive my transgressions."

"He didn't marry you! And he is my husband!" Liyue complained.

"He did marry me. You just didn't notice." The Spirit's fingertips started to brighten in a faintly golden color, and she pressed the finger on Han Li's forehead; he didn't resist. With her Existential Bond in place, she couldn't harm anyone at least.

A memory entered his mind. It was a technique. A Heart Qi Technique.

"It is a technique that I just made, but it is also part of a wider technique. I thought your wife must love it."

Liyue looked sideways at them, feeling irritated at becoming a third wheel in her own room.

Fuck the Spirit! She hated it already.

Han Li assimilated the technique in his mind with a slow breath, and his horizons were immediately expanded. It was a way of using Heart Qi that he hadn't known was possible. He might have learned to use it someday, but it would never have been this easy.

Taking Liyue's face with one of his hands, he brought her closer, and both of their lips met. It was followed by a confused minute of deep kissing.

At the same time, unbeknownst to Liyue, a tiny golden mark began to imprint itself on the roof of her mouth, and she was filled with a tingly feeling throughout the kiss.

Then, he parted from her beautiful and full lips. She asked, slightly out of breath, "…What was that feeling?"

Han Li winked at her. "Guess what?"

"I don't know, just say!"

Suddenly, Liyue felt a weird sensation all over her body, and she began to squirm. Then, she fell into his embrace—from where the Spirit jumped into the air—before she asked with a bit of a pitiful face: "What is this weird feeling?"

The Spirit said amusedly from up above: "It is a profound technique that would only work if the woman he uses it on loves him deeply. He had only given you the First Mark of the Three Marks, so he now has the ability to sense you from even the other side of the continent."

"If you move, old man, I will slide my sword up, and your head will fly. So don't move, as for the other seven, don't move either—or I'll kill this poor elderly."

Han Li threw up the necklace, and Liyue caught it.

'Spirit, are you ready?'

'Always have been.'

With a thought, all of the people in the room, including Liyue, just vanished into thin air. Only Hua Yu was a distinction, and with the pearl necklace in her hand, she slowly floated down to stand beside Han Li.

Her face was full of sweat.

"What are you?" The hunchback's eyes were full of bloodlines. He was wheezing.

Han Li winked. "Nothing but a little thief."

Understanding the situation, Hen Shin calmly said, "Have a clear picture of where you want to go in your mind, and I will send you there using teleportation—have a clear picture of the place you want to be in. And don't you dare mistreat my disciple!"

Smiling, Han Li nodded. "Thank you for the past three years."

Hua Yu held Han Li's hand.

The Sacred Cult members were all agitated and worried—that necklace was even more valuable than the hunchback's life. It was a true treasure of the Sacreds.

What was that necklace?

It was the embodiment of a Demon World. Just like the Lust Demon World that Han Li had visited three years ago, this treasure also contained a Demon World!

A DEMON WORLD!

The Detached Empty Space of a Sacred Demon.

Of which Sacred Demon, Han Li was unaware, but it was a treasure nonetheless.

Inside it, an actual world with all fauna and flora was there, and only the Sacred Cult's master could control entry in and outside it. Usually, it was used to make travels easier and almost all of the cult's younger generation was inside it.

Hundreds of Evolution Stage cultivators!

Even the Evolution Stage cultivator Han Li had murdered was part of the ones that had been released from the necklace earlier—the Demon World inside was a camp. It was their moving headquarters, sort of, and there was not even a single Power Born protecting it.

With a buzz, Hua Yu also vanished, and Han Li took hold of the necklace. He wouldn't have been able to steal it even if he wanted to, as it was covered in an Existential aura that normal people couldn't even sense.

But since the Spirit and Han Li knew that trick, it was easy.

The other six Power Borns wanted to retaliate against Han Li. But they couldn't.

They were not only worried about the Hunchback or the Necklace. Fear. Cosmic fear was coursing through their veins as Han Li looked at them indifferently.

Heart Qi—Fear.

Spirit and him working together made all these Power Borns just freeze in horror. It was temporary, but that was enough.

"I have been wishing for this treasure. Thank you for bringing it to me. I will treat the juniors really well on your behalf, seniors."

Hen Shin brought out red mist out of nowhere and the mist flowed towards Han Li before beginning to wrap around him, he wore the necklace over his head. Then, as the stunned men watched helplessly, he vanished.

The Spirit's cocky chuckle echoed around like a maddened howl.

Oh, what a haul.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 235 - 235: Memories
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Big Brother, Big Brother, how do the Power Born fight?" Han Li was tying Ruo's hair in braids, getting her ready for training when she asked.

He paused for a moment. Then asked with a tinge of embarrassment, "How am I supposed to know how the Power Born fight? I am just an Evolution Stage cultivator…"

Looking at the mirror, he felt even more embarrassed when Ruo sighed exaggeratedly.

"You are an ignoramus. Right, Mom?" she teased and then asked Yue Li, who was busy looking at Han Li and herself. Already, Yue Li had started building some wrinkles around the edges of her face while, her hair had started to whiten too.

"Oh, Xiao Li is not an ignoramus, dear." Yue Li chuckled. "He is just young. And aren't you asking a question way above his fighting range? That's your fault, not my son's."

Ruo spat. "There you go again, standing on your son's side like always and singling me out. Tch, tch—What was it called again? Simp?"

Yue Li blushed.

From the side, Han Li looked into the mirror and couldn't help but clench his hands. Eyes as dark as the abyss looked back at him. Immaturity coated his face while confidence alluded his grasp.

Am I enough?

Can I protect those dear to me the way I currently am?

How can I? I am so weak. Ruo, are you telling me to be more mature and learn things a step ahead of me? Or is it just an innocent question?
