"I will go check how the Power Born fight. I'll be back quick," saying that, his face blushing slightly, Han Li left the room and closed the door behind him.

Whatever it was, looking into it and answering her question sounded like the right thing to do.

For a second, the room was silent, and then the mother-daughter duo burst into laughter.

***

Han Li floated above a silent ocean, waveless and soundless—alone. His eyes were closed, his mind unconscious. A pearl necklace hung around his neck while his robes had vanished at some point, leaving him naked.

Suddenly, the necklace around his neck lit up, and a person materialized in thin air around him. She first looked around. Ocean blue was the expanse she saw, and so was the sky the same blue.

White clouds, barely one single mass of it, floated above them slowly. While below them, as she looked, the seawater reflected her face. Crystalline green eyes, green hair, a green robe that she had grown used to wearing for the last couple of years which accentuated her beauty.

She then breathed gently and looked back at Han Li. She had seen him the first thing after she appeared in this place from the necklace. He was floating above the ocean, and even though he was unconscious, the ocean here was not normal.

Landing slowly from her levitating form, the woman frowned as her legs touched the water. Something pushed her away from the world underwater. Frowning, she muttered, "Strange."

Her hand went for Han Li, ready to wake him up. He must know in what kind of place they were. Yes, the place looked truly beautiful, but such beauty often hid the danger. He must know.

She just had to wake him up.

"I wouldn't do that if I were you, Hua Yu."

Hua Yu froze.

Where did that come from?

Her slender fingers quivered slightly.

"Don't search for me. You can't see me unless I wish to be seen, yes, not even if I wish—I am currently residing in his necklace."

"N—necklace?"

"Munyu, people call me. Spirit is my other name. But whatever it is, one thing is that you shouldn't wake him up."

Hua Yu used multiple Magical Powers on herself to boost her senses. Having the Moss Dao Soul didn't help her—it was restricting her in many ways, and senses were not its best forte.

She could find no one around even after she spread unseeable, dust-like moss around for a long distance.

"Why shouldn't I wake him up?" Hua Yu warily asked.

"Look, woman. I don't have time to spend talking about bullshit and proving my identity. Go in and bring out that lass Liyue from the necklace world, and she will tell you I am no enemy."

Hua Yu was conflicted for a second before she said with a neutral face, "I can't take her out. To move out even by myself, I had to fly up into the sky of the 'Necklace World' and reach the limit of the skyline, a barrier, sort of, and that was draining—I am sure I can't handle two people at once."

Munyu mumbled. "I see. The Necklace is bonded to Han Li's will; you walked out without his permission, so it is the same as going against his will. We will have to wait until he wakes up for others to be pulled out normally.

"Well, all of them are useless except that mother of his anyway. But I need them all out, though—only then can this benefit us the most."

Feeling less wary but still on alert, Hua Yu asked, "Why is he unconscious?"

Munyu casually said, "He is not unconscious."

"…?"

"He is conscious. Probably more conscious than he had ever been in his entire life. He is currently watching the world with closed eyes, I suppose."

"What jibberish ar—"

"Vital Enlightenment. He once had it when he fought me years ago, but I destroyed his once-in-a-lifetime opportunity by interrupting it. Had he had the chance, he would have gotten his own Vital Weapon by now."

"So he is experiencing something fortuitous?"

"He is. He is searching for his own meaning in existence, his own ambition, reason for living, and much more. Only when he is out would you know what his Vital Weapon turned out to be in the end."

"What is a Vital Weapon?" Hua Yu asked in frustration.

The Spirit chuckled inwardly.

It was such a unique fact that, after one reached a certain level in their cultivation, all of the world's people would talk even with others they considered their enemies, thus so peacefully. Hua Yu was seeing her as an enemy, still.

No doubt, one of Han Li's people for sure. Cautious little fucks.

She liked it.

"Vital Weapons are a different way of cultivation itself—it is an unopened door for you. A door that Han Li might open today for himself. He must be swimming through his memory now, the reason for his existence."

***

Han Li took a book from the library of the Wisdom Seeking Villa. He was a disciple of the sect, so he was allowed to borrow books and gain knowledge.

With the Gu that the sect planted on him while he joined, it was impossible for him to betray them and spread the knowledge to outsiders. Whenever he was out in the open courtyards of the Sect, he felt the gnawing sense of slavery.

No matter how powerful he became from this point in his life, he will be the Sect's slave. The Gu grew stronger with himself after all. If he were a Transcendent, the Gu would also be a Transcendent-binding Gu.

If he were a Sacred, he would be bound by that level of Gu. It was similar to his Life Sword, which grew stronger along with himself.

'I have to find a way to get rid of this thing—damn it is making me pissed.'

He had to save his mother, and for that, he needed a lifespan-increasing resource. Which was a Life Dao Resource—too rare for a weakling to receive. And for that, he had joined the Wisdom Seeking Villa, one of the Four Sects.

He was of the lowest echelon of disciples, and getting the resource was a distant goal.

'How do Power Borns fight?' the Book read. Not that interesting or useful but Han Li could not be seen as incompetent. Not by that cheeky girl. He swore he would learn how it worked.

He would make Ruo bend her knees for making him learn too.

Awakening Stage Cultivators don't have many methods of fighting when it comes to it. The only thing they had to rely on that the average mortal didn't have was... Soul Sense.

Using the Soul Sense, they could use weapons and other tools better than a mortal, making them impossible to fight against for their inferiors. But that was it. Against Evolution Stage cultivators, Awakening Stage cultivators were too weak.

Evolution Stage Cultivators had unique Magical Powers, (one that the Awakening Stage Cultivators would have no way of resisting,) if they were the Ethereal Type—the kind of cultivator that had an elemental or magical Spark.

If the Cultivator had a Transformation Type Spark, which made them incapable of using Magical Powers, they had unique Transformations—unique and beastly forms. Deadly at close range.

"True, true." Han Li nodded.

Thinking back on his own life, he had also fought a Transformation Type years ago.

Raven Clan cultivator, Kevin Raven. He used to have a Raven Spark and a Raven Dao Soul. He could transform his body into a powerful Raven Form and throw feathers as sharp as blades around. He was a Transformation type.

Then, there was Ruo. Who had a White Sparrow Spark—she could even take flight when she transformed, which the Raven Clan cultivators couldn't due to the metallic nature of their wings.

Han Li himself was an Ethereal Type. His Spark was Lust, and he had already Evolved, which made his Spark into a Minor Dao Soul of Lust. He excelled in Magical Powers. But with the Demonic Transformations, he was not weak in close combat either.

He continued reading again.

A Power Born is even stronger than an Evolved.

Before them, Evolved men are nothing. A Power Born, for one, can have a much stronger physical body. But that is not what makes them fearful against their inferiors.

A Power Born can have a Soul Sense that is much, much deadlier than an Evolved. Their Soul Sense can physically crush an Evolved with a thought. It is impossible to resist!

Even the strongest Evolution Stage physique will be like crumpling paper.

That Soul Sense is called Psychokinesis.

This alone put the Power Born at a different level.

But there is more to a Power Born fight than just that.

Majorly speaking, the Transformation Type still relies on their body, and the Ethereal Type on their Magic Powers.

But the differences are massive.

Transformation Type cultivators now would have a body that is extremely potent against Ethereal, to the point that a win was most of the time certain as long as the Ethereal enemy let a Transformation enemy closer to them.

But so does Ethereals have their deadly advantage, too.

Han Li's heart tightened as he read on. There it was—no wonder the Power Born were just so disastrously mighty.

Evolution Stage cultivators can use Magic Powers, too. But the way they use it is different from a Power Born.

If they have two Magic Powers, for example, Fire Ball and Fire Explosion, those are two different powers.

If the Evolution Stage tried to combine those powers, they would fail miserably.

"Yep, that's impossible… I've tried it many times. It just doesn't work that way." Even if two powers were used simultaneously, they would just act as separate attacks.

Two different powers fired at the same time.

But a Power Born could use two powers and fuse them into a single type of attack. For example, they can fuse a Fire Ball and Fire Explosion to get a Fireball Explosion. The weirder the abilities and the better they fuse it, the better powers they can get.

This literally sent a chill down Han Li's back.

He had the Seduction Magic Power and also Telepathy. Can he fuse it to literally whisper thoughts into others that could seduce them to do what they wouldn't? Wasn't that nearly mind control?

"Damn, that could be used in disgusting ways if it ended up in the hands of a Demon," Han Li could also see why Evolution Stage cultivators would so utterly fear the Power Born.

They were not using simple Magic Powers any longer but complex Magic Powers. The Power Born also had quick casting speeds, so that was one.

Han Li closed the book and sighed.

"So that's how Power Born fight…" he shook his head. "Using complex Magic Powers that are a mix of many. I wonder if someone can use so many Magic Powers at once…"

What if someone could use three magic powers at once?

What if someone could do that with… 10?

Damn.

What if some ridiculous monster could use all 300 of the Magic Powers at once?

No way.

The highest recorded had apparently been 20 or so, and that Complex Magic Power decimated an entire city with one ability—Killing tens of Power Borns single-handedly. What would happen if someone had the power to mix 300 was a wonder.

But for now, he had what it took to make Ruo bend her knees in defeat. Han Li chuckled as he ran away—back to his place.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 236 - 236: Entering a Fantasy with Han Li
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Is… it done?" Hua Yu asked.

The Spirit growled. "Don't be impatient."

"Han Li is still not waking up."

"Of course, he won't wake up this quick. At least 3 hours and at most seven days, he won't wake up at any other time."

A passing wind ruffled her hair, and Hua Yu held it with her hand. They were still above that mystical ocean, and Han Li was still unconscious. According to what she learned from the Spirit, Han Li was apparently giving birth to his own Vital Weapon.

It had been a great revelation to Hua Yu. The fact that Vital Weapons existed was somewhat of a headroller for her. The fact that it was the primary cultivation system of another world made her even more baffled.

Apparently, in the vast cosmos, there were two Everlasting Worlds. Worlds that would never die. Yes, even Worlds apparently died when they grew too old. Even the Seven Continents would vanish at some point in billions of years.

But the two Everlasting Worlds were immortal—Everlasting Human World and Everlasting Beast World. Those worlds didn't cultivate Sparks or Dao Souls; that was the way the people of Seven Continents cultivated.

For one, Everlasting Beast World cultivated Beast Cores and evolutions. As for the Everlasting Human World, they cultivated Vital Weapons. It was a path much more sturdy than the Dao Tree Path of their Seven Continents.

Seven Continent's people were too reliant on the Heavens. Even their Magical Powers were not their own. Only the 7 Demon Inheritors could even be considered actual cultivators.

All of that was head-spinning news for Hua Yu, and the Spirit gave them for free. Of course, Munyu was not lazy while she did it; she was overwhelming Han Li's will on the Necklace and taking control of it.

She needed to bring the people inside of it out. She needed to do that.

"So, what is Han Li currently seeing?"

"Hm?" The Spirit chuckled. "His past. He is reliving his life's most major moment—where his mind decided his purpose in living."

"He is unconscious, yet you can't force away this will of his from the necklace?" Hua Yu was still wary of the Spirit but couldn't even see it. The best she could do was to take the necklace away from Han Li's physical body.

He wouldn't be harmed, at least.

"His will is nearly as strong as mine when I was at my peak, and I am at my bottom now." The Spirit sighed, and then finally, the necklace brightened, and three women popped out into the real world.

Yue Li, Liyue, and Ruo.

"Just enough!" Munyu laughed, and the other three women looked around nervously.

What was this calm ocean?

Everything was so blue.

Liyue was the first to calm down, and she asked with a bit of worry, "Why are we standing above the ocean and not sinking?"

"It's some kind of mystery ocean," Hua Yu said.

Ruo stood up and silently looked at the ocean. There was a frightened look on her face, which soon turned into horror. "We're above the fucking ocean…"

Yue Li came beside her and held her. "You at least fear something. Humph, don't worry, you won't sink and die when Hua Yu is around."

Ruo held Yue Li's hand tightly and looked away from the ocean. Her eyes focused on Han Li, who was gracefully asleep. "Why the fuck is he sleeping now?"

Liyue sneaked behind her and pushed Ruo away from Yue Li. The frightened young lady immediately screamed. "This is no joke, big sis; I'll die!"

Ruo turned on her heels and immediately caught Hua Yu's hand. She really was frightened. Looking down at the ocean… it was so dark deep down, and it just gave her the chills.

She clung to Hua Yu's hand as her life depended on it.

"Sister Hua Yu, push her away," feeling snarky, Liyue chided.

Hua Yu smiled and pushed Ruo away. Even Yue Li was looking at her with amusement but Ruo just felt breathless. "This—this is no joke. I don't like this. Please, no."

All of them simultaneously frowned. Ruo had shrunken into a ball and had closed her eyes and ears. "Big Brother. Bi—Big Brother, save me!"

"Tsk." Liyue clicked her tongue and moved beside Ruo. She pinched the girl's cheek. "I didn't think you were that frightened."

The Spirit's voice came from the necklace in Hua Yu's hand, "Liyue, sit around Han Li and hold onto him. All of you."

"You are here too?" Liyue muttered. "Why the hell do I have to listen to you?"

"It's for Han Li's sake."

"Come Ruo, let's sit with Big Bro."

Eyes teary, the Young Lady Ruo nodded and just clung onto Liyue. When they finally reached Han Li, they sat around him, and each of them held onto a part of him.

Yue Li was the one frowning now. "Liyue, who is speaking? I don't see anyone, but I can hear the noise."

"Don't worry, she is trustworthy—I'll introduce her later."

Yue Li took Han Li's head and placed it on her lap, Ruo took his left hand, and Liyue took his right hand. Hua Yu was beside Ruo, holding onto his left wrist.

"We will all be moving into Han Li's memory scape now and will experience his major life event with him. You will have complete autonomy in that time, but I will warn you, there is no assurance that everything you see there will be the same as reality."

"Why are we doing this?"

"This is a technique I can use. It would help you awaken your own Vital Weapons along with him—but beware, all of our memory scapes, Han Li's, mine, Liyue's, Yue Li's, Ruo's, and Hua Yu's. We will all lose ourselves in a fantasy world. It will change from his old memories entirely because of us."

"I don't get it," Ruo said weakly. "What is going on?"

"Just know that from now on, we will all enter multiple fantasies, and our only goal in each of them is to find and interact with Han Li. If possible, try to become conscious and break out of the dream. When we wake up, we will all have our Vital Weapons."

"Can't we bring the rest of the sisters?"

"I tried, but his will on the necklace is too hard; I'll be exhausted before I bring all of them out," The Spirit was calm.

Liyue touched the necklace and felt the Existence inside. There was also a Heart Qi will that utterly blocked her way in. Her mind was calm; she felt for the will and smiled inwardly. Indeed, she was not the Spirit.

Han Li's will would, of course, not reject her.

She was special to him.

"Sadly, I don't have the ability to make them levitate. I guess they have to work hard themselves to awaken this Vital Weapon later—for now, it's only us."

"It's not that hard to Awaken if you know how to do it. I'll help the others awaken the Vital Weapon when it's their turn." The Spirit said, "But for us, it is now. And let me tell you one last time: we will be in all kinds of situations, but if you die in there, you will fail to awaken the weapon now and will lose the ability to do that forever. So never die."

Ruo scratched her cheeks, and her hand tightened around Han Li's. "We wouldn't sink into the sea, right?"

"No. This place is safe."

"I don't know what is going on now… I just want to go home…"

"Shut up, Ruo, don't be such a coward."

"It's all Han Li's fault," Ruo gave Han Li's hand a squeeze in anger, but it was too powerful. "Now I can't go back to the Temple for so long…"

Suddenly, she started to feel sleepy, and her eyes blurred.

'What is going on?'

Her head spun, and she fell onto Han Li's chest. The last thing she heard was his vigorous heartbeat and then, it was silence and darkness.

'Hm, so I should try to awaken this Vital Weapon, huh? If I do, everyone will be proud of m…'

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 237 - 237: Stillness of the Soul
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Yue Li's hand rested against Han Li's.

"Don't cry now, will you?"

"…"

"Everybody dies, Xiao Li," she said through deep breaths. "Everybody does."

"Mom…"

"Ah, there you go again, don't cry…"

He tightened his grip over her hand, and his tears fell.

"Goodbyes are only for those who love with their eyes," she held his hand as tight as she could. "Because for those who love with heart and soul, there is no such thing as separation."

Gritting his teeth gently, Han Li asked, "Who are you to teach me?"

She smiled. "I always told you to give me a baby. You would have had a piece of me with you if you did." Ruo was leaning against her from the side, and she, too, was sobbing. "At least, you are here… I am content."

"…"

"Xiao Li, sing me a poem. Tell me, would you miss me?"

"…"

To ask me if I miss her,

It would be like asking the stars if they missed the night,

Asking the sun if it missed its warmth,

Or asking the ocean if it missed the rivers that give it life.

"You know what I like to hear, huh…"

I would recognize you in total darkness.

Were you mute and I deaf,

I would recognize you in another lifetime entirely in different bodies, different times.

And I would love you in all of this,

Until the very last star burnt out to oblivion.

"Do you love her to death?"

Speak of her over my grave,

And watch how she brings me back to life.

"..."

Han Li breathed heavily.

Warm tears streaked down his cheek. He stood rooted to his spot.

His whole body was covered in blood. Every part of him was silent. War had torn him to pieces. All for what?

Blank, he sat.

Sleeping before him was the corpse of his beloved mother; she had aged far too much. Something within him shattered.

Han Li silently stood up and looked at his palm. On it was a crushed insect.

"…Ruo, I found a way to remove the Gu inside me."

Ruo felt her whole body shrink.

Her mother had died; that gentle soul who was always with her and always cared for her had died. There will no longer be any arguments she could have with her, no more sweet dishes only her mother could make. Everything seemed over.

Without touching the corpse, Han Li calmly left the room, and Ruo couldn't bring herself to move her weak body. She just laid against Yue Li.

On his way, Han Li stopped and slumped against the wall.

"…"

An old man squatted before him.

"Young man," he said somberly, "you performed well in the war. Tell me, what do you want from me? Or care tell me your sorrows?"

Han Li smiled at him. "If I tell of the sorrows of my heart, it will burn my tongue. If I keep it in my heart, I'm afraid it will burn me from the inside," tears broke and he began to tremble, "But if I let it out, I am afraid it will burn the whole world."

"…"

"Have you ever known a love that transcends reason, distance, and time?" Han Li asked, "A love so profound you could never be convinced it wasn't destined before you existed."

"I have not."

Han Li closed his eyes and his tears fell, but he didn't speak anymore.

"I have died. Even before my life ended, I have died."

Face red with sorrow, Ruo walked out of her room and leaned against the wall as she stumbled towards him. Her whole body seemed soulless as she laid down and leaned onto his chest.

"Ruo… she left us."

A Man was in shambles.

"I am here, Big Brother. I am here…" Ruo said amidst her own chokes.

How dare the heavens take from me what is mine. How dare it!

How dare the world control my destiny!

How dare it steal my mom's life!

How wonderful it would have been if all of this were just a dream. One year. I just want one year more with her. Take me back in time. Please. Something help me, please.

Please.

Please…

Suddenly, Han Li frowned, and his eyes grew strangely red. It had always been black, but somehow, it flickered, and a profound sensation of awakeness spread throughout him. He looked to his side and found the sobbing Ruo.

Immediately, his heart began to beat a little faster.

This situation. This moment.

Right after my mother's death?!

His gaze moved toward the old man in front of him, and he frowned at the unclear face. Who was this person, I do not remember. But then he looked sideways, and his heart ached at the sight of Ruo's sobs.

An illusion?

Who is casting this on me?

Ruo muttered something incoherent and clung powerfully to him. An encroaching sense of horror began to appear in Han Li's heart. Suddenly, the old man's face became immensely clear.

The War General of the Wisdom Seeking Villa. A person Han Li himself murdered in wrath after Ruo died. A good man who had done no wrong to Han Li personally.

A fearful thought echoed at the back of Han Li's mind.

Was the past few years a dream?

Was going back to the past not real?

Then, a calm heart chuckled at the thoughts.

He unconsciously chuckled in reality.

Ruo looked at him worriedly. The sight of this Ruo, the one who had walked ups and downs with him, made Han Li freeze.

He took a deep breath.

This was not real. He knew this was not real.

But did there exist a real and fake?

As long as a human experienced everything one could experience in real life inside an illusion, was it not just as real?

He turned and looked straight at Ruo.

He hugged her.

"When I had you, I never saw how much you were important to me. When I lost our mom, I was too blinded by the grief, and only when you hung yourself did I understand how much you meant."

"…Big Brother."

"Ruo. Don't worry about mom."

"Big Brot—"

"Not Mom, not you. None of us are going to die." She looked at him with fearful eyes. He comfortably kissed the woman's forehead.

"I know you are but an illusion and that you would once again vanish when I wake up. But let me live a life with you without any regrets."

Taking her hand, he returned to the room with his mother. Face still growing slightly slack with sorrow, Han Li carried her with him and left the Wisdom Seeking Villa, left the bustling city surrounding it, and withdrew from everything.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 238 - 238: Bamboo
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li and Ruo moved into a bamboo forest. Using strength that he had cultivated through wars and bloodshed, he built a simple bamboo hut and, along with the sorrowful Ruo, who had just lost her mother-in-law, settled in.

Unlike Ruo, Han Li knew this was just an illusion and not real life. He knew his mother hadn't died. He knew nothing was real. Even this version of Ruo, who seemed life-like to the touch, was not real.

Yes, Ruo's touches, her caresses, her sobbing heart. Everything was what it would have been like if he had moved out of that sect after his mother died. She was alive, living a great life even through her sorrows.

But in his past life, Han Li hadn't moved out of the sect. He hadn't paid much heed to what he had. When he lost his mother, he had believed he had lost everything. He was the most narrow-minded, selfish prick.

When, in fact, he still had Ruo. He had still had this gentle soul of a woman who only had him in return.

When she was young, she was seen as a tool by her own family. When she grew up, she was sold as a slave. In all of this, Yue Li had been her first familial figure and Han Li had been her first and last love. She had no one now except for him.

He had become a maddened oxen after his mother died, and he had lost himself in blood and hatred towards the heavens. Even as he carved a simple flute made of bamboo, and even as Ruo slowly sobbed on his lap, Han Li reflected on it.

In the end, she was killed. To be exact, someone acted as HIM using some Magic Power and told her 'he' hated her. Of course, she had killed herself after seemingly losing everything she had.

Han Li still remembered the words she had left behind for him as clear as day.

Betrayed by the person I love, there is no longer a reason to live.

She had hung herself.

Han Li's hand paused and a single drop of tear fell on Ruo's cheek. The already sobbing woman bit her lips.

"Ruo," he called. Five hundred years of sorrow were refilling his heart with regrets. He liked that feeling. When he returned to real life once more, he would make sure he would live a life of no regrets.

He was now a True Demon. He would live with a heart free of regrets.

Ruo's saddened gaze moved towards him, and Han Li pinched her red cheeks. "I am a very narrow-minded person. I am quite dumb, to be fair. I am very, very emotion prone." He rubbed away her tears. "There is a story I want to tell you."

"…Story?"

"Story of a dumb boy who lost his mother and got blinded."

Once upon a time, long, long ago, there lived a young boy. He was very dumb, but he had an even dumber mother. She would get scammed, she would get her money robbed, she would easily get emotional, and she was a very pure woman.

But this young boy lost her at a certain point, and he became very disillusioned with life. Yet, he didn't notice that he not only had a mother so dear to him, but he also had a young angel who relied on him to live her life.

Only when he lost her did he realize she was just as precious as his mother. The little gem that he should have protected in his life at all times. Sadly enough, the realization came too late. The young angel had also left him.

The narrow-minded, dumb little boy shut out his heart then.

"And he has always wanted to say sorry, both to his little angel and mother. Sorry that he couldn't protect them, sorry that he was so useless. He had already lost the chance. Both his angel and mother were dead. Heavens had taken them from him. So then, he had decided."

"*Sniff* what?" Ruo asked, clenching his hand and trying to hold back a tear.

"Why does the world decide when people die? Why can't I decide so? How dare the world!" Han Li said exaggeratedly with puffed-up cheeks, and Ruo couldn't hold back a sobbing smile. "And this narrow-minded young boy decided. That was it; I was bringing back my angel and mother from death and taking control of everything. How dare the stupid world take anything from me."

"And?"

Han Li sighed and spread his arms. "The stupid little boy grew older and older, but his heart's wounds only grew deeper and deeper. His desire for control made a shell around him, and years passed like flowing clouds. The shell was strengthened by the passing years."

"Then?"

"There were many hungry wolves all over the world who didn't like the stupid little boy. He was too unruly. They all decided to end him." Han Li snorted. "Those fucking sects all teamed up because they couldn't do anything to him alone."

Ruo smiled; she had very faint dimples when she smiled. One had to know how to look, and only then would they be able to see it. Han Li knew exactly how to look.

"He died. They killed him."

Ruo paled slightly. "So he couldn't save his little angel and mother? The little boy never became happy?"

"He did. He was a narrow-minded little boy but even he had grown a lot after going through so many years. The hardened shell had become obsidian. He had become cold. But then, suddenly, his dead mind woke up all the way back in time when his mother was alive."

"Ah!" Ruo mumbled. "So he saved his angel and mother?"

"Yes. He did. He was still that narrow-minded when it came to some things but he too could do things he had already done once easily.

"He saved his angel and mother, but sadly, his angel didn't remember him. But she is, in the end, still that bratty, snort-nosed woman with a mischievous heart. He just wanted to cherish her now."

Ruo scrunched her nose. "It's as if you are telling your story. Big Brother, am I this angel?"

"Of course."

"I feel insulted. I will not be rude—atleast, um, not hurtfully so—to you of all people, even if I lost all my memories."

"Well, what would you do if you didn't have any memories?"

"I'll play hard to get. Maybe look at you weirdly for looking at me the way you look at me now."

"I have a way in which I look at you?"

"Of course you do. Recently, its all the more clear…" Ruo rubbed her eyes and smiled. "You are always looking at others as If you are looking at a tree or some other inconsequential thing, but when your eyes shift to me, I feel like I still want to live."

"You feel like dying otherwise?" Han Li gently rubbed Ruo's white hair.

"Of course, I feel like dying…" Ruo sniffed, and her body trembled. "Mom. I could never see her again. She is dead, after all. Her cooking, her stupid and annoyingly cute behavior, it is all in the past now."

"What if I tell you this is all but a dream?"

"I… wish."

"Would you mind losing all our memories together for another chance at life? A life where you can go back in time and save all of us?"

"I would, of course; as long as you fall in love with me, I can gain memories once again," Ruo sighed. "Memories are not impossible to create, but a person is. If I can, I will go back in time and save Mother first, somehow, then I will make you fall in love with me all over again."

Ruo looked at Han Li. "What would you do if you got the chance to go back in time?" she asked softly.

Han Li looked up at the sky. "I will find everyone and bring them all under me first. Then, I will find a way to attain eternal life for everyone I care about."

"Well…" Ruo took one of Han Li's hands and led it down into her pants. He smilingly complied while wondering how the current Ruo, without memories of her older self, would react if he did something like this.

She would freak out.

"It will be easy to make me fall for you. You just have to save me from my troubles."

"Ruo, tell me, what do you want me to do for you if I can go back in time?"

She hesitated for a second and just felt for the hand in her pants. Then, she beckoned him closer to her with her hand, and he closed his ear to her.

"If you go back in time, one day, come out of nowhere and hug me to sleep. I kinda love that the most in life now."

"Anything more?"

She shook her head, and Han Li kissed her forehead.

"Big Brother. I have a wish. You never handed it to mother. Will you give me children?"

"You want them?"

"Hmm. One or two—It's not like we have any more enemies or anything as long as we don't make much stir. Let us live the rest of our lives in this bamboo forest. With one or two little ones."

"Okay." Han Li sighed and went to the room with her in a princess carry. All week long, her moans echoed throughout the bamboo forest.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 239 - 239: Big belly
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the passage of time's whispering little hymns, days moved. In one small bamboo forest, in a bamboo hut, Ruo's belly started growing in size at some point, and she became very religious.

Han Li felt it to be quite dumb but he couldn't help but say it was the cutest thing ever.

Like, the white-haired woman had drawn a picture of their mother, and she would kneel in front of it with a puffy face the first thing in the morning. She would then cry or laugh, maybe even bicker, then pray for a good afterlife for their mother.

It was a funny sight, and it made him melancholic. Due to being so forgotten in hatred, he couldn't see this in his past life. The sight of a cute Ruo struggling with a pregnant belly and her wish to pray.

If he didn't wake up early in the morning and help her pray, the woman would totally blow her top and kick him awake. This was one fierce wife with no respect for her husband.

Praying had replaced their morning training. Han Li rarely ever trained anymore. Once a week or so. Most of the time, he would sit outside the bamboo hut and sing his flute. He was getting better at it.

Ruo would be gardening most of the time, but she would also pause to listen. She seemed to like mocking him a bit too much, though she was very abrasive about finding out his false.

"You are such an annoying little woman, aren't you?" he asked, irritated.

"Of course, you'd call me that when I call your flute bad. And, of course, I am pregnant and "

"Because it's not bad. It is better than any I've heard."

"Nah," Ruo rolled her eyes. "I've heard mother say that good music would make the children inside bellies dance. My little ones don't kick me in the gut when you sing, so it is bad."

"Nah, nah," Han Li gave her head a small smack. "They don't kick you because they know their dad would be mad at them if they did. They fear their old man."

"Yeah, yeah, big man," Ruo rubbed her belly and laughed. "You be the vicious dad, and I'll be the kind mom. Let's see who our babies love and who they hate. Don't come to me when they ignore you after growing up."

Han Li glared at her and rubbed his nose. "You are so irritating, and I can't even push you to bed and punish you for it because your belly is so fat."

"Oh god, it's not fat!" Ruo elbowed the side of his stomach, and Han Li grimaced. "I'll not even give you head if you say that again."

Her belly was not that big of an issue in having their bedtime. Pregnancy and sex was never a conflicting subject. One could have both at the same time if their was no complication. It was just that they liked to refrain from anything rough for now.

She liked it that way.

Han Li squinted at her. "So it is time, Miss Ruo."

"For what?" she squinted at him.

"I will now go out to buy milk. You should look after our kids well. Let me see if I can get head from somewhere else."

Her squint turned into a bemused glare. "You are incorrigible, you rascal. I always knew it. Did you fuck some other woman while you were out in the war?"

"Harsh," Han Li's expression darkened. "I was filled with thoughts of saving Mother and coming back to you; where did I have time to do something else?"

"Ah, ah, Han Li, do you think I am seeing my hubby for the first time?" Ruo pushed his face away from hers—his kiss attempt failed. "You can't lie to me, tell me. Who do you love the most, me or some other wench?"

"Of course. Nobody is more important than my Ruo to me now." Han Li hugged her and planted a forceful kiss on her cheeks. She wiped it away. He kissed again. She wiped it away. This time, Han Li's face darkened for real.

"What are you doing?"

Ruo rolled her eyes. "Who is this Liyue you speak of?"

"Li…Liyue? When did I talk about her?"

"I knew it, dumbass." She muttered under her breath and snorted. Sighing, she laid back on the wooden floor of their home and looked up at the sky. Han Li's gaze followed, and he gently clasped her hand.

Through the bamboo shoots, he could see the horizons. In the blue sky, white clouds floated past, along with some birds at times. Sun's harsh rays also gently drifted down in warm coziness.

"Do you want something to eat?" he asked.

"Says the cheater who told me nothing about this Liyue woman. I don't want anything."

"What if I say it is going to be something sweet and something you never ate before?"

Ruo hesitated. Her face scrunched up. "Get lost fuckface; I don't care."

Han Li smirked. "What if it is something good for the babies?"

"That's unfair!"

"You know what? Never mind." Han Li laid back on the floor himself. She blinked and scooted closer to him before using his hand as a pillow and his chest as something to hug.

Han Li smirked. "You are so easy. I just have to sleep beside you, and you come running to hug me."

"Hugging doesn't count. Maybe kissing, too. I am still very mad. It is just that my face doesn't show it. You see, acting angry is too tiresome, and I am very lazy…"

"Does an expression of bliss on your face count?"

"No, that doesn't count either, and let me tell you. This expression is because of the warm sunlight and totally not because of me hugging you."

"Surely."

"Why are you being sarcastic? I am very serious here."

"Surely not."

"Fuck you, believe whatever you want."

Han Li gently placed his hand on her belly and sighed. "Don't teach them your cocky personality, please. Make them more like me."

"Yeah, yeah, now I only have to go find this Liyue so that I can ask her to teach my children her personality."

"Eh…" Han Li sighed. "Liyue is a good girl, you don't have to be angry at her of all people out there."

"So she is good, and I am bad?" Ruo actually bit Han Li's cheek, and he could only sigh inwardly. Thankfully, his body was tough.

He hugged Ruo tightly. "So Ruo, now we will have to wait some more days before this place becomes noisy with children's cries and your shouts, huh? Our little peace would be broken."

"Don't villanize my children." Ruo smiled and hugged him back tightly. "They wont break the peace but make it more peaceful. No matter how many I have from now on."

Han Li didn't oppose it.

Ruo smiled. "Actually, Big Brother, I don't mind any Liyue. If you want to, maybe, bring her here and I will treat her like my younger sister. I am kinda lonely all by myself too—and I will have a woman to talk about all these things."

"Why do you need a woman for when you have me?" Han Li brooded.

"Oi, fuckface." Ruo bit his cheeks again. "I am letting you bring another woman home, so don't act like you are giving me charity. I am the one doing that. I have some things I can only talk about with other women."

"What kind of things?"

"I won't tell you, heh."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 240 - 240: Surprising Visit
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Nearly nine months had passed since Ruo became pregnant. She was bursting with what one could only call a humongous belly by now, and she couldn't even walk well at this point. She was bossing around her husband to get her needs done these days.

And her temper was at an all-time worst. Even though Han Li had once boasted that his children would have enough respect for them not to kick their mother's belly, there was no such thing.

At first, she laughed at the little kicks here and there that she felt. She even boasted about how she already knew the child's gender. She had guessed it by the force of the kick, apparently.

Han Li couldn't even know that with his Soul Sense.

Soul Sense at the Evolution Stage was weak. It could see far and wide, but it was like normal eyes in that it couldn't pierce through solid objects. It couldn't see into Ruo's belly. Only the Soul Sense of a Power Born could do that. Sadly, Han Li was just an Evolved.

A strong Evolved who could give a fight to Power Born, but still just Evolved.

Today, Han Li was grinding a special herb that he had picked up. Ruo had a really sweet mouth and she had been bugging him every night crying for something she had never tasted, he had decided to make something absolutely sweet.

So, so, so sweet, that it would make her hate sweet things forever. Nah, even he was not that mad—she was too vengeful of a woman to play that on.

Just as he was grinding the herb though, his eyes shifted around nervously. Red eyes became cold for a second before they relaxed. "Oh, it is you. Even you entered this illussion?"

A crow fluttered down into the bamboo forest and, amidst a bunch of scattering feathers, transformed into a divine goth beauty. Han Li looked towards the bamboo hut nearby and sighed.

"Thankfully, the little bomb is asleep now, or she would cook me alive. I might honestly not be allowed to see her delivery—she is crazy like that sometimes."

The goth woman straightened silently and looked around. Han Li did the same.

The old bamboo forest was now very different from nine months ago. It had become a Hamlet with hundreds of little bamboo houses—the bamboo forest remained but there were houses around now—and the houses were filled with people.

"Are you starting some tribe? Didn't expect you to be the type to do that."

"Nah, who are you kidding?" Han Li stretched himself, and his deeply carved physique, bare of any upper cloth, shined in the shadows of waving bamboo. "These people ended up creating houses around my own when they saw no beasts would dare to come near me."

"Oh, if you didn't like it, you could just shoo them away."

"Nah." Han Li smirked. "My wife is the lonely type—she is already friends with all of them."

"Yeah, for all her snarkiness, it's easy to like Ruo. She is very extroverted."

"Don't talk like you know her Spirit. Hmm, have you been watching me?"

"Yep. Since your mother died, I've been watching you."

"I just noticed." Damn. "You really can creep me out in the most subtle of ways. So, did you cast this illusion?"

The Spirit's form… glitched. It was like she was not a part of this reality. Something wrong. Almost like one of those bugs in video games that he had only seen in his mother's memories.

Han Li frowned.

"Well shit," The Spirit shrugged. "As you see, I didn't expect you to be conscious in this Vital Enlightenment. That never happens, you see. But here I am, again surprised by the impossible."

"What is this illusion meant to be?" Han Li asked.

"If I tell you, the chance of failure will be high. It would have been better if you weren't conscious. Sigh, well, you get what you get. I can only guess that your weird red eyes is the cause of you being so conscious now."

"So why did you come to meet me at this specific moment?"

"To give you some vital info, Partner." The goth beauty walked beside him and took a bunch of herbs before she joined him in grinding them. "I didn't want you to emotionally fall in some ditch. Everything here is fake—so don't fall in love with the tribe you would end up building here."

"Do you see me as some kid?" Han Li rolled his shoulders. He only had emotional investment on Ruo and that was it.

"That's it, Han Li. Are you really going to have kids inside an illusion?"

Han Li shrugged. "Ruo wanted them. And you don't have to question what I do."

"I have to. Now we are a team."

"Just shut up and get to the point. Why are you here?"

"I already have a vital weapon, and entering your mind is extremely exhausting because of that. But I still entered because I wanted to tell you one sole thing—live without regrets. Live a life that you'd laugh at no matter who says what."

Suddenly, she glitched out, and the only thing that remained was a black feather.

Han Li caught it and went deep into thought. Live a life without regret.

Such simple words. But the Spirit made the effort to come here and say that... she must have her reasons. But Han Li smiled. The Spirit had a slight misconception of what he was like.

He was already hell-bent on living a life that he wouldn't regret. Of course, he was not going to do anything else. He would listen to what his heart wanted.

The feather, too, transformed into glittering pearls and vanished.

Han Li felt a poke on his back and turned around to look.

Ruo was behind him, her face filled with an expression of consternation and her body dragging a huge belly with her. Face sweaty, she said, "I don't know who that was, and I don't care, Big Brother, but my belly is bursting. I feel like I am dying with pain. Please save your sweet Ruo."

That night, when the moon replaced the sun, a piercing cry of an infant filled the hamlet.

Han Li made something so sweet that day, he felt as if it was the sweetest thing ever. Maybe, sweet things were not so bad after all.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 241 - 241: Little Tyrant
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Three years had passed since the birth of little Han Fei (Han Li's first child).

She had grown up to be a very fierce little devil that was no less a little devil than her own mother. The small hamlet had now grown into a well and true city and she was the little tyrant around this area.

Oh? A tourist entering her city to see her Dad's efforts?

'Pay me a candy, you bitch.'

Han Li sometimes wondered where she got that vocabulary. Couldn't be from himself, he was a graceful man. If she was just a tyrant, it would have been okay, but the girl had a super strong dad who could put Power Borns in their place, apparently.

Three months ago, a stupid Power Born had come from nowhere with a momentous aura to take over the city and become its king.

But apparently, the shadow lord of the city, the dad of the tyrant daughter, had just touched the Power Born, and the Power Born immediately fainted. Then, it was the single slice of a black sword.

Red Eyed Devil. The sheer sight of that man's naked spine as he battled would send chills down a man. He was a legend.

Of course, Han Li just wanted to act cool in front of Ruo, who was watching him. She was closing Han Fei's eyes and ears desperately and calling him all sorts of obscenity for bringing violence into the city, but he could see the pride in her highly-held chest.

In the end, though, she made Little Fei ignore her dad for a whole day.

Ruo now had an incentive against him.

Oh, we got sidetracked.

The story of the Little Tyrant who asks for candies doesn't end there. The girl, the devil, the tyrant, was a storm. She not only had a strong dad, but she herself was, for some reason, crazy strong right from the day of her birth.

Apparently, when she was just three months old, she had slapped a tree and made it fall into splinters. When her little foot once touched a boulder, it had apparently been destroyed somewhat.

To her enraged mother, who didn't know what the fuck was going on, the dad only shrugged. When the mother got too enraged, the dad only closed the door, and the girl could hear noises from inside their home.

"Oh dear, I am so sorry!"

"I am dying!"

"Ahh!"

The house had echoed with her mother's cries.

Only hours later did she see her mother in fresh clothes and a slightly red face. From then on, the little Tyrant had been mad at her father. How dare he beat her mother. Only I, the Little Tyrant, was allowed to beat my father and mother.

"Humph. How dare you, Mr. Father?" she asked, still an immaturity in her tone as she put her hands on her hips and questioned.

Han Li looked at her sideways. "Whatever the Little Tyrant may be mad about this time?"

"I shall have my vengeance. How dare you harm my mother?"

Han Li's eyes twitched. "Did she send you to make an issue with me?"

"No."

Han Li looked at her, feeling a headache coming on. She had the same white hair as Ruo and the exact same temper. Moreover, she had the strength to back up her temper.

For some weird reason, the moment she was born, she was born with his refined Sacred Demon Skin, Organs, and Bones. His children seem to have the power to inherit his body Transformations, or she could be a special case.

That was good news. But the bad news was, he had had to use a unique magical resource—which he had acquired due to abnormally huge luck, to make her strength reduce to a certain level. She couldn't control it otherwise.

But even with that level of weakened power, she could beat up your average Evolved.

But still, she had no control over her strength.

She even broke Ruo's hands in an accident once, and then, the lass came crying for her dad to train her to control her powers.

She had been sad until Han Li healed her mother when she was not looking. Only then did she start to be the Little Tyrant again.

Han Li had been training her since then and now, she could already control quite a bit of her strength.

Her existence put everything in perspective for Han Li.

His life kinda revolved around her these days.

"So, what are you going to do about me beating Momma Ruo?" he asked.

Ruo came from the side of the bamboo forest gracefully and smirked. "If you beat her mom, her mom would beat you up first."

The Little Tyrant nodded. "Yes, we will beat him up together. Don't worry, Momma, he only looks strong, he is very easy to defeat. I only had to push slowly, and he died once."

Ruo gasped and went beside Han Li before slinking down on his back. She lazily laid her head against his shoulder and asked exaggeratedly, "You died once?"

"I did, but she brought me back to life with her gentle pats."

"You are so weak your little daughter defeated you. Shame."

Han Li moved slightly and slammed Ruo's whole body into the air. She was stunned and tried to fight, but Han Li easily curveballed her into the ground full of leaves, and she screamed as the ground neared her.

But then, she came to a halt and found herself looking at her husband's mocking face. He was carrying her in the old, princess way, and she was all panting in fear, hugging his neck, and looking wide-eyed.

"Daddy, you are awesome!" Han Fei screamed. "Teach Han Fei how to do that, or I'll death you like yesterday again!"

Han Li snorted and sat down. The Little Tyrant grew angry at how she was being ignored and gave him a kick. Han Li's eyes rolled, and he closed them.

He died. Even his tongue was out.

Ruo smiled. "Now revive him. He's your sweet dad, after all, and don't be so disrespectful."

The Little Tyrant nodded with a pout and said, "Pain, pain, go away."

Han Li's eyes snapped open. Then, he sighed. "I am back again. Back to the hell with two disrespectful women killing me all the time."

The duo shook their heads simultaneously. They seemed so alike. Together, they chuckled and said, ""You don't know, we do that because we love you.""

Soon enough, both of their curses echoed in Han Li's backyard.

Little Tyrant soon ran away, but Ruo was not going to get the chance—later that day, the Little Tyrant came to learn that her mother had taken a day full of spanking from her father.

"Poor mother, dad is so unworthy of you… Don't worry, Fei Fei will beat him up for real after I grow stronger."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 242 - 242: Fei Fei's Adventures
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

It was a serene night, and Fei Fei was not home. She was out.

Han Li gently embraced Ruo and pushed aside her white strands of hair to reveal her pretty face. He placed a kiss on her forehead.

"Am I growing old, hubby?" she asked.

"Of course not. You look as gorgeous as the first day I met you."

Ruo smiled, grabbed her man's hand, and placed it over her buttox. She whispered seductively into his ears, "Want another?"

It was as if something had been triggered as the door to their room slammed open. Han Li and Ruo both looked at the cute intruder with red faces. They hadn't kept their Soul Sense wide; they hadn't expected someone to come in.

Ruo blushed like a tomato. She almost seemed guilty.

Han Li smiled awkwardly. "Where have you been this late, Little Fei?"

The Little Tyrant's eyes twitched. She snorted and walked on top of the bed. Looking coldly at her dad, she ordered, "Move aside, Daddy. Give Little Fei's place back to her."

Han Li looked at her in irritation and scoffed. He just hugged Ruo tighter and his wifey smirked at the tyrant.

Their daughter fumed at them and growled. She gently pushed aside her mom from her dad's embrace and pushed her dad away forcefully without a hint of mercy.

He would not be hurt anyway. He was too strong. Humph.

"Mommy is Fei Fei's to hug, not yours." She said proudly and hugged her mother. Ruo smiled cheekily at Han Li.

"Oi Oi, little freak, have you heard of your father's reputation? He likes to throw unruly little children into rivers with monsters in them."

The Little Tyrant rolled her eyes. "I am the monster."

Han Li's eyes twitched.

She didn't let her dad hug her mom, and Ruo just kept chuckling.

"My child loves her mother way more than her father."

Fei Fei scoffed, but she didn't say anything to support that fact.

Looking scornfully at her father, she said, "If you want to hug Mom so bad, I don't mind you hugging us both."

Hmph. Hmph.

Han Li's eyes just twitched, and he kicked the girl out.

Hmph. Hmph.

He was the father.

Sadly, she could cry, and her cry was too effective on Ruo. He ended up not getting to lay with her that day. The little freak kept staring at him disdainfully from her mother's back. Crying had brought her back in here.

She really inherited all of Ruo's traits and all of his own stubbornness.

When she fell asleep on his chest like a little angel, though, Han Li just smiled in satisfaction.

Ruo hugged them both to sleep beside him.

This was the kind of life he wanted.

Forever.

To live without any regrets. To never die unless he wanted to.

***

Little Fei began to grow older.

Han Li and Ruo could do nothing about that even though both of them secretly hated that fact—their Little Tyrant should have forever remained little. Maybe all parents felt that way.

But it was the way of things, they just watched over her.

Ruo was way too overprotective of her and never even let her fall and bleed. Han Li was harsher, though; he did throw her into rivers with bigger monsters than her inside them.

The girl put salt into his tea as revenge.

He wanted to beat her up, but she knew how to escape beatings. Her mother was one way, and her cute acts were another.

She had her dad all in her hand. He loved her too much to hurt her.

Humph. Humph.

Just like that, simple years passed like water in a river.

She was as aggressive as ever, but as she grew older, her aggression shifted to the people outside their family instead of her dad and mom. She let them be.

Soon enough, Fei Fei became a truly spoiled young mistress.

Day in and day out, she would be going around trying out her powers on people who were nearly equal to her in strength—who had come to her city for many different reasons.

She was bullying someone at all times.

Sometimes, people who heard about the super-strong Evolved Han Li and had wanted to challenge him to see his mettle would be faced with his daughter instead and would get defeated by her.

Indeed, being born with Han Li's transformations made her fierce. He just looked at her lazily from the side as he felt his body age slowly.

When Han Fei was old enough, she Awakened her Spark and became a Cultivator.

It was a bizarre thing—her Spark was useless.

Her Spark seemed to do nothing. No Sparrow Transformation Powers of Ruo, nor any Lust Spark powers of her dad.

It was just there for no reason—no magic powers, no Dao, nothing. Nada.

Useless.

Han Li could guess the reason. She was not under the skies where the Heaven and its 3000 Daos and 7 Great Daos ruled supreme. She was in a world of his imagination.

Of course, Her Spark would not be useful if it was not the Lust Spark—that was HIS to give or take, not the Heavens. No powers worked inside this world other than his own.

Just like that, more years passed.

At 16, Fei Fei failed her Evolution Attempt, as everyone had predicted. Her spark came out of her body and went poof. Han Fei was infuriated.

She could already beat every Evolved there was, and now she couldn't even cultivate to their level. Just why?

Why couldn't she evolve? Why was her Spark alone so useless when others had clearly working Sparks?

Of course, others were all Sparks Han Li had seen in real life. It was being emulated in some way, but it didn't seem to work that way on Han Fei.

He just looked at her cute anger and smiled.

Fei Fei came to him one day, and she seemed hesitant to reveal her intention. She just asked her dad to tell her some stories and feeling melancholic, Han Li let her sleep on his chest as he told her about his life stories.

She didn't believe most of them.

The day after, a tearful Fei Fei left the city to travel around.

She even left a note for Han Li. He read it while looking at her back, which was growing further away.

"Father, your daughter is ashamed. She has failed your legacy—even dumbasses can Evolve, but your daughter of all couldn't. You should be ashamed of me. At least, I am. I shall come back stronger. Mother, don't spoil father too much.

Maximum of ten years, I won't be away for more than that. Don't you dare forget me or reduce your love for me!!"

"And don't you dare have another child!"

The parents could only sigh. She was Han Li's daughter, indeed.

Stubborn as a brick.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 243 - 243: Princess Fei
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The parents could only sigh. She was Han Li's daughter, indeed.

Stubborn as a brick.

Han Li and Ruo secretly followed her with smirks and watched all her battles from the sidelines.

Sometimes, she would be at the brink of death, and Ruo would be losing her shit in anger, but then, the seal over her Transformations would miraculously loosen, and she would be able to use the full power of her transformations.

She'd easily come out victorious.

Little Tyrant saved many villages from monsters and killed many evil beings. She came to be known as the Little Hero.

She beat the one who named it half to death and changed it into Big Heroine.

Ruo had a blast.

Fei Fei then walked through cities alone and met many young masters who coveted her beauty, many who wanted her for themselves. These were the times when Han Li nearly lost his calm.

Ruo was chuckling all throughout.

Fei Fei met thousands upon thousands of marriage confessions and powered through it all. She was too disdainful, too cold.

Noble clans chased her at times, but she outsmarted them and ran away after looting their houses. In all of the places she stole from, she left a messy drawing of a monkey's ass.

Ruo was speechless, and Han Li was covering his forehead.

"I didn't raise this graceless girl," he said.

"I did!" Ruo laughed.

But now, Fei Fei was actually making strong enemies, and if she didn't grow stronger, she'd be in trouble. Han Li smiled and carried Ruo behind him to follow the thief who had just run away from a noble house's backyard with a sack full of treasures.

In one place, in particular, Han Fei met an old man, and the old man said he was an old friend of her Dad.

She was delighted to hear some boastful stories of her dad and was full of awe. The old man gifted her a functional Spark when he faded away.

He had been a ghost all along who had remained in the world to fulfill the regret of boasting about Han Li. Apparently.

Han Fei felt slightly weird but pushed it out of her mind after enough time.

Now she had the Lust Spark. She could cultivate and evolve.

Using the Spark, she Evolved at the age of 21 and defeated her greatest enemy so far, an annoying and hateful Noble Clan.

Hmph.

Pathetic scum.

Her bloody name, the Cruel Princess, echoed all over the cities.

To her friends, she was an angel. To her enemies, she was the devil incarnate.

Now, she was finally not a shameful child. She could finally go back home. But before that, she wanted to achieve something great enough to make her family truly proud.

Merely becoming an Evolved was not enough.

She could nearly fight Power Born now, she guessed. Surely.

Without any wait, Han Fei directly went to a certain family to challenge a Power Born. The Power Born leader of this faily was famous for her power of the Moss.

She was named Hua Yu.

Han Fei was ready to head home after defeating the woman in battle.

The Little Tyrant finally knew what fear was.

I have been too arrogant…

Moss nearly drowned her to death.

"Dad, save me…"

Han Fei was full of fear.

Hua Yu easily defeated her but thankfully, let her go without killing her. But the woman seemed curious about her origins. In the end though, that was behind her.

Fuck doing great things, she was going back home.

Moving through a certain river atop a bamboo raft, Han Fei looked at the sky and adjusted her bamboo hat.

She was already 23. It had been more than seven years since she had seen her parents. Everything else felt worthless. She wanted to go home and annoy her father to death and bring him back to life.

They must miss me too, right?

She saw no way to progress anyway, so she shifted her bamboo raft's route and moved toward her home. At night, as she was drifting by, she heard the tender flirting that their parents only did when no one was around, and her eyes flashed open.

"Father…" she said with a choking tone.

She was riding on her father's back.

When? How?

They were climbing a mountain.

Looking at their faces, though, her expression changed, and she became silent.

They had both been around 20 when they first had her, but now, both of them looked older. Han Li seemed to have matured, and her mother's—who was flying on the side with her sparrow wings—white hair had lost its luster by a lot.

They were middle-aged now.

"But this doesn't make any sense…" She hugged her father's neck tightly, and her tears trickled down. "Doesn't Evolved live for 300 years? You two are young. Supposed to be young."

Han Li silently reached up the mountain and looked into the distance. He looked down at the rising sun. "Fei Fei. I don't know why, but your mother and I seem to be aging very fast. It must be because we are different from everyone else."

"Yeah, yeah," Ruo said, "Donno why, though. Your father tried a bunch of things to prolong our lifespan, but it didn't work."

Han Fei just silently cried.

She felt lonely a lot. She felt happy that her father was here to pick her up when she was finally ready to go back.

She felt choked with emotion.

She had lost a significant part of her life that she could have spent with her parents.

She and her bullshit pride was the reason for that.

Han Fei cried like a baby and her parents were only happy to oblige.

They built a new bamboo house by the mountain and began to live free of the city's hustle and bustle.

It was a serene life.

Many people found traces of the Cruel Princess and came to challenge her for a battle. Han Fei fought all of them and won all of them.

She was proud of her growth at least, and her father and mother always laughed at the scenes where she bullied arrogant young masters. They seemed to think that she'd marry anyone who defeated her in battle, but she scoffed at their idiotic thought process.

If you defeat me, I'll just go to my dad and make him beat you up, dumbo…

But even the challengers wouldn't have guessed the middle-aged couple who drank tea from the side calmly and laughed were not as ordinary as they seemed. Just like that, a couple more years passed, and her mother, Ruo, was full of complaints.

She could guess why, though.

Her face flushed as she looked at her father, who was sleeping naked beside her. Then she looked at her own clothless figure, and her blush deepened. She snuggled back up to her father and kissed him before falling asleep.

Mother, you can't blame me. Blame father for being such a playboy.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 244 - 244: Sneaky games
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ruo constantly snorted at them or twisted her ears painfully whenever she found the father and daughter in bed like that or flirting like a couple. But the duo kept repeating it without a bit of bad conscience.

His daughter was his, her father was hers.

They felt totally great.

Watching the distant sun falling onto the horizon, both of them smiled as they once again embraced each other. Yes, this was it. Truly great life was this.

Who cared about traveling?

Her mother didn't let her enter the house that day and made her sleep outside. Her bastard father never helped.

She could only fume inwardly.

"You don't deserve my pure heart, Daddy!" she cursed.

"You shouldn't have fallen to his looks then, dumb girl," her mother shouted back at her. "Now sleep on the grass and repent for your actions for stealing your mother's husband."

But on a wonderful day, Fei Fei found herself sleeping with her father… along with her mother. He had a proud smirk, and she gave him a secret high five.

He was a good dad; he finally did something good for his daughter.

From then on, Mother Ruo never complained.

Father did have a queer knack with women.

Nobody before had ever been able to move her heart so quickly.

Now, she could proudly say that as much as Han Li was her mothers, her dad was her own in all the ways possible.

And honestly, she didn't know he was way more aggressive than his daughter in bed until she was moaning in tiredness and hoping for a quicker release.

She chuckled with a flushed face as they watched the sun—all three of them.

Lovely.

***

Time passed again.

Their life was mundane.

Nights and days were spent without doing anything special other than their intimacy, but as people who had seen the darkness of the world—a world that was not at peace—the family of the trio just wanted peace.

Indeed, people who had experienced wars and people who hadn't had different views of the world.

Han Fei had gained new life goals.

Ruo taught her daughter the roots, and Han Fei was a proud woman deep down.

Even more prideful than her mother. So, she wanted to dominate her father at least for once. It was her great wish now, but he was too damn good at this, and he seemed to know her body up and down.

Every night, she tried to outplay him, but it never worked.

Luckily, she didn't gain a massive bulge in her stomach, though.

But she couldn't make him submit either. Her father was too good at this.

And he was stubborn.

"I am not going to let my own daughter win against me. If I do, wouldn't I die of shame?"

Humph. Humph.

Just like that, years streamed by, and before they knew it, this mountain also had a small population of its own. Distant tribes conglomerated around this place, and it became a small clan. They didn't shoo them away.

Her father also liked to play with some women of the tribe, to all the chagrin of many husbands who became wifeless due to moving here.

Her father was such a playboy.

And his stamina was even more monstrous than her own. After all, he had the purest form of the Organ Transformation that came from their bloodline.

His stamina would be more powerful, and he was also the true Dao Lord of Lust.

One day, he was swinging his black sword around in their mountain's meadow.

She moved up to him, pushing aside the wild grass, and asked curiously, "Daddy, why are you still training when you have already given up cultivating higher and all conflicts in general?"

He came to a halt and looked at her before the sword vanished from his hand. He moved closer to her, and she smirked as he pushed her down the meadow. Her hands were pinned hard before she could react.

"Here?" she asked with a slightly red face.

"Here."

"Answer me first," she asked.

"It is better to be a warrior in a garden than a gardener in a war," he said, and soon enough, she didn't need any answers.

His Little Tyrant became a moaning mess, and he had to carry her back home.

Her legs hurt.

"I wish these days would continue forever…" she said.

He kissed her.

"I do too," he said, "I do too."

I do, too.

***

Han Li was the first to grow too old.

Even in his final days, he still stayed fit and buff. For the past couple of months, he and his family had been in one bed most of the time. It was heartwrenching, but all three of them knew his time was due.

Maybe the more he was inside this illusion, the more mundane he was becoming.

During these days, if Han Fei had wanted, she could have gotten her lifelong wish to dominate her father; he was too old.

But she was still as proud underneath her emotional wreck of a self. She wouldn't do that to a helpless Han Li.

"Han Li…" Ruo, too, was old now. She held her husband's hand and said with a smile, "I will soon follow."

Han Li looked at her emotionally. "Are you satisfied with the life I gave you?"

Ruo snickered. "Don't ask me that."

Han Fei was silently holding back tears, her fair hand red and trembling. Her back, which was as straight as a sword, was bent in defeat, and her throat felt as if something was trying to crawl out of it.

Han Li took her hand. Even in his final moment, his hand didn't tremble.

Life was leaving him, though. He could sense his vitality diminishing by the moment.

"You can't bring me back to life this time," he said playfully.

Han Fei's hand only tightened. "Not a funny joke."

Han Li smirked painfully.

"If you find nothing to do in this world, Fei Fei." He said, feeling his emotions roil. "There are some people you would like to meet. They are probably around, and you might know when you meet them. People who age faster in this world like us even though they have higher cultivation."

Hua Yu was here, after all.

Maybe the rest were, too. She might meet them or might not.

If she could, he hoped they would remember her after this was over.

Han Fei bit her lips and nodded. "I will."

Han Li passed away.

A grief as great as all his other hidden grief was added to his heart.

He felt sick of this illusion. He didn't want to continue this.

It hurt so damn much. SO. SO. Damn much.

The Little Tyrant spent the next part of her life caring for her old mother, and when Ruo passed away, she buried her along with her father and left the mountain with a melancholic heart.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 245 - 245: What happened to Fei?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Han Li was revived.

He woke up one day on "Earth" and began living a very normal life there. For some reason, he regained his memories of his true life pretty early on.

And the Lust Dao—which was a part of himself was along with him.

Then, he lived with all the burdens for another lifetime. He was very blank, emotionless, and cold to everything.

School, University, Work. Normal life on Earth.

Illusions. All of these are illusions. I want this to end; maybe I should try dying in this illusion, too.

But he didn't commit suicide. That was not following his heart.

He didn't like that prospect.

Throughout the illusionary dream, he met many of his women, but he didn't bother with any. He had had enough of this illusion.

Let them live and let him die but not be killed. Let the illusion end, and let the reality begin. Kill. Kill. Kill.

Fei Fei…

Han Li broke the mirror before him with a punch, and blood oozed out of his fist.

Crack.

The wall behind the mirror also cracked lightly.

Life moved on.

One thing made him surprised. He had the lifespan of a simple mortal in this life too, even though he was an Evolved.

I wish I could live and die how I pleased.

As he grew old again, he ended up gathering all the women he met in this life—Ruo, Hua Yu, and Liyue—who had no memories of the previous illusions. They acted as if this was their first rodeo at living.

Old with all three of those women surrounding him, he shook his head inwardly. He had just grown colder due to this lifetime.

This all lacked meaning. Only eternal life and full control over it mattered.

Illusions.

He hated it; he did.

Han Li died with a sigh.

Fei Fei...

He lived hundreds of lives from then on and as many deaths, his face grew more and more cold. Why wasn't the illusion breaking?

Break. Break. Break. Break. Break.

Break already.

As he coldly walked amidst a war zone, he met a woman. She looked like a stranger. Pretty face, pretty eyes, pretty black hair, pretty everything.

He looked at her with disbelief.

He had never seen the woman. He never had any relationship with her, but for some reason, looking at her calmed his chaotic soul.

He sat down with a painful grimace and embraced her.

"Mom…" he called in disbelief.

After Yue Li died in the first illusion, she had never been there in any other illusions. He had always been an orphan in all the lives he had lived. But for some reason, he was sure, the clueless, dumb-faced woman sleeping amidst this warzone—she couldn't be anyone but her.

The golden halo over her Existence that he had seen on no one else but her. That was enough confirmation. She looked at him strangely, "Let me sleep. Where am I?"

Han Li awkwardly shifted in his place. "You are in a warzone."

She scratched her head. "Oh… a truck hit me, and I died, though. Oh shit, was I isekaied?"

Han Li nearly burst into laughter. "I know exactly what I want. I know what my ambitions are, why they are there and how much I want it."

It was time.

A poem came to his mind.

I've seen the most beautiful flowers, but they all smell the same.

I got to see the most beautiful places in the world, but the wind winded same everywhere.

Only in your presence, the wind whispered a fragrance to the flower,

a scent untold anywhere else.

Fuck illusions. He was breaking out.

Red eyes glinted with unshed tears as everything broke apart and turned to smoke.

Han Li opened his eyes to the real world.

Clouds drifted up in the skies, and a sense of incredible reality immediately spread throughout his being. He was sleeping above a watery substance. The illusionary life in his mind vanished one after the other, and his confused mind became incredibly vivid.

Before him, the Spirit floated gently in its semi-transparent form.

"I told you not to grow attached," she said with a sigh.

Han Li gritted his teeth, but his tears couldn't help but fall.

The Spirit sighed again. "Crying is of no use."

Han Li felt Ruo's weight against his chest and smelled Liyue's scent. He felt Yue Li just near him. There was also Hua Yu. All of them were just beside him. They were real and lifelike. Most probably, they remembered everything in the illusion.

"Am I in the real world?" he asked, pushing back his hair with an anxious expression.

Suddenly, Yue Li woke up with a start and sat up. Seeing Han Li's red eyes, she calmed down and stretched.

"Xiao Li… I feel like I slept forever…"

Han Li held her hand.

Ruo was sleeping against his chest, but slowly, her blurry eyes opened, and she looked straight at him with a sharpness the girl had lacked before.

Han Li looked at her with a complicated expression.

The girl blinked for a second before she clenched his collar. "My Fei Fei. I want her!"

She sounded more desperate than threatening.

Han Li looked up at the skies. "So, indeed, you were there, experiencing my memories and other fragmented realities as illusions."

She had experienced everything as the Ruo of his memories while being herself. She had seen all that had to be seen in one way or the other.

He wondered what kind of memories she had gotten from the Ruo of the past life…

Ruo seemed to be near a panic attack; she kept muttering Fei Fei again and again. Han Li was silent for a second, and as he closed his eyes, a tear streaked down his cheek.

Ruo nearly burst with fear.

"Don't be confused. These are not tears of grief, Ruo…" he said and rubbed off his tears. His strong aura was now wavering with gentleness and fear, but there was hope; there was genuine gratefulness to his expression.

All of the women surrounding him looked at the scene in silence.

Out of all the illusions, only the one with the Little Tyrant was so life-changing. The rest were all just fading from their minds.

The Spirit took a deep breath. "So, did you all gain your Vital Weapons?"

Han Li, Hua Yu, Liyue, Yue Li, Ruo, and the Spirit. Only these many had been inside the memory scape of illusions and the Vital Enlightenment. They were going to gain a Vital Weapon of their own if they survived without losing their heart.

They just needed to figure out what their heart wanted deep down in each life. As long as it was the same thing in every life, their Vital Weapon would awaken.

In all of Han Li's innumerable lives inside the illusion, he only had one dream. To not lose people to death and to not be incapable of doing anything.

Hua Yu and Liyue lowered their heads. "I didn't get anything… It's just not there." Liyue said, her face downcast. "I failed to get it."

Hua Yu nodded with a sigh.

Yue Li seemed to understand Ruo's and Han Li's mood and she didn't say anything and just watched her kids.

Han Li spread his hands wide at the sky.

They were above a calm ocean-like place with no waves. They were sleeping above mythical waters that acted like land. The waters reflected the sky. It was beautiful.

Suddenly, a woman materialized out of thin air beside them all and everyone, including the Spirit, stared at her with wide eyes. The girl was of utmost beauty and there was a sort of arrogance to her face that seemed etched in her bones.

White hair tumbled behind her while a katana rested on her hips. She was in a black kimono, and her eyes disdainfully glanced at everyone around before they focused on Han Li and Ruo.

She kicked her father on the side, "What are you laying down there for?"

"Who are you kicking?" he asked irritably.

Both of them suddenly couldn't stop their chuckling, and Ruo looked nervously between them.

The Spirit asked with fear, "What is going on?"

Han Fei looked straight at it with curious eyes. "An Existence? Funny sight."

Han Li looked at the calmly floating clouds.

He could already feel Liyue's and his Mother's eyes staring daggers at his neck. They were going to kill him.

"She is Han Fei. My first daughter and my Vital Weapon."

Liyue looked away in absolute anger. Yue Li was a bit awkward, Hua Yu was curious, and the Spirit was just in shock.

Han Fei stared around in curiosity. "A Funny—"

"Shut up, and don't add oil to the fire."

He unsummoned her.
